En: If he really wants to save the economy he'd let you pay with net worth. Go over and ask him if you can
Zh: 如果他真的想拯救经济，他会让你用净资产支付。过去问他可不可以。

En: How about some premium-class service over here? Like paying with net worth. Go over and ask him if you can do that.
Zh: 这里的高级会员服务怎么样？比如用净资产支付。过去问他可不可以。

En: [Leave for now.]
Zh: [暂时离开。]

En: Find something worth salvaging from that pile of rags.
Zh: 从那堆破烂里找出一些可以废物再利用的东西。

En: Dresscode: Rummager.
Zh: 着装要求：翻查者。

En: There are clothes inside. Cheap second-hand clothes, smelling of strangers' body odours.
Zh: 里面有衣服。便宜的二手服饰，闻起来有陌生人的体味。

En: You find your hands deep in tattered and faded garments made from weird polyester blends that make your body itch and sweat in all the wrong places.
Zh: 你发现自己的双手深陷在一堆破旧褪色的衣服里——是由古怪的涤纶混纺制成的，它们让你身上所有错误的地方开始发痒、出汗。

En: Browse through the box.
Zh: 浏览箱子。

En: Something cold grazes your hand. Synthetic and sleek -- a windbreaker. 'SURF', it says, but also: 'WIND', 'SUMMER', '100% WATERPROOF', and 'SPORT'. All in different typefaces.
Zh: 有些冰冷的东西擦伤了你的手。人造的，很光滑——是一件防风夹克。上面写着“冲浪”，还有：“风”，“夏天”，“100%防水”还有“运动”。所有字体都不一样。

En: No good. All you can come up with are some treated wool pants. You can tell they're too tight around your thighs and too loose around the waist.
Zh: 不太好。你唯一发现的只有些处理过的羊毛裤子。你能看出大腿的位置太紧，或者腰部周围太松。

En: I want to buy the windbreaker.
Zh: “我想买这件防风夹克。”

En: I want to buy those itchy pants.
Zh: “我想买那些痒痒的裤子。”

En: You won't regret it, officer! The street vendor smiles broadly.
Zh: “你不会后悔的，警官！”街头小贩笑的更欢了。

En: Gotta prepare for springtime, right? The street vendor seems pleased.
Zh: “为春天做准备，是吗？”街头小贩好像很高兴。

En: Economical, but also trendy! the street vendor shouts. "Look first-hand, buy second-hand. Keep the economy moving!"
Zh: “经济划算，不过也很时髦！”街头小贩嚷嚷着。“看就看一手的，买就买二手的。保持经济发展！“

En: The box smells like cat piss -- or like an old person with no money.
Zh: 箱子闻起来有股猫尿味——或者是一个没钱的老人家的味道。

En: Don't be shy, these are premium-class clothes! Good quality fabrics, best retro design! Save the economy with your style, officer!
Zh: “别害羞，这些可是高档衣服！面料高档，最棒的复古设计！用你的风格拯救经济吧，警官！”

En: THE ITCH IS SO BAD YOU'LL WANT TO SMASH SOMEONE'S FACE IN.
Zh: 实在是太痒了，让你想把别人的脸打烂。

En: All natural materials, see? The street vendor interrupts, guiding your hand over the fabric. "Real wool, 100% skin-friendly! And they're yours for only 2 reál!"
Zh: “全都是纯天然材料，看见没？”街头小贩打断了你，拉着你的手拂过面料。“真羊毛，对皮肤100%友好！只要2雷亚尔就是你的啦！”

En: Save the economy? What are you talking about?
Zh: “拯救经济？你在说什么呢？”

En: Haven't you heard, officer? We've got to be economically conscious. Recycle your cash, keep it in circulation! Don't buy new things! Buy eco!
Zh: “你没听说吗，警官？我们必须有经济意识。回收你的现金，保持流通！别买新东西！买下经济！”

En: Save the economy? That sounds off.
Zh: 拯救经济？听起来有点怪。

En: This jacket is the apex of human evolution -- the moment at which man became weatherproof.
Zh: 这件夹克是人类进化的顶峰——就在那一刻，人类将不再受到天气的影响。

En: Practical, and yet it may deaden your senses to the world around you. Possibly because of the awful typeface.
Zh: 实用，而且也许会让你对周围世界的感觉更加麻木。很可能是因为那个可怕的字体吧。

En: Good choice, officer! Mega-sporty. And it's only 4.50 for you, sir!
Zh: “选的好，警官！超级轻便。给你的话，只要4.5雷亚尔，先生！”

En: This doesn't make any sense. Why exactly does the economy need saving?
Zh: “这根本说不通啊。为什么经济需要被拯救呢？”

En: But I don't have children. I think.
Zh: “但是我没有小孩啊。我觉得。”

En: You're right. We've got to save Mother Economy!
Zh: “你说得对。我们必须拯救经济母亲！”

En: It's just nature: Powerful economies expand, weak economies go extinct.
Zh: “这很自然：强大的经济会扩展，虚弱的经济会灭绝。”

En: Touch them.
Zh: 碰触它们。

En: They itch. Like hell. Like an itch-pit in hell.
Zh: 它们很痒。超级痒。就像掉进了地狱里的痒痒窝。

En: Discard the woolly pants.
Zh: 把羊毛裤子扔掉。

En: This *economy* is really just a distraction from the cultural issues. You know -- *immigrants* and...
Zh: “这个*经济*其实只是为了转移对文化问题的注意力。你知道的——*移民*还有……”

En: Look man, I just want to buy some clothes.
Zh: “老兄，我只想买些衣服。”

En: Look around, officer! You see all these premium goods, just sitting there, *not* getting bought? We've got to keep the flow of goods moving!
Zh: “看看周围，警官！看见这些高档货了吗，就这么坐在那里，*无人*问津？我们必须保持货物流通！”

En: Is this really the economy we want to leave to our children?
Zh: “这真是我们想要留给后代的经济吗？”

En: And what?
Zh: “还有什么？”

En: And genetic atrophy.
Zh: “还有遗传萎缩。”

En: That's good, officer! That's exactly what the economy wants you to do.
Zh: “很好啊，警官！经济就想让你这么做。”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: Oh. The street vendor pauses. "Look man, I'm not into *politics*. I like everyone. Check out the box!"
Zh: “哦。”街头小贩停顿下来。“老兄，我对*政治*不感兴趣。我喜欢所有人。快来看看这个箱子吧！”

En: Doesn't matter, the street vendor replies. "All that matters is the box. Check it out, officer."
Zh: “不要紧，”街头小贩回复到。“要紧的只有这个箱子。快来看看吧，警官。”

En: Too bad, officer. Kids make it all worthwhile. Without kids, who's going to be around to enjoy the economy? He points at the clothes. "Don't let me stop you. Open the box and browse a little!"
Zh: “太可惜了，警官。孩子让这一切都变得有意义。没了孩子，还有谁来享受经济呢？”他指向衣服。“别让我阻止了你。打开箱子，稍微看一眼吧！”

En: That's the spirit! We've all got to do our part. Your part is to open the box and buy some clothes.
Zh: “就是这种精神！我们都要尽自己的一份力。而你的那份就是打开这个箱子，然后买点衣服。”

En: I can't go extinct, officer! I've got kids to feed. Once an economy goes extinct, it messes up the whole eco-system. You got to think about the consequences!
Zh: “我不可以灭绝，警官！我还有孩子要养。如果一种经济灭绝，它就会破坏整个经济系统。你要考虑一下后果！”

En: No luck? the street vendor says, visibly disappointed. "Why not take another look, officer? Keep supporting the economy!"
Zh: “运气不佳？”街头小贩说着，明显很失望。“为什么不再看一眼呢，警官？继续支持经济！”

En: Thanks for the advice, I'll try to be more economically conscious.
Zh: “感谢你的建议，我会努力多注意一些经济问题的。”

En: You keep coming back. That's good, officer! Keep browsing those clothes, keep saving that economy!
Zh: “你又回来了。真的太棒了，警官！继续看看那些衣服，继续拯救经济吧！”

En: Very cool. The economy thanks you, officer.
Zh: “太酷了。经济谢谢你，警官。”

En: Those socialists in academia.
Zh: “那些学术界的社会党。”

En: Stylish shades, huh? They'd be even more stylish if you paid for them with net worth. Go over and ask him.
Zh: 时尚的墨镜，是吧？如果你能用净资产买的话就更时尚了。过去问他。

En: Alright, but these actually make your vision worse. It's like literally being under water.
Zh: 好吧，但是这样确实让你的视力变得更差了。真的感觉像在水下一样。

En: Yes, but they also make your soul quiver like jello. So deep.
Zh: 是的，但是它们也会让你的灵魂像果冻一样颤抖。如此深的地方。

En: The shine on these sunglasses lasts a lifetime, officer! One hundred percent guaranteed!
Zh: “警官，这些墨镜的光芒一生都不会褪色！100%保证！”

En: There's a pile of cheap sunglasses in a small box, a variety of shapes and colours.
Zh: 一个小盒子里装着一堆廉价墨镜，种类各异，颜色众多。

En: Rummage through the box.
Zh: 在箱子里翻找。

En: [Leave for now.]
Zh: [暂时先离开。]

En: Try again, maybe you can find *some* interesting sunglasses in the box.
Zh: 再试一次，也许你可以在箱子里找到*一些*有意思的墨镜。

En: Dresscode: Rummager.
Zh: 着装要求：翻查者。

En: These are all boring. Boring third-rate ho-hum sunglasses made of cheap Seraise plastic. The kind of plastic that melts in the sun.
Zh: 这些都太无聊了。无聊的让人直打哈欠的三流墨镜，用廉价的萨弗里塑料做出来的。这类塑料在太阳底下会融化。

En: Ah, I see... A pair of waterblue shades. The writing on the left temple says: "Sub-Insulindic Rendezvous." The frame appears to be hand-carved out of bone.
Zh: 啊，我看见了……一副水蓝色墨镜。左侧的镜腿上写着：“伊苏林迪附属集合点”。镜框好像是用骨头手工雕刻出来的。

En: No luck. All you find is this... lime-coloured cellophane visor? Produced by a bargain sportswear brand called Amphibian, apparently. There's a malformed green frog on its bent cap.
Zh: 运气不佳。你唯一能找到的只有……绿黄色的玻璃纸遮阳帽？似乎是一个价廉物美的运动品牌，叫两栖动物。弯折的帽檐上有一只难看的绿青蛙。

En: Hey, at least it might actually offer some protection against the sun, not like those cheap plastic shades.
Zh: 喂，至少还是有可能遮挡一些太阳光的，不像那些廉价的塑料墨镜。

En: Those UV-stickers are almost certainly just there for show. If anything, these lenses probably direct more UV light *into* your pupils -- a UV magnifier.
Zh: 那些防紫外线标签很可能只是作秀用的。如果有什么区别的话，这些镜片可能会让更多紫外线*照射进*你的瞳孔——它就是个紫外线放大镜。

En: (Turn to the lieutenant.) "Kim, are firefights something we should be prepared for?"
Zh: （转向警督。）“金，交火需要我们提前做准备吗？”

En: Put the visor back.
Zh: 把遮阳帽放回去。

En: You don't like it? Sure, square-jaw, no problem. Let's get you some real shades.
Zh: “你不喜欢？当然了，方下巴，没问题。让我帮你找些真正的墨镜吧。”

En: I hope not, he says, looking up from his browsing.
Zh: “希望不用，”他停止浏览，抬起头说道。

En: Give me those shades you recommended. (Try them on.)
Zh: “把你推荐的墨镜给我。”（试戴一下。）

En: Try the shades on.
Zh: 试戴墨镜。

En: Abort! These are hideous. What's more, they don't even fit your face. You can feel them pinching your nose and chafing against your brow.
Zh: 中止行动！这玩意太惨不忍睹了。更重要的是，它们甚至都不合适你的脸。你感觉它们箍紧了你的鼻子，蹭到了你的眉毛。

En: No, the lieutenant gently removes the glasses from your face, setting you free again. "You're definitely not buying those."
Zh: “不，”警督轻轻从你脸上取下墨镜，让你重获自由。“你绝对不能买这个。”

En: Don't tell me what to do, Kim. I like those sunglasses.
Zh: “别告诉我该怎么做，金。我喜欢那些墨镜。”

En: You're right, I'm too sensible for those.
Zh: “你说得对，我对那些东西太敏感了。”

En: Damn, officer, you look like a mega-secret spy, very secret, the man nods eagerly. "They're practically made for you. I'll let you have them for... two reál and fifty cents!"
Zh: “该死，警官，你看起来像是一个秘密间谍，非常神秘，”男人急切地点点头。“它们完全就是为你而生的。那就卖给你……2.5雷亚尔吧。”

En: No, I can't... We can't walk around with you looking like this. His hand has already risen mid-air, but he stops. "Okay, fine. Go ahead. If you want to look like a walking mid-life crisis, then who am I to interfere?"
Zh: “不，我不能……肯定不能让你这个样子到处跑。”他的手已经举到了半空中，不过却停住了。“好吧，好吧。随你吧。如果你希望自己看起来像是个会行走的中年危机，那我又为什么要干涉你呢。”

En: Are you sure? But they look so good on you! The street vendor frowns. "You should think this through, officer."
Zh: “你确定？但是你戴起来很好看啊！”街头小贩皱起眉头。“你应该好好考虑一下，警官。”

En: I'll take those ugly glasses.
Zh: “我要那副丑墨镜。”

En: A good starter pair, officer! And when you're ready to upgrade, come back and see me!
Zh: “作为新手来说挺不错的，警官！等你准备好升级的时候，记得回来找我哦！”

En: A man who knows his style, the street vendor nods. "Much respect."
Zh: “你是个很了解自己的风格的男人，”街头小贩点点头。“我尊重你。”

En: Sporty *and* practical, officer! Good choice.
Zh: “运动*又*实用，警官！很不错的选择。”

En: The lieutenant tilts his head and steps back, eyes narrowed in a thorough examination. It's a case to him.
Zh: 警督歪着头，后退一步，眯起眼睛打量着，好像这对他来说是一出案子。

En: What, change your mind, officer?
Zh: “怎么，改变主意了，警官？”

En: They're perfect for concealing your bloodshot and baggy eyes.
Zh: 它们完美的遮住了你那双充满血丝又肿胀的眼睛。

En: It's going to be very difficult for anyone to take you seriously with these things on your face.
Zh: 脸上戴着这种玩意，别人真的很难把你当回事。

En: You look like a musician, he declares, eyes still squinted behind his own glasses. "Like a *blind* musician. But you could do worse. Take them if you want."
Zh: “你看起来像个乐师，''他宣布，依旧在镜片后面眯着眼睛，''一个*盲人*乐师。但是你选的可能更糟糕。想要就买了吧。

En: This is how a sea monster sees the world. You've become a sea monster, Harry -- giant, hidden and... strangely tender at heart. All is blue.
Zh: 海底怪兽就是这样看待这个世界的。你变成了一个海底怪兽——巨大，隐蔽，而且……内心异常温柔。全都是蓝色的。

En: This is how a sea monster sees the world. You've become a sea monster -- giant, hidden and... strangely tender at heart. All is blue.
Zh: 海底怪兽就是这样看待这个世界的。你变成了一个海底怪兽——巨大，隐蔽，而且……内心异常温柔。全都是蓝色的。

En: And they can be yours for a mere three reál. My regular customers have passed them all up because they've got no taste, but *you* found them...
Zh: “只要3雷亚尔，它就是你的了。我的老主顾都没看上，因为他们都没品味，但是*你*却发现它们了……”

En: The lieutenant's reverse psychology fourberie doesn't work on you. If you want to look like a mega-secret mid-life crisis, that's your choice.
Zh: 警督的激将法对你不管用。如果你希望自己看起来像是个超级神秘的中年危机男子，这就会是你的选择。

En: You like sunglasses, officer? I've got the latest styles, right here! The vendor takes a pair of sunglasses and sticks them under your nose.
Zh: “你喜欢墨镜吗，警官？我这里有最新款的！”小贩取出一副墨镜，递到了你的鼻子下面。

En: These are all first-rate sunglasses! the man declares. "Premium design, superb material, *very cool* UV-resistant! These will definitely keep your eyes safe *and* cool while doing your dangerous police work."
Zh: “这些都是最上等的墨镜！”男人声明着。“高端设计，上乘材质，*超级酷炫*的防紫外线功能！它们肯定能保护你的眼睛，*同时*在做高危警察工作的时候，为你增添一丝酷劲。”

En: Try them on.
Zh: 试戴一下。

En: Oh, very interesting choice, officer, very *high-culture*... For the first time the street vendor's voice trails off, as he watches you inspect the glasses.
Zh: “哦，真是个有趣的选择，警官，非常*阳春白雪*……”看着检查眼镜的你，街头小贩的声音第一次变小了。

En: Wow, officer, you look so cool, the street vendor has picked up his pace again, as you observe the world through deep sea-tinted lenses.
Zh: “哇哦，警官，你看起来真酷，”当你透过深海色的镜片观察这个世界的时候，街头小贩又加快了节奏。

En: Kim, what about these?
Zh: “金，这个怎么样？”

En: Put them back.
Zh: 把它放回去。

En: I'll take those cool deep blue glasses.
Zh: “我要那副酷炫的深蓝色墨镜。”

En: I'll take that Amphibian sports visor.
Zh: “我要那个两栖动物运动遮阳帽。”

En: Oh, that visor is perfect for you, officer. It'll definitely keep the sun out of your eyes while you're shooting criminals. The street vendor makes a bang-bang sound. "And all for a mere six reál."
Zh: “哦，那个遮阳帽对你来说简直就是完美，警官。在你朝犯罪分子开枪的时候，它肯定能保护你的眼睛不受太阳光伤害。”街头小贩发出砰砰的声响。“这些都只要6雷亚尔。”

En: A pity, the lieutenant says. "Those actually weren't half bad."
Zh: “很遗憾，”警督说道。“那些其实还算不错。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Just plug that in there, would you?
Zh: “就插在那里，好吗？”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Got it. Are we ready?
Zh: “知道了，咱们准备妥当了吗？”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: It's done. I believe we're ready. The lieutenant wipes his brow.
Zh: “已经可以了。我觉得咱们已经准备妥当了。”警督擦了擦眉毛。

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Yeah, as ready as we're going to be. He cracks his neck before putting on a pair of headphones. "Grab one of these can sets, both of you. I've got it rigged so that we can all listen, but only your cop-talk will broadcast..."
Zh: “是啊，再准备下去也就这样了。”他扭了扭脖子再把耳机戴上。“拿个这样的罐头耳机，你们俩都得拿。我已经把它装好了，这样我们都可以听，但只有你俩的警察谈话会广播出去……”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Yeah, as ready as we're going to be. He cracks his neck before putting on a pair of headphones. "Grab one of these can sets. I've got it rigged so that we can both listen, but only your cop-talk will broadcast..."
Zh: “是啊，就像我们要准备的那样。”在戴上耳机之前，他扭了扭脖子。“拿个这样的罐头耳机。我已经把它装好了，这样咱俩都可以听，但只有你的警察谈话会广播……”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: No idea what we're gonna hear when I turn this thing on, so be prepared for *anything*. Trick is to keep transmitting your request until the Big Bad acknowledges you. You got that?
Zh: “我完全不知道当我打开这东西时咱们会听到什么，所以准备好，*任何事都有可能*。诀窍在于要不断发送你的请求，直到大坏蛋们收到你的信号。你明白了吗？”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Wait, what if they *never* acknowledge me?
Zh: “等等，万一他们*一直都*接收不到呢？”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Yes.
Zh: 是的

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Hold on, I need a minute.
Zh: “等一下，就一分钟。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Honestly, that might be for the best. Like I said, the sines coming off this whole project are seriously uncharted...
Zh: “老实说，这样也许才是就最好的结果。就像我说的，这整个项目下来的正弦波是极不确定的未知数……”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: “嗯。这里写着，'主要步骤：音量调到最大‘…… …… 这条能顶什么用。好吧，还写着说：'检查连接的硬核清晰度'，让我们试试……”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: You don't exactly cut a lithesome figure, but after several moments of scrambling you manage to hoist yourself atop the monument.
Zh: 你并不是一个体态轻盈的人，但经过几分钟的攀爬后，你终于爬到了纪念碑的顶端。

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: Your gloves give you a solid grip on the metal bar. This feels pleasingly familiar...
Zh: 你的手套得以让你牢牢抓住金属棒。这给你一种愉悦的熟悉感……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: 因为下雨，金属棒摸起来冰冷而滑腻。有那么一瞬间，你觉得自己好像要滑脱了，但你把所有的注意力都集中在胸肌上，然后发力……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: 振作起来，冠军！你能行……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: 滑石粉。可以吸收水分，使你的手握得更牢。

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: 你的握力很大，但同时也在控制着力道。耳机里的声音引导着你的手，就像你的手引导着青铜马头一样，简直就像你在*通过*马来听取声音……

En: Firewalker, this is Coalition Warship Archer. We are acknowledging and accepting you.

Zh: “接通了，大坏蛋。赶紧的，我会尽量让我们保持在线……”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: 就是这样……你终于可以和手中握有*真正*权力的人说话了。

Zh: 我们是指那些拥有大炮和战舰的人……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: 你有很多话想问。你的努力为你争取了一些时间，但你不能忘记你来这里的真正目的……

Zh: 你有很多话想问，但在失去信号之前你可能只有一两个问题的时间……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: ”可能是因为你把它弄坏了吧…“

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: “事实上，只有尝试是不够的。现在结果才是最重要的…”

Zh: “听着，我并不喜欢指责别人。我只是想说，事情本来就很糟糕了，这会儿却更糟糕了…… ”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

Zh: “我要把所有的旋钮调到最大。可能会烧掉我们的电源，但这也许是唯一的办法了……”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
En: That piece of junk didn't even work!
Zh: “那件垃圾根本没用！”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION
JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- NOID VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: A soft rustling. The snow seems to have gotten between your ears somehow...
Zh: 一阵轻微的沙沙声。雪花不知怎地落进了你的脑袋里……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: A soft rustling. Between your ears, a winterscape with fast falling snow...
Zh: 一阵轻微的沙沙声。你的脑中是一片冬景，雪花飞速坠下……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: “至少我们已经可以开始试试了。请把耳麦戴上，我已经把频道配置好了，只有你的麦克风被设置为打开。我们都能听得到你们的谈话，但与我们连线的人都只能听到*你的*声音……”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Where's the sound coming from?
Zh: 声音是从哪里来的？

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: 马丁内斯的每一个电灯开关，每一辆車，每一个门铃，每一个茶壶以及每一台收音机，都和欧佐纳尔上空遍布的雷雨造成的信号干扰混杂在一起。

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: “联盟战舰弓箭手号，这里是RCM警官龙舌兰日落。收到请确认。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Coalition Warship Archer, this is Detective Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau of the RCM. Please acknowledge.
Zh: “联盟战舰弓箭手号，这里是RCM的拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托警探，收到请确认。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Coalition Warship Archer, this is Lieutenant Harrier du Bois of the RCM. Please acknowledge.
Zh: “联盟战舰弓箭手号，这里是RCM的哈里尔·杜博阿警督，收到请确认。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: What should I say?
Zh: “我应该说什么？”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: You're all alone out there, wandering a blasted heath, calling out to the night, but there is no reply, except for the buzzing of invisible machines...
Zh: 你独自一人在外徘徊，在一片荒凉的草地上呼唤着黑夜，但除了隐形机器的嗡嗡声没收到任何回应……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: 我……有……有……有人…… 在……在吗？

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: The lieutenant looks up at you with a nervous glance.
Zh: 警督紧张地注视着你。

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: He is nervous for your sake, of course, but also his own.
Zh: 他这么紧张当然是因为你，但也有他自己的缘故……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: “再试一次，长官。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: 在为了谁而紧张，只是你说不出来。

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: 看着他现在双手按着耳机的样子，你能看到警督刚加入RCM时的模样……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Then he turns to you. His expression seems to say, 'You'd better not leave this mess for me alone'.
Zh: 然后他看向你。他的表情好像在说，“你最好不要把这个烂摊子抛给我一个人”……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Then he turns to you. He gives you a half smile along with an almost imperceptible nod of encouragement.
Zh: 然后他看向你。他冲你咧开半张嘴笑了一下，还以几乎无法察觉到的幅度颔首以表鼓励……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: 然后他看向你。他微微摇了摇头，然后回过头看着增幅器……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Perhaps you're simply imagining it, but it seems as though you're learning to pull apart the fibres of this auditory felt. You focus on one strand in particular, one that sounds very nearly human...
Zh: 也许你只是单纯在想象，但似乎你正在学会将这张听觉构成的毡子上的纤维一条条撕开。你把注意力集中在其中的一条上，一条听起来非常接近人声的纤维……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: What does this remind me of?
Zh: 这让我想起了什么？

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Soona, is this more of that entroponetic crosstalk?

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Keep listening.
Zh: 继续听下去。

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: “这些鸟语我一个字也听不懂。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: 就像电门铃里的那个女人一样……电路里的鬼魂……

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: 秋叶窸窣作响。远处涛声阵阵。千翼同时振翅飞翔。

Zh: “苏娜，这台收音机里好像有鬼。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: What's going on?
Zh: “发生了什么事？”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Liebling, bitte… Er ist unser einziges Kind. Er kann doch auch nächstes Jahr zur Akademie gehen.
Zh: “亲亲，倷做个好宁……吾笃就伊一个倪子，来年伊好剋学堂哉。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION
En: Not ghosts, officer. Crosstalk. You have to keep trying until we get a signal through.
Zh: “不是鬼，长官，串线而已，你必须接着试，直到我们有信号连上。”

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

ConvTitle: JAM / COALITION WARSHIP ARCHER -- SOONA VERSION

Zh: “这是串线。他们很可能听不到你的声音。你必须继续尝试，直到我们有信号连上。”

Zh: 想到这些声音在空气中飞行着， 直到你用你的小金属天线捕捉到它们，像萤火虫一样，你觉得莫名其妙的舒心……

En: Who is this?

En: A silver plaque on the statue's pedestal reads: 'I am Filippe III, the Squanderer, the Greatest of the Filippian Kings of Revachol; Son of Filippe II, the Opulent; Father of Filippe IV, the Insane.'
Zh: 雕像基座上有一块银色饰板，上面写着：“我，放荡者菲利普三世，瑞瓦肖菲利普家族最伟大的国王；菲利普二世，丰饶者的儿子；菲利普四世，疯狂者的父亲。”

En: Not a good track record of mental health in that family.
Zh: 那个家族的心理健康记录可不太好。

En: Lieutenant -- has someone *shot* the king? (Point to the indentation)

En: Don't mention it.

En: The monument stands high above you. Mute and indifferent to your sightings.

En: What do you think?
Zh: “你有什么想法？”

En: Well... Martinaise is riddled with bullet holes. This place saw a lot of action during the Revolution. But the statue *is* recently renovated -- so maybe a joke? Target practice? Or a political statement?
Zh: “嗯……马丁内斯到处都是弹孔。大革命期间，这个地方发生了许多事。不过这座雕像最近*确实*被翻新过——所以，也许这是有人搞的恶作剧？或者是射击练习？或者是一种政治声明？”

En: Yes, a practical joke.
Zh: “是的，一个恶作剧。”

En: Mhm, target practice.
Zh: “嗯，射击练习。”

En: Political. It's a *king* and he's shot.
Zh: “跟政治有关。这是一个*国王*，而且他中枪了。”

En: None of the above. Maybe it's connected to the murder?
Zh: “都不是。也许它跟凶杀案有关？”

En: If we connect every bullet hole in Martinaise to the murder, we'll get an overwhelming amount of loose threads... He takes his notebook out. "But then again, our guy *was* shot. And that hole would be recent."
Zh: “如果马丁内斯的每一个弹孔都跟凶杀案有关，那我们就会有海量需要追查的线索……”他掏出笔记本。“不过话又说回来，我们的受害者*的确*中枪了。而且那个洞是最近出现的。”

En: He takes a note. "It's not *completely* tangential -- at least worth noting down. I wouldn't hold my breath, though."
Zh: 他记下笔记。“这并不算*完全*离题——至少值得记录下来。不过，我不会抱太大希望。”

En: We'll see.
Zh: 我们等着瞧吧。

En: If we connect every bullet hole in Martinaise to the murder, we'll get an overwhelming amount of loose threads... He takes his notebook out. "But then again, we don't really know what we're dealing with here, so..."
Zh: “如果马丁内斯的每一个弹孔都跟凶杀案有关，那我们就会有海量需要追查的线索……”他掏出笔记本。“不过话又说回来，我们确实不知道到底面对的是什么，所以……”

En: He takes a note. "I've made a note. Don't hold your breath."
Zh: 他记下笔记。“我会记下来的。你不要抱太大希望。”

En: The king stands high above you, surveying the bay. Mute and indifferent to your sightings.
Zh: 国王高高地耸立在你之上，审视着整片海湾。他对你的视线默不作声，毫不关心。

En: Why not? He shrugs. "What this shows us is guns aren't too uncommon here. And people still shoot them. Sometimes at kings." He takes a note in his notebook.
Zh: “有何不可呢?”他耸耸肩。“这表明枪支在这里并不稀罕，而且大家还在用呢，有时候枪口还会朝着国王。”他在笔记本里做了点笔记。

En: What did this king do?
Zh: 这个国王做了什么？

En: Heard crater story from René.
Zh: 从雷内那里听说弹坑的故事。

En: Reality lowdown gotten.
Zh: 得知事实真相。

Zh: 着装要求：保皇派重演者。

En: The more you ask yourself, the less you know. One thing is for certain, though: you're brain-damaged. It's serious. And permanent.
Zh: 你问自己越多，你知道的就越少。不过，有一件事很肯定：你有脑损伤。而且是永久性的。

En: High above you, the king stands triumphantly oblivious to your memory trouble.
Zh: 国王耀武扬威地高高在上，对你的记忆问题毫不关心。

En: Even by the standards of the Filippian kings, Old Sumptuous Filippe was known for his profligacy.
Zh: 就算以国王的标准来看，奢侈的老菲利普也是出了名的挥金如土。

En: In what way?
Zh: 怎么个挥霍法呢？

En: Beneath the cold waters of the Insulindian Bay, thrown there by the revolutionaries after they cleaned out the royal mausoleum.
Zh: 在伊苏林迪海湾冰冷的水面下，革命分子搬空皇家陵墓之后，就把他扔过去了。

En: Well, he blew through the whole national treasury, starting the decline of one of the penultimate century's greatest superpowers: the Suzerain of Revachol...
Zh: 呃，他挥霍掉了整个国库，开启了倒数第二个世纪里最强大的超级大国，也就是瑞瓦肖宗主国的衰败篇章……

En: I don't want to know.
Zh: 我不想知道。

En: His own maladministration foreshadowed the fall of the monarchy during the Antecentennial Revolution, an end to his family line and the monarchy on the Insulindian isola.
Zh: 他的暴政为君主专制在世纪前大革命中的覆灭埋下了伏笔，也使他的宗族血统和伊苏林迪洲的君主专制制度的得以终结。

En: How did he manage to blow through *the entire national treasury*?
Zh: 他怎么能挥霍掉*整个国库*呢？

En: A group of marble pleurants still surrounds the king's vandalized tomb... A deathly cold wind sweeps up candy wrappers and old newspapers.
Zh: 一群大理石的人形雕塑，仍然哭嚎着环绕在被肆意破坏的坟墓周围……一阵致命的寒风卷走了地面上的糖果皮和旧报纸。

En: Fair enough, I have other questions. (Stop the knowledge from pouring in.)
Zh: 有道理，我还有别的问题。（阻止知识的涌入。）

En: Stories have it that he had his bedroom converted into a treasure chamber where he stored unfathomable wealth: *krugerrands*, bars of gold, ornate weaponry, armour, and various chalices.
Zh: 据说他把寝宫变成了一个宝库，在那里贮藏了深不可测的宝藏：*克鲁格金币*、金条、华丽的兵器、盔甲，还有各种各样的圣餐杯。

En: Filippe III's ludicrous bronze likeness looks defiantly up into the sky.
Zh: 菲利普三世滑稽的青铜雕像挑衅地望向天空。

En: That's what the revolutionaries said -- 150 years later. Right before they emptied out the royal mausoleum and dumped His Majesty's mortal remains in the Insulindian Bay.
Zh: 革命分子也是这么说的——就在150年前。就在他们搬空皇家陵寝，把国王陛下的遗骸扔进伊苏林迪海湾之前。

En: His Majesty's courtiers said it helped him connect with the *Higher Realms*.
Zh: 国王陛下的侍臣说这样能帮他与*至高领域*建立联系。

En: He called it the *Sol Aurum*. It was obscene. There were whispers he slept on a huge pile of gold-dipped feathers like some obese dragon, instead of a bed like a normal person.
Zh: 他称他的宝库为*索尔金*。它是污秽的。有传言说他睡在一堆巨大的浸金羽毛之上，就像一头肥胖的恶龙，不像普通人一样睡在床上。

En: Not just *any* nose candy, though. We're talking royal *Filippian* blow, allegedly twice as potent as the stuff you find nowadays. And purple, Filippian cocaine was purple.
Zh: 不过，可不是*任何*毒粉都可以成瘾哦。我们说的可是皇家*菲利普*毒粉，据说效果是现在的两倍。而且还是紫色的，菲利普的可卡因是紫色的。

En: The man certainly knew how to live!
Zh: 这个男人确实很懂生活！

En: So he was addicted to nose candy. A bloated druggie?
Zh: 所以说他对毒粉上瘾，他是一个奢侈的瘾君子吗？

En: The what now?
Zh: 什么？

En: But wait! You haven't even heard about his fabled cocaine addiction.
Zh: 先等等，等我把他可卡因成瘾的传说讲完！

En: You see, Old Filippe wasn't just good at squandering the national treasury on gold and ceremonial weaponry. He was also a prodigious snorter of nose candy.
Zh: 你要知道，老菲利普不仅善于把国库里的资源浪费在金子和仪仗武器上，他还是毒粉的重度成瘾者。

En: A deplorable farce. No wonder everything went to shit.
Zh: 一出令人震惊的闹剧。难怪一切都变得乱七八糟。

En: This is... a lot to process.
Zh: 这还真是……信息量很大啊。

En: Wait, really? There's no way that's true.
Zh: 等等，真的吗？这绝对不可能是真的。

En: Okay. Where is he buried now? (Proceed.)
Zh: 好吧。现在他被埋在什么地方？（继续。）

En: I would like to sleep on gold. Hustler-style.
Zh: 我也想睡在金子上面。

En: The king is the king and he can do *anything*.
Zh: 国王就是国王，他可以做*任何事情*。

En: Okay, what is nose candy?
Zh: 好吧，毒粉是什么？

En: Cocaine.
Zh: 可卡因。

En: This is slander. The king was just and beautiful. The revolutionaries made this all up.
Zh: 这是诽谤。国王是公正的，美丽的。这全都是革命分子编造的。

En: What happened to the statue?
Zh: 雕像出什么事了？

En: The original was blown apart by communards, then further damaged during the landing of the Coalition's airships during the Turn-Of-The-Century Revolution. When Martinaise was levelled.
Zh: 原本的雕像被公社炸成了碎片，然后在世纪之交的大革命期间，联盟国飞艇登陆的时候又遭到了进一步破坏。那个时候马丁内斯被夷为平地了。

En: That's such a bad idea.
Zh: 那可真是个坏主意。

En: Who restored the monument?
Zh: 谁修好了纪念碑？

En: Some years ago a group of liberal, artistically inclined individuals (designers mostly) thought it would be *ironic* to restore the statue of the most wasteful ruler of Revachol in the poorest part of the city...
Zh: 几年前，一群自由主义派的艺术爱好者（大部分都是设计师）觉得修复瑞瓦肖最浪费的统治者雕像是一件很*讽刺*的事情，更别说还是在这片最贫穷的地区里了……

En: The statue is supposed to capture the moment it was blown apart, like *an instant frozen in time*. A rare butterfly, trapped in amber, floating on a sea of... shit.
Zh: 这个雕像本应捕捉到它被炸毁的那一瞬间，就像*凝固在时间里的一瞬间*。一只珍贵的蝴蝶，深陷在琥珀里，漂浮在一片……狗屎的海洋上。

En: Most historians think the Coalition's hasty landing may have ultimately *saved* the statue: If the communards had more time they would have reduced it up to even *finer* pieces.
Zh: 大部分历史学家认为联盟国的仓促登陆最后还算是*拯救*了这座雕像：如果公社社员拥有足够的时间，肯定会把它弄成更*细小*的碎片。

En: Neither do people in Martinaise. The statue has proved to be *controversial* in a neighbourhood mostly populated by the left-leaning working class.
Zh: 马丁内斯人也不懂。在这样一个主要由左倾工人阶级组成的社区里，这座雕像已经被证实是相当具有*争议性*的。

En: That's brilliant! So funny and nihilistic.
Zh: 妙啊！这么有趣，还有虚无主义的味道。

En: I *really* don't get art.
Zh: 我*真心*不懂艺术。

En: Not that he ever did in life, either.
Zh: 也不是说他生前就会在意。

En: People in Martinaise tend to disagree, as do many prominent art critics and thought leaders with more *nuanced* social awareness than the young ironists.
Zh: 马丁内斯人不太赞同，还有很多杰出的艺术评论家和思想领袖，他们比那些年轻的批判家更具有一种*微妙*的社会意识。

En: People in Martinaise tend to agree, as do many prominent art critics and thought leaders with more *nuanced* social awareness than the young ironists.
Zh: 马丁内斯人更倾向于赞同这一点，还有很多杰出的艺术评论家和思想领袖，他们比那些年轻的批判家更具有一种*微妙*的社会意识。

En: Filippe III, the Squanderer, however, with his bronze face up in the air, doesn't seem concerned about what the *hoi polloi* think of him in death.
Zh: 然而，放荡者菲利普三世高高扬起他那张青铜色的面孔，好像并不在意那些*庶民*对他的死亡的看法。

En: Those critics might have it wrong, though. There's more to it than just 'ironism'... but you can't say what precisely. Perhaps this art mystery will be solved at a later time?
Zh: 不过，那些评论家可能搞错了。这可不仅仅是‘讽刺主义……’不过你也说不清楚。也许以后可以解决这个艺术之谜？

En: What did he do? You have no idea what *you* did a week ago! How would you know what this guy did many centuries ago?
Zh: 他做了什么？你都不知道*你*一周前做了什么！你怎么知道好几个世纪之前的人做了什么呢？

En: As you look up, you notice something about the statue. There are some odd indentations on the king's chest piece...
Zh: 你向上张望，注意到雕像上有些东西。国王的胸部有些奇怪的缺口……

En: Examine the scaffolding.
Zh: 检查脚手架。

En: A matrix of metal tubes and wooden planks jut out at odd angles. A miserable tarpaulin covers up the horse and rider, flapping in the cold coastal air...
Zh: 纵横交错的木板和金属管以一个奇怪的角度伸出。一块破烂的油布盖住了马和骑手，在海边寒冷的空气中飞舞……

En: This is *not* what that Personal Brand Strategist of yours promised. No one even knows that you're paying for this re-conceptualization! Go and have a word with him. Find out what's going on.
Zh: 这和你的个人品牌战略顾问说好的可*不一样*，甚至都没人知道是你在为这种概念再设定付费！去和他谈谈，看看到底是怎么回事。

En: There's not an *ounce* of artistry here! It's even worse than expected.
Zh: 连*一两*的艺术性都没有！ 甚至比预期的还要糟糕。

En: The scaffolding around the old monument has been taken down. In its place are the spoils of your investment. Numerous rods and ropes still hold the original reassemblage in place.
Zh: 古老纪念碑周围的脚手架已经被拆掉了，取而代之的是你投资的战利品，众多的杆子和绳索仍在原地固定着重组后的雕像。

En: Look at the re-conceptualization.
Zh: 看看这个二次概念化作品。

En: Reflect on the re-conceptualization.
Zh: 反思这个二次概念化作品。

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The doom crier embraces the blackened sky, his cardboard omen hiding its ill fortune in the dark...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人拥抱着漆黑的天空，他那写着神谕的硬纸板将厄运隐藏在黑暗之中……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The doom crier welcomes the wet erosion, his cardboard omen starting to sag under the weight of the weather...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人迎接着湿气的侵蚀，他写着神谕的硬纸板在雨天的重压下开始下垂……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The doom crier embraces the deathly cold, his cardboard omen dusted with what looks like sky-fallen ash...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人拥抱着死亡的寒冷，他那写着神谕的硬纸板上沾满了像是堕天的灰烬……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The doom crier in the centre waits to be swallowed by the bright sky, his cardboard omen flapping in the wind like live bait...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人站在中央，等待着被明亮的天空所吞噬，他那写着神谕的硬纸板像活饵一样在风中飞舞……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The apricot sceptre illuminates the disco king like a beacon in the dark, calling all dancers to wait for the drop of the next funky beat...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。杏色的权杖照亮了迪斯科之王，就像黑暗中的灯塔，呼唤着所有舞者等待下一个时髦的节拍落下……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. Drops fall around the apricot sceptre and bounce off the glitter balls, yet the noble king of disco still hunts unperturbed for the next funky beat...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。水滴落在杏色权杖的周围，跃起一颗颗闪烁的水珠，而高贵的迪斯科之王仍在不慌不忙地猎取下一个时髦的节拍……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The apricot sceptre glows fiercer upon the white sheets, held up by the frozen king of disco who hunts for the next funky beat...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。冻僵的迪斯科之王高举杏色权杖，在银白色的床单上闪耀着更耀眼的光彩，猎取下一个时髦的节拍……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sun. The king of disco is lit up like a stage, anticipating a funky beat that will never come...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。迪斯科之王就像身处于舞台中心一样闪耀，期待着一个永远不会到来的时髦节拍……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. Darkness completely engulfs the penumbral griever, the flowers lifting him in requiem starting to wilt...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。黑暗完全吞噬了迷离恍惚的哀悼者，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. Drops fall like tears down the veil of the penumbral griever, while a few fallen petals drift like funerary boats downstream, weeping into drains...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。雨滴像眼泪一样落在迷离恍惚的哀悼者的面纱上，几片落下的花瓣像葬仪船一样顺流而下，流进了排水沟……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The penumbral griever cuts black its misery against the white weather, the flowers lifting him in requiem starting to wilt in the cold...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。迷离恍惚的悲哀者在一片银色中将哀伤切成黑色，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The penumbral griever is a silhouette against the bright sky, the flowers lifting him in requiem starting to wilt...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。迷离恍惚的哀悼者在在明亮的天空下只是一个剪影，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The tickets remain true to their duty, ready for the night shift -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to brushing dirt from the asphalt below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。罚单们依然在坚守岗位，准备上夜班——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配到下面刷起沥青上的泥土……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The sodden tickets remain true to their duty against the storm -- except for a few that lie face down below, honourably drowned in a puddle...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。那些湿漉漉的罚单依然在坚守岗位，迎接着暴风雨的侵袭——除了一些朝下躺在地上，光荣地淹没在水坑里的罚单……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The tickets remain true to their duty, like a crowd of commuters huddled for warmth -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to raking the drifts below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雪地里安静得几近诡异。罚单依然在坚守岗位，就像一群挤在一起取暖的上班族——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配到下面扫雪。

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The tickets remain true to their duty -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to brushing dirt from the asphalt below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。罚单依然在坚守岗位——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配去下面清扫沥青上的泥土……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The formless complexion glows at the centre, each street lamp failing to replicate the shining brilliance of its rebellion...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。无形的气色在中心闪耀，而周围的每一盏路灯都无法复制其叛逆的闪耀辉煌……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The painted surface at the centre welcomes the droplets like a water slide. Around it, the coast's older architecture enjoys nothing but slow erosion...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。中心的彩绘表面像水滑梯一样迎接着水滴的到来。在它的周围，海岸的老建筑只是在享受着缓慢的侵蚀，之外别无所求……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The paint is a runny yolk at the centre, the birth cell of a new style -- ready to be dipped into and nourish the youth with sticky inspiration...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。涂鸦中心是一个流淌的蛋黄，是一种新风格诞生时的第一个细胞——随时可以供人蘸取，随时可以用粘稠的灵感滋养青年……

En: The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The formless complexion glows at the centre, while the old architecture surrounding it groans in the heat with looming irrelevance...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。无形的气色在中心闪耀，而周围的老建筑却在炎热中呻吟着，隐隐约约，显得无足轻重……

En: An old monument stands in the middle of the traffic island. It is surrounded by scaffolding that has been haphazardly erected around it.
Zh: 一座古老的纪念碑矗立在交通岛中央，胡乱搭起的脚手架立在周围。

En: An old monument stands in the middle of the traffic island. It is surrounded by scaffolding that has been haphazardly erected around it.
Zh: 一座古老的纪念碑矗立在交通环岛中央，胡乱搭起的脚手架立在周围。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The old king and his horse have been covered in posters with radical slogans like 'NO KINGS, NO BOSSES' and 'AGAIN, AND ONCE MORE AFTER THAT!'
Zh: 先王和他的马已经被海报覆盖，上面写着‘没有国王，没有老板’和‘再来一次，一次又一次！’诸如此类的激进口号。

En: An old monument stands in the middle of the traffic island, pointing toward the sea. It's been covered in posters with radical slogans like 'NO KINGS, NO BOSSES' and 'AGAIN, AND ONCE MORE AFTER THAT!'
Zh: 一座古老的纪念碑矗立在交通岛中央，指向大海的方向。上面贴满了海报，写着‘不要国王，不要老板’和 ‘前仆后继，继往开来！’之类的激进口号。

En: At the bottom of the posters, in smaller lettering, are details about meeting at a local coffeehouse.
Zh: 在海报的底部有一行小字，写着在当地一家咖啡馆见面的细节。

En: Looks like the students took your advice to heart, detective.
Zh: “看来学生们把你的话都听进去了，警探。”

En: An old monument stands in the middle of the traffic island, pointing toward the sea. It looks as if it's been reassembled piece-by-piece, secured and mounted in the air with the aid of numerous ropes and rods.
Zh: 一座古老的纪念碑矗立在交通岛中央，指向大海的方向。它看起来像是一块块重新拼接起来的，在大量绳索和木杆的帮助下稳稳地固定在风中。

En: The old monument is wrapped in vibrantly coloured cables and coiled wire. Where the horse's head should be, only twisted pieces of rebar remain.
Zh: 古老的纪念碑被色彩鲜艳的缆绳和盘绕的铁丝所包裹。在本该是马头的地方，只剩下几截扭曲的钢筋。

En: An old monument stands in the middle of the traffic island, pointing toward the sea. The whole thing is wrapped in vibrantly coloured cables and coiled wire. Where the horse's head should be, only twisted pieces of rebar remain.
Zh: 一座古老的纪念碑矗立在交通岛中央，指向大海的方向。整个纪念碑被色彩鲜艳的缆绳和盘绕的铁丝所包裹。在本该是马头的地方，只剩下几截扭曲的钢筋。

En: The old king and his horse appear to be struggling against the vibrantly coloured cables and coiled wire.
Zh: 先王和他的马看起来像是想拼命挣脱身上那些色彩鲜艳的缆绳和盘绕的铁丝。

En: An old monument stands in the middle of the traffic island, pointing toward the sea. The horse and rider appear to be struggling against the vibrantly coloured cables and coiled wire.
Zh: 一座古老的纪念碑矗立在交通岛中央，指向大海的方向。马和骑手看起来像是想拼命挣脱身上那些色彩鲜艳的缆绳和盘绕的铁丝。

En: There are no lights that spell out your name, no workers with calloused hands leering from above -- just construction materials displayed as if to mourn your misplaced dreams.
Zh: 没有拼出你名字的彩灯，也没有双手长满老茧的工人在上面斜睨着你——只剩一堆建材陈列，仿佛在哀悼你错付了的梦想。

En: That son of a bitch scammed you! Probably to *make fun of you*...
Zh: 那个狗娘养的骗了你！大概是为了*取笑你吧*……

En: The old king looks defiantly to the west, wrapped in makeshift scaffolding like a mummy...
Zh: 先王挑衅地望向西边，像木乃伊一样裹在临时搭建的脚手架里……

En: Don't be distracted by the flattery and funny-man act. *Questions*.
Zh: 不要因为那些阿谀奉承和滑稽可笑的表演而分心。*问问题*。

En: Hyper cool. He nods solemnly, then breathes out a sigh of pride surveying the city around him.
Zh: “超级酷。”他严肃地点点头，然后骄傲地吐出一口气，打量着周围的城市。

En: You're pretty sure you can live in a tree elsewhere too -- but he wouldn't believe it anyway.
Zh: 你很确定自己可以住在某地的一棵树上——反正他是不会相信。

En: No use prying.
Zh: 窥探是没用的。

En: Yes. It's a business secret. Let him keep it.
Zh: 是的。这是商业秘密。就让他保守这个秘密吧。

En: As if you've committed some faux pas.
Zh: 就好像你犯了什么错误一样。

En: Siigay, Apricot Suzerainty -- it's all the same to me, it's all cool. I'm interested in sales. Go ahead, let's make one. He gestures to the boxes. "Super high quality, friendly prices."
Zh: “希依盖，杏味附属国——对我来说都是一样的，一样都很酷。我对做生意比较感兴趣。来吧，我们做笔交易吧。”他朝箱子比了个手势。“品质优良，价格低廉。”

En: I don't know why I'm talking about this. It's some kind of... mind-reaction. (Point to your head.)
Zh: “我不知道为什么要说这个。这是某种……精神反应。”（指着你的脑袋。）

En: No, no. It's very cool, officer. You're a cool cop for caring about those things. You should buy cool glasses too. He points to a pile of sunglasses.
Zh: “不不不。这样挺酷的，警官。你还担心那些事，真是个酷警察。所以你应该买一副酷劲十足的墨镜。”他指向一堆墨镜。

En: No-no, apricots come from Siigay, the vendor explains. "My grandma used to grow them. But Siigay is a shithole, that's why I came to Revachol. Here's much better for an independent entrepreneur. Less *laws*."
Zh: “不不，杏子产自希依盖，”男人解释到。“我的祖母以前就会种。不过希依盖是个鬼地方，所以我才会来瑞瓦肖。这里更适合个体商户发展。*法律*管的没那么多。”

En: Okay. No permit then. Cool.
Zh: “好吧。那就不用许可证了。没事了。”

En: Doesn't that beat the idea of humanitarian aid? It's *supposed* to be free. That's what makes it humanitarian.
Zh: “那不就击败了人道主义救援的理念吗？它*应该*是免费的。那样才叫人道主义。”

En: It *is* -- *practically* free. Yours for five cents a piece! Rock that tuna -- why not some macaroni too?
Zh: “*是的*——*基本上*是免费的。只要5分钱一份！那份金枪鱼很棒——为什么不再来点通心粉呢？”

En: No hassle? There's a *little* of a hassle here, it appears. A moral hassle.
Zh: 没有烦恼？感觉这里好像有那么一点点*烦恼*。道德上的烦恼。

En: Okay, well -- if they're delicious it's okay.
Zh: “好吧，那——如果好吃的话，那就没问题。”

En: They're *very* delicious. *And* shelf stable. You can keep all this out in the sun come summer! Try it -- buy some tuna. Won't rot. Un-rottable.
Zh: “它们*非常*美味。*而且*耐于存放。大夏天的你都可以直接把它们放在外面晒！试试吧——买点金枪鱼。不会过期，不会腐烂的。”

En: We'll need more if we're going to pin this one down, he thinks -- good questions, though. Sharp eyes, officer.
Zh: 如果想钉死这人的话，我们还需要更多证据，他想到——不过这是个好问题。真是好眼力，警官。

En: It's for show, my liege. He knows you need more than this to bust this guy. Later, perhaps?
Zh: 这是在作秀，大人。他知道要想搜捕这个人，现在这些证据还远远不够。也许过一会儿再说？

En: Wait... *HUMANOX*, *humanitarian* aid...
Zh: 等等……*纽曼奥克斯*，*人道主义*救援……

En: I don't know, it was just the only thing I could come up with in my head -- to ask you. I need to get money *somehow*.
Zh: “我不知道，这是我脑子里唯一能想到的东西——问你。我必须*想办法*弄到钱。”

En: Oh, okay. The man stops, his face suddenly serious. "But why, officer?"
Zh: “哦，好吧。”男人停顿了一下，神情突然有些严肃。“但是为什么呢，警官？”

En: Ah, yes. Money is very important. The street vendor nods, dead-serious.
Zh: “啊，是的。钱非常重要。”街头小贩非常认真地点点头，

En: Tugging at his heart strings like that... doesn't it feel a bit manipulative?
Zh: 像这样牵扯他的心弦……不觉得有点操纵人心的感觉吗？

En: Ten reál is a bargain for that kind of investment! You got it, my man. He takes a ten-note from a leather pouch.
Zh: “对于这种投资来说，10雷亚尔已经很便宜了！给你啦，老兄。”他从皮夹子里掏出一张10元的钞票。

En: Persuade him to give you some money.
Zh: 说服他给你钱。

En: Humanitarian aid macaronis.
Zh: 人道主义救援通心粉。

En: Suspicious Humanox truck.
Zh: 可疑的纽曼奥克斯货車。

En: Poor underprivileged grandma.
Zh: 可怜又贫穷的祖母。

En: Need funds for Island.
Zh: 需要资金建设岛屿。

En: Ratted out the lady driver.
Zh: 出卖女司机。

En: But officer, I don't have that kind of money. There's got to be a more modest investment.
Zh: “但是警官，我真没那种钱啊。肯定还有更适度的投资吧。”

En: It's very cool of you to ask again -- it's the third time now. How about you give *me* money and I give *you* cool pants and sneakers. He points to them.
Zh: “你又问一遍还真是挺酷的——现在已经第三遍了。不如你把钱给*我*，然后我给*你*酷炫的裤子和运动鞋。”他指向商品的方向。

En: The air between you becomes a little awkward as it becomes clear you don't know the first thing about bribes.
Zh: 你们之前的气氛变得有些尴尬，因为你显然就对贿赂那套一窍不通。

En: *Now* we're getting somewhere, the street vendor nods. "If you'd only give me a good reason as well..."
Zh: “*现在*我们有些进展了，”街头小贩点点头。“只要你能给我一个好理由的话……”

En: The lieutenant nods calmly and says: "Corruption."
Zh: 警督冷静地点点头，说道：“腐败。”

En: Somewhere not so distant, officer Chad Tillbrook refuses a bribe from a local hood. He doesn't know you've just made his job, and the job of all honest cops, a little bit harder.
Zh: 不远处某个的地方，查德·提尔布鲁克警官拒绝收受本地小混混的贿赂。他不知道你让他和所有正直警察的工作变得更艰难了一些。

En: Because you need to solve the biggest case in the history of criminal sciences.
Zh: 因为你需要解决犯罪科学历史上最大的一起案件。

En: Wow! So cool... you keep saying *the same sentence*, mister. No difference. Same words. He grins in amazement. "Let me donate you some sunglasses. For only 20 cents to 2 reál."
Zh: “哇哦！真酷……你一直在重复*相同的话*，先生。毫无差别。一字不差。”他惊讶地笑了。“我给你捐几副墨镜吧。只要20分到2雷亚尔。”

En: I'm a policeman. It's an investment in good relations with the RCM.
Zh: “我是一个警察。这是能与RCM保持良好关系的一项投资。”

En: What, only a fiver? You can't even buy a pack of Royal Extras with that -- let's make this one *stick*, my man.
Zh: “什么，只要五块吗？那点钱甚至连一包精装皇家都买不来——我们来个*一锤定音*吧，老兄。”

En: God, a pack of Royals would taste *so* good right now. Get you some of those tar-sticks, baby!
Zh: 天啦，现在来一包皇家的话，滋味肯定会*特别*美妙。感觉去弄点焦油棒吧，宝贝！

En: God, a pack of Royals would taste *so* good right now. Get you some of those tar-sticks, baby!
Zh: 天啦，现在来一包皇家的话，滋味肯定会*特别*美妙。感觉你去弄点焦油棒吧，宝贝！

En: You see, I'm an honest entrepreneur. I can't help you if you don't give me a good reason as to why I should -- sorry, detective. The man grins as if the entire incident is already forgotten.
Zh: “你看，我是个诚实的企业家。如果不给我个充分的理由，我是不会帮你的——很抱歉，警探。”男人咧嘴笑了，仿佛已经把整件事抛在脑后。

En: I'm *not* gonna confiscate this humanitarian aid that you're reselling.
Zh: “我*不会*没收你转卖的这些人道主义救援物资。”

En: Think of it as an *investment*.
Zh: “就把这个当成是一种*投资*吧。”

En: I'm *not* going tell HUMANOX about the little side business you've got here.
Zh: “我*不会*把你做的这些小生意告诉纽曼奥克斯。”

En: See this island over there? (Point at the sea.) "I need money to fund an expedition there. It's important for the case."
Zh: “看见那座岛了吗？”（指向大海。）“我需要钱去那边探索。这对于这个案子来说很重要。”

En: Officer, do I look like a drug lord? I don't have that kinda money. Be reasonable here.
Zh: “警官，我看起来像是个大毒枭吗？我没那么多钱。能讲点道理吗？”

En: God, a pack of Royals would taste *so* good right now. Get you some of those tar-sticks, baby!
Zh: 天啦，现在来一包皇家的话，滋味肯定会*特别*美妙。感觉你去弄点焦油棒吧，宝贝！

En: What, only a fiver? You can't even buy a pack of Royal Extras with that -- let's make this one *stick*, my friend!
Zh: “什么，只要五块吗？那点钱甚至连一包精装皇家都买不来——我们把这个*坚持下去*吧，老兄。”

En: An investment? He raises a brow, intrigued. "What kind of investment?"
Zh: “一项投资？”他饶有兴趣的扬起眉头。“哪种类型的投资？”

En: Oh... I like expeditions, and I like you. I can give you... He counts in his head. "... 10 reál."
Zh: “哦……我喜欢探索，而且我也喜欢你。我可以给你……”他在脑子里数了数。“……10雷亚尔。”

En: Hah -- that! You drive a hard bargain, officer. I respect that. He nods. "Okay, what's it going to cost me? Be reasonable..."
Zh: “哈——那个啊！你砍价可真够狠的，警官。我很尊敬这一点。”他点点头。“好吧，那我要花多少钱？要理智一点哦……”

En: Because I want you to bribe me. I'm a police officer, remember? That's what you're supposed to *do* around here.
Zh: “因为我希望你贿赂我。我是个警官，还记得吗？在这种地方，你就应该这么*做*。”

En: But officer, bribe you to do what? The street vendor's expression is dead serious.
Zh: “但是警官，贿赂你做什么呢？”街头小贩的表情非常严肃。

En: Sorry, detective. The man grins as if the entire incident is already forgotten.
Zh: “抱歉，警探。”男人咧嘴笑了，仿佛已经把整件事抛在脑后。

En: It's just like *bus*, or *seagull* -- a kid watching out of a window describing things going by. He doesn't like it too much, but what do you do.
Zh: 这就像是*公交車*，或者*海鸥*——一个孩子看着窗外，正描述着来来往往的东西。他不是特别喜欢，但是又能怎么办呢。

En: Officer, do I look like *I* have that kinda money? Be reasonable.
Zh: “警官，*我*看起来像是有那么多钱的人吗？讲点道理嘛。”

En: What, only a fiver? You can't even buy a pack of Royal Extras with that -- let's make this one *stick*, my man.
Zh: “什么，只要五块吗？那点钱甚至连一包精装皇家都买不来——我们把这个*坚持下去*吧，老兄。”

En: Alright, this should help you get back on your feet. He takes a ten-note from a leather pouch.
Zh: “好吧，这些应该能帮你重新站起来。”他从皮夹子掏出一张十块的钞票。

En: He looks you straight in the eye. "I hear you, officer," he agrees. "What kind of a sum are we talking about here?"
Zh: 他直视你的双眼。“我明白了了，警官，”他表示赞同。“我们这里说的总数是多少呢？”

En: It's an investment in me, a highly experimental human being. My risk/reward ratio is insane.
Zh: “这是一项对我的投资，一个很有实验性的人类。我的投资回报率是疯狂的。”

En: It's an investment in your customer base. Gotta prime the pump, man!
Zh: “这是一项针对你客户基础的投资。总该采取点措施吧，老兄！”

En: A guy told me I need money to live, otherwise it's game over -- and I don't want to die.
Zh: “这个家伙告诉我说生活需要钱，要不游戏就结束了——而且我不想死。”

En: Okay, officer, he finally says, "I can't leave a man in need like that. How much do you need?"
Zh: “好吧，警官，”他终于开了口，“我不能丢下一个这么需要帮助的人。你需要多少呢？”

En: I don't think you really understand how bribes work, detective... He looks at you, then the vendor.
Zh: “我觉得你并不了解受贿是怎么一回事，警探……”他先是看了看你，然后是那个小贩。

En: Sounds like a fair deal all around. He takes a ten-note from a leather pouch.
Zh: “听起来挺公平的。”他从皮夹子掏出一张十块的钞票。

En: One million reál.
Ten reál.
The air between you becomes a little awkward as it becomes clear you don't know the first thing about bribes.
Zh: 你们之间的气氛变得有些尴尬，因为你显然就对贿赂那套一窍不通。

En: Five reál.
Zh: “5雷亚尔。”

En: Five reál.
Zh: “5雷亚尔。”

En: Ten reál.
I don't even want happiness, or *un-unhappiness* -- I just don't want to die.
Zh: “我甚至都不想要幸福，或者*没有不幸福*——我只是不想死。”

En: One million reál.
Because I... am corrupt? I don't really remember how it works, though -- being corrupt, I mean.
Zh: “因为……我很腐败？我真的不记得是怎么一回事了——我是说，怎么个腐败法。”

En: Ah, yes. Lots of cops are. The street vendor nods, dead-serious.
Zh: “啊，是的。很多警察都是。”街头小贩点点头，表情十分认真。

En: You said I was a cool cop, but it's not very cool to freeze to death on a cold park bench.
Zh: “你说我是个很酷的警察，但是冻死在冰冷的公园长椅上可算不是上酷吧。”

En: I guess it can't be any riskier than speculating in exotic derivatives... how much are we talking about here?
Zh: “我猜，这不会比投机外国衍生品的风险更大……所以这个数是多少呢？”

En: Fair point, he agrees, "You can't have a successful business without a loyal customer base. How much is your loyalty worth?"
Zh: “说得好，”他表示赞同，“没有忠诚的客户基础，生意是不可能成功的。那你的忠诚值多少钱呢？”

En: Are you trying to ask for a *bribe*? If so, you're not doing a very good job... He looks at the vendor.
Zh: “你是想要别人*贿赂*你吗？如果是的话，你表现的不是特别好……”他看着小贩。

En: A bold move! Let's see if it pays off.
Zh: 真是个大胆的举动！让我们看看结果会怎么样。

En: I... officer, he nods. He is genuinely perplexed.
Zh: “我……警官，”他点点头。他是真的有些不知所措。

En: Oh, okay. Siileng stops, his face suddenly serious. "But why, officer?"
Zh: “哦，好吧。”席勒停了下来，脸色突然变得很严肃。“但是为什么呢，警官？”

En: It feels great.
Zh: 感觉很棒。

En: One million reál.
Ten reál.
Five reál.
Zh: “5雷亚尔。”

En: Invest into me not telling your employer about your operation here.
Zh: “投资我，我就不会把你的小生意告诉你老板。”

En: After all this mess -- the broken seals, lying to you? C'mon!
Zh: 经过这么些乱七八糟的事情之后——撕破的封条，对你撒谎？拜托！

En: *Or* you could *not* make this about corruption and go with something even wilder.
Zh: *或者*你可以*不要*把它跟腐败联系在一起，而是更疯狂的事。

En: So, Siileng -- what's *your* stance on drugs?
Zh: “所以，席勒——*你*对毒品的立场是怎么样的？”

En: Unless *you're* into drugs, of course -- in which case, drugs are *excellent*. He kisses his fingers. "Tasty-tasty drugs."
Zh: “当然了，除非*你*吸毒——这样的话，毒品就是*极好的*。”他亲吻了一下手指。“美味的毒品。”

En: Drugs? For a moment he's unsure how to respond. "I don't go in for that, officer. Drugs ruin lives."
Zh: “毒品？”有那么一瞬间他不确定该怎么回应。“我不会掺合那个的，警官。毒品会毁掉你的生活。”

En: (Proceed.) "We're looking for a lorry driver who is transporting drugs out of the harbour. He or she is in this traffic jam."
Zh: （继续。）“我们正在寻找一个在港口运送毒品的货車司机。他或者是她此刻也被堵在这里了。”

En: That's even cooler -- you investigating that and all. But -- he points to the goods. "I am not a lorry driver, I'm just a street vendor. I don't know anything about that."
Zh: “那就更酷了——你的调查之类的。但是——”他指向货物。“我不是货車司机，我只是个街头小贩。所以那件事我不太清楚。”

En: But you *are* a lorryman. Another driver has identified you *and* your lorry.
Zh: “但你*是*一个货車司机。其他司机认得你，*还有*你的货車。”

En: I'm super into drugs.
Zh: “我吸毒都吸疯了。”

En: I actually *don't* like drugs.
Zh: “其实我真的*不喜欢*毒品。”

En: That's very cool, a lot of the coolest detectives do drugs. Sadly, I don't have any drugs on sale -- or at my home. Or on my person. He smiles.
Zh: “那很酷啊，很多顶级酷的警察都吸毒的。可惜的是我这里没有毒品卖——家里也没有。我自己身上也没有。”他笑了。

En: Sir, it appears to be true. No drugs in sight -- not in the box of sunglasses *or* under the speakers.
Zh: 长官，看起来好像是真的。没有发现毒品的迹象——墨镜盒子，*或者*扬声器下面都没有。

En: That's cool. Especially after you said you *don't* like them. All the cool detectives do drugs without liking them. Sadly I don't have any drugs on sale here. He smiles.
Zh: “那很酷啊。特别是你说过*不喜欢*毒品之后。所有酷警察吸毒都不是因为喜欢的。可惜我这里没有毒品卖。”他笑了。

En: That's very cool. I don't like drugs, either. I only said I do because I didn't want to sound *lame*. He smiles. "Peer pressure."
Zh: “那很酷啊。我也不喜欢毒品。我这么说只是因为不想让自己听起来*很差劲*。”他笑了。“同辈压力。”

En: That's cool. Especially after you already said you're into them. All the coolest detectives in the movies do drugs *and* are very conflicted about it. Still -- no drugs here. He smiles.
Zh: “那很酷啊。特别是你说过喜欢毒品之后。电影里那些顶级酷警察都吸毒，*而且*对此的态度都很矛盾。不过——我这里没有毒品。”他笑了。

En: That's *even* cooler -- you investigating narcotics crimes like that. But -- he points to the goods. "I am not a lorry driver, I'm just a street vendor. I don't know anything about that."
Zh: “那就*更酷*了——调查贩毒之类的。但是——”他指向货物。“我不是货車司机，我只是个街头小贩。所以那件事我不太清楚。”

En: A blatant lie, sire. Yet he tells it with such conviction. We'd believe him -- if we didn't know better.
Zh: 这是一个厚颜无耻的谎言，大人。然而，他说话的态度是如此坚定。如果不知道的话——吾辈会相信他的。

En: That's *so* cool -- you investigating the local drug trade like some cool Narc. But -- he points to the goods. "I am not a lorry driver, I'm just a street vendor. I don't know anything about that."
Zh: “那真是*太酷*了——像缉毒刑警一样调查本地的毒品交易。但是——”他指向货物。“我不是货車司机，我只是个街头小贩。所以那件事我不太清楚。”

En: *Who* said that? It's the fat racist, right? I bet it's him. He has an agenda against me, because I'm an immigrant who works harder than he does. He's a hater.
Zh: “*谁*说的？那个信奉种族主义的死胖子，对吗？我敢打赌就是他。跟我做对是他的日常工作，因为我是个比他工作努力很多的移民。他就是个喷子。”

En: So you admit you're a lorry driver?
Zh: “所以你承认自己是货車司机了？”

En: So you admit you're a lorry driver.
Zh: “所以你承认自己是货車司机了。”

En: No! I just said I work harder and he's an asshole. I'm... He stops to think.
Zh: “没有！我只是说我工作比较努力，但他是个混球。我……”他停下来想了一会儿。

En: Realizing he can't get out of it. Smart man.
Zh: 他应该意识到自己已经无法脱身了。聪明的家伙。

En: Okay, maybe I'm a lorry driver too -- a little. But that's not the most important thing about me, that's my day job. This is my *dream*. He spreads his arms.
Zh: “好吧，也许我也是个货車司机——有那么一点算吧。但对我来说这不是最重要的事，那是我的日常工作。这个是我的*梦想*。”他张开双臂。

En: So you *forgot* to tell me?
Zh: “所以你*忘记*告诉我了？”

En: So you were *embarrassed* to tell me?
Zh: “所以你因为太*尴尬*而不想告诉我？”

En: Stop squirming. What do you know about the drug operation at the harbour?
Zh: “别扭扭捏捏的了。港口的贩毒活动你知道多少？”

En: No. I just forgot. It's such a small part of my life. It's in the rear view mirror now. I'm climbing out of that hole -- with ingenuity.
Zh: “不。我只是忘记了。那只是我生活中的一小部分。现在它已经是过眼云烟了。我已经从那个深坑里爬了出来——因为我的足智多谋。”

En: Exactly! It's such a small part of my life. It's in the rear view mirror now. I'm climbing out of that hole -- with ingenuity.
Zh: “没错！那只是我生活中的一小部分。现在它已经是过眼云烟了。我已经从那个深坑里爬了出来——因为我的足智多谋。”

En: Siileng, my source tells me *you're* the one transporting drugs for the Union.
Zh: “席勒，我这边收到的消息说*你*是为工会贩毒的其中一员。”

En: No. That's insane... It's the fat hater! He's been eyeing me for a week and he sent you here. Maybe *he's* the one, huh? Have you thought about that?
Zh: “不。真是太疯狂了……是那个胖喷子！他已经盯了我整整一星期，是他让你来的吧。也许*他*才是贩毒的人？你有没有想过这种可能性？”

En: Nothing, I told you. I'm not a dumb guy -- I don't get involved with that crowd.
Zh: “什么都不知道，我告诉过你了。我不是傻瓜——我不会跟那帮人搅在一起。”

En: Crowd -- you know? The drug crowd.
Zh: “你知道的——那帮人。贩毒的那帮人。”

En: Wait, what crowd?
Zh: “等等，什么那帮人？”

En: And what *crowd* is that?
Zh: “你说的*那帮人*是谁？”

En: There's more here. He's *afraid* of this crowd, whoever they are. More than he is of the racist lorryman.
Zh: 远不止这些。不管那帮人是谁，他*害怕*他们。远超那个种族主义的货車司机。

En: No. He wasn't talking about an abstract crowd. It was *that crowd*.
Zh: 不。他说的不是一个抽象的团体，而是*那帮人*。

En: Who are you afraid of, Siileng?
Zh: “你在害怕谁，席勒？”

En: It wasn't *some drug crowd*. You know who they are. Tell me now!
Zh: “并不是*某些贩毒的人*。你知道他们是谁。赶紧告诉我！”

En: You're wasting my time. Tell me who the *fuck* is transporting the drugs here!
Zh: “你是在浪费我的时间。*他妈的*赶紧告诉我，是谁在这里运毒！”

En: He doesn't want to talk about them... he's *afraid*.
Zh: 他不想谈论他们……他在*害怕*。

En: Okay. If you don't know then I'll tell your employer you've been selling his stuff.
Zh: “好吧。如果你不知道，那我就告诉你老板，说你在偷卖他的东西。”

En: Look... He looks around and lowers his voice. "There's bad people doing *bad* things here -- that's all I know. Please don't get me into this mess, I've spent fifteen years working my way up..."
Zh: “听着……”他看了看周围，然后压低声音。“这里有帮坏人在做*坏事*——我只知道这么多。请别把我卷入这个烂摊子，我为了向上爬已经奋斗了十五年的时间……”

En: Shush, please... He looks around, then lowers his voice. "There's *bad* people doing *bad* things here -- that's all I know. Please don't get me into this mess, I've spent *fifteen* years working my way up..."
Zh: “拜托，小点声……”他看了看周围，然后压低声音。“这里有一帮*坏人*在做一些*坏事*——我只知道这么多。请别把我卷入这个烂摊子，我为了向上爬已经奋斗了*十五*年……”

En: No use -- he's not telling us. He's too afraid. We need to take him to my station and ask him there. *After* we've called his boss.
Zh: “没用的——他不会告诉我们。他太害怕了。我们得把他带回我的警局，然后在那边审问他。等打电话给他的老板*之后*就这么办。”

En: Okay, look... The vendor looks around, then lowers his voice. "There's *bad* people doing *bad* things here -- that's all I know. Please don't get me into this mess, I've spent *fifteen* years working my way up..."
Zh: “好吧，是这样的……”小贩看了看周围，然后压低声音。“这里有一帮*坏人*在做一些*坏事*——我只知道这么多。请别把我卷入这个烂摊子，我花了*15*年努力工作，就为了……”

En: Okay, look... He looks around and lowers his voice. "There's *bad* people here. Doing *bad* things. That's all I know. Please don't get into this mess. I've been Revachol for *fifteen* years, working my way up..."
Zh: “好吧，是这样的……”他看了看周围，然后压低声音。“这里有一帮*坏人*在做一些*坏事*——我只知道这么多。请别把我卷入这个烂摊子，我为了向上爬已经奋斗了*十五*年……”

En: Here we go. There's a *tiny bit* of truth on the table. Zoom in on it.
Zh: 好吧。现在总算是有那么*一点点*事实摆上了台面。再放大一些。

En: If you don't want to get into this mess... (Raise your voice) "You HAVE to give us a REASON to move on."
Zh: “如果你不想卷入这个烂摊子……”（提高你的声音。）“那你就必须给我们一个离开的理由。”

En: We're buddies, Siileng. Help us out. No one will know it was you.
Zh: “我们是兄弟，席勒。帮帮我们。没人会知道是你说的。”

En: It's a *she*, okay? he whispers. "The other drivers call her the *lady driver*. You're better off staying away from her -- the way they talk about her..." He shakes his head. "She's no lady."
Zh: “是一个*她*，好吗？”他低语到。“其他司机都叫她*女司机*。你最好离她远点——他们提到她的时候……”他摇摇头。“她可不是什么淑女。”

En: Who are these 'other drivers' who talk?
Zh: “这些话是哪些‘其他司机’说的？”

En: Is the lady driver the old woman back there? (Point to the Paledriver.) "Dazed out, *strange*?"
Zh: “你说的女司机是后面那个老妇人吗？”（指向灰域驾驶员。）“很茫然，很*奇怪*的那个吗？”

En: Okay. We're cool now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，现在就没事了。”（总结。）

En: All of them, I don't know. I told you all I know. Are we cool now? I really want us to be cool now.
Zh: “我不知道，全都有吧。我知道的都告诉你了。现在可以了吗？我真心希望现在已经没问题了。”

En: All of them. Even the ones who've left. I don't hang out with them, I don't remember who has tattoos.
Zh: “所有人。甚至是那些离开的人。我不怎么和他们交往，我不记得谁身上有纹身。”

En: Who *exactly* is talking about this lady-driver of yours? The racist? Or the other one, with the tattoos? He points north.
Zh: “*到底*都是谁在议论你们要找的女司机？那个种族主义者？还是其他那个，有纹身的？”他指向北边。

En: I don't know... maybe? If she is, I haven't gone near her. I don't get *involved*, I told you.
Zh: “我不知道……也许吧？如果她是的话，我跟她走得不是很近。我告诉过你，我不想*卷进去*。”

En: All right! He snaps back to his usual self. "Ice cold. Let's cap this off with a purchase. You can walk away from here with funky sunglasses, detectives -- both of you. You deserve it!"
Zh: “好吧！”他又变回平常的那个他了。“酷毙了。就让我们用采购来结束这一切吧。不如买副时髦的墨镜再离开吧，警探们——你们俩都是。这是你们应得的！”

En: All right! He snaps back to his act. "Very cool. Let's cap this off with a *purchase*. A pair of funky sunglasses, detective. You deserve it! And *I* deserve it too, don't you think?"
Zh: “好吧！”他又开始表演了。“很酷。就让我们用*采购*来结束这一切吧。来一副时髦的墨镜怎么样，警探？这是你应得的！而且这也是*我*应得的，你不觉得吗？”

En: He's *aching* to get back to his usual shtick. All this whispering is bad for business.
Zh: 他*渴望*回到自己一贯的风格。说悄悄话什么的对生意可不太好。

En: She? Where is she? I haven't yet met a woman on the roundabout.
Zh: “她？她在哪？我没在环岛见过女人啊。”

En: There's one by the statue of that king. He points towards the roundabout. "I haven't gone near her. And I *won't* either."
Zh: “国王雕像那边有一个。”他指向环岛。“我没有靠近过她，而且我也*不会*这么做。”

En: The third driver, he nods. "We haven't talked to her yet. Where exactly is she -- near the horseback monument?"
Zh: “第三个司机，”他点点头。“我们还没跟她聊过。她到底在哪——在马背纪念碑附近吗？”

En: He nods. "In her lorry there, but... I don't know if it's her... Are we cool now? I think we're cool now."
Zh: 他点点头。“在她的货車那边，不过……我不知道是不是她……现在没事了吧？我觉得应该没问题了。”

En: To the west, dark brown ribbons of tape hang from the tree, flowing in the wind...
Zh: 西边，深褐色的磁带挂在树上，在风中上下翻飞……

En: It could be. She was strange.
Zh: 有可能。她很奇怪。

En: He's not ruling her out.
Zh: 他没有把她排除在外。

En: A lady driver? Perhaps connected to *Ruby* somehow?
Zh: 女司机？也许跟*露比*有所关联？

En: Could her name be *Ruby* by any chance?
Zh: “她的名字不会刚好叫*露比*吧？”

En: Interesting. Could this driver be connected to the Hardie boys?
Zh: 有意思。这个司机会不会跟哈迪兄弟有关联呢？

En: Could she be associated with the Hardie boys?
Zh: “她可能跟哈迪兄弟有关联吗？”

En: I don't know. I told you... I don't know her name.
Zh: “我不知道。我告诉过你了……我不知道她的名字。”

En: I don't know... I'm not local. I don't know anything about that.
Zh: “我不知道……我不是本地人。我什么都不知道。”

En: You should have a look. It's better than nothing.
Zh: 你应该去看看。这样总比什么都没有的好。

En: Do you accept net worth as a form of payment?
Zh: “你接受以净资产作为付款方式吗？”

En: I only accept *legal* tender, officer. He lowers his voice. "Unless that's code for something?"
Zh: “我只接受*合法*的货币，长官。”他压低了声音。“除非那是什么的代号？”

En: It is not code. He really means his individual net worth, the lieutenant jumps in. "It is *not* legal tender."
Zh: “这不是代号。他真正的意思是他的个人净资产。”警督跳了出来。“*不是*什么法定货币。”

En: In that case, I cannot accept that as payment, officer. I run everything above board here. Cash only.
Zh: “既然如此，我不能接受以这个来付款，长官。我这一切都是明码标价，只收现金。”

En: Nah, it's not code. I really mean my individual net worth.
Zh: “不，这不是代号。我真正的意思是我的个人净资产。”

En: Hey, a quick question -- do you sell any tapes?
Zh: “喂，问你个小问题——你卖磁带吗？”

En: Tapes, you mean like music tapes? No, music is *out*! Don't listen to music. I sell extremely cool sunglasses if you want get your *mojo* going. He points at the shoddy box on the left.
Zh: “磁带，你是说音乐磁带吗？不，音乐已经*过时*了！不要听音乐。如果你想让自己更有*范儿*一些的话，我这里有卖非常酷炫的墨镜。”他指向左边的一个劣质箱子。

En: Alright, so you have no idea whatsoever where I could find tapes?
Zh: “好吧，所以你不知道我能去哪里搞到磁带？”

En: Tapes? The notion sounds preposterous to him. "Tapes are *everywhere*! They're worthless, kids throw them in the trees -- there's one in the bushes, right behind this lorry."
Zh: “磁带？”这个概念在他听来有些可笑。“磁带*到处*都有啊！它们一文不值，小孩子会把它们扔到树上——灌木丛里就有一盘，就在这台货車背后。”

En: He nods at the empty lorry cabin behind his back. "No one would ever throw a good pair of *high quality plastic* sunglasses in the bushes, mister." His smile widens.
Zh: 他朝着背后的空货車厢点点头。“不会有人把一副*优质塑料*墨镜扔进灌木丛的，先生。“他的笑容越发灿烂了。

En: Start with a little compliment, then work your way up from there -- this is about business, remember.
Zh: 开始是巧妙的恭维，然后在那个基础上慢慢发展——记住，这是在谈生意。

En: Hey, you seem like a really successful entrepreneur... would you like to support a member of the local police force?
Zh: “喂，你看起来像是个特别成功的企业家……想不想支持一下本地警察局的成员呢？”

En: You, I want your money. Now.
Zh: “你，我想要你的钱。马上。”

En: No need to dress this one up, just tell him what you want.
Zh: 没必要搞这一套，告诉他你想要什么就行。

En: I'll leave you to it for now. [Leave.]
Zh: “我暂时就不打扰你了。”[离开。]

En: I'll look around, thanks. [Leave.]
Zh: “我会四处看看的，谢谢。”[离开。]

En: Sure, mister, very cool. Take a nice long look, I've got all sorts of things here.
Zh: “当然了，先生，酷炫的不得了呢。好好看看，多久都行，我这里各种各样的东西都有。”

En: What's so cool?
Zh: “有什么那么酷的？”

En: *Everything's* cool. The goods are cool, the customers are cool, the place is cool -- and one more thing, officer...
Zh: “*一切*都很酷。商品很酷，顾客很酷，这个地方很酷——还有一件事，警官……”

En: ...from out on the bay a cool wind gathers. It sweeps into the city, tugging at the textiles hanging around the stand. Some distance away the sound of a tin can clattering across the street can be heard...
Zh: ……海湾那边吹来一阵冷风。它席卷了整座城市，拖拽着挂在架子上的纺织品。远处的某个地方，一个锡罐在街道上滚动着，发出哗啦哗啦的声响……

En: Do you have a permit to sell all that?
Zh: “你卖这些东西有许可证吗？”

En: The food packages you're selling have *humanitarian aid* written on them. How come?
Zh: “你卖的这些食品包装上印着“人道主义救援”的字样。这是为什么？”

En: Good joke, officer! Here we don't have permits, just economic freedom. Take a look around... He takes a deep breath.
Zh: “你真爱开玩笑，警官！我们这里没有许可证，只有经济自由。你看看周围……”他深吸一口气。

En: Everything's still cool here, officer, the street vendor assures you.
Zh: “这里的一切还是很酷，警官，”街头小贩向你保证。

En: Is there a discount for cool officers like me?
Zh: “像我这么酷的警官，能打个折吗？”

En: No need for discounts at Siileng's, officer. Everything's already on sale. He nods towards the crates. "Anything you want, 50% off!"
Zh: “席勒这里不需要打折，警官。所有东西本来都在打折。”他朝着大箱子点点头。“随便你想要什么，通通5折！”

En: ... you're *very* cool. He makes both hands into finger-pistols and fires a few finger-bullets into the air.
Zh: “……你确实*非常*酷。”他用两只手做出手枪的样子，有模有样的朝着空气中开了几枪。

En: This man probably comes from Siigay, sometimes known as the 'Apricot Suzerainty,' an archipelago in the Samaran isola.
Zh: 这个人也许是希依盖来的，那里有时候也被称为‘杏味附属国’，属于萨马拉洲的一个列岛。

En: But you're not a local, are you?
Zh: “但你不是本地人，对吧？”

En: You're from the 'Apricot Suzerainty', right?
Zh: “你是‘杏味附属国’来的，对吧？”

En: That's right, officer, but it's a bad scene for business there. Too many regulations, extremely bad for an independent local entrepreneur.
Zh: “没错，警官，不过对做生意来说可不是件好事。规矩太多，对于我这种本地个体商户来说特别糟。”

En: Very sharp, officer. I'm Seraise, from the Siigay province of the Seraise Empire. The 'Apricot Suzerainty', you know?
Zh: “好眼光，警官。我是萨弗里人，来自萨弗里塞莱帝国的希依盖省。就是‘杏味附属国’，你知道吧？”

En: Yum. Apricots are delicious.
Zh: “是的。杏子很好吃。”

En: Why's it called that? Lots of apricots there?
Zh: “为什么会这么叫？因为有很多杏子吗？”

En: The *Apricot Suzerainty*, the lieutenant explains, "is what the Siigay archipelago is commonly known as in Revachol." He pauses. "It's a bit of a *fraught* term, I'm sure you understand."
Zh: “*杏味附属国*，”警督解释到，“它是希依盖群岛在瑞瓦肖众所周知的代号。”他停顿了一下。“这是个有点*问题*的叫法，我敢肯定你能理解。”

En: Yes, apricots come from Siigay, the man explains. "My grandma used to grow them. But Siigay is a shithole, that's why I came to Revachol. Here's much better for an independent entrepreneur. Less *laws*."
Zh: “是的，杏子产自希依盖，”男人解释到。“我的祖母以前就会种。不过希依盖是个鬼地方，所以我才会来瑞瓦肖。这里更适合个体商户发展。*法律*管的没那么多。”

En: Speaking of -- why not support an independent local entrepreneur? I send half my profits back to my grandma in Siigay.
Zh: “说到这里——为什么不支持一下本地的个体商户呢？我要把一半的利润都寄给希依盖的祖母。”

En: Where are you from, Siileng?
Zh: “你是从哪里来的，席勒？”

En: So... where are you from?
Zh: “所以……你是从哪里来的？”

En: Me? It's a boring story, officer. Who cares about the past? I'm all business now -- all Revachol.
Zh: “我？我的故事挺无聊的，警官。谁在乎过去呢？我现在只关心生意——全心全意的为了瑞瓦肖。”

En: (Better not to mention.) "Sounds good. Let's get back to business then."
Zh: （最好别提。）“听起来不错。那我们就说回生意吧。”

En: Very cool. I like your style, officer.
Zh: “酷毙了。我喜欢你的风格，警官。”

En: Well. If it's for his *grandma*... you should buy a lot of things.
Zh: 呃，如果是为了他的*祖母*……你应该多买点东西。

En: You feel a twitch in your index fingers. There's a finger-shootout a-brewing.
Zh: 你感觉食指有些抽搐。一场手指枪战正在酝酿中。

En: Whip 'em out!
Zh: 掏出你的手指枪！

En: No, it's a safety hazard.
Zh: 不，这是安全隐患。

En: He raises his arms in mock alarm. "Don't shoot, officer! Have we got an arrest here, or just a shakedown?"
Zh: 他假装惊恐地举起双手。“别开枪，警官！你要是逮捕我，还是要勒索我？”

En: A shakedown.
Zh: “勒索。”

En: An arrest.
Zh: “逮捕。”

En: Good one, officer. You're a funny guy! Now what can I do for you? He nods toward his shabby wares.
Zh: “不错啊，警官。你是个很有意思的人！我有什么能帮你的吗？”他朝着那些破旧的商品点点头。

En: This isn't a joke. Give me your money. I need money.
Zh: “这不是在开玩笑。把你的钱交出来。我需要钱。”

En: Just kidding. I'm really here to shake you down for some *answers* to official police questions.
Zh: “只是开个玩笑。我来这里其实是警方有些问题，想从你这里找到*答案*”

En: Even better, it's civil asset forfeiture. (Make an imaginary warning shot.)
Zh: “比这个更好，是没收民间资产。”（来一发假想中的鸣枪警告。）

En: *Apricot Suzerainty* calls to mind an era when the Siigay archipelago was colonized by Revachol. It's a bit of a slur, in other words.
Zh: *杏味附属国*让人想起希依盖群岛被瑞瓦肖殖民的时代。换句话说，有点诽谤的意味。

En: But did he first mark them up 100%, just so he could put them on "sale"?
Zh: 他难道不是先标上100%的价格，然后就可以“打折”了吗？

En: Are those things really on sale or did you just jack up the price first?
Zh: “这些东西是真打折，还是你先涨过价了？”

En: My man, you know how the game is played! You and me, we should work together. What do you think -- shake things up? He winks.
Zh: “这位朋友，看来你知道游戏是怎么玩的！你和我，我们应该一起合作。你觉得怎么样——重新组合一下？”他朝你眨眨眼。

En: Even better! It's like a shakedown, but somehow legal. This is "civil asset forfeiture."
Zh: 比这个更好！比较像是勒索，不过是合法的。这叫做“没收民间资产。”

En: You're threatening my inviolable property rights? He inhales dramatically, then simmers down.
Zh: “你在威胁我不可侵犯的财产权？”他夸张地用力吸了一口气，然后冷静下来。

En: This isn't a joke. Give me your money. I need money.
Zh: “这不是在开玩笑。把你的钱交出来。我需要钱。”

En: Yeah, I'm funny. This isn't a civil forfeiture, I just want to ask some questions.
Zh: “是啊，我很有意思。我不是要没收民间资产，只是想问几个问题。”

En: Then fire away, officer! He settles back into the pile of boxes he's sitting on, getting comfortable.
Zh: “那就问吧，警官！”他又重新坐回身下的那堆箱子上，摆了个舒服的姿势。

En: I would, officer, gladly, but I don't have any cash on hand, you know? He winks. "But maybe if you *buy* something first *then* I'll have some money you can shake me down for. Sound fair?"
Zh: “可以，警官，我很乐意，不过我现在手上没多少现金，你懂的吧？”他眨眨眼。“如果你能先*买*点东西，*然后*我就有钱给你了。听起来比较公平吧？”

En: What, no finger-pistols, officer? The street vendor makes a little *pew-pew* sound.
Zh: “什么，不玩手指手枪了吗，警官？”街头小贩发出一个*Biu-Biu*的声音。

En: I'm not here to play with finger-pistols. I'm here on official business.
Zh: “我来这里可不是玩什么手指手枪的。我是来办公事的。”

En: What do you think is going to happen when all those finger-bullets come raining down to earth?
Zh: “如果所有手指子弹都像雨点一样坠落在地面上，你觉得会发生什么？”

En: You're right, officer, 100%. He adjusts his shirt collar. "No playing around then. Strictly business. Maybe I can interest you in some premium menswear instead?"
Zh: “你说得对，警官，100%。”他整了整衬衣衣领。“那就不玩了。聊正经生意。也许你会对高级男装感兴趣？”

En: You're right, officer, 100%. He returns his finger-pistols to their finger-holsters. "No playing around then. Strictly business. Maybe I can interest you in some premium menswear instead?"
Zh: “你说得对，警官，100%。”他把手指手枪收回手指枪套。“那就不玩了。聊正经生意。也许你会对高级男装感兴趣？”

En: ...it's beautiful. Beautiful freedom!
Zh: “……它很美。美丽的自由！”

En: Sweet, no permits means less work for me. Let's move on.
Zh: “亲，没有许可证就意味着我的工作更少了。我们继续前进吧。”

En: Wait, but what are *we* doing here, if there are no permits? (Look at Kim and the police insignia on his sleeve.)
Zh: “等等，但是如果不用检查许可证的话，还要*我们*在这里做什么呢？”（看着金，还有他袖子上的警徽。）

En: Wait, but what am *I* doing here, if there are no permits? (Stare at the police insignia on your sleeve.)
Zh: “等等，但是如果不用检查许可证的话，还要*我*在这里做什么呢？”（盯着袖子上的警徽。）

En: A gossamer state? You're right, this *is* cool. I'm part of a hip, thin, almost non-existent state apparatus!
Zh: “蛛丝帝国？你说得对，这*真的*挺酷的。我是一个时尚、孱弱、几乎不存在的国家机器的一部分！”

En: So 'cool' guys protect businessmen while everyone else lives in the ruins? I can think of cooler things.
Zh: “所以‘酷’家伙会保护商人，却任由其他人都生活在废墟里？我可不觉得这很酷。”

En: Yeah, that's the main thing about you. He nods admiringly. "Freedom is great, it's what lets independent entrepreneurs, like me, sell quality goods at competitive prices."
Zh: “是啊，你主要就是做这个的。”他钦佩地点点头。“自由很伟大，是它让独立企业家，就像我这种，能够以很有竞争力的价格贩卖优质产品。”

En: Yeah -- *cool* ruins, he agrees. "I get to sell quality goods like these by cutting out the middlemen. *You* don't have to rebuild your house. Live in a crater! Or in a tree!" He points to a tree. "*Where* can you do that? Only here."
Zh: “是啊——废墟*很酷*，”他表示赞同。“我可以不经过中间商来贩售这些优质商品。*你*不需要重建自己的房子。可以生活在弹坑里！或者是树上！”他指向一棵树。“你还能在*哪里*这么做呢？只有这里。”

En: That's right. Revachol is the best place for an independent self-employer like myself. That's why it's so *cool* with all the models coming here and cool celebrities... He smiles his bright toothpaste-ad smile.
Zh: “没错。瑞瓦肖对于我这种个体商户来说是最棒的地方。还有那些模特和明星都会来这里，所以它就更*酷*了……”他露出灿烂的笑容，很像牙膏广告里那种。

En: Of course, I'll come quietly. A sly wink. "But perhaps I could convince you to arrest some of my wares, officer? With your money, maybe?"
Zh: “当然。我会悄悄地来。”他狡猾地朝你眨眨眼。“不过也许我可以说服你逮捕一些我的货物，警官？也许可以用你的钱？”

En: Sorry, I didn't mean to say that. I meant to say that you're from... (Pronounce it very painstakingly.) "... See-guy..."
Zh: “抱歉，我不是故意这么说的。我是想说你来自……”（费力地发音）……“希-盖……”

En: Don't fret. Let him answer.
Zh: 别急。等他回答。

En: Very cool, Siileng nods, his eyebrows furrowed, "I admire your awareness of our intertwined histories. It's super nice of you to apologize for colonialism. But the Apricot Suzerainty is a shithole -- that's why I left."
Zh: “很酷，”席勒点点头，眉头紧锁，“你能意识到我们之间错综复杂的历史，这一点让我很钦佩。能为殖民主义道歉这一点就更好了。不过杏味附属国是一个鬼地方——所以我才会离开。”

En: Right, but isn't it only a shithole *because* of Revachol?
Zh: “好吧，但它成为一个鬼地方是*因为*瑞瓦肖吗？”

En: You're welcome. I do try to be supportive of other peoples and cultures.
Zh: “不用客气。我确实在尝试支持其他人和其他文化。”

En: *Apricot Suzerainty* calls to mind an era when the Siigay archipelago was colonized by Revachol. It's a bit of a slur, in other words.
Zh: *杏味附属国*让人想起希依盖群岛被瑞瓦肖殖民的时代。换句话说，有点诽谤的意味。

En: (Wince.) "Isn't that name kind of... insensitive?"
Zh: （退避）“那个名字不是有点……不顾及别人的感受吗？”

En: Very cool, the street vendor nods, his eyebrows furrowed, "I admire your awareness of our intertwined histories. It's super nice of you to apologize for colonialism. But the Apricot Suzerainty is a shithole -- that's why I left."
Zh: “很酷，”席勒点点头，眉头紧锁，“你能意识到我们之间错综复杂的历史，这一点让我很钦佩。能为殖民主义道歉这一点就更好了。不过杏味附属国是一个鬼地方——所以我才会离开。”

En: That's *so* cool, officer. Speaking of, why not support an independent local entrepreneur by buying some pants and glasses?
Zh: “真的*挺酷*的，警官。说到这里，为什么不支持一下本地的个体商户，买些裤子和眼镜呢？”

En: He smiles brightly. "If you say so, officer. I don't worry too much about politics. I'm an entrepreneur, you know? Whatever's good for business is good for me."
Zh: 他灿烂地笑了。“你说是就是吧，警官。我不太担心政治问题。我是个企业家，你知道吗？只要对生意有帮助，对我来说就没问题。”

En: Hey -- why not support *this* local entrepreneur? You can start by buying a pair of sexy pants. Or cool sunglasses... maybe some macaroni?
Zh: “喂——为什么不支持一下*我这个*本地企业家呢？你可以从买一条性感的裤子开始。或者是酷炫的墨镜……也许可以来点通心粉？”

En: Man, this apricots business sounds complicated all of a sudden.
Zh: “老兄，这个杏子的事一下子就复杂起来了。”

En: Oh! Oh no! It's very simple, actually. Siigay is a shithole. Not good for independent entrepreneurs like this one here. That's why I came to Revachol.
Zh: “哦！没那回事！其实非常简单。希依盖是一个鬼地方。这里对个体商户要好多了。所以我才会来瑞瓦肖。”

En: Speaking of, could I convince you to support an independent local entrepreneur right now?
Zh: “说到这里，现在可以给我个机会，说服你支持一下本地个体商户了吗？”

En: My man, officer, *you* make all this possible! Without you, this climate would be extremely bad for business. You're part of the gossamer state!
Zh: “警官，*你*让这一切变成了可能！没有你，这里的气候对做生意来说肯定特别糟糕。你是这个蛛丝帝国的一部分！”

En: If you wanted to be *cynical* about it, you could say we're here to protect the interests of property holders. I'm not, however.
Zh: “如果你是想*冷嘲热讽*的话，其实可以说我们会保护财产所有者的利益。不过我没有。”

En: There you go. A witness with corroborating evidence: it's Ruby.
Zh: 有了。一个有佐证的目击者：就是露比。

En: No, it's still Ruby. Interesting that he doesn't seem aware of her existence, though...
Zh: 不，还是露比。不过有意思的是，他好像不知道她的存在……

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Good joke, officer, you're very funny, you know that?
Zh: “很会说笑嘛，警官，你很有意思，你知道吗？”

En: Really? You think I'm cool?
Zh: “真的吗？你觉得我很酷？”

En: I'm not cool.
Zh: “我不酷。”

En: Oh yes! You got style, you got *personal* style. You know what you like. He surveys his consumerist kingdom with an air of satisfaction.
Zh: “是的！你很有风格，很*个人*的风格。你知道自己喜欢什么。”他满意的审视着自己的消费主义王国。

En: What? No. I can't believe you said that! You got *personal* style! You know what you like.
Zh: “什么？不会的。不敢相信你居然会这么说！你很有*个人*风格啊！你知道自己喜欢什么。”

En: You like premium menswear! Look around and browse, everything looks cool on a guy like you. Take your time. He settles back into the pile of boxes he's sitting on.
Zh: “你喜欢高档男装！随便看看，像你这种型男穿什么都好看。慢慢逛。”他又重新坐回身下的那堆箱子上。

En: Whether I'm cool is unimportant, I have *police* questions for you.
Zh: “我酷不酷不重要，我有*警方*有关的问题要问你。”

En: I think that's fair. He settles back into the pile of boxes he's sitting on, making himself comfortable.
Zh: “我觉得挺公平的。”他又重新坐回身下的那堆箱子上，摆了个舒服的姿势。

En: Of course. Fire away! He settles back into the pile of boxes he's sitting on, making himself comfortable.
Zh: “当然可以。说吧！”他又重新坐回身下的那堆箱子上，摆了个舒服的姿势。

En: Wow.
Zh: “哇哦。”

En: Wow what?
Zh: “哇哦什么？”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: A cop limping down the street, bleeding from the shoulder, face bruised, looking like hell... You know what that is?
Zh: “一个警察在街上一瘸一拐，肩膀流血，脸上淤青，看起来跟鬼一样..,你知道这叫什么吗？”

En: Cool. That's *cool*. He nods and settles back into the pile of boxes.
Zh: “酷啊。这就叫*酷*。”他又重新坐回身下的那堆箱子上，摆了个舒服的姿势。

En: Happy shopping, officer! Everything's cool here! He gives you a thumbs up.
Zh: “祝你购物愉快，警官！这里的一切都太酷了！”他朝你竖起大拇指。

En: The lieutenant nods approvingly.
Zh: 警督赞同地点点头。

En: Where'd you get those *comfy* pants?
Zh: “你从哪里搞到那些*舒服*的裤子的？”

En: Kim, is it true? He doesn't need a permit to sell?
Zh: “金，是真的吗？他不需要许可证就能贩卖商品？”

En: It is, yes. Anyone can set up their shop whenever they feel like it.
Zh: “是的，没错。任何人都能随时随地开店做生意。”

En: I'm an entrepreneur, officer! He begins counting on his fingers. "I've got sources, buyers, suppliers, distributors, manufacturers, wholesalers... all extremely cool and aboveboard."
Zh: “我是个企业家，警官！”他开始数起手指。“我有资源、买家、供货商、分销商、生产商，零售商……全都特别酷，而且都是正大光明的。”

En: He does not need your money. Please disregard that. We're just here to ask some questions.
Zh: “他不要你的钱。请忽略他吧。我们只是来问些问题的。”

En: What kind of stuff are you selling here?
Zh: “你这里都卖些什么东西？”

En: Only the coolest goods in Revachol. I've got sneakers, speakers, *extremely* comfy pants too. Try them on right here. No shame -- only freedom.
Zh: “只有瑞瓦肖最酷的商品。我这里有运动鞋，扬声器，还有*特别*舒服的裤子。就在这边，可以穿上试试。没有害羞——只有自由。”

En: That's all then. Let me ask you something else.
Zh: “先这样吧。我再问你点别的。”

En: The lieutenant winces at the words -- slightly.
Zh: 警督听了这话眉头都皱起来了——不过只有一点点。

En: Does your supplier drive that HUMANOX lorry over there? (Point at the lorry with the big HUMANOX sign on it.)
Zh: “那你的供货商开的是不是纽曼奥克斯的货車？”（指向那辆贴着巨大纽曼奥克斯标志的货車。）

En: No problem here, officer. I get all this from one of my suppliers, an extremely reputable guy.
Zh: “我这里没问题，警官。我的货都是从一个供货商那里弄到的，那家伙的声誉非常好。”

En: You mean these *delicious* pre-packaged, shelf-stable meal kits? Really easy to cook, no hassle, *really* cheap too -- buy some, try them out!
Zh: “你是说这些预先包装好又耐储藏的*美味*餐包吗？烹饪起来特别简单，烦恼全扫光，而且*真心*便宜——买一点吧，试试看！”

En: What? The street vendor looks over his left shoulder and stares at the lorry -- as though he's never seen a lorry in his entire life.
Zh: “什么？”街头小贩的视线越过你的左肩，看向那台货車——仿佛这辈子从没见过这种东西。

En: Look, officer. I'm just the retailer. You'd have to talk to my supplier if you got questions about the supply chain. That's how the game works.
Zh: “你看，警官。我只是个零售商。如果你对供应链有什么问题的话，应该去跟我的供应商聊。游戏都是这么玩的。”

En: A convincing performance, bravo!
Zh: 一场很让人信服的表演，太棒了！

En: I think one said 'Not for Resale' on the packaging.
Zh: “我想有一个包装上面肯定写着‘非卖品’吧。”

En: Interesting. The lieutenant whips out his blue notebook and makes a note.
Zh: “有意思。”警督掏出他的蓝色笔记本，做了些笔记。

En: You glance around the decrepit buildings, the miserable weather, the sidewalks strewn with sunflower seeds and the dust-choked air...
Zh: 你环顾四周破败的建筑，糟糕的天气，丢满瓜子壳的人行道，还有灰扑扑又呛人的空气……

En: Don't play games with me, hawker. What's going on?
Zh: “别跟我耍花招，小贩子。出什么事了？”
