En: And *who* is your supplier, exactly?
Zh: “到底*谁*是你的供应商？”

En: Oh, he's a good guy. I think you'd get along. I'll let you know the next time he's around.
Zh: “哦，他是个好人。我觉得你们一定能愉快相处。下次他过来的时候，我会告诉你。”

En: You see a Samaran street vendor surrounded by a motley assemblage of goods. When he realizes you're looking at him his face breaks into a wide welcoming grin...
Zh: 你看见一个萨马拉街头小贩，周围堆满了各种各样的货物。当他发现你在看他的时候，脸上立马绽放出热情的笑容……

En: Wait, is it really true that you don't need a *single permit* to set up a shop?
Zh: “等等，*一个许可证*都不用就能开店是真的吗？”

En: That's right, no permits, no bureaucracy! That's why this city -- *and* its law officials -- are *so* cool!
Zh: “没错，不用许可证，没有官僚主义！所以这座城市——*还有*这里的执法人员——才会*这么酷*！”

En: Gossamer is a fine, filmy substance consisting of cobwebs spun by small spiders, seen especially in autumn. It's safe to say he's referring to the near absence of regulations.
Zh: 蛛丝是一种又细又薄的物质，是由小蜘蛛吐出的蜘蛛网组成，在秋天尤其常见。可以肯定他指的是近乎不存在的规则。

En: But you're a foreigner. I'm protecting the business interests of foreign nations!
Zh: “但你是个外国人。我要保护外国的商业利益！”

En: Then I'm protecting a state lawlessness. I'm not an officer of law, I'm an officer of common anarchy.
Zh: “那我是在保护一个无法无天的国度。我不是执法人员，我是无政府的执法者！”

En: Yeah, Revachol's the coolest. Doesn't care about where you're from, who you are... It's all about *freedom*. He nods admiringly.
Zh: “是的，瑞瓦肖就是最酷的。根本不在乎你从哪里来，你是谁……这里的一切只跟*自由*有关。”他赞同地点点头。

En: The name "Siileng" is embroidered over his breast pocket.
Zh: 他胸前的口袋上绣着“席勒”这个名字。

En: Anything for you... He smiles again.
Zh: “乐意效劳……”他又笑了。

En: Everything is good here!
Zh: “这里一切都很棒！”

En: There's something here for everyone!
Zh: “每个人都能在这里找到想要的东西！”

En: I let the goods do the talking.
Zh: “我只拿商品说话。”

En: Step right up, mister!
Zh: “过来看看吧，先生！”

En: You're *guaranteed* to find something exciting!
Zh: “你*一定*能找到激动人心的东西的！”

En: Astonishing quality! Unbelievable prices!
Zh: “惊人的品质！难以置信的价格！”

En: Deals so good you just *can't* pass this up!
Zh: “这么好的买卖，你绝对*不能*错过！”

En: I'm practically giving goods away here.
Zh: “我这里基本上都算白送好东西了。”

En: This green FALN A-Z 'Contemporain' is parked in the shadow of the ruins looming overhead. It's seen better days.
Zh: 这辆绿色的FALNA-Z‘现代’停靠在是上方压抑的废墟投下的阴影里。它也曾经有过自己的光辉岁月。

En: A 20 kilometre radius at least. Perhaps extended by an attachable antenna that's not here right now?
Zh: 半径至少20公里。也许是现在已经不在这里的附加天线扩大了范围？

En: Do the honours, he thinks. Connect it yourself.
Zh: 尽主人之谊吧，他想到。你自己联系。

En: The movie stars are still smiling from the walls... the radio transmitter sits on the centre console and a faint smell of perfume is in the air.
Zh: 車壁上的电影明星还在微笑……无线电发射机安装在中心仪表台上，空气中弥漫着一丝香水的味道。

En: This green FALN A-Z 'Contemporain' is parked in the shadow of the ruins looming overhead. It's seen better days.
Zh: 这辆绿色的FALN-A-Z‘现代’停靠在上方压抑的废墟投下的阴影里。它也曾经有过自己的光辉岁月。

En: This is the one our man pointed to... The lieutenant peeks inside.
Zh: “那人指的就是这辆……”警督朝里面窥探着。

En: This must be the one he told us about. Unless he was lying... The lieutenant peeks inside.
Zh: “他告诉我们的肯定就是这辆。除非他在撒谎……”警督朝里面窥探着。

En: This is the one the man pointed to. His friend's lorry.
Zh: 男人指的就是这辆。他朋友的货車。

En: This is the one he pointed to. The green lorry...
Zh: 男人指的就是这辆。绿色的货車……

En: Posters cover the small windows in the back -- you can't make out what's on them.
Zh: 后面的小窗户上贴着很多海报——你看不清上面是什么。

En: It feels like you're peeking into someone's home residence. Inside it's private, cozy, warm. Dusty, too.
Zh: 这种感觉就好像你在偷窥别人的居所。里面很私密，温暖而舒适。而且还有点脏。

En: Try the door handle.
Zh: 试着拉一下门把手。

En: The door is locked. The handle looks shiny, like it's recently replaced.
Zh: 门上锁了。把手看起来闪闪发亮，好像是最近刚换过。

En: There's no picking this lock. The owner has put special care into it. It's a recessed design.
Zh: 撬锁是不太可能的。业主对此特别用心。这是嵌入式设计。

En: You push the key into the lock and turn. It makes a cracking sound -- then the door pops back a few centimetres. You can just...
Zh: 你把钥匙插进锁里旋转。一阵咔哒声响起——然后门向后弹开了几厘米。你可以直接……

En: Open it.
Zh: 打开它。

En: Let's get the prybar from my Kineema and smash in the window.
Zh: “去我的锐影把撬棍拿出来，然后把窗户砸破。”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: Good idea.
Zh: “好主意。”

En: I don't know how *good* it is, but this investigation has taken long enough. We can't afford it to take any longer.
Zh: “我不知道它有多*好*，不过这次调查时间已经够长了。我们不能再这么拖延下去。”

En: Good thing you have one!
Zh: 还好你有一个！

En: But you don't *have* a prybar. The lieutenant would, but he drove away with it in his toolbox.
Zh: 但是你*没有*撬棍。警督可能有，不过他带着工具箱开車走了。

En: Open the door from the inside.
Zh: 从里面把门打开。

En: The window appears to be shatterproof.
Zh: “窗户好像是抗震的。”

En: I'm sorry, it didn't happen.
Zh: “很抱歉，没用。”

En: Really? (Hit again.)
Zh: “真的吗？”“该死。”（再砸一次。）

En: No, it's not *shatterproof*. Give me that. He takes the prybar from you and gestures for you to step back. Then, with little warning...
Zh: “不，它不是*防震的*。把东西给我。”他从你手中接过撬棍，示意你后退。然后，几乎没有预兆的……

En: This has been hard enough. No need to make it any harder. The lieutenant takes the prybar from you and gestures for you to step back. Then, with short warning...
Zh: “本来就已经够难了。没必要把它搞得更难。”警督从你手中接过撬棍，示意你后退。然后，简短地警告之后……

En: He smashes it into the window. Droplets of glass fly everywhere, shattering over the lorry floor and pavement. You can just reach in now.
Zh: 他砸碎了玻璃。玻璃渣向四处飞溅而去，碎裂在货車地板和人行道上。现在你能够着了。

En: Another futile *thunk* sounds in the brisk coastal air.
Zh: 又一阵徒劳的*砰*声回荡在海岸凛冽的空气中。

En: Nice.
Zh: “厉害。”

En: Just open the door from the inside.
Zh: 从里面把门打开。

En: Mhm, he nods and gives you the tool back. "It went better than I expected -- open it from the inside."
Zh: “嗯，”他点点头，然后把工具还给你。“比我想象的要好——从里面打开吧。”

En: The actresses -- and the rare actor -- all smile you a warm good bye. A radio transmitter is attached to the dashboard and a toolbox sits under the driver's seat.
Zh: 女演员——还有少数男演员——全都微笑着目送你离开。一台无线电发射机连接在仪表板上，驾驶座下面有一个工具箱。

En: The smell of a thousand cigarettes, some dead actresses and a rusty old toolbox under the seat.
Zh: 几千只香烟的味道，一些去世的女演员，座椅下面还有一个锈迹斑斑的旧工具箱。

En: The ghostly actresses and the rusty toolbox under the driver's seat. And the oddly bulging seat cover.
Zh: 幽灵般的女演员，驾驶座下锈迹斑斑的工具箱。还有鼓得很奇怪的座套。

En: The ghostly actresses and the rusty toolbox, under the driver's seat... And the oddly bulging seat cover.
Zh: 幽灵般的女演员，驾驶座下的锈蚀工具箱……还有鼓得很奇怪的座套。

En: The smell of a thousand cigarettes, some dead actresses and a rusty old toolbox under the seat...
Zh: 几千只香烟的味道，死去的女演员，还有座椅下那个锈迹斑斑的旧工具箱……

En: *Bonne prise*, the lieutenant commends you as you shift through the treasure -- well worn and folded into neat squares.
Zh: “*很棒的奖赏*，”警督称赞在宝藏中翻找的你——东西磨损很严重，折成了整齐的四方形。

En: The stack of maps looks just like before -- barely noticeable. The movie stars look silently by and the pull-out toolbox has a rubber handle, worn from years of use.
Zh: 这堆地图跟之前那些很像——几乎很难发现。电影明星在一旁默默注视，工具箱上有一个橡胶把手，由于多年的使用而磨损了。

En: You stuff the maps back under the seat cover. Better not to leave traces of you being here yet... the movie stars look silently by.
Zh: 你把地图重新塞回座套下面。最好还是不要在这里留下你的痕迹……电影明星们在一旁默默注视着。

En: Remove the radio transceiver.
Zh: 取下无线电收发器。

En: It takes a few moments, but eventually the transceiver slides from its greased socket mount with an eminently satisfying *chhhnk*.
Zh: 花了点时间，但收发器最终还是从涂了油的插口支架上滑出，发出令人满意的*嚓咔*声。

En: Nearly the same sound produced by the action on a good breechloader, you can't fail to notice.
Zh: 发出的声音几乎和给后膛枪上好弹一样，十分明显。

En: As you take the transceiver in your hands, the lieutenant bites his lower lip, apparently deep in thought.
Zh: 当你把收发器拿在手里时，警督咬着下嘴唇，显然在沉思中。

En: Funny, isn't it?
Zh: “很有趣，不是吗？”

En: Yeah, sounds just like an old breechloader.
Zh: “是啊，听起来就像一把老式后膛枪。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “什么意思？”

En: I don't really get jokes.
Zh: “我不太懂笑点在哪。”

En: Oh, I didn't mean that. It's just ironic that you're using the lady driver's radio to contact the very authorities she was so keen to evade.
Zh: “哦，我不是这个意思。你竟然用女司机的无线电联系她拼尽全力想要躲避的当局，这还挺讽刺的。”

En: The lady driver used this same radio to evade Coalition authorities, and now you're using it to contact them directly. It's just ironic, that's all.
Zh: “女司机用这台无线电躲避开联盟当局，现在你用它直接联系他们。这有点讽刺，仅此而已。”

En: It's not really a *joke*, per se. Just ironic that you're using the lady driver's radio to contact the very authorities she was so keen to evade.
Zh: “这本身并不是什么*笑点*。只是很讽刺，你用女司机的无线电来联系她避之唯恐不及的当局。”

En: This has *got* to be better than your DynaWave.
Zh: “这*肯定*要比你車里的动力波质量更好。”

En: Do you think we'll actually be able to reach the Coalition with this?
Zh: “你觉得我们真的能用这个联系到联盟吗？”

En: Examine the transceiver.
Zh: 检查收发器。

En: Put the transceiver away. (Conclude.)
Zh: 把收发器收起来。(结束)

En: You've surprised me more than once during the course of our investigation, but I have to say it still seems like a *remote* scenario. He shrugs.
Zh: 警督耸耸肩。“在我们调查的过程中，你已经不止一次地让我感到惊讶，但我不得不说，这个场面看起来仍然有些*陌生*……”

En: To be perfectly frank, I have my reservations about this whole antenna scheme. But who knows? We may yet be surprised.
Zh: “说实话，我对整个天线方案持保留意见。但谁知道呢？也许还会有惊喜等着咱们……”

En: I wouldn't like to guess. Your performance so far doesn't exactly inspire confidence, but all sorts of things are possible...
Zh: “我不想瞎猜。你到目前为止的表现并不能让人完全放心，但什么事都有可能……”

En: I'm more interested in *why* you're so fixated on this idea in the first place.
Zh: “我更感兴趣的是，你*为什么*一开始就这么执着于这个想法。”

En: *You* could reach the Coalition and have your talk about *la responsabilité*... *I* could grow 10 centimetres and become a flanker for the Stormers.
Zh: “*你*要是可以联系到联盟，和他们进行你*职责内*的对话……那*我*就可以立马长高10厘米，去当暴风队的侧卫……”

En: Who's to say what's possible and what's not?
Zh: “谁敢说哪些可能哪些不可能？”

En: But here's the thing, detective. *We're* the proper channels. Doing our work, right here on the ground, is how we'll solve this case. There's no need to bring the Coalition into this.
Zh: “但问题是这样的，警探，*我们*就是正当的渠道。做好我们的份内事，就在大地上，这才是我们办案的正途，没必要把联盟也牵扯进来。”

En: Let me make a prediction, detective. You are not going to see the inside of a Coalition warship anytime soon.
Zh: “我做个预测吧，警探。你近期应该是看不到联盟战舰的内部构造了。”

En: I certainly understand the impulse, but this is our job, detective. Our whole *raison d'être*. At some point, we'll need to return to it.
Zh: “我当然明白这种冲动，但这才是我们的工作，警探，我们全部*存在的理由*。到了某些时候，我们就需要回归到工作中去。”

En: But now you've got what you came for, so I suppose we'll have to see this business through.
Zh: “但现在你已经得到了你想要的东西，所以我想我们只能把这件事办完了。”

En: We can't assign *la responsabilité* if we don't go through the proper channels.
Zh: “如果不经由正当的渠道，我们就无从分配*职责*了。”

En: To be honest, I mostly just want to ride on Coalition Warship Archer.
Zh: “说实话，我主要是想坐一坐联盟战舰弓箭手号。”

En: I think I just need a break from our main case for a bit.
Zh: “我想我只是需要从我们的主要案件中抽身休息一下。”

En: An Esterhaz is certainly nothing to sniff at. The lieutenant nods. "But if you compare the feature sets, I would say they're more-or-less equivalent."
Zh: “一台伊特海兹的产品当然不可小觑，”警督点点头。“但如果你比较一下功能，我觉得两台差不多是一样的。”

En: I've no reason to be jealous, detective. The lieutenant smiles to himself.
Zh: “我没有理由嫉妒，警探。”警督自嘲地笑了笑。

En: Yes, another draw. It appears we are quite equally matched.
Zh: “是的，又是一场平局。看来我们的实力相当。”

En: So it would appear.
Zh: “看来是这样。”

En: It should. Assuming we can find a way to replace the frequency dial.
Zh: “应该是这样。如果我们能找到可以替代调频旋钮的办法的话。”

En: That's okay. As long as it works.
Zh: “没关系，只要能用就行。”

En: Fine, it appears we've reached a draw.
Zh: “好吧，看来我们打了个平手。”

En: So you're not jealous?
Zh: “那你不嫉妒？”

En: It's about the size of a small paving stone, and nearly as heavy. Now that you're looking at it more closely, you realise the various dials and meters make it look vaguely like a face. A distinctive triangular logo forms the nose.
Zh: 这东西和小铺路石差不多大，也差不多重。现在你再仔细看看，才发现各种表盘和仪表使它看起来隐隐约约像一张脸。一个独特的三角形标志构成了鼻子的形状。

En: Where the transceiver used to be, there's just a rectangular shadow now.
Zh: 收发器原本的位置上现在只剩一个长方形的阴影。

En: That's the marque of Esterhaz Elektronika, one of the major manufacturers of advanced radio equipment.
Zh: 那是伊特海兹电子的商标，先进无线电设备的主要制造商之一。

En: Not just any face... it reminds you of the grotesque carvings sometimes found in prehistoric caves.
Zh: 这不是一张普通的脸……这让你想起有时在史前洞穴中发现的怪异雕刻。

En: Wait, Ruby?
Zh: 等等，露比？

En: Wait, the missing eighth Hardie?
Zh: 等等，失踪的第8个哈迪兄弟？

En: Looks like it, yes.
Zh: 看起来是了，没错。

En: Looks like her, yes.
Zh: “看起来就是她，没错。”

En: Wait, the missing eighth Hardie? Here?
Zh: “等等，失踪的第8个哈迪兄弟？在这里？”

En: Wait, Ruby?
Zh: “等等，露比？”

En: Not necessarily. Ruby could have kept the drug trade a secret.
Zh: “不一定。露比可能把贩毒的事保密了。”

En: Not necessarily. The lady driver could have kept the drug trade a secret.
Zh: “不一定。女司机可能把贩毒的事保密了。”

En: You close the rusty old lorry door.
Zh: 你关上锈迹斑斑的旧货車门。

En: You close the rusty old lorry door -- your job here is done.
Zh: 你关上锈迹斑斑的旧货車门——你的工作已经完成了。

En: You close the rusty old lorry door.
Zh: 你关上锈迹斑斑的旧货車门。

En: You close the rusty old lorry door for now -- your job here isn't done yet.
Zh: 你暂时关上锈迹斑斑的旧货車门——你的工作还没有完成。

En: Return the papers under the seat.
Zh: 把那堆纸塞回座位底下。

En: Return the papers under the seat.
Zh: 把那堆纸塞回座位底下。

En: Return the stack of papers under the seat. (Conclude.)
Zh: 把那堆纸塞回座位底下。（总结。）

En: Peel off the cover on the passenger seat.
Zh: 扯开乘客座椅的罩子。

En: Examine the radio.
Zh: 检查无线电。

En: Call down the hammer of truth and justice! With a firm punch that could shatter bone, you smash through the window. Why you didn't equip and use the prybar, who knows -- but now, release your secrets, lorry cabin!
Zh: 召唤真理与正义之锤吧！随着一记能让人粉身碎骨的重拳，你砸碎了窗户。为什么没有使用撬棍，谁知道呢——不过现在，揭露出你的秘密吧，货車車厢！

En: So Ruby really is running a *complex operation* out of her lorry.
Zh: “所以露比真是在自己的货車里进行*复杂的活动*。”

En: The movie stars are still smiling from the walls... the radio transmitter sits on the centre console and a faint smell of perfume is in the air.
Zh: 車壁上的电影明星还在微笑……无线电发射机安装在中心仪表台上，空气中弥漫着一丝香水的味道。

En: At least we can now ask Titus some concrete questions about it.
Zh: “至少现在我们能找提图斯问一些具体的问题了。”

En: She is, yes. You should talk to Titus about it, now you have some concrete evidence.
Zh: 她有的，没错。现在你有了具体的证据，应该去问问提图斯。

En: Ruby really is running a *complex operation* out of her lorry.
Zh: 露比真是在自己的货車里进行*复杂的活动*。

En: Unlock the door with the key Tommy gave you.
Zh: 用汤米给你的钥匙打开車门。

En: Smash the window in with the prybar.
Zh: 用撬棍砸开窗户。

En: Tool not equipped.
Zh: 没有装备工具。

En: Icosohedral Red Die.
Zh: 二十面体红骰子。

En: Lifted weights.
Zh: 举起重物。

En: Giant multitool.
Zh: 巨大的多用工具。

En: Try to peek in the window.
Zh: 试着朝窗户里面偷窥。

En: Try to peek in the window.
Zh: 试着朝窗户里面偷窥。

En: How are we going to get this open, Kim?
Zh: “我们要怎么打开它呢，金？”

En: How am I going to get this open?
Zh: 我怎么才能打开它呢？

En: Use the prybar to smash the window. Open it from the inside.
Zh: “用撬棍砸碎窗户。从里面打开。”

En: With a key -- or a tool. Like, say, a prybar.
Zh: 用钥匙——或者是一个工具。比如说，一根撬棍。

En: The glass on the side windows is tinted and covered with dust. You can barely make out the shape of a seat and two steering levers.
Zh: 侧窗上的玻璃是有色的，上面盖满尘土。你花了好大劲才辨别出一个座位和两个操舵柄的形状。

En: With a firm grip you raise the prybar, a glint of light catching on the tip. Call down the hammer of truth and justice! Release your secrets, lorry cabin!
Zh: 你握紧撬棍举了起来，闪烁的灯光照在撬棍尖端。召唤真理与正义之锤吧！揭开出你的秘密吧，货車車厢！

En: Prybar in hand, you take a hard swing at the window. A loud *thunk* rings out and the prybar bounces back, leaving a tiny spider web on the glass.
Zh: 手中握着撬棍的你用力朝窗户挥去。伴随着响亮的*砰*的一声，撬棍弹了回来，玻璃上只留下一个小小的蜘蛛网。

En: The smell of cigarettes and perfume welcomes you. The cabin inside is plastered with old movie posters, actresses smile from the walls. There's a rectangular shadow where the radio used to be and a toolbox tucked under the driver's seat. Some tools lie scattered near the pedals.
Zh: 烟味和香水味扑鼻而来。车厢里贴满了老电影海报，墙上的女演员们在微笑。本应放收音机的地方有一个长方形的影子，驾驶座下塞着一个工具箱。一些工具散落在踏板附近。

En: The smell of cigarettes and perfume welcomes you. The cabin inside is plastered with old movie posters, actresses smile from the walls. There's a radio transmitter in the front and a toolbox tucked under the driver's seat. Some tools lie scattered near the pedals.
Zh: 香烟混杂着香水的味道朝你迎面扑来。車厢里贴满旧电影海报，女演员们在車壁上朝你微笑。前方安装了一个无线电发射机，驾驶座下塞着一个工具箱。有些工具零乱地散落在脚踏板周围。

En: Admire the posters.
Zh: 欣赏海报。

En: Pull out the pull-out toolbox.
Zh: 拉出工具箱。

En: [Close the door.]
Zh: [关上门。]

En: This FALN A-Z, abandoned by its owner, still stands in the shadow of the ruins above it.\n
Zh: 一台被主人抛弃的FALNA-Z，仍然站立在废墟的阴影之下。

En: A lorry, abandoned by its owner, stuck in the interminable traffic jam.
Zh: 一台被主人抛弃的货車，困在冗长的堵車大军中。

En: There's something odd about the passenger seat... The seating fabric has been pulled tight over the lower side of the seat where the toolbox should be.
Zh: 这个驾驶座有些奇怪……座椅蒙皮朝下部拉得过紧，那里应该是放工具箱的地方。

En: There's definitely perfume in the air. It's spicy, with a hint of ambrette, wafting through the bitter air of the cabin.
Zh: 空气中肯定有一股香水味。它很辛辣，还有一丝麝香梨的味道，飘荡在車辆内刺骨的空气中。

En: Voila! A stack of neatly folded papers has been stashed behind the seating fabric. You see three maps depicting a large metropolitan area. It's Revachol, some of its routes and highways have been outlined with a pen.
Zh: 快看！座椅蒙皮背后藏着一堆整齐叠放的文件。你看见描绘着大城市区域的三张地图。这是瑞瓦肖，有些路线和高速公路用笔画出来了。

En: A metallic drawer slides out from the seat: It's empty, except for a folded newspaper.
Zh: 一个金属抽屉从座椅下滑了出来：里面是空的，只有一张折起来的报纸。

En: Open the door.
Zh: 打开门。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Check the pedals.
Zh: 检查脚踏板。

En: Smash it.
Zh: 砸碎它。

En: The window shatters and droplets of glass fly everywhere, shattering over the lorry floor and pavement.
Zh: 窗户被砸了个粉碎，玻璃渣向四处飞溅而去，掉落在货車地面和人行道上。

En: Looks like the window might be shatterproof. You may have to rethink your approach.
Zh: 看来窗户是抗震的。也许你要重新考虑自己的方法。

En: Subtle, says the lieutenant and sticks his hand in the window -- like a common car thief -- and then opens it.
Zh: “很微妙，”警督一边说着，一边把手伸进窗户——就像个普通的偷車贼——然后打开了它。

En: Study the centrefold.
Zh: 研究中间插页。

En: Enough of the posters.
Zh: 看够海报了。

En: The movie stars are still smiling from the walls... But there's something strange about the passenger seat. The radio transmitter sits on the centre console and the pull-out toolbox lies hidden under the driver's seat. Some tools lie scattered near the pedals.
Zh: 車壁上的电影明星还在微笑……不过乘客座椅有些奇怪。无线电发射机安装在中心仪表台上，工具箱隐藏在驾驶座下面。有些工具零乱地散落在脚踏板周围。

En: The movie stars are still smiling from the walls... But there's a rectangular shadow where the radio used to be and something strange about the passenger seat. The pull-out toolbox lies hidden under the driver's seat. Some tools have been left scattered near the pedals.
Zh: 墙上的电影明星还在微笑……但收音机的位置有一个长方形的影子，副驾驶座也有些奇怪。抽出式工具箱藏在驾驶座下。一些工具散落在踏板附近。

En: The movie stars are still smiling from the walls... But there's a rectangular shadow where the radio used to be. The pull-out toolbox lies hidden under the driver's seat. Some tools have been left scattered near the pedals.
Zh: 墙上的电影明星还在微笑……但收音机的位置有一个长方形的影子。抽出式工具箱藏在驾驶座下。一些工具散落在踏板附近。

En: The movie stars are still smiling from the walls. There's a radio transmitter in the front and a pull-out toolbox tucked under the driver's seat. Some tools lie scattered near the pedals.
Zh: 車壁上的电影明星还在微笑。前方安装了一个无线电发射机，驾驶座下面塞着一个工具箱。有些工具零乱地散落在脚踏板周围。

En: The actress is draped in a sheath dress, one of her shoulders bared. The faded remains of an autograph run across the poster. She's looking past the camera.
Zh: 女演员身穿紧身裙装，一边肩膀裸露在外。褪色的签名横跨整面海报。她的目光穿透了照相机背后。

En: This is Tippe Tijonne, a starlet from the dawn of cinematography. Less known for her talent, than her tragic, untimely death.
Zh: 这是蒂比·缇约妮，电影艺术的黎明时期的小明星。相比她的才华，人们还是对她悲剧般的香消玉殒更耳熟能详。

En: She's delicate, yet lost. Ruined underneath it all.
Zh: 她很娇弱，但是也很迷茫。一切都被毁掉了。

En: A feeling of tenderness washes over you -- a longing even, perhaps. And gentle tragedy.
Zh: 一种亲切的感觉笼罩你的全身——甚至可以说是一种渴望。还有温柔的悲剧。

En: What happened to her?
Zh: 她出什么事了？

En: She wasted away in a drug den called 'The Door to the River', not far from here on Boogie Street. A mixture of cocaine and morphine. She was a afraid of the world. And the camera too.
Zh: 她在一个叫‘河流之门’的毒窝日渐衰弱，就在离布吉街不远的地方。可卡因和吗啡混着使用。她害怕这个世界，还有照相机。

En: Stop and wonder: What's that smell?
Zh: 停下来想想：那是什么味道？

En: The remnants of a sweet juniper-scented perfume. Probably 'Granate No 5'.
Zh: 一种杜松味香水残留的味道。可能是‘格拉纳特5号’。

En: You wedge yourself under the steering-wheel to get a better look. Seems like the few tools lying around here -- a hammer, a pair of pliers, a rusty wrench -- have been casually thrown there by the disorganized driver.
Zh: 你把自己塞到方向盘下面，想看得更仔细一点。这里好像散落着一些工具——一把锤子，一副老虎钳，一个锈蚀的扳手——被那个没有条理的司机随手扔的到处都是。

En: This particular lorry is a FALN A-Z 'Tempo', a model infamous for its irritating design mistake: the slippery-smooth throttles.
Zh: 这台货車是FALNA-Z‘飞驰’，这个型号因为让人恼火的设计缺陷臭名远扬：超级滑的油门。

En: Unfold the newspaper.
Zh: 展开报纸。

En: Push in the pull-out toolbox.
Zh: 把工具箱推进去。

En: The pull-out toolbox slides back into its nest. The rest is as it was: posters, a radio, dust on the windows.
Zh: 抽出式工具箱再次滑入自己的老巢。剩下的还是那些：海报，无线电，还有窗户上的灰尘。

En: The pull-out toolbox slides back into its nest. The rest is as it was: radio, posters, a trace of motor oil smell under all the cigarettes.
Zh: 抽出式工具箱再次滑入自己的巢穴。剩下的还是那些：无线电，海报，还有香烟味下那一丝机油的味道。

En: It's an issue of 'Périphérique' from last Wednesday. A piece of paper falls out from its pages.
Zh: 这是上周三发行的‘环城公路’。有几张纸从书页里掉了出来。

En: Pick up the note.
Zh: 捡起纸条。

En: It looks like an article ripped out from a radio-enthusiast magazine. Complex mathematical equations explain the basics of something called 'the ULAN frequency system'.
Zh: 看起来像是一篇从无线电爱好者杂志上撕下来的文章。复杂的数学方程式解释着某种‘ULAN频率系统’的基本原理。

En: Adapting a high-quality radio-transmitter to emit this frequency may make it powerful enough to transmit a signal from isola to isola. But that's just a guess.
Zh: 改装一台能发射这种频率的高质量无线电发射机，也许能让它的功率强大到能在各洲之间发送信号。但这只是一个猜想。

En: These formulas look oddly *painful*. Maybe it's the hangover, but they give you a headache.
Zh: 这些公式看起来有种古怪的*痛苦感*。也许是因为宿醉，但是它们让你有些头痛。

En: The lieutenant leans over your shoulder to examine the note: "The ULAN frequency system? I've never heard of that before. I know of FM, AM, UKV, but..." His thought trails off.
Zh: 警督从你的肩膀上方探过脑袋来查看纸条：“ULAN频率系统？我之前从来没有听说过。我知道FM，AM，UKV，但是……”他的声音逐渐减弱。

En: Looks like the frequency dial is absent. It requires a key to work, but the key has been removed -- likely by the missing lady driver.
Zh: 看来频率刻度盘不见了。它需要一把钥匙才能工作，但是钥匙被拔出来了——很可能是那个失踪女司机做的。

En: The lieutenant leans closer to the radio and hums: "Strange. There are so many radio stations saved here... must be over one hundred at least."
Zh: 金凑近无线电，嘟囔着：“奇怪。这里存储了好多无线电台啊……至少100个往上。”

En: Why would anyone need so many radio stations?
Zh: “为什么有人需要这么多无线电台？”

En: What about the Speedfreaks FM? Is it one of the saved stations here?
Zh: “那狂飙怪人.FM呢？里面也存了那个电台吗？”

En: Is there anything we can do with the radio?
Zh: “我们能用这台无线电做些什么吗？”

En: No, that's on 78.9. He stops, coughing awkwardly.
Zh: “不，那是78.9FM。”他停下来，尴尬地咳嗽到。

En: Doesn't look like it -- it's completely inoperable without the dial key.
Zh: “看起来不太可能——没了拨号键，它根本不能用。”

En: What else is here... (Conclude.)
Zh: “这里还有什么……”（总结。）

En: I'm sorry. (Smash it, apologetically.)
Zh: “很抱歉。”（带着歉意砸碎它。）

En: Fold open the topmost map.
Zh: 展开最上面那张地图。

En: Fold open the second map.
Zh: 展开第二张地图。

En: Look at the third map.
Zh: 看看第三张地图。

En: Return the papers under the seat.
Zh: 把报纸放回座位下面。

En: So it would seem. The lieutenant examines the maps with a furrowed brow.
Zh: “有这个可能。”警督看着地图，眉头紧锁。

En: Hard to say. This distribution network looks certainly large, yet still vague enough... It doesn't reveal much about the *besmertie* behind it.
Zh: “很难说。这个分销网络看起来确实很大，不过还是很模糊……它并没有揭露太多*贝斯莫蒂*的信息。”

En: *Besmertie*? That sounds vaguely familiar.
Zh: “*贝斯莫蒂*？听起来隐约有些熟悉。”

En: A besmertie is a Revacholian crime syndicate. They see themselves as the inheritors of the 14 Revacholian indotribes, but really they're just violent gangs vying for control on the West side of Revachol...
Zh: “贝斯莫蒂是瑞瓦肖的一个犯罪集团。他们把自己当成是瑞瓦肖14寡头的继承者，不过其实他们只是一个暴力团伙，想要争夺瑞瓦肖西部的控制权……”

En: Who do you think is behind this?
Zh: “你觉得是谁在背后操纵这一切？”

En: Especially bad news for cops who may have something in their past they don't even *know* is there.
Zh: 对于那些过去发生过一些连他们自己都*不知道*什么事的警察来说，这是个尤其坏的消息。

En: He's *bad news*. The lieutenant removes his glasses and polishes them with a handkerchief. "There have been attempts at a serious investigation before, but they... haven't ended well for those involved."
Zh: “他就是*坏消息*。”金取下眼镜，用手帕擦拭着。“之前有人想要深入调查，不过那些人……那些参与其中的人的结局并不是很好。”

En: Somewhere on a hidden field, a flash of light, a whirring sound. Camera lenses focus on a man in a ragged police uniform standing in a field of blood-red poppies. A blurry figure dressed in white stands right by the camera...
Zh: 在某个隐蔽的地方，一道闪光，一阵飕飕声。镜头聚焦在一个穿着破旧警服的男人身上，他正站在一片血红的罂粟地里。一个身穿白衣的模糊人影站在相机的右侧……

En: The bedraggled cop is holding a manure fork. "Don't hurt me," he whimpers.
Zh: 全身泥泞的警察握着一把粪叉。“别伤害我，”他低语到。

En: But why... why would I hurt my own *peone*? asks the man in white. "What kind of an *animal* would do such thing?"
Zh: “但是为什么……我为什么要伤害自己的*代理人*？”白衣男人问道。“什么样的*动物*会做出这种事情？”

En: This large map displays the elevated motorway called 8/81. The intake leading to Martinaise is marked with a blue X. There's another X on the off-ramp at a place called the Old South.
Zh: 这张大地图展示着一个名为8/81的高架高速公路。通往马丁内斯的入口标记着一个蓝色的X。还有一个X在出口匝道，一个名叫旧南城的地方。

En: There, hundreds of thousands of motor carriages roar on the 8/81, high above the mass of brown and red roofs that is Jamrock. The commuters don't even look down; the world ceases to exist outside the windshield.
Zh: 成百上千辆汽車在8/81高速公路上轰鸣，它高高耸立在加姆洛克大片棕红色的屋顶之上。通勤者甚至没有低头张望；挡风玻璃外的世界仿佛不复存在。

En: Tollbooths at the intakes are marked with a circle. It looks like there are scant few ways of getting onto the elevated motorway that runs over Jamrock -- and this person knows them all.
Zh: 入口处的收费亭用圆圈标记出来。看来要想走上横穿加姆洛克的高架高速公路，方法并不是太多——-而且这个人全都知道。

En: Where does the road lead...
Zh: 这些路通向什么地方……

En: To Couron, through the middle income neighbourhoods there -- by the river -- and then to Stella Maris and La Delta, for work. While the men and women of Jamrock scuttle to their fates below the road.
Zh: 前往科戎，穿过河边的那个中等收入社区——然后前往斯特拉里斯和德尔塔上班。加姆洛克的男男女女在公路下飞速奔向自己的命运。

En: This municipal map from the Thirties displays a complex system of storm sewers underneath a sub-district called The Pox (Old Military Hospital), right adjacent to the 41st Precinct.
Zh: 这张三十年代的市政地图展示了一个名叫帕克斯（旧军事医院）次级区域下方错综复杂的排雨管道系统，正好跟41辖区相邻。

En: No storm will ever drown Revachol, the great solution to the riddle of history.
Zh: 没有什么暴风雨能将瑞瓦肖——历史之谜的伟大答案——淹没。

En: Wind rips through the empty hallways of the once great military hospital, now just a ruin under an overgrown park. Beneath the hospital, great sewer tunnels hum and vibrate with life of their own...
Zh: 风在前军事医院那条空旷的走廊里狂啸，现在那里不过是一个破败的废墟，掩藏在一个杂草丛生的公园之下。医院下面，大雨水管道在轰鸣，用它们自己的生命震动着……

En: What is that sound...
Zh: 那是什么声音……

En: The rattle of motor carriages and lorries driving through long forgotten tunnels. Lit by gas lights.
Zh: 货車和货車轰鸣着，开过那条早已被遗忘的隧道。那里使用的是煤气灯照明。

En: The final map displays a labyrinth of service tunnels left over from the construction of Motorway 8/81. A few routes have been marked with a pen -- where the tunnels and sewers surface near the Eminent Domain and a traffic island in Central Jamrock, by the lake.
Zh: 最后一张地图展示了8/81高速公路建造时遗留下来的如迷宫般复杂的工作隧道。有一些路径用笔标记出来了——在那里，隧道和下水道在艾米涅特区和加姆洛克中央的交通岛附近浮现，正好在一个湖边。

En: These service tunnels were probably used during the construction of the foundation beneath the motorway.
Zh: 这些工作隧道可能是在建设高速公路的时候使用的。

En: Despite the cold, figures move about cloaked in shadow, their breath escaping in white clouds that fade into the darkness. Wherever you look, the night gives cover to her messengers.
Zh: 尽管天气寒冷，人影还是藏匿在阴影下面来来往往，他们呼出的水汽逃进云朵里，消散在黑暗中。不管你往哪个方向看，夜晚都庇护着她的使者。

En: Where are they going?
Zh: 他们要去哪？

En: They travel from Eminent Domain to Perdition and on to the Main. Over the River Esperance, on Route 8/81 to the Old South -- the exit ramp is supposed to be shut down, but there's much activity.
Zh: 他们从艾米涅特区，穿过永劫路，一路到达主路。跨过埃斯佩兰斯河，走上通往旧南城的8/81高速公路——出口匝道应该被关闭了，不过那里仍然很活跃。

En: The road spreads like the tentacles of a great octopus.
Zh: 道路向一只巨大的章鱼，张开了它的触角。

En: The smugglers have infiltrated the Motor Tract.
Zh: “走私犯已经渗透高速公路了。”

En: The cluttered cabin of the abandoned lorry offers no reply.
Zh: 这台被抛弃货車的凌乱車厢没有任何回应。

En: What does all this mean?
Zh: “这是什么意思？”

En: It means that the smugglers are secretly using the motorway to transport their goods and materials. They've infiltrated East Motor Tract, most likely...
Zh: “意思是说走私犯在秘密使用这些高速公路运送商品和物料。他们很可能已经渗透了东高速公路……”

En: The RCM patrols most of these auxiliary roads, though apparently not all of them.
Zh: “RCM会在大部分辅路上巡逻，不过显然并没有全部覆盖到。”

En: It's definitely not the Union. They just do *some* logistics. This operation has spread everywhere in Jamrock. If it's that widespread, then Madre remains the most likely suspect.
Zh: “这肯定不是工会做的。他们只参与了*一些*物流。这个经营范围扩展到了加姆洛克的每一个角落。如果范围真有这么广，那马德雷依然是最大的嫌疑人。”

En: Best not to disturb the scene. The lieutenant nods. "I'll have Forensics go over the lorry and pick these up later."
Zh: 警督点点头。“最好不要破环现场——等下我找法医重新检查一遍货車，之后再回来检查。”

En: There's something odd about the passenger seat... The seating fabric has been pulled tight over the lower side of the seat where the toolbox should be.
Zh: 乘客座椅有些奇怪的地方……座椅蒙皮朝下部拉得过紧，那里应该是放工具箱的地方。

En: Wait, doesn't she... resemble someone you know? But you can't put a finger on *whom* exactly.
Zh: 等等，她是不是……跟你认识的一个人很像？但是你不能确切地指出到底是*谁*。

En: Wait, this actress really does look *a lot* like Klaasje. She may have been right about this Ruby after all...
Zh: 等等，这个女演员看起来跟卡拉洁*很像*。看来她对露比的看法很有可能是对的……

En: Sandpaper adds extra grip.
Zh: 砂纸可以增加额外的抓地力。

En: You should probably still talk to them, see what they've got to say for themselves.
Zh: 也许你应该和他们谈谈，看看他们有什么要为自己辩护的。

En: God damn, maggot! Drag your ass to the gym and do some reps right now!
Zh: 该死的，蛆虫！赶紧把你的屁股挪到健身房去，马上给我锻炼起来！

En: If only Lieutenant Kitsuragi were here to help you piece together the evidence.
Zh: 如果曷城警督在这里就好了，他能帮你把这些证据拼凑起来。

En: This has been hard enough. No need to make it any harder.
Zh: “这本来就已经够难的了。没必要把它搞得更难。”

En: These are movie posters featuring starlets from long-forgotten films -- from the Twenties, the Teens, even the Nineties of the last century.
Zh: 这些是电影海报，主角都是些被早已被遗忘的小明星——来自二十年代，一〇年代，甚至是上世纪九十年代。

En: One of them particularly catches your eye: a centrefold of an ingénue attached right above the back seat.
Zh: 其中有一个吸引了你的眼球：一个少女的插页正好落在后座的正上方。

En: Raise your eyebrows.
Zh: 扬起眉头。

En: What? I happen to have committed the frequency to memory. I have no problem admitting that.
Zh: “什么？我碰巧把频率记在脑海里了。承认这一点我完全没问题。”

En: Say "Fuck the police!" and *then* smash it.
Zh: 说一句“警察去死！”，*然后*砸碎它。

En: Say "Welcome to Revachol, window!" and smash it.
Zh: 说一句“欢迎来到瑞瓦肖，死玻璃！”，*然后*砸碎它。

En: But why the sandpaper?
Zh: 不过为什么是砂纸呢？

En: No idea... You could peel off the sandpaper to peak underneath, but that would be tampering with the scene.
Zh: 不知道……你可以把砂纸剥下来看看下面是什么，但是这样就破坏现场了。

En: ... matching one of the footprints found from the crime scene. Which means that the missing lady driver was present at the lynching!
Zh: ……正好跟犯罪现场发现的一组脚印是匹配的。这就意味着私刑发生的时候，那个失踪的女司机就在现场！

En: But one odd detail does catch your eye: A piece of sandpaper has been glued to the throttle.
Zh: 但是一个古怪的细节确实吸引了你的眼球：一张砂纸被粘在油门上了。

En: Why would anyone glue a piece of sandpaper to the gas pedal?
Zh: “为什么会有人把砂纸粘在油门上？”

En: Sandpaper? the lieutenant repeats. "A novel technique... Must be to offer some extra grip to the driver's foot."
Zh: “砂纸？”警督重复到。“一种很新奇的技巧……肯定为了给司机的脚增加额外的抓地力。”

En: Looks like the driver has glued a piece of sandpaper to the throttle to offer some extra grip.
Zh: “看来司机把一张砂纸粘在了油门上，以此来提供更多抓地力。”

En: Looks like the driver has glued a piece of sandpaper to fix the slippery throttle of their FALN A-Z 'Tempo'.
Zh: “看来司机把一张砂纸粘在了油门上，来解决FALNA-Z‘飞驰’油门太滑的问题。”

En: What a novel technique... The lieutenant leans closer. "You sure do know your lorries, officer."
Zh: “一种很新奇的技巧……“警督靠近了一些。“你确实很懂货車，警官。”

En: Sandpaper? the lieutenant repeats. "A novel technique..."
Zh: “砂纸？”警督重复到。“一种很新奇的技巧……”

En: I don't care about rules, I need to get some *results*! (Peel off the sandpaper.)
Zh: 我不在乎规则，我需要的是*结果*！（撕掉砂纸。）

En: I could just take a peak... A teensy-weensy peek... (Peel off just the edge.)
Zh: 我可以瞄一眼……很小很小的一眼……（撕开边缘。）

En: The sandpaper would also rub off the pattern from the driver's right boot sole.
Zh: “砂纸还可以磨掉司机右边鞋底的纹路。”

En: Back up into the cabin again.
Zh: 再次进入車厢。

En: And she's also the one running the drug trade... What a handful.
Zh: “所以她还在贩毒……真是棘手。”

En: Yes? He likes where this is going...
Zh: “是吗？”他喜欢这种走向……

En: First the drug smuggling, now this... How deep does this rabbit hole go?
Zh: “首先是贩毒，现在又是这个……这个兔子洞到底会有多深呢？”

En: The throttle underneath looks as smooth as glass, slippery even... The sandpaper must be there to add some extra *grip*!
Zh: 下面的油门看起来跟玻璃一样光滑，甚至有点太滑了……砂纸肯定是为了增加额外的*抓地力*！

En: This looks like proof the missing eighth person at the lynching was Ruby... Do you think that Hardie and his 'boys' could also be involved in the drug operation?
Zh: “看来这就证明私刑现场的第八个人是露比……你觉得哈迪和他的‘兄弟们’是不是也参与了贩毒？”

En: Now we know for *certain* who's the missing eighth person at the lynching. Do you think that Hardie and his boys could also be involved in the drug operation?
Zh: “现在我们可以*确定*私刑现场失踪的第八人是谁了。你觉得哈迪和他的兄弟们是不是也参与了贩毒？”

En: Women.. (Shake your head.) "Always up to something."
Zh: “女人啊。”（摇摇头。）“总是喜欢搞鬼。”

En: Yes, well... He quickly disregards your statement as another one of your  neurotic outbursts.
Zh: “是的，呃……”他很快就忽略了你的发言，把它当成了又一次的神经质爆发。

En: We've identified one of the footprints, but there are still seven to go... He writes something in his little blue notebook.
Zh: “我们已经辨认出了一组脚印，不过还有七组要查……”他在自己的黑色小笔记本里写了些什么。

En: The sandpaper would also rub off the pattern from the driver's right boot sole...
Zh: 砂纸还可以磨掉司机右边鞋底的纹路。

En: Look around the cabin again.
Zh: 再四下查看一下車厢内部。

En: One of the footprints at the crime scene had an aberration -- one sole was smoother than the other.
Zh: “犯罪现场的有一组脚印有点异常——有一只鞋印要比另一只更平滑一些。”

En: Which means that the missing lady driver was present at the lynching. The lieutenant's eyes light up behind his prescription lens.
Zh: “意思是这位失踪的女司机当时就在私刑现场。”处方镜片后面，警督的眼睛亮了。

En: And she's also the one running the drug trade... What a handful.
Zh: 所以她还在贩毒……真是棘手。

En: First the drug smuggling, now this... How deep does this rabbit hole go?
Zh: 首先是贩毒，现在又是这个……这个兔子洞到底会有多深呢？

En: Women... (Shake your head.) Always the most *deceitful* ones.
Zh: 女人啊……（摇摇头。）总是最会*骗人*的。

En: You've identified one of the footprints, but there are still seven to go...
Zh: 你已经辨认出了一组脚印，不过还有七组要查……

En: This looks like proof the missing eighth person at the lynching was Ruby. Do you think that Hardie and his 'boys' could also be involved in the drug operation?
Zh: 看起来这就是证据，能证明露比就是私刑现场消失了的那位第八人。你觉得哈迪和他的‘兄弟们’有没有参与贩毒活动？

En: You've identified the missing eighth person at the lynching. Do you think that Hardie and his boys had anything to do with the drug trade?
Zh: 现在你已经确定私刑现场失踪的第8个人的身份。你觉得哈迪和他的兄弟们也参与贩毒了吗？

En: I think the entire Union is involved. Maybe even all of Martinaise... (Look around suspiciously.)
Zh: “我觉得整个工会都参与了。甚至是整个马丁内斯……”（怀疑地看着周围。）

En: Those jerks? Definitely.
Zh: “那些混球？绝对的。”

En: This would fit what Joyce told us, but I don't want to make any assumptions.
Zh: “这跟乔伊斯告诉我们的信息对上了，但我不想再做任何猜想。”

En: Understood. We should still go and see what Titus has got to say on the matter. He writes something down into his little notebook. "Are we finished with the lorry?"
Zh: “理解。我们应该去找提图斯，看看他对这件事情有什么说法。”他在小笔记本里记了些东西。“货車这边已经完事了吗？”

En: We should talk with Titus, see what he's got to say on the matter. He writes something down into his little notebook. "Are we finished with the lorry?"
Zh: “我们应该跟提图斯聊聊，看看他对这件事情有什么说法。”他在小笔记本里记了些东西。“货車这边已经完事了吗？”

En: We should see what Titus has got to say on the matter. He writes something down into his little notebook. "Are we finished with the lorry?"
Zh: “我们应该看看提图斯对这件事情有什么说法。”他在小笔记本里记了些东西。“货車这边已经完事了吗？”

En: You're right. But we should still go and see what Titus has got to say on the matter. He writes something down into his little notebook. "Are we finished with the lorry?"
Zh: “你说得对。不过我们应该去找提图斯，看看他对这件事情有什么说法。”他在小笔记本里记了些东西。“货車这边已经完事了吗？”

En: The entire Union might be involved. Maybe even all of Martinaise... (Look around suspiciously.)
Zh: 整个工会都参与了。甚至是整个马丁内斯……（怀疑地看着周围。）

En: Those jerks? Definitely.
Zh: 那些混球？绝对的。

En: Not necessarily. Ruby could have kept the drug trade a secret.
Zh: 不一定。露比可能把贩毒的事保密了。

En: Not necessarily. The lady driver could have kept the drug trade a secret.
Zh: 不一定。女司机可能把贩毒的事保密了。

En: This would fit what Joyce told us, but I don't want to make any assumptions.
Zh: 这跟乔伊斯告诉我们的信息对上了，但我不想再做任何猜想。

En: You should talk to them, see what they've got to say for themselves.
Zh: 你应该和他们谈谈，看看他们有什么要为自己辩护的。

En: Great, I think we got everything. A word, detective? He steps away from the lorry. "*Before* we return to Joyce."
Zh: “很好，看来要找的都找到了。先聊几句，警探？”他从货車里走出来。“在我们回到乔伊斯那里*之前*先说几句。”

En: We definitely need to return some time later. He nods at the lorry. "We didn't get enough."
Zh: “我们确实应该之后再回来。”他朝货車点点头。“我们找到的信息还不够。”

En: You close the rusty old lorry door.
Zh: 你关上锈迹斑斑的旧货車门。

En: For contacting an entire fleet of lorrymen, for example. He flicks a switch on the radio. "This is all shortwave, UW and UKV..."
Zh: “比如说，为了与所有货車司机取得联系。”他轻轻打开收音机开关。“这里全是短波，UW还有UKV……”

En: It has to be an advanced model to fit so many frequencies next to each other without blending them together.
Zh: 它肯定是个很高级的型号，这样才能把这么多频率存储在一起，而且还不会彼此混合。

En: Looks like we're dealing with an impressive organizational tool -- the nerve centre of a huge operation. With quite the range too.
Zh: “看来我们面对的是一个惊人的组织工具——一个大型运营网络的神经中枢。而且规模十分可观。”

En: It has to be an advanced model to fit so many frequencies next to each other without blending them together.
Zh: 它肯定是个很高级的型号，这样才能把这么多频率存储在一起，而且还不会彼此混合。

En: The peculiar frequency tableau indicates that at least 128 stations have been saved on this radio.
Zh: 这个特殊的调频面板表明，这台无线电里至少储存了128家电台。

En: Why would anyone need so many radio stations?
Zh: 为什么有人需要这么多无线电台？

En: What about the Speedfreaks FM? Is it one of the saved stations here?
Zh: 那狂飙怪人.FM呢？里面也存了那个电台吗？

En: Is there anything I can do with the radio?
Zh: 我能用这台无线电做些什么吗？

En: Look around the cabin. (Conclude.)
Zh: 四下查看車厢。（摘要。）

En: Not without the dial key, no.
Zh: 没有拨号键可不行。

En: Unclear.
Zh: 不清楚。

En: For contacting an entire fleet of lorries, for example. This must be the nerve centre of a well-organized operation.
Zh: 比如说，为了与所有货車司机取得联系。它肯定是一个组织良好的运营网络的神经中枢。

En: Doesn't look like it.
Zh: 看起来不太像。

En: Open the door.
Zh: 打开门。

En: Looks like the smugglers have infiltrated the road network belonging to East Motor Tract.
Zh: 看来走私犯已经渗透了东高速公路网。

En: Where does the contraband end up?
Zh: “这些走私品最后到哪里去了？”

En: Return the stack of papers under the seat.
Zh: 把那堆纸塞回座位底下。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi is a brave man for saying Madre's name without the winces and whispers that usually accompany it.
Zh: 曷城警督是一个勇敢的人，别人说出马德雷这个名字的时候，经常会畏畏缩缩、不敢张扬，但是他没有。

En: ...with *cool* names, he adds with contempt. "Like *La Puta Madre* and *Ahura Mazda*. It's a dark parody."
Zh: “……还有很*酷炫*的名字，”他轻蔑地补充到。“比如说*马德雷教父*，还有*阿胡拉·马兹达*。它是一种黑暗的戏仿。”

En: Petal wreaths cluster the monument in gloves of colour. Nectar scents the air and calls attention to the blossom. Atop it all sits a figure, veiled and black, sorrowful due to an unknown loss.
Zh: 花瓣花环将纪念碑簇拥在色彩斑斓的手套中。花蜜的香气在空气中弥漫，让人留意到花朵的盛开。上面坐着一个身影，蒙着面纱，全身漆黑，为无名者的逝去而悲伤。

En: A fitting testament to the lives lost, the lieutenant says as he turns toward the plaza. He holds one hand against his chest, the other is outstretched -- palm flat to the air.
Zh: “这是对逝者恰如其分的缅怀。”警督转身走向广场时说道。他一只手抵在胸前，另一只手向前伸出——手掌向上张开。

En: That's a variation of the Stations of the Breath. It's used when the body of the deceased is not available to hold a hand against.
Zh: 那是呼吸归所礼的一种变体，当逝者遗体的手已经无法被握住时才会使用。

En: Come on, we've stood here long enough. And money doesn't sleep.
Zh: 拜托，我们已经站在这里够久了，而钱永远不会原地不动等人去捡的。

En: You *had to* pay her so little, otherwise you wouldn't have made any profit -- and if you don't make a profit, you don't have money to invest!
Zh: 你*只能*给她很少的钱，不然你就没任何利润剩下了——如果你没有利润剩下，你就没钱去投资了！

En: Net worth, net worth, net worth... At least I've got my net worth! Watch the numbers go by.
Zh: 净资产，净资产，净资产…… 至少我还有自己的净资产！ 看着这些数字刷新。

En: And don't forget about the lying... or the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……以及你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: No need to act out of line here. It may even be wise to bow your head out of respect.
Zh: 现在不用做的太过，也许你也该低下头来表示敬意。

En: Why dwell on dead tissue? *Your* body survived the battle. *Your* muscles proved the mightier. Celebrate the living instead.
Zh: 为什么要纠结于一坨死肉？*你*的肉身在战斗中幸存了下来，*你*的肌肉证明了谁才是强者。还是为生者庆祝吧。

En: Indeed. We must mourn their absence.
Zh: “的确，我们必须为他们的逝去哀悼。”

En: Mhm. (Bow your head.)
(Perform the Stations of the Breath.)
Zh: （做呼吸归所礼）。

En: Spoken like a true hustler. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 你现在讲话像是一个真正的骗子。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Maybe... But we still have a long way to go -- your net worth is *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 也许吧……但我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: It's very on brand. We can work with it.
Zh: 这很有品牌感。我们可以借此赚一笔。

En: The lieutenant speaks as if you're *rich* -- a common misconception -- especially if you count the tax. No, we've got a long way to go before we can feel financially comfortable. The hustle never stops!
Zh: 警督说得好像你很*有钱*——这是种很常见的误会——尤其是在你没算税后的情况下。不，想要获得财务自由，我们还有很长的路要走。搞钱永不止步！

En: Yes, let's get back to making money.
Zh: 是的，我们回去赚钱吧。

En: No, you should own up to it, claim it as *yours*. Forget about the haters.
Zh: 不，你应该承认它，声称这是属于*你的*。别管那些黑子。

En: He holds the position for two seconds, then relaxes, turning to face you...
Zh: 他保持了这个姿势两秒钟，然后放松下来，转身面对你……

En: Khm. Interrupted, he relaxes his arms and turns to you...
Zh: “咳咳。”他打断了你，放松了一下手臂，转身对着你……

En: And was it all worth it?
Zh: “这一切都值得吗？”

En: A Sorry Cop? No. You're a hustler -- and you've got a long way to go. Your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 做一个抱歉警察？不，你是个骗子——而且你还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Maybe it's an honest portrait of who I am and what I've done.
Zh: 也许这就是我的本质以及所作所为的最真实的写照。

En: The veiled figure towers above, mounted on a horse. Numerous rods and ropes still hold the original reassemblage in place.
Zh: 蒙面的身影骑在马上高高耸立。众多的杆子和绳索还在固定着原本重组后的样子。

En: Detective, I have a few questions if you don't mind...
Zh: “警探，如果你不介意的话，我有几个问题……”

En: Underpaying? You paid according to the market -- not too much, not too little...
Zh: 给少了? 你是按市场价付钱的……不多也不少……

En: Underpaying? But you paid the highest possible price!
Zh: 给少了？但你已经出了合理范围内的最高价！

En: One more question, if you don't mind -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “还有一个问题，如果你不介意的话——这一切都值得吗？”

En: Never mind. Another question -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “没事了。还有一个问题——这一切都值得吗？”

En: About what?
Zh: “你是指什么？”

En: No, I don't mind.
Zh: “不，我不介意。”

En: Somewhere in a hospital bed the lieutenant turns onto his side, feverish and exhausted...
Zh: 某处医院的病床上，发着高烧、疲惫不堪的警督翻身侧躺着……

En: A fiasco?! No, it's beautiful...
Zh: 惨败？！不，这太美了……

En: It's a shame we can't all know the victories afforded by hardened flesh.
Zh: “很遗憾我们不能全然知晓钢铁之躯所带来的胜利。”

En: About this... pursuit of wealth... pursuit of recognition. He looks up, trying to find a name for your actions.
Zh: “我指的是你这……追求财富……追求认可的行为。”他抬起头来，试图找到一个词来描述你的举动。

En: He nods, eyes still on the veiled apparition above, on the troves and troves of flowers below, their sickly sweet scent pungent in the dark.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛仍然盯着上面那个蒙面的幽灵，盯着下面的一丛丛鲜花，那种病态的芳香在黑暗中变得刺鼻。

En: He nods, eyes still on the veiled apparition above, on the troves and troves of flowers below, their sickly sweet scent pungent in the cold air.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛仍然盯着上面那个蒙面的幽灵，盯着下面的一丛丛鲜花，那种病态的芳香在寒冷的空气中变得刺鼻。

En: He's talking about pussy. Our guy's gonna be *swimming* in it now that he's loaded!
Zh: 他说的是胆小鬼。我们的好小伙现在赚大钱了，要在钞票里*游泳*了！

En: A white lie -- of course he has opinions.
Zh: 一个善意的谎言——他当然有意见。

En: Actually, I'd prefer silence.
Zh: “其实我宁可保持沉默。”

En: Okay. The lieutenant snaps shut like a lily in the afternoon.
Zh: “好吧。”警督闭上了嘴，就像午后的莲花一样闭合了花瓣。

En: Why did you do it?
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？”

En: It fits the day. Anything else would be grossly inappropriate.
Zh: 这才符合今天的氛围，其他任何事都不大合时宜。

En: The cop radio is buzzing on silent...
Zh: 警用无线电在静音状态下嗡嗡作响……

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, even the street lamp.
Zh: “但这一切都值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，甚至那盏路灯。”

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, the thing with Idiot Doom Spiral...
Zh: “但这一切都值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，白痴厄运漩涡的事情……”

En: Beauty doesn't cut it. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 美丽并不能解决问题。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Sorry. I don't know what got into me. Ask your questions.
Zh: “对不起，我不知道自己是怎么了。问你的问题吧。”

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: Sure, whenever you're ready.
Zh: “当然，你准备好了就行。”

En: I've made it.
Zh: 我做到了。

En: Yes, it's not very good for PR...
Zh: 是的，不是很有利于公关……

En: A seagull lands on the monument, surveying the town below. Then it takes off again.
Zh: 一只海鸥落在纪念碑上，观察着下面的小镇。过了会儿它又飞走了。

En: Thanks. (Keep staring at the monument.)
Zh: “谢谢。”(继续盯着纪念碑)

En: Don't you see? Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: 你瞧见没有？白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: Nothing wrong with raw ambition.
Zh: 不加矫饰的野心是无罪的。

En: I don't think we have time for that. And even if we did, I doubt he's around anymore. Everyone's left.
Zh: “我认为咱们没这个时间去管他。就算咱们真去了，我也怀疑他人还在不在。所有人都走了。”

En: Savvy, hey Savvy... where did you go?! Talk to me!
Zh: 鬼鬼，嘿，鬼鬼…… 你去哪了？说话呀！

En: Savvy's here, watching the little *fiasco* of the horseback monument. You should distance yourself from this project. Say it's your Personal Brand Strategist gone rogue.
Zh: 鬼鬼在这里，看着雕塑的小*惨败*。你应该和这个项目保持距离，就说是你的个人品牌策划师发神经了。

En: 'Gone rogue'? No, Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: “发神经”？不，白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: Okay, let me have one more look at the monument...
Zh: “好吧，让我再看一眼这个纪念碑……”

En: The free market demands it.
Zh: “这是自由市场的市场需求。”

En: I wanted better for myself.
Zh: “我想对自己更好一点。”

En: Do you think I was wrong? I'm sorry...
Zh: “你觉得我错了吗？对不起……”

En: It's not what I wanted. This is all Idiot Doom Spiral's work.
Zh: “我想要的不是这个。这都是白痴厄运漩涡干的。”

En: To win her back.
Zh: “为了赢回她的心。”

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the sodium lights.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连钠灯照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling rain.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雨中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling snow.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雪中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连灿烂的阳光照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: Wait, I want to talk to Idiot Doom Spiral first.
Zh: “等等，我想先和白痴厄运漩涡谈谈。”

En: Wind takes some petals across the pavement, littering the streets with white and red...
Zh: 风卷起花瓣划过人行道，在街道上洒满白色和红色……

En: And don't forget about the lying...
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……

En: Alright, shall we? We still have some things left to do before we can go home.
Zh: “好了，我们走吧？在我们可以回家之前还有些事情没有做完。”

En: Who? The lieutenant meets your eyes with a questioning glance -- then looks up again.
Zh: “谁？”警督带着疑问的目光看向你的眼睛——然后又抬头往上看。

En: She will come back.
Zh: 她会回来的。

En: You can feel the lieutenant's stillness next to you...
Zh: 你能感觉到身边警督一动不动……

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the sodium lights.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像钠灯下的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling rain.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像雨中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling snow.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像雪中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像灿烂阳光下的环岛。

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. Darkness completely engulfs the penumbral griever. The flowers lifting him in requiem are starting to wilt...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。黑暗完全吞噬了迷离恍惚的哀悼者，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. Drops fall like tears down the veil of the penumbral griever, while a few fallen petals drift like funerary boats downstream, weeping into drains...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。雨滴像眼泪一样落在迷离恍惚的哀悼者的面纱上，几片落下的花瓣像葬仪船一样顺流而下，流进了排水沟……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The penumbral griever cuts black his misery against the white weather. The flowers lifting him in requiem are starting to wilt in the cold...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。迷离恍惚的悲哀者在一片银色中将哀伤切成黑色，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The penumbral griever is a silhouette against the bright sky, the flowers lifting him in requiem starting to wilt...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。迷离恍惚的哀悼者在在明亮的天空下只是一个剪影，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: Go away, stupid thoughts.
Zh: 走开，愚蠢的想法。

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. Darkness completely engulfs the penumbral griever, the flowers lifting him in requiem starting to wilt...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。黑暗完全吞噬了迷离恍惚的哀悼者，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。迷离恍惚的悲哀者在一片银色中将哀伤切成黑色，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The penumbral griever is a silhouette against the bright sky, the flowers lifting him in requiem starting to wilt...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。迷离恍惚的哀悼者在在明亮的天空下只是一个剪影，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: King Filippe III, the Squanderer, the Greatest of the Filippian Kings of Revachol, agrees.
Zh: 放荡者菲利普三世，瑞瓦肖菲利普王朝最伟大的君王，表示同意。

En: The veiled figure above seems to agree.
Zh: 上面那个蒙面的身影似乎表示同意。

En: I don't think anything. I'm just asking questions.
Zh: “我没任何想法，我只是在问问题。”

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. Darkness completely engulfs the penumbral griever. The flowers lifting him in requiem are starting to wilt...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。黑暗完全吞噬了迷离恍惚的哀悼者，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。雨滴像眼泪一样落在迷离恍惚的哀悼者的面纱上，几片落下的花瓣像葬仪船一样顺流而下，流进了排水沟……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The penumbral griever cuts black his misery against the white weather. The flowers lifting him in requiem are starting to wilt in the cold...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。迷离恍惚的悲哀者在一片银色中将哀伤切成黑色，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The penumbral griever is a silhouette against the bright sky, the flowers lifting him in requiem starting to wilt...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。迷离恍惚的哀悼者在在明亮的天空下只是一个剪影，令其安魂的花朵开始在寒冷中枯萎……

En: Your eyes glide over the horse, over its patina green coat. Something about the mount's sad animal-eyes makes you pause...
Zh: 你的视线再那匹马身上，它的铜锈绿衣上逡巡。这匹马悲伤的的眼镜让你的视线停了下来……

En: Didn't we talk about saying sorry? Why are you saying sorry again?
Zh: 我们刚刚不是说了不要道歉吗？怎么你又道歉了？

En: Of course you're going to distance yourself from your misdeeds -- the lying, the conning, the exploitation...
Zh: 你当然要与自己的恶行保持距离——说谎、诈骗、盘剥……

En: You managed to gather quite a fortune, kicked off by underpaying a young artist...
Zh: “你通过压价买下年轻艺术家的作品，从中赚了一大笔钱……”

En: Was it worth it?
Zh: 这值得吗？

En: Tax? What tax? We're in Revachol. He looks around, as if looking for hidden taxation, then shakes his head.
Zh: “税？什么税？我们可是在瑞瓦肖。”他环顾四周，好像在寻找隐藏的课税，然后摇了摇头。

En: It's fucking lonely at the top.
Zh: 他妈的高处不胜寒啊。

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: What are you talking about?
Zh: “你在说什么？”

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true philanthropist. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的慈善家。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: No, the free market demands something much more PR-friendly than what Idiot Doom Spiral has done here.
Zh: 不，自由市场所需求的是比白痴厄运螺旋留下的这个大作更有利于公关的项目。

En: Demands *what*? Endless reconceptualization? The lives we lost? The charade?
Zh: 需求*什么*？无止无休的概念再设定？我们同胞的生命？骗局？

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true businessman. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的生意人。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Honest. Maybe even *too* honest... Didn't we talk about giving back to the community?
Zh: 诚实。也许*太过*诚实了……我们不是说过要回馈社会吗？

En: Good -- take no responsibility over this disaster. It was supposed to *promote* you, not mock you for your failings.
Zh: 很好——不要对这场灾难负责。这个项目应该是为了*推销*你，而不是讪笑你的失败。

En: Yes, who are you talking about?
Zh: 是啊，你在说谁？

En: I really don't need to be judged like this, especially after the amount of tax I've paid.
Zh: “我真的不想被人这样评判，尤其是在我交了这么多税之后。”

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community. Even told Idiot Doom Spiral to stop drinking.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。我甚至还劝说白痴厄运漩涡戒酒。

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。”

En: 100% worth it. I'd do it all over again -- but this time with more *profit*.
Zh: “…杜博阿警督？请回答……”

En: A cardboard sign hangs from the arm pointing out to sea. Written in shaky marker pen are the words: 'PALE SWALLOWS ALL'. What was once a gesture of great power has been hijacked to signal the ceaseless march of annihilation.
Zh: 雕像的手臂上挂着一个纸板牌子，指着大海，上面用马克笔歪歪扭扭地写着：“灰域吞噬一切”。曾经象征丰功伟业的雕像现在却被劫持，用来发出湮灭无法阻止的信号。

En: A mosaic of parking tickets cover the bronze horse and rider. Each one bears the same message from patrolman to culprit. 'This vehicle's parking permit has expired. Please pay your fines and move along.'
Zh: 青铜造的马和骑手身上覆盖着停车罚单组成的马赛克，每张罚单上都印有巡警给违规者的同一段话：“此车的停车证已过期。请交纳罚款后离开。”

En: A bright chromatic splash lounges across the surface of the monument. It's not obvious if the paint has any intentional form or if it being there in the first place is statement enough.
Zh: 一道明亮的色彩泼洒在纪念碑的表面。看不出到底是这些颜料的表达形式有意义，还是泼洒颜料本身就是意义。

En: An apricot sceptre shines party-bright across the monument. Glitter balls dangle like severed heads below the eternal king of disco. It is unmistakably a vision of you in your prime -- a killer on the performance floor, icon for all.
Zh: 杏色的权杖在纪念碑上闪耀着派对的光芒。闪光球像割下的首级一样垂在永恒的迪斯科之王的下方。这无疑是你巅峰时期的样貌——舞台上的杀手，所有人的偶像。

En: Doesn't feel right to party now, does it? The lieutenant solemnly looks toward the plaza, then turns to you. "Are you at least happy with it?"
Zh: “现在这场合感觉不适合派对吧？”警督严肃地看向广场，然后转向你。“但至少你满意了吧？”

En: I've made it.
Zh: 我已经做到了。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the sodium lights.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连钠灯照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling rain.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雨中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling snow.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雪中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连灿烂的阳光照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: Your eyes glide over the horse, over its patina green coat. Something about the mount's sad animal-eyes makes you pause...
Zh: 你的视线再那匹马身上，它的铜锈绿衣上逡巡。这匹马悲伤的的眼镜让你的视线停了下来……

En: Was it worth it?
Zh: 这值得吗？

En: No, I don't mind.
Zh: “不，我不介意。”

En: Still keeping the superstar dream alive...
Zh: 仍然怀抱天王巨星之梦……

En: The lieutenant speaks as if you're *rich* -- a common misconception -- especially if you count the tax. No, we've got a long way to go before we can feel financially comfortable. The hustle never stops!
Zh: 警督说得好像你很*有钱*——这是种很常见的误会——尤其是在你没算税后的情况下。不，想要获得财务自由，我们还有很长的路要走。搞钱永不止步！

En: The disco danseur towers above, mounted on a horse. Numerous rods and ropes still hold the original reassemblage in place.
Zh: 迪斯科舞者骑在马上高高耸立着。众多的杆子和绳索还在固定着原本重组后的样子。

En: Nothing wrong with raw ambition.
Zh: 不加矫饰的野心是无罪的。

En: Don't you see? Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: 你瞧见没有？白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: Net worth, net worth, net worth... At least I've got my net worth! Watch the numbers go by.
Zh: 净资产，净资产，净资产…… 至少我还有自己的净资产！ 看着这些数字刷新。

En: I'd be happier if I was blasting some chem right now.
Zh: “如果我现在能嗨些药，我会更高兴。”

En: It's a job well done. Any happiness should be reserved for now.
Zh: “这件事干得相当漂亮，说现在有多幸福都不为过。”

En: We all need a pick-me-up and I'm glad to provide -- as entertainer, style leader, superstar law-officer of the world!
Zh: “我们都需要提神水，而我，作为艺人，时尚领袖，国际天王巨星级的执法人员，很乐意提供！”

En: Somewhere in a hospital bed the lieutenant turns onto his side, feverish and exhausted...
Zh: 某处医院的病床上，发着高烧、疲惫不堪的警督翻身侧躺着……

En: (Shrug.)
Zh: （耸肩）

En: Savvy, hey Savvy... where did you go?! Talk to me!
Zh: 鬼鬼，嘿，鬼鬼…… 你去哪了？说话啊！

En: She will come back.
Zh: 她会回来的。

En: You can feel the lieutenant's stillness next to you...
Zh: 你能感觉到身边警督的一动不动……

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the sodium lights.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像钠灯下的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling rain.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像雨中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling snow.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像落雪中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像灿烂阳光下的环岛。

En: Go away, stupid thoughts.
Zh: 走开，愚蠢的想法。

En: It's fucking lonely at the top.
Zh: 他妈的高处不胜寒啊。

En: Perhaps. The free market does work in mysterious ways. At least Idiot Doom Spiral has made you look like the cool cat you are.
Zh: 也许吧。自由市场运作的方式确实神秘。至少白痴厄运漩涡让你看起来像个沉稳的冷酷角色。

En: You managed to gather quite a fortune, kicked off by underpaying a young artist...
Zh: “你通过压价买下年轻艺术家的作品，从中赚了一大笔钱……”

En: Underpaying? But you paid the highest possible price!
Zh: 给少了？但你出的是合理范围内的最高价！

En: Underpaying? You paid according to the market -- not too much, not too little...
Zh: 给少了? 你是按市场价付的钱……不多也不少……

En: You *had to* pay her so little, otherwise you wouldn't have made any profit -- and if you don't make a profit, you don't have money to invest!
Zh: 你*只能*给她很少的钱，不然你就没任何利润剩下了——如果你没有利润剩下，你就没钱去投资了！

En: Yes, who are you talking about?
Zh: 是啊，你在说谁？

En: Good -- take no responsibility over this disaster. People are trying to mourn and all they can see is your gaudy figure mocking them from above.
Zh: 很好——不要对这场灾难负责。人们想要来哀悼，但却只能看到你华丽的身影在上面嘲笑他们。

En: I don't think we have time for that. And even if we did, I doubt he's around anymore. Everyone's left.
Zh: “我认为咱们没这个时间去管他。就算咱们真去了，我也怀疑他人还在不在。所有人都走了。”

En: Sure, whenever you're ready.
Zh: “当然，你准备好了就行。”

En: Honest. Maybe even *too* honest... Didn't we talk about giving back to the community?
Zh: 诚实。也许*太过*诚实了……我们不是说过要回馈社会吗？

En: Demands *what*? Endless reconceptualization? The lives we lost? The charade?
Zh: 需求*什么*？无止无休的概念再设定？我们同胞的生命？骗局？

En: Of course you're going to distance yourself from your misdeeds -- the lying, the conning, the exploitation...
Zh: 你当然要与自己的恶行保持距离——说谎、诈骗、盘剥……

En: A white lie -- of course he has opinions.
Zh: 一个善意的谎言——他当然有意见。

En: Wait, I want to talk to Idiot Doom Spiral first.
Zh: “等等，我想先和白痴厄运漩涡谈谈。”

En: Okay, let me have one more look at the monument...
Zh: “好吧，让我再看一眼这个纪念碑……”

En: And don't forget about the lying...
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……

En: And don't forget about the lying... or the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……以及你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: And was it all worth it?
Zh: “这一切值得吗？”

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The apricot sceptre illuminates the disco king like a beacon in the dark, calling all dancers to wait for the drop of the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。杏色的权杖照亮了迪斯科之王，就像黑暗中的灯塔，呼唤着所有舞者等待下一个时髦的节拍落下……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. Drops fall around the apricot sceptre and bounce off the glitter balls, yet the noble king of disco still hunts unperturbed for the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。水滴落在杏色权杖的周围，跃起一颗颗闪烁的水珠，而高贵的迪斯科之王仍在不慌不忙地猎取下一个时髦的节拍……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The apricot sceptre glows fiercer upon the white sheets, held up by the frozen king of disco who hunts for the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。冻僵的迪斯科之王高举杏色权杖，在银白色的床单上闪耀着更耀眼的光彩，猎取下一个时髦的节拍……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sun. The king of disco is lit up like a stage, anticipating a funky beat that will never come...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。迪斯科之王就像身处于舞台中心一样闪耀，期待着一个永远不会到来的时髦节拍……

En: I don't think anything. I'm just asking questions.
Zh: “我没任何想法，我只是在问问题。”

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The apricot sceptre illuminates the disco king like a beacon in the dark, calling all dancers to wait for the drop of the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。杏色的权杖照亮了迪斯科之王，就像黑暗中的灯塔，呼唤着所有舞者等待下一个时髦的节拍落下……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. Drops fall around the apricot sceptre and bounce off the glitter balls, yet the noble king of disco still hunts unperturbed for the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。水滴落在杏色权杖的周围，跃起一颗颗闪烁的水珠，而高贵的迪斯科之王仍在不慌不忙地猎取下一个时髦的节拍……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The apricot sceptre glows fiercer upon the white sheets, held up by the frozen king of disco who hunts for the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。冻僵的迪斯科之王高举杏色权杖，在银白色的床单上闪耀着更耀眼的光彩，猎取下一个时髦的节拍……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sun. The king of disco is lit up like a stage, anticipating a funky beat that will never come...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。迪斯科之王就像身处于舞台中心一样闪耀，期待着一个永远不会到来的时髦节拍……

En: He nods in respectful agreement then closes his eyes for half a beat. When he opens them, it's clear he has something to say to you...
Zh: 他恭敬地点点头表示同意，然后闭上眼睛半拍。当他睁开眼睛时，很明显他有话要对你说……

En: Khm, the lieutenant clears his throat, keen to move on.
Zh: “咳咳。”警督清了清嗓子，很想继续说下去。

En: Yes, it's not very good for PR...
Zh: 是的，不是很有利于公关……

En: A seagull lands on the monument, surveying the town below. Then it takes off again.
Zh: 一只海鸥落在纪念碑上，观察着下面的小镇。过了会儿它又飞走了。

En: Savvy's here, watching the little *fiasco* of the horseback monument. You should distance yourself from this project. Say it's your Personal Brand Strategist gone rogue.
Zh: 鬼鬼在这里，看着雕塑的小*惨败*。你应该和这个项目保持距离，就说是你的个人品牌策划师发神经了。

En: One of the lights flickers and abandons a part of the bronze figure to the murk, if only for a second...
Zh: 其中一盏灯闪了一下，将青铜人像的一部分遗弃在阴暗中，哪怕只有一秒……

En: King Filippe III, the Squanderer, the Greatest of the Filippian Kings of Revachol, agrees.
Zh: 放荡者菲利普三世，瑞瓦肖菲利普王朝最伟大的君王，表示同意。

En: The disco sovereign above seems to agree.
Zh: 上面的迪斯科帝王似乎表示同意。

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The apricot sceptre illuminates the disco king like a beacon in the dark, calling all dancers to wait for the drop of the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。杏色的权杖照亮了迪斯科之王，就像黑暗中的灯塔，呼唤着所有舞者等待下一个时髦的节拍落下……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. Drops fall around the apricot sceptre and bounce off the glitter balls, yet the noble king of disco still hunts unperturbed for the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。水滴落在杏色权杖的周围，跃起一颗颗闪烁的水珠，而高贵的迪斯科之王仍在不慌不忙地猎取下一个时髦的节拍……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The apricot sceptre glows fiercer upon the white sheets, held up by the frozen king of disco who hunts for the next funky beat...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。冻僵的迪斯科之王高举杏色权杖，在银白色的床单上闪耀着更耀眼的光彩，猎取下一个时髦的节拍……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sun. The king of disco is lit up like a stage, anticipating a funky beat that will never come...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。迪斯科之王就像身处于舞台中心一样闪耀，期待着一个永远不会到来的时髦节拍……

En: Maybe... But we still have a long way to go -- your net worth is *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 也许吧……但我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Alright, shall we? We still have some things left to do before we can go home.
Zh: “好了，我们走吧？在我们可以回家之前还有些事情没有做完。”

En: Who? The lieutenant meets your eyes with a questioning glance -- then looks up again.
Zh: “谁？”警督带着疑问的目光看向你的眼睛——然后又抬头往上看。

En: One more question, if you don't mind -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “还有一个问题，如果你不介意的话——这一切值得吗？”

En: Detective, I have a few questions if you don't mind...
Zh: “警探，如果你不介意的话，我有几个问题……”

En: 'Gone rogue'? No, Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: “发神经了”？不，白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true businessman. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的生意人。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true philanthropist. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的慈善家。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Tax? What tax? We're in Revachol. He looks around, as if looking for hidden taxation, then shakes his head.
Zh: “税？什么税？我们可是在瑞瓦肖。”他环顾四周，好像在寻找隐藏的课税，然后摇了摇头。

En: Come on, we've stood here long enough. And money doesn't sleep.
Zh: 拜托，我们已经站在这里够久了，而钱永远不会原地不动等人去捡的。

En: Never mind. Another question -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “没事了。还有一个问题——这一切值得吗？”

En: He's talking about pussy. Our guy's gonna be *swimming* in it now that he's loaded!
Zh: 他说的是胆小鬼。我们的好小伙现在赚大钱了，要在钞票里*游泳*了！

En: Beauty doesn't cut it. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 美丽并不能解决问题。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: It may have good intentions but it's come at the completely wrong time.
Zh: 可能是出于好意，但时机却完全不对。

En: 100% worth it. I'd do it all over again -- but this time with more *profit*.
Zh: “100%值得。我还想再来一次呢——但这次得有更多的*利润*。”

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community. Even told Idiot Doom Spiral to stop drinking.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。我甚至还劝说白痴厄运漩涡戒酒。

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。”

En: About what?
Zh: “你是指什么？”

En: Actually, I'd prefer silence.
Zh: “其实我宁可保持沉默。”

En: I really don't need to be judged like this, especially after the amount of tax I've paid.
Zh: “我真的不想被人这样评判，尤其是在我交了这么多税之后。”

En: No, you should own up to it, claim it as *yours*. Forget about the haters.
Zh: 不，你应该承认它，声称这是属于*你的*。别管那些黑子。

En: It's very on brand. We can work with it.
Zh: 这很有品牌感。我们可以借此赚一笔。

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, even the street lamp.
Zh: “但这一切值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，甚至那盏路灯。”

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, the thing with Idiot Doom Spiral...
Zh: “但这一切都值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，白痴厄运漩涡的事情……”

En: About this... pursuit of wealth... pursuit of recognition. He looks up, trying to find a name for your actions.
Zh: “我指的是你这……追求财富……追求认可的行为。”他抬起头来，试图找到一个词来描述你的举动。

En: He nods, eyes still on the disco danseur above, his face tinged by the citrus beams illuminating the dark.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛依然盯着上面的迪斯科舞者，他的脸庞被刺破黑暗的橘色光束给染黄。

En: He nods, eyes still on the megastar above, his face tinged by the citrus beams warming the cold air.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛依然盯着上面的超级巨星，他的脸庞被融化寒气的橘色光束给染黄。

En: Okay. The lieutenant snaps shut like the final move of a dance routine.
Zh: “好吧。”警督闭上了嘴，像跳完舞蹈最后一个动作。

En: Yes, let's get back to making money.
Zh: 是的，我们回去赚钱吧。

En: Maybe it's an honest portrait of who I am and what I've done.
Zh: 也许这就是我的本质以及所作所为的最真实的写照。

En: A fiasco?! No, it's beautiful...
Zh: 惨败？！不，这太美了……

En: Thanks. (Keep staring at the monument.)
Zh: “谢谢。”(继续盯着纪念碑)

En: A Superstar Cop? No. You're a hustler -- and you've got a long way to go. Your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 做一个明星警察？不，你是个骗子——而且你还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: I'm only kidding. Ask your questions.
Zh: “我开玩笑的。你问吧。”

En: To win her back.
Zh: “为了赢回她的心。”

En: It's not what I wanted. This is all Idiot Doom Spiral's work.
Zh: “我想要的不是这个。这都是白痴厄运漩涡干的。”

En: Why did you do it?
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？”

En: The cop radio is buzzing on silent...
Zh: 警用无线电在静音状态下嗡嗡作响……

En: Fame and riches come naturally to me, don't you think?
Zh: “名利双收对我来说就是水到渠成的事，你说呢？”

En: I wanted better for myself.
Zh: “我想对自己更好一点。”

En: What are you talking about?
Zh: “你在说什么？”

En: The free market demands it.
Zh: “这是自由市场的市场需求。”

En: Spoken like a true hustler. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 你现在讲话像是一个真正的骗子。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Hard to disagree with the sentiment considering what we just survived. The lieutenant solemnly looks toward the plaza, then turns to you.
Zh: “考虑到我们刚刚的经历，我很难不抱有同感。”警督严肃地看着广场，然后转向你。

En: I've made it.
Zh: 我已经做到了。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the sodium lights.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连钠灯照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling rain.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雨中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling snow.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雪中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连灿烂的阳光照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: Your eyes glide over the horse, over its patina green coat. Something about the mount's sad animal-eyes makes you pause...
Zh: 你的视线再那匹马身上，它的铜锈绿衣上逡巡。这匹马悲伤的的眼镜让你的视线停了下来……

En: Was it worth it?
Zh: 这值得吗？

En: No, I don't mind.
Zh: “不，我不介意。”

En: You've been through a catastrophe and still haven't reaped a *single* benefit from this doomspeak. Drop it already!
Zh: 你已经历过一场灾难，但仍然没有从这种末日论中得到*一丁点*好处。放弃吧！

En: The lieutenant speaks as if you're *rich* -- a common misconception -- especially if you count the tax. No, we've got a long way to go before we can feel financially comfortable. The hustle never stops!
Zh: 警督说得好像你很*有钱*——这是种很常见的误会——尤其是在你没算税后的情况下。不，想要获得财务自由，我们还有很长的路要走。搞钱永不止步！

En: The doom crier towers above, mounted on a horse. Numerous rods and ropes still hold the original reassemblage in place.
Zh: 末日使者骑在马上高高耸立着。众多的杆子和绳索还在固定着原本重组后的样子。

En: Nothing wrong with raw ambition.
Zh: 不加矫饰的野心是无罪的。

En: Don't you see? Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: 你瞧见没有？白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: Net worth, net worth, net worth... At least I've got my net worth! Watch the numbers go by.
Zh: 净资产，净资产，净资产…… 至少我还有自己的净资产！ 看着这些数字刷新。

En: (Shout.) "Listen to your leader, people of the coast -- the worst is yet to come!"
Zh: (大喊)“海边的人们，听你们领袖的话——最糟糕的还在后面呢！”

En: Maybe a tribute to the deceased would make more sense now...
Zh: “也许现在哀悼死者会更有意义……”

En: This is only the beginning. (Grin wickedly.)
Zh: “这只是个开始。”(邪恶地笑)

En: Somewhere in a hospital bed the lieutenant turns onto his side, feverish and exhausted...
Zh: 某处医院的病床上，发着高烧、疲惫不堪的警督翻身侧躺着……

En: (Nod.)
Zh: （点头）

En: Savvy, hey Savvy... where did you go?! Talk to me!
Zh: 鬼鬼，嘿，鬼鬼…… 你去哪了？说话呀！

En: She will come back.
Zh: 她会回来的。

En: You can feel the lieutenant's stillness next to you...
Zh: 你能感觉到身边警督一动不动……

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the sodium lights.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像钠灯下的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling rain.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像雨中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling snow.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像雪中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像灿烂阳光下的环岛。

En: Go away, stupid thoughts.
Zh: 走开，愚蠢的想法。

En: It's fucking lonely at the top.
Zh: 他妈的高处不胜寒啊。

En: No, the free market demands something much more PR-friendly than what Idiot Doom Spiral has done here.
Zh: 不，自由市场所需求的是比白痴厄运螺旋留下的这个大作更有利于公关的项目。

En: You managed to gather quite a fortune, kicked off by underpaying a young artist...
Zh: “你通过压价买下年轻艺术家的作品，从中赚了一大笔钱……”

En: Underpaying? But you paid the highest possible price!
Zh: 给少了？但你已经出了合理范围内的最高价！

En: Underpaying? You paid according to the market -- not too much, not too little...
Zh: 给少了? 你是按市场价付的钱……不多也不少……

En: You *had to* pay her so little, otherwise you wouldn't have made any profit -- and if you don't make a profit, you don't have money to invest!
Zh: 你*只能*给她很少的钱，不然你就没任何利润剩下了——如果你没有利润剩下，你就没钱去投资了！

En: Yes, who are you talking about?
Zh: 是啊，你在说谁？

En: Good -- take no responsibility for this disaster. It was supposed to *promote* you, not make people fear you.
Zh: 很好——不要对这场灾难负责。这个项目应该是为了*推销*你，而不是让人害怕你。

En: I don't think we have time for that. And even if we did, I doubt he's around anymore. Everyone's left.
Zh: “我认为咱们没这个时间去管他。就算咱们真去了，我也怀疑他人还在不在。所有人都走了。”

En: Sure, whenever you're ready.
Zh: “当然，你准备好了就行。”

En: Honest. Maybe even *too* honest... Didn't we talk about giving back to the community?
Zh: 诚实。也许*太过*诚实了……我们不是说过要回馈社会吗？

En: Demands *what*? Endless reconceptualization? The lives we lost? The charade?
Zh: 需求*什么*？无止无休的概念再设定？我们同胞的生命？骗局？

En: Of course you're going to distance yourself from your misdeeds -- the lying, the conning, the exploitation...
Zh: 你当然要与自己的恶行保持距离——说谎、诈骗、盘剥……

En: A white lie -- of course he has opinions.
Zh: 一个善意的谎言——他当然有意见。

En: Wait, I want to talk to Idiot Doom Spiral first.
Zh: “等等，我想先和白痴厄运漩涡谈谈。”

En: Okay, let me have one more look at the monument...
Zh: “好吧，让我再看一眼这个纪念碑……”

En: And don't forget about the lying...
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……

En: And don't forget about the lying... or the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……以及你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: And was it all worth it?
Zh: “这一切值得吗？”

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The doom crier embraces the blackened sky, his cardboard omen hiding its ill fortune in the dark...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人拥抱着漆黑的天空，他那写着神谕的硬纸板将厄运隐藏在黑暗之中……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The doom crier welcomes the wet erosion, his cardboard omen starting to sag under the weight of the weather...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人迎接着湿气的侵蚀，他写着神谕的硬纸板在雨天的重压下开始下垂……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The doom crier embraces the deathly cold, his cardboard omen dusted with what looks like sky-fallen ash...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人拥抱着死亡的寒冷，他那写着神谕的纸板上沾满了像是堕天的灰烬……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The doom crier waits to be swallowed by the bright sky, his cardboard omen flapping in the wind like live bait...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人站在中央，等待着被明亮的天空吞噬，他那写着神谕的纸板像活饵一样在风中飞舞……

En: I don't think anything. I'm just asking questions.
Zh: “我没任何想法，我只是在问问题。”

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The doom crier embraces the blackened sky, his cardboard omen hiding its ill fortune in the dark...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人拥抱着漆黑的天空，他那写着神谕的硬纸板将厄运隐藏在黑暗之中……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The doom crier welcomes the wet erosion, his cardboard omen starting to sag under the weight of the weather...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人迎接着湿气的侵蚀，他写着神谕的硬纸板在雨天的重压下开始下垂……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The doom crier embraces the deathly cold, his cardboard omen dusted with what looks like sky-fallen ash...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人拥抱着死亡的寒冷，他那写着神谕的纸板上沾满了像是堕天的灰烬……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The doom crier waits to be swallowed by the bright sky, his cardboard omen flapping in the wind like live bait...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人站在中央，等待着被明亮的天空吞噬，他那写着神谕的纸板像活饵一样在风中飞舞……

En: He nods in woeful agreement then closes his eyes for half a beat. When he opens them, it's clear he has something to say to you...
Zh: 他恭敬地点点头表示同意，然后闭眼了半秒。当他睁开眼睛时，很明显有话要对你说……

En: Khm, the lieutenant clears his throat, keen to move on.
Zh: “咳咳。”警督清了清嗓子，很想继续说下去。

En: Yes, it's not very good for PR...
Zh: 是的，不是很有利于公关……

En: A seagull lands on the monument, surveying the town below. Then it takes off again.
Zh: 一只海鸥落在纪念碑上，观察着下面的小镇。过了会儿它又飞走了。

En: Savvy's here, watching the little *fiasco* of the horseback monument. You should distance yourself from this project. Say it's your Personal Brand Strategist gone rogue.
Zh: 鬼鬼在这里，看着雕塑的小*惨败*。你应该和这个项目保持距离，就说是你的个人品牌策划师发神经了。

En: Wind makes the cardboard omen swing menacingly, like a pendulum ticking down to a dreaded epilogue...
Zh: 写了预兆的纸板在风中激烈地摇摆，就像钟摆滴答摆向恐怖的尾声一样……

En: King Filippe III, the Squanderer, the Greatest of the Filippian Kings of Revachol, agrees.
Zh: 放荡者菲利普三世，瑞瓦肖菲利普王朝最伟大的君王，表示同意。

En: The doom crier above seems to agree.
Zh: 上面的迪斯科帝王似乎表示同意。

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The doom crier embraces the blackened sky, his cardboard omen hiding its ill fortune in the dark...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人拥抱着漆黑的天空，他那写着神谕的硬纸板将厄运隐藏在黑暗之中……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The doom crier welcomes the wet erosion, his cardboard omen starting to sag under the weight of the weather...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人迎接着湿气的侵蚀，他写着神谕的硬纸板在雨天的重压下开始下垂……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The doom crier embraces the deathly cold, his cardboard omen dusted with what looks like sky-fallen ash...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人拥抱着死亡的寒冷，他那写着神谕的纸板上沾满了像是堕天的灰烬……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The doom crier waits to be swallowed by the bright sky, his cardboard omen flapping in the wind like live bait...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。末日的传令人站在中央，等待着被明亮的天空吞噬，他那写着神谕的纸板像活饵一样在风中飞舞……

En: Maybe... But we still have a long way to go -- your net worth is *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 也许吧……但我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Alright, shall we? We still have some things left to do before we can go home.
Zh: “好了，我们走吧？在我们可以回家之前还有些事情没有做完。”

En: Who? The lieutenant meets your eyes with a questioning glance -- then looks up again.
Zh: “谁？”警督带着疑问的目光看向你的眼睛——然后又抬头往上看。

En: One more question, if you don't mind -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “还有一个问题，如果你不介意的话——这一切值得吗？”

En: Detective, I have a few questions if you don't mind...
Zh: “警探，如果你不介意的话，我有几个问题……”

En: 'Gone rogue'? No, Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: “发神经”？不，白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true businessman. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的生意人。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true philanthropist. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的慈善家。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Tax? What tax? We're in Revachol. He looks around, as if looking for hidden taxation, then shakes his head.
Zh: “税？什么税？我们可是在瑞瓦肖。”他环顾四周，好像在寻找隐藏的课税，然后摇了摇头。

En: Come on, we've stood here long enough. And money doesn't sleep.
Zh: 拜托，我们已经站在这里够久了，而钱永远不会原地不动等人去捡的。

En: Never mind. Another question -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “没事了。还有一个问题——这一切值得吗？”

En: He's talking about pussy. Our guy's gonna be *swimming* in it now that he's loaded!
Zh: 他说的是胆小鬼。我们的好小伙现在赚大钱了，要在钞票里*游泳*了！

En: Beauty doesn't cut it. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 美丽并不能解决问题。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: It's far too soon. This portent is grossly inappropriate.
Zh: 来得太早了。这个预兆有些不合适。

En: 100% worth it. I'd do it all over again -- but this time with more *profit*.
Zh: “100%值得。我还想再来一次呢——但这次得有更多的*利润*。”

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community. Even told Idiot Doom Spiral to stop drinking.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。我甚至还劝说白痴厄运漩涡戒酒。
