En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。”

En: About what?
Zh: “你是指什么？”

En: Actually, I'd prefer silence.
Zh: “其实我宁可保持沉默。”

En: I really don't need to be judged like this, especially after the amount of tax I've paid.
Zh: “我真的不想被人这样评判，尤其是在我交了这么多税之后。”

En: No, you should own up to it, claim it as *yours*. Forget about the haters.
Zh: 不，你应该承认它，声称这是属于*你的*。别管那些黑子。

En: It's very on brand. We can work with it.
Zh: 这很有品牌感。我们可以借此赚一笔。

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, even the street lamp.
Zh: “但这一切值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，甚至那盏路灯。”

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, the thing with Idiot Doom Spiral...
Zh: “但这一切都值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，白痴厄运漩涡的事情……”

En: About this... pursuit of wealth... pursuit of recognition. He looks up, trying to find a name for your actions.
Zh: “我指的是你这……追求财富……追求认可的行为。”他抬起头来，试图找到一个词来描述你的举动。

En: He nods, eyes still on the doom crier above, on the arm holding out the portent, barely readable in the dark.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛依然盯着上面的末日使者，盯着那只伸出凶兆的手臂，上面的文字在黑暗中几乎看不清。

En: He nods, eyes still on the doom crier above, on the arm holding out the portent in the cold air.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛依然盯着上面的末日使者，盯着那只在寒气中伸出凶兆的手臂。

En: Okay. The lieutenant snaps shut like a world-ending implosion.
Zh: “好吧。”警督闭上了嘴，像世界末日的内爆。

En: Yes, let's get back to making money.
Zh: 是的，我们回去赚钱吧。

En: Maybe it's an honest portrait of who I am and what I've done.
Zh: 也许这就是我的本质以及所作所为的最真实的写照。

En: A fiasco?! No, it's beautiful...
Zh: 惨败？！不，这太美了……

En: Thanks. (Keep staring at the monument.)
Zh: “谢谢。”(继续盯着纪念碑)

En: An Apocalypse Cop? No. You're a hustler -- and you've got a long way to go. Your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 做一个末日警察？不，你是个骗子——而且你还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Well, it's not The End yet. Ask your questions while you can.
Zh: “好吧，还没结束呢。趁你还能问问题的时候问吧。”

En: To win her back.
Zh: “为了赢回她的心。”

En: It's not what I wanted. This is all Idiot Doom Spiral's work.
Zh: “我想要的不是这个。这都是白痴厄运漩涡干的。”

En: Why did you do it?
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？”

En: The cop radio is buzzing on silent...
Zh: 警用无线电在静音状态下嗡嗡作响……

En: Don't you think we should make the most of the short time we all have left?
Zh: “你不认为我们应该充分利用我们仅剩的短暂时间吗?”

En: I wanted better for myself.
Zh: “我想对自己更好一点。”

En: What are you talking about?
Zh: “你在说什么？”

En: The free market demands it.
Zh: “这是自由市场的市场需求。”

En: Spoken like a true hustler. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 你现在讲话像是一个真正的骗子。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Is this a parody? It's... not bad. The lieutenant looks towards the plaza and sighs. "We're lucky to to be able to enjoy this."
Zh: “这是戏仿吗?”这……还不错。”警督望着广场，叹了口气。“我们很幸运，还能够享受到这一切。”

En: I've made it.
Zh: 我已经做到了。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the sodium lights.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连钠灯照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling rain.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雨中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling snow.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雪中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连灿烂的阳光照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: Your eyes glide over the horse, over its patina green coat. Something about the mount's sad animal-eyes makes you pause...
Zh: 你的视线再那匹马身上，它的铜锈绿衣上逡巡。这匹马悲伤的的眼镜让你的视线停了下来……

En: Was it worth it?
Zh: 这值得吗？

En: No, I don't mind.
Zh: “不，我不介意。”

En: That's *all* it was to you? You should have never got out of bed.
Zh: 这对你来说就只有那么*点意义*？你不应该下床的。

En: The lieutenant speaks as if you're *rich* -- a common misconception -- especially if you count the tax. No, we've got a long way to go before we can feel financially comfortable. The hustle never stops!
Zh: 警督说得好像你很*有钱*——这是种很常见的误会——尤其是在你没算税后的情况下。不，想要获得财务自由，我们还有很长的路要走。搞钱永不止步！

En: The ticket creature towers above, mounted on a horse. Numerous rods and ropes still hold the original reassemblage in place.
Zh: 这个由罚单构成的生物骑在马上高高耸立着。众多的杆子和绳索还在固定着原本重组后的样子。

En: Nothing wrong with raw ambition.
Zh: 不加矫饰的野心是无罪的。

En: Don't you see? Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: 你瞧见没有？白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: Net worth, net worth, net worth... At least I've got my net worth! Watch the numbers go by.
Zh: 净资产，净资产，净资产…… 至少我还有自己的净资产！ 看着这些数字刷新。

En: Gives me the itch to write a ticket myself, actually.
Zh: “看得我心里都痒痒了，也想亲自开张罚单。”

En: Makes you proud to be part of the RCM.
Zh: “这让你为成为RCM的一员而自豪。”

En: Enjoy what? It's hardly a front seat in the bowls of mirth at La Comédie Revachol.
Zh: “享受什么？就连瑞瓦肖喜剧中心的前排座都算不上。”

En: Somewhere in a hospital bed the lieutenant turns onto his side, feverish and exhausted...
Zh: 某处医院的病床上，发着高烧、疲惫不堪的警督翻身侧躺着……

En: (Groan and hold your wound.)
Zh: (呻吟，按紧你的伤口。)

En: Savvy, hey Savvy... where did you go?! Talk to me!
Zh: 鬼鬼，嘿，鬼鬼…… 你去哪了？说话呀！

En: She will come back.
Zh: 她会回来的。

En: You can feel the lieutenant's stillness next to you...
Zh: 你能感觉到身边警督一动不动……

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the sodium lights.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像钠灯下的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling rain.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像雨中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling snow.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像落雪中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像灿烂阳光下的环岛。

En: Go away, stupid thoughts.
Zh: 走开，愚蠢的想法。

En: It's fucking lonely at the top.
Zh: 他妈的高处不胜寒啊。

En: No, the free market demands something much more PR-friendly than what Idiot Doom Spiral has done here.
Zh: 不，自由市场所需求的是比白痴厄运螺旋留下的这个大作更有利于公关的项目。

En: You managed to gather quite a fortune, kicked off by underpaying a young artist...
Zh: “你通过压价买下年轻艺术家的作品，从中赚了一大笔钱……”

En: Underpaying? But you paid the highest possible price!
Zh: 给少了？但你已经出了合理范围内的最高价！

En: Underpaying? You paid according to the market -- not too much, not too little...
Zh: 给少了? 你是按市场价付的钱……不多也不少……

En: You *had to* pay her so little, otherwise you wouldn't have made any profit -- and if you don't make a profit, you don't have money to invest!
Zh: 你*只能*给她很少的钱，不然你就没任何利润剩下了——如果你没有利润剩下，你就没钱去投资了！

En: Yes, who are you talking about?
Zh: 是啊，你在说谁？

En: Good -- take no responsibility over this disaster. It was supposed to *promote* you, not bore people.
Zh: 很好——不要对这场灾难负责。这个项目应该是为了*推销*你，而不是让人感到厌烦。

En: I don't think we have time for that. And even if we did, I doubt he's around anymore. Everyone's left.
Zh: “我认为咱们没这个时间去管他。就算咱们真去了，我也怀疑他人还在不在。所有人都走了。”

En: Sure, whenever you're ready.
Zh: “当然，你准备好了就行。”

En: Honest. Maybe even *too* honest... Didn't we talk about giving back to the community?
Zh: 诚实。也许*太过*诚实了……我们不是说过要回馈社会吗？

En: Demands *what*? Endless reconceptualization? The lives we lost? The charade?
Zh: 需求*什么*？无止无休的概念再设定？我们同胞的生命？骗局？

En: Of course you're going to distance yourself from your misdeeds -- the lying, the conning, the exploitation...
Zh: 你当然要与自己的恶行保持距离——说谎、诈骗、盘剥……

En: A white lie -- of course he has opinions.
Zh: 一个善意的谎言——他当然有意见。

En: Wait, I want to talk to Idiot Doom Spiral first.
Zh: “等等，我想先和白痴厄运漩涡谈谈。”

En: Okay, let me have one more look at the monument...
Zh: “好吧，让我再看一眼这个纪念碑……”

En: And don't forget about the lying...
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……

En: And don't forget about the lying... or the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……以及你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: And was it all worth it?
Zh: “这一切值得吗？”

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The tickets remain true to their duty, ready for the night shift -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to brushing dirt from the asphalt below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。罚单们依然在坚守岗位，准备上夜班——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配到下面刷起沥青上的泥土……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The sodden tickets remain true to their duty against the storm -- except for a few that lie face down below, honourably drowned in a puddle...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。那些湿漉漉的罚单依然在坚守岗位，迎接着暴风雨的侵袭——除了一些朝下躺在地上，光荣地淹没在水坑里的罚单……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The tickets remain true to their duty, like a crowd of commuters huddled for warmth -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to raking the drifts below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雪地里安静得几近诡异。罚单依然在坚守岗位，就像一群挤在一起取暖的上班族——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配到下面扫雪。

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The tickets remain true to their duty -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to brushing dirt from the asphalt below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。罚单依然在坚守岗位——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配去下面清扫沥青上的泥土……

En: I don't think anything. I'm just asking questions.
Zh: “我没任何想法，我只是在问问题。”

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The tickets remain true to their duty, ready for the night shift -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to brushing dirt from the asphalt below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。罚单们依然在坚守岗位，准备上夜班——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配到下面刷起沥青上的泥土……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The sodden tickets remain true to their duty against the storm -- except for a few that lie face down below, honourably drowned in a puddle...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。那些湿漉漉的罚单依然在坚守岗位，迎接着暴风雨的侵袭——除了一些朝下躺在地上，光荣地淹没在水坑里的罚单……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The tickets remain true to their duty, like a crowd of commuters huddled for warmth -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to raking the drifts below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雪地里安静得几近诡异。罚单依然在坚守岗位，就像一群挤在一起取暖的上班族——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配到下面扫雪。

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The tickets remain true to their duty -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to brushing dirt from the asphalt below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。罚单依然在坚守岗位——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配去下面清扫沥青上的泥土……

En: He nods in dutiful agreement then closes his eyes for half a beat. When he opens them, it's clear he has something to say to you...
Zh: 他顺从地点点头表示同意，然后闭上眼睛半拍。当他睁开眼睛时，很明显他有话要对你说……

En: Khm, the lieutenant clears his throat, keen to move on.
Zh: “咳咳。”警督清了清嗓子，很想继续说下去。

En: Yes, it's not very good for PR...
Zh: 是的，不是很有利于公关……

En: A seagull lands on the monument, surveying the town below. Then it takes off again.
Zh: 一只海鸥落在纪念碑上，观察着下面的小镇。过了会儿它又飞走了。

En: Savvy's here, watching the little *fiasco* of the horseback monument. You should distance yourself from this project. Say it's your Personal Brand Strategist gone rogue.
Zh: 鬼鬼在这里，看着雕塑的小*惨败*。你应该和这个项目保持距离，就说是你的个人品牌策划师发神经了。

En: Wind takes some of the tickets across the pavement, littering the streets with paper police officers...
Zh: 风卷起一些罚单划过人行道，在街道上洒满纸制的警察……

En: King Filippe III, the Squanderer, the Greatest of the Filippian Kings of Revachol, agrees.
Zh: 放荡者菲利普三世，瑞瓦肖菲利普王朝最伟大的君王，表示同意。

En: The ticket creature above seems to agree.
Zh: 上面那个罚单构成的生物似乎表示同意。

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The tickets remain true to their duty, ready for the night shift -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to brushing dirt from the asphalt below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。罚单们依然在坚守岗位，准备上夜班——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配到下面刷起沥青上的泥土……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The sodden tickets remain true to their duty against the storm -- except for a few that lie face down below, honourably drowned in a puddle...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。那些湿漉漉的罚单依然在坚守岗位，迎接着暴风雨的侵袭——除了一些朝下躺在地上，光荣地淹没在水坑里的罚单……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The tickets remain true to their duty, like a crowd of commuters huddled for warmth -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to raking the drifts below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在雪地里安静得几近诡异。罚单依然在坚守岗位，就像一群挤在一起取暖的上班族——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配到下面扫雪。

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The tickets remain true to their duty -- except for a few that have come unstuck, now assigned to brushing dirt from the asphalt below...
Zh: 交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。罚单依然在坚守岗位——除了少数几张已经脱落的，现在被分配去下面清扫沥青上的泥土……

En: Maybe... But we still have a long way to go -- your net worth is *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 也许吧……但我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Alright, shall we? We still have some things left to do before we can go home.
Zh: “好了，我们走吧？在我们可以回家之前还有些事情没有做完。”

En: Who? The lieutenant meets your eyes with a questioning glance -- then looks up again.
Zh: “谁？”警督带着疑问的目光看向你的眼睛——然后又抬头往上看。

En: One more question, if you don't mind -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “还有一个问题，如果你不介意的话——这一切值得吗？”

En: Detective, I have a few questions if you don't mind...
Zh: “警探，如果你不介意的话，我有几个问题……”

En: 'Gone rogue'? No, Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: “发神经”？不，白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true businessman. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的生意人。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true philanthropist. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的慈善家。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Tax? What tax? We're in Revachol. He looks around, as if looking for hidden taxation, then shakes his head.
Zh: “税？什么税？我们可是在瑞瓦肖。”他环顾四周，好像在寻找隐藏的课税，然后摇了摇头。

En: Come on, we've stood here long enough. And money doesn't sleep.
Zh: 拜托，我们已经站在这里够久了，而钱永远不会原地不动等人去捡的。

En: Never mind. Another question -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “没事了。还有一个问题——这一切值得吗？”

En: He's talking about pussy. Our guy's gonna be *swimming* in it now that he's loaded!
Zh: 他说的是胆小鬼。我们的好小伙现在赚大钱了，要在钞票里*游泳*了！

En: Beauty doesn't cut it. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 美丽并不能解决问题。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: People are trying to mourn and all they can hear are those little tickets flapping in their ears.
Zh: 想要来哀悼的人，耳边却只能听到那些小票据拍打的声音。

En: 100% worth it. I'd do it all over again -- but this time with more *profit*.
Zh: “100%值得。我还想再来一次呢——但这次得有更多的*利润*。”

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community. Even told Idiot Doom Spiral to stop drinking.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。我甚至还劝说白痴厄运漩涡戒酒。

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。”

En: About what?
Zh: “你是指什么？”

En: Actually, I'd prefer silence.
Zh: “其实我宁可保持沉默。”

En: I really don't need to be judged like this, especially after the amount of tax I've paid.
Zh: “我真的不想被人这样评判，尤其是在我交了这么多税之后。”

En: No, you should own up to it, claim it as *yours*. Forget about the haters.
Zh: 不，你应该承认它，声称这是属于*你的*。别管那些黑子。

En: It's very on brand. We can work with it.
Zh: 这很有品牌感。我们可以借此赚一笔。

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, even the street lamp.
Zh: “但这一切值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，甚至那盏路灯。”

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, the thing with Idiot Doom Spiral...
Zh: “但这一切都值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，白痴厄运漩涡的事情……”

En: About this... pursuit of wealth... pursuit of recognition. He looks up, trying to find a name for your actions.
Zh: “我指的是你这……追求财富……追求认可的行为。”他抬起头来，试图找到一个词来描述你的举动。

En: He nods, eyes still on the ticket creature above, on the hundreds of parking notes rustling like feathers in the dark.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛依然盯着上面的迪斯科舞者，盯着那几百张像羽毛一样在黑暗中沙沙作响的违停罚单。

En: He nods, eyes still on the ticket creature above, on the hundreds of parking notes rustling like feathers in the cold air.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛依然盯着上面那个罚单构成的生物，盯着那几百张像羽毛一样在寒气中沙沙作响的违停罚单。

En: Okay. The lieutenant snaps shut like a pocket book.
Zh: “好吧。”警督闭上了嘴，就像一本合上的口袋本。

En: Yes, let's get back to making money.
Zh: 是的，我们回去赚钱吧。

En: Maybe it's an honest portrait of who I am and what I've done.
Zh: 也许这就是我的本质以及所作所为的最真实的写照。

En: A fiasco?! No, it's beautiful...
Zh: 惨败？！不，这太美了……

En: Thanks. (Keep staring at the monument.)
Zh: “谢谢。”(继续盯着纪念碑)

En: A Boring Cop? No. You're a hustler -- and you've got a long way to go. Your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 做一个无聊警察？不，你是个骗子——而且你还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Khm, that was an inappropriate joke. Ask your questions.
Zh: “嗯，这是个不合适的玩笑。你问吧。”

En: To win her back.
Zh: “为了赢回她的心。”

En: It's not what I wanted. This is all Idiot Doom Spiral's work.
Zh: “我想要的不是这个。这都是白痴厄运漩涡干的。”

En: Why did you do it?
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？”

En: The cop radio is buzzing on silent...
Zh: 警用无线电在静音状态下嗡嗡作响……

En: Why do you think? It gave me something to do.
Zh: “你觉得是为什么？这让我有事可做。”

En: I wanted better for myself.
Zh: “我想对自己更好一点。”

En: What are you talking about?
Zh: “你在说什么？”

En: The free market demands it.
Zh: “这是自由市场的市场需求。”

En: Spoken like a true hustler. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 你现在讲话像是一个真正的骗子。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: The lieutenant considers the restoration for a few seconds. "Am I not seeing something here? There's not much to it."
Zh: 警督想了几秒原来是什么样子。“我是不是看漏了什么？这里没有多少东西还在。”

En: Good -- take no responsibility over this disaster. It was supposed to *promote* you, not confuse people.
Zh: 很好——不要对这场灾难负责。这个项目应该是为了*推销*你，而不是让人感到迷惑。

En: Net worth, net worth, net worth... At least I've got my net worth! Watch the numbers go by.
Zh: 净资产，净资产，净资产…… 至少我还有自己的净资产！ 看着这些数字刷新。

En: That's what you get when you commission a drunk, I guess.
Zh: “我想，这就是你委托一个醉鬼的结果吧。”

En: What you're seeing here is a total lack of appreciation for both art *and* history.
Zh: “你在这里看到的是完全缺乏对艺术和历史的鉴赏力的后果。”

En: It's setting a precedent. We must overwrite the harms of the past with the glory of the future.
Zh: “这是开创先河。我们必须用未来的荣耀掩盖过去的伤害。”

En: He nods in hopes of humouring you then closes his eyes for half a beat. When he opens them, it's clear he has something to say to you...
Zh: 他迁就地点点头表示同意，然后闭眼了半秒。当他睁开眼睛时，很明显他有话要对你说……

En: Khm, the lieutenant clears his throat, keen to move on.
Zh: “咳咳。”警督清了清嗓子，很想继续说下去。

En: Detective, I have a few questions if you don't mind...
Zh: “警探，如果你不介意的话，我有几个问题……”

En: About what?
Zh: “你是指什么？”

En: No, I don't mind.
Zh: “不，我不介意。”

En: Somewhere in a hospital bed the lieutenant turns onto his side, feverish and exhausted...
Zh: 某处医院的病床上，发着高烧、疲惫不堪的警督翻身侧躺着……

En: A fiasco?! No, it's beautiful...
Zh: 惨败？！不，这太美了……

En: Even *I'm* not sure what that's supposed to be.
Zh: “就连*我*也不确定那是什么。”

En: About this... pursuit of wealth... pursuit of recognition. He looks up, trying to find a name for your actions.
Zh: “我指的是你这……追求财富……追求认可的行为。”他抬起头来，试图找到一个词来描述你的举动。

En: He nods, eyes still on the defaced statue above, on the deviant smear delivering a florid punch to the dark.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛依然盯着上面那座无损的雕像，盯着那一抹离经叛道的污迹为黑暗点上一杯柠檬潘趣。

En: He nods, eyes still on the defaced statue above, on the deviant smear delivering a florid punch to its dreary surroundings.
Zh: 他点了点头，眼睛依然盯着上面那座被污损的雕像，看着那一抹离经叛道的污迹给周围沉闷的环境送上一记艳丽的冲击。

En: Actually, I'd prefer silence.
Zh: “其实我宁可保持沉默。”

En: Okay. The lieutenant snaps shut like a lid on a paint tin.
Zh: “好吧。”警督闭上了嘴，就像油漆罐的盖子。

En: Why did you do it?
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？”

En: The cop radio is buzzing on silent...
Zh: 警用无线电在静音状态下嗡嗡作响……

En: Beauty doesn't cut it. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 美丽并不能解决问题。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Relax, Kim. I'm just playing. Ask your questions.
Zh: “放松，金。我只是开个玩笑。你问吧。”

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: I've made it.
Zh: 我做到了。

En: A seagull lands on the monument, surveying the town below. Then it takes off again.
Zh: 一只海鸥落在纪念碑上，观察着下面的小镇。过了会儿它又飞走了。

En: Thanks. (Keep staring at the monument.)
Zh: “谢谢。”(继续盯着纪念碑)

En: The free market demands it.
Zh: “这是自由市场的市场需求。”

En: I wanted better for myself.
Zh: “我想对自己更好一点。”

En: Why do you think we do anything? It's the question behind all art.
Zh: “那你觉得我们做的任何事情有什么意义吗？所有艺术背后都存在这个问题。”

En: It's not what I wanted. This is all Idiot Doom Spiral's work.
Zh: “我想要的不是这个。这都是白痴厄运漩涡干的。”

En: To win her back.
Zh: “为了赢回她的心。”

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the sodium lights.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连钠灯照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling rain.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雨中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, in the falling snow.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连雪中的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: The thought rings hollow in your silent mind. It's even quieter on the roundabout, under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 这个念头在你沉默的思绪中嗡嗡作响。甚至连灿烂的阳光照射下的交通环岛都比你的脑子里更安静。

En: Who? The lieutenant meets your eyes with a questioning glance -- then looks up again.
Zh: “谁？”警督带着疑问的目光看向你的眼睛——然后又抬头往上看。

En: You can feel the lieutenant's stillness next to you...
Zh: 你能感觉到身边警督一动不动……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The formless complexion glows at the centre, each street lamp failing to replicate the shining brilliance of its rebellion...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。无形的气色在中心闪耀，而周围的每一盏路灯都无法复制其叛逆的闪耀辉煌……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The painted surface at the centre welcomes the droplets like a water slide. Around it, the coast's older architecture enjoys nothing but slow erosion...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。中心的彩绘表面像水滑梯一样迎接着水滴的到来。在它的周围，海岸的老建筑只是在享受着缓慢的侵蚀，之外别无所求……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The paint is a runny yolk at the centre, the birth cell of a new style -- ready to be dipped into and nourish the youth with sticky inspiration...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。涂鸦中心是一个流淌的蛋黄，是一种新风格诞生时的第一个细胞——随时可以供人蘸取，随时可以用粘稠的灵感滋养青年……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The formless complexion glows at the centre, while the old architecture surrounding it groans in the heat with looming irrelevance...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。无形的气色在中心闪耀，而周围的老建筑却在炎热中呻吟着，隐隐约约，显得无足轻重……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The formless complexion glows at the centre, each street lamp failing to replicate the shining brilliance of its rebellion...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。无形的气色在中心闪耀，而周围的每一盏路灯都无法复制其叛逆的闪耀辉煌……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The painted surface at the centre welcomes the droplets like a water slide. Around it, the coast's older architecture enjoys nothing but slow erosion...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。中心的彩绘表面像水滑梯一样迎接着水滴的到来。在它的周围，海岸的老建筑只是在享受着缓慢的侵蚀，之外别无所求……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The paint is a runny yolk at the centre, the birth cell of a new style -- ready to be dipped into and nourish the youth with sticky inspiration...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。涂鸦中心是一个流淌的蛋黄，是一种新风格诞生时的第一个细胞——随时可以供人蘸取，随时可以用粘稠的灵感滋养青年……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The formless complexion glows at the centre, while the old architecture surrounding it groans in the heat with looming irrelevance...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。无形的气色在中心闪耀，而周围的老建筑却在炎热中呻吟着，隐隐约约，显得无足轻重……

En: King Filippe III, the Squanderer, the Greatest of the Filippian Kings of Revachol, agrees.
Zh: 放荡者菲利普三世，瑞瓦肖菲利普王朝最伟大的君王，表示同意。

En: The defaced statue above seems to agree.
Zh: 上面的迪斯科帝王似乎表示同意。

En: I don't think anything. I'm just asking questions.
Zh: “我没任何想法，我只是在问问题。”

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the sodium lights. The formless complexion glows at the centre, each street lamp failing to replicate the shining brilliance of its rebellion...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在钠灯的照射下安静得几近诡异。无形的气色在中心闪耀，而周围的每一盏路灯都无法复制其叛逆的闪耀辉煌……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the rain. The painted surface at the centre welcomes the droplets like a water slide. Around it, the coast's older architecture enjoys nothing but slow erosion...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雨中安静得几近诡异。中心的彩绘表面像水滑梯一样迎接着水滴的到来。在它的周围，海岸的老建筑只是在享受着缓慢的侵蚀，之外别无所求……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so in the snow. The paint is a runny yolk at the centre, the birth cell of a new style -- ready to be dipped into and nourish the youth with sticky inspiration...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在雪中安静得几近诡异。涂鸦中心是一个流淌的蛋黄，是一种新风格诞生时的第一个细胞——随时可以供人蘸取，随时可以用粘稠的灵感滋养青年……

En: He nods. The roundabout is quiet, almost eerily so under the brilliant sunlight. The formless complexion glows at the centre, while the old architecture surrounding it groans in the heat with looming irrelevance...
Zh: 他点了点头。交通环岛很安静，在灿烂的阳光下安静得几近诡异。无形的气色在中心闪耀，而周围的老建筑却在炎热中呻吟着，隐隐约约，显得无足轻重……

En: Your eyes glide over the horse, over its patina green coat. Something about the mount's sad animal-eyes makes you pause...
Zh: 你的视线再那匹马身上，它的铜锈绿衣上逡巡。这匹马悲伤的的眼镜让你的视线停了下来……

En: Was it worth it?
Zh: 这值得吗？

En: It's fucking lonely at the top.
Zh: 他妈的高处不胜寒啊。

En: A figure in a day dress, cuddled up in an armchair... she looks up and smiles at you across the living room.
Zh: 一个穿着便装的身影，蜷缩在扶手椅上……她抬起头，隔着客厅对你微笑。

En: It's very on brand. We can work with it.
Zh: 这很有品牌感。我们可以借此赚一笔。

En: Okay, let me have one more look at the monument...
Zh: “好吧，让我再看一眼这个纪念碑……”

En: Maybe... But we still have a long way to go -- your net worth is *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 也许吧……但我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: 100% worth it. I'd do it all over again -- but this time with more *profit*.
Zh: “100%值得。我还想再来一次呢——但这次得有更多的*利润*。”

En: No, you should own up to it, claim it as *yours*. Forget about the haters.
Zh: 不，你应该承认它，声称这是属于*你的*。别管那些黑子。

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community. Even told Idiot Doom Spiral to stop drinking.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。我甚至还劝说白痴厄运漩涡戒酒。

En: Hey, it's the system. I did my best to support artists, employ people, give back to the community.
Zh: “喂，体制就是这样。我尽我所能支持艺术家、雇佣员工来回馈社会。”

En: I really don't need to be judged like this, especially after the amount of tax I've paid.
Zh: “我真的不想被人这样评判，尤其是在我交了这么多税之后。”

En: What are you talking about?
Zh: “你在说什么？”

En: The lieutenant speaks as if you're *rich* -- a common misconception -- especially if you count the tax. No, we've got a long way to go before we can feel financially comfortable. The hustle never stops!
Zh: 警督说得好像你很*有钱*——这是种很常见的误会——尤其是在你没算税后的情况下。不，想要获得财务自由，我们还有很长的路要走。搞钱永不止步！

En: The defaced statue towers above, mounted on a horse. Numerous rods and ropes still hold the original reassemblage in place.
Zh: 被污损的雕像骑在马上高高耸立着。众多的杆子和绳索还在固定着原本重组后的样子。

En: Underpaying? But you paid the highest possible price!
Zh: 给少了？但你已经出了合理范围内的最高价！

En: Underpaying? You paid according to the market -- not too much, not too little...
Zh: 给少了? 你是按市场价付的钱……不多也不少……

En: Maybe it's an honest portrait of who I am and what I've done.
Zh: 也许这就是我的本质以及所作所为的最真实的写照。

En: You *had to* pay her so little, otherwise you wouldn't have made any profit -- and if you don't make a profit, you don't have money to invest!
Zh: 你*只能*给她很少的钱，不然你就没任何利润剩下了——如果你没有利润剩下，你就没钱去投资了！

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, the thing with Idiot Doom Spiral...
Zh: “但这一切都值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，白痴厄运漩涡的事情……”

En: But was it all worth it? Everything that came before -- Cindy, the stocks, even the street lamp.
Zh: “但这一切值得吗? 之前的一切——辛迪，股票，甚至那盏路灯。”

En: Spoken like a true hustler. We have a long way to go -- your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 你现在讲话像是一个真正的骗子。我们还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: I don't think we have time for that. And even if we did, I doubt he's around anymore. Everyone's left.
Zh: “我认为咱们没这个时间去管他。就算咱们真去了，我也怀疑他人还在不在。所有人都走了。”

En: Sure, whenever you're ready.
Zh: “当然，你准备好了就行。”

En: Of course you're going to distance yourself from your misdeeds -- the lying, the conning, the exploitation...
Zh: 你当然要与自己的恶行保持距离——说谎、诈骗、盘剥……

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the sodium lights.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像钠灯下的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling rain.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像雨中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout in the falling snow.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像雪中的环岛。

En: Your mind is silent, just like the roundabout under the brilliant sun.
Zh: 你的心一片寂静，就像灿烂阳光下的环岛。

En: Yes, let's get back to making money.
Zh: 是的，我们回去赚钱吧。

En: Yes, who are you talking about?
Zh: 是啊，你在说谁？

En: Come on, we've stood here long enough. And money doesn't sleep.
Zh: 拜托，我们已经站在这里够久了，而钱永远不会原地不动等人去捡的。

En: He's talking about pussy. Our guy's gonna be *swimming* in it now that he's loaded!
Zh: 他说的是胆小鬼。我们的好小伙现在赚大钱了，要在钞票里*游泳*了！

En: You managed to gather quite a fortune, kicked off by underpaying a young artist...
Zh: “你通过压价买下年轻艺术家的作品，从中赚了一大笔钱……”

En: An Art Cop? No. You're a hustler -- and you've got a long way to go. Your net worth is still *nothing* if you factor in the tax!
Zh: 做一个艺术警察？不，你是个骗子——而且你还有很长的路要走——如果算上税，你的净资产仍然是*零*！

En: Tax? What tax? We're in Revachol. He looks around, as if looking for hidden taxation, then shakes his head.
Zh: “税？什么税？我们可是在瑞瓦肖。”他环顾四周，好像在寻找隐藏的课税，然后摇了摇头。

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true philanthropist. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的慈善家。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Ah yes. Spoken like a true businessman. He smiles gently.
Zh: “啊，是。你说话的感觉像个真正的生意人。”他温和地笑了笑。

En: Honest. Maybe even *too* honest... Didn't we talk about giving back to the community?
Zh: 诚实。也许*太过*诚实了……我们不是说过要回馈社会吗？

En: Demands *what*? Endless reconceptualization? The lives we lost? The charade?
Zh: 需求*什么*？无止无休的概念再设定？我们同胞的生命？骗局？

En: Wait, I want to talk to Idiot Doom Spiral first.
Zh: “等等，我想先和白痴厄运漩涡谈谈。”

En: No, the free market demands something less niche than what Idiot Doom Spiral has done here.
Zh: 不，自由市场所需求的是和白痴厄运螺旋留下的这个大作相比更普适性的项目。

En: And don't forget about the lying...
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……

En: And don't forget about the lying... or the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了说谎的事情……以及你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: Go away, stupid thoughts.
Zh: 走开，愚蠢的想法。

En: Don't you see? Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: 你瞧见没有？白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: Its colour really doesn't fit in with the mood right now. People are trying to *mourn*.
Zh: 这颜色真的不适合现在的气氛。人们都在*哀悼*。

En: A white lie -- of course he has opinions.
Zh: 一个善意的谎言——他当然有意见。

En: Never mind. Another question -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “没事了。还有一个问题——这一切值得吗？”

En: One more question, if you don't mind -- was it all worth it?
Zh: “还有一个问题，如果你不介意的话——这一切值得吗？”

En: Alright, shall we? We still have some things left to do before we can go home.
Zh: “好了，我们走吧？在我们可以回家之前还有些事情没有做完。”

En: 'Gone rogue'? No, Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Zh: “发神经”？不，白痴厄运漩涡终于取得了一些*瞩目*的成就。

En: Savvy's here, watching the little *fiasco* of the horseback monument. You should distance yourself from this project. Say it's your Personal Brand Strategist gone rogue.
Zh: 鬼鬼在这里，看着雕塑的小*惨败*。你应该和这个项目保持距离，就说是你的个人品牌策划师发神经了。

En: Savvy, hey Savvy... where did you go?! Talk to me!
Zh: 鬼鬼，嘿，鬼鬼…… 你去哪了？说话呀！

En: Yes, it's not very good for PR...
Zh: 是的，不是很有利于公关……

En: She will come back.
Zh: 她会回来的。

En: And was it all worth it?
Zh: “这一切值得吗？”

En: Geez, you artsy types are unbearable. No wonder you struggle to make your millions...
Zh: 天啊，你们这些玩艺术的真让人受不了。难怪你们挣不到大钱……

En: Nothing wrong with raw ambition.
Zh: 不加矫饰的野心是无罪的。

En: Wind blows hard enough to chip away at the edges of the paint. The bright rebellion is already starting to dull...
Zh: 风吹得很大，足以刮掉油漆的边缘。明亮的叛逆已经开始黯淡……

En: Has The End not already come? Stones were wetted, bloods were coughed, the locals cast under greater shadow. Is this not inappropriate when tribute should instead be placed?
Zh: 难道末日还没有到来吗？石头被打湿，鲜血被咳出，当地人被更大的阴影所笼罩。放置贡品难道有什么不合适的吗？

En: Look -- up there! It is he who sirens. Syntagmatarchis Trypa! Here, upon us, to warn of the mangy jowls of The Gloaming.
Zh: 看——上面！ 是他发出了警报。空洞上校！ 在这里，在我们上面，提醒大家末日那污秽的獠牙正在靠近。

En: Citizens must be warned. This is not only a declaration of the dead but a warning for those still alive. It comes, indomitably.
Zh: 必须有人警告市民。这不仅是来自死者的宣告，也是对活人的警告。它的到来不可避免。

En: Putting on a brave face and partying as hard as possible is the best way to get through tough times.
Zh: 装出一副勇敢的面孔，竭尽全力地狂欢，这是度过艰难时期的最好办法。

En: Happy isn't the word. You can be *satisfied* with a job well done. But the current mood doesn't invite any analogues to joy.
Zh: 快乐这词不合适。你可以对一项已经出色完成过的工作表示*满意*，但当下的心情并没有任何接近于喜悦的地方。

En: Not bad, not bad. It's just lacking some *objets de destruction*. Needs a ciggy or a half-drunk bottle. In fact, so do *you*.
Zh: 不坏，不坏。只是缺少一些*破坏性的东西*。需要来根烟或半瓶酒。事实上，*你*也需要。

En: How dull! Entertainment isn't a wall to read. Where are the performers and pyrotechnics? No one will pay attention to this.
Zh: 真无聊！ 娱乐不是一面贴满启示的墙。艺人和烟火在哪？不会有人会注意到这个。

En: This is the orderly pattern of duty; a picture of a job well done, over and over.
Zh: 这就是履行职责的常规模式；一幅工作出色完成的画面，一遍又一遍。

En: Such busy fingers. Each ticket written by hand, ripped from the pad, then pressed against the metal until stuck.
Zh: 手指真忙碌。每一张票据都是手写的，从垫板上撕下来，然后摁在金属上，直到黏住。

En: Oh yes, much is to be discussed at length here. The éclaboussure breaks up the lines of the original bronze piece, setting up an act of rebellion for us all to follow. It is an anarcho-modern calling -- destruction of the past, cause for the future.
Zh: 哦，是的，这里有很多可以展开讨论。色彩飞溅打破了铜器的原本的线条，向我们所有人作了了某种叛逆的表率。这是一个无政府的当代的号召——摧毁过去，开创未来。

En: Nothing to see here but disrespect. The old figure will outlive this pathetic lick of paint. The past always endures.
Zh: 除了不尊重，这里没有什么好看的。这个老雕塑可要比这可悲的一抹油漆长寿多了。过往总是历久弥新。

En: This splash was obviously made with a clumsy hand. Perhaps it adds to the artistry?
Zh: 涂下这一抹颜色明显是一只笨拙的手。也许手的笨拙也增添了艺术性？

En: And don't forget about the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: And don't forget about the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: And don't forget about the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: And don't forget about the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: And don't forget about the way you conned an honest pawnshop owner.
Zh: 还有，不要忘了你当初是怎么诈骗老实的当铺老板的。

En: Thank you for now. [Leave.]
Zh: “先谢谢你了。”[离开。]

En: Before I came, you seemed... *away*.
Zh: “我过来之前，你好像……*不在*。”

En: Blacked out. Should you be driving a lorry if you get like that?
Zh: “昏过去。如果你会这样的话，还能开货車吗？”

En: The woman is still hunched over the railing, her head swaying to the music, her eyes looking at nothing in particular.
Zh: 女人仍然蜷缩在栏杆上，她的脑袋随着音乐晃动，眼睛并没有特别在看什么。

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: Because I'm one of the best camionneurs around, that's why. I drive routes no one else will.
Zh: “我是附近最棒的货車司机之一，这就是为什么。我开的路线别人都不会开。”

En: You were in a dream. Inactive. Tuned off.
Zh: “你在做梦。怠惰，不着调。”

En: Yes. What about it?
Zh: “是的。怎么了？”

En: You go right ahead, lawman. Let me know when you find them. I can't wait to read their *report*...
Zh: “你去吧，警官。发现的时候记得告诉我。我等不及要看他们的*报告*了……”

En: This should be reported to the proper authorities.
Zh: “应该把这件事报告给相关部门。”

En: Huh? She comes to, as if nothing happened before. "What is it? What do you want?"
Zh: “哈？”她回过神来，好像之前什么也没发生过。“怎么了？你想要什么？”

En: Huh? That flicked a switch somewhere. "What is it? What do you want?"
Zh: “哈？”你碰到了某个开关。“怎么了？你想要什么？”

En: Snap your fingers in front of her face.
Zh: 在她的面前打个响指。

En: Never mind, sorry to bother you. [Leave.]
Zh: “没事，很抱歉打扰到你了。”[离开。]

En: I'm something of an expert in blacking out. You should take better care of yourself.
Zh: “我可以算是个昏倒方面的专家吧。你应该照顾好自己。”

En: She looks you over and scoffs. "You're right, lawman. *I'm* the one who should take *my* health more seriously. Thank you for looking out for me..."
Zh: 她看着你，嘲弄到。“你说得对，警官。*我*应该更关注*自己*的身体健康。感谢你这么为我着想……”

En: Is that all you woke me up to say?
Zh: “你把我叫醒就为了说这个？”

En: Is that all you wanted to know, lawman?
Zh: “你就想知道这些吗，警官？”

En: Show me the soles of your boots.
Zh: “你的鞋底让我看看。”

En: If you had to guess, who do you think is smuggling drugs around here?
Zh: “如果要你猜的话，你觉得是谁在这里贩毒？”

En: Alright. Are you *the* lady driver?
Zh: “好吧。你就是*那个*女司机吗？”

En: You seem like a woman who knows a thing or two about drugs.
Zh: “你看起来像是对毒品略知一二的人。”

En: Are you sure you don't know what you're hauling? Maybe it's...
Zh: “你肯定自己不知道拖的是什么？也许是……”

En: What is it that you're hauling, exactly?
Zh: “那你到底拉的是什么货呢？”

En: What do I need drugs for, lawman? What I see, what I *feel*, the Great Adversary... no drugs can compare.
Zh: “我要毒品做什么？我看到的，我*感觉*到的，大反派……任何毒品也不能匹敌。”

En: How can you not know what you're hauling in your own lorry?
Zh: “你怎么可能不知道自己拉的是什么货？”

En: Just this month I made half a dozen trips from Saramiriza to Graad, on the U41-A. What do you think they take from Saramiriza to Graad, lawman?
Zh: “就这一个月，我就开着U41-A从萨拉米尔泽往格拉德跑了六次。你觉得他们会从萨拉米尔泽往格拉德运什么？”

En: I think I know what's going on with you.
Zh: “我好像知道你是怎么回事了。”

En: Now what do you want with an old woman's boots, *xerife*?
Zh: “你要一个老女人的靴子干嘛，*治安官*？”

En: Did you just call me a *lady*, xerife?
Zh: “你刚刚叫我*女士*吗，治安官？”

En: No, lawman. Diamonds. She grins.
Zh: “不，警官。是钻石。”她咧开嘴笑了。

En: Okay. If you're not involved with the drug trafficking then why are you still waiting here? (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。如果你没参与毒品走私，那为什么还要在这里等呢？”（总结。）

En: Where do you want me to go? This isn't so bad. I can listen to music, or the seagulls. Look at all the colours and the features of this world. It's a good palate-cleanser, this jamboree...
Zh: “你想让我去哪？这里也没那么糟糕。我可以听听音乐，或者是海鸥的叫声。看看这个世界的色彩和样貌。这场欢愉的集会是一种很好的调色盘清洁剂……”

En: What are you hauling?
Zh: “你拉的是什么货？”

En: She shrugs. "Whatever stupid things they put in the lorry, I imagine."
Zh: 她耸耸肩。“他们随便往货車里塞的那些蠢东西咯，我猜。”

En: So you don't know what you're hauling in your own lorry?
Zh: “所以你不知道自己拉的是什么货？”

En: What if the cargo is contraband?
Zh: “如果货物是违禁品呢？”

En: I quit concerning myself with that a long time ago. She smiles a careless smile. "Besides, I don't drive the lorry for the cargo, if you know what I mean..."
Zh: “我很早以前就不太在乎自己的事了。”她无忧无虑地笑着。“再说了，我开货車可不是为了拉货，如果你懂我的意思……”

En: Then it's contraband, lawman. What? Do you want to take an old woman in? Be my guest. Lock me away like Bad Hand Hermenegildo.
Zh: “那就是违禁品了，警官。怎么？你想拘留一个老女人吗？那就别客气。像坏蛋埃梅内希尔多一样把我关起来。”

En: The woman sways her wrinkled shell back and forth, a strange grin across her face.
Zh: 女人来回摇摆着自己那皱巴巴的躯壳，脸上露出一种诡异的笑容。

En: Well, that's good enough for me.
Zh: “呃，对我来说已经挺好的了。”

En: It's for important police business, ma'am.
Zh: “这是很重要的警方工作，夫人。”

En: Please? Help me out here. It's important.
Zh: “拜托？帮帮我吧。这很重要。”

En: Nothing, I guess. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “没什么，我猜。我们聊聊别的吧。”

En: You said earlier you don't know what cargo you're hauling. Could it be drugs?
Zh: “你之前说不知道自己拉的是什么货。有可能是毒品吗？”

En: Who the fuck is Evrart?
Zh: “艾弗拉特他妈的又是谁？”

En: Because of all those hauls through the pale?
Zh: “因为所有货物都会穿过灰域？”

En: That's not really what I was getting at.
Zh: “我真不是这个意思。”

En: Where could I get my hands on an *experience* like that?
Zh: “那我能从哪里得到那种*体验*呢？”

En: She glares at you. "Oh, this *xerife* is a smart one."
Zh: 她瞪着你。“哦，这个*治安官*挺聪明的。”

En: Then what *were* you getting at?
Zh: “那你又是*什么*意思呢？”

En: If you don't know, pfft! She flicks her wrist in a gesture of casual dismissal.
Zh: “如果你不知道，那就……啐！”她挥舞着手腕，一副要随意解雇人的样子。

En: Evrart sent me. (Place finger on the side of your nose and tap twice.)
Zh: “艾弗拉特派我来的。”（把手指放在你的鼻侧，然后轻拍两次。）

En: I wanted to ask if you'd be interested in smuggling some drugs?
Zh: “我想问你有没有兴趣走私毒品？”

En: Okay. Let me put this another way -- are you smuggling drugs through Terminal B?
Zh: “好吧。我们换个说法——你是在从B中转站走私毒品吗？”

En: Maybe if she thought you're... *corrupt*?
Zh: 也许她觉得你是个……*腐败*警察？

En: Your *mãe*, lawman. What of it?
Zh: “你*老娘*，警官。又怎么样呢？”

En: Her shoulder bones crack as she shrugs. "Maybe. Probably not. Makes no difference to me either way."
Zh: 她耸耸肩，骨头发出噼里啪啦的声响。“也许是。也许不是。反正对我来说也没什么区别。”

En: The Union boss?
Zh: “工会老大？”

En: *Ah*. And what do I care about the Union boss? He's not Gabriel, he's not Franconegro. He's not even Hermenegildo the Hand.
Zh: “*啊*。那我为什么要关心工会老大呢？他不是加百列，不是弗兰考格斯。他甚至都不是埃梅内希尔多。”

En: Why would I want to do that?
Zh: “我为什么要这么做呢？”

En: For commerce, the lifeblood of the isolas.
Zh: “为了贸易，这是洲的生命线。”

En: For the glory of the world republic. Liberation of the spirit and body.
Zh: “为了世界共和国的荣耀。为了精神和身体的解放。”

En: I don't know, the thrill of the criminal lifestyle?
Zh: “我不知道，违法犯罪的生活方式比较刺激？”

En: Lawman... what in the name of god are you talking about?
Zh: “警官……你知不知道自己到底在说些什么？”

En: Drugs!?
I don't know, that's why I'm asking you.
Zh: “我不知道，所以我要问你啊。”

En: It's diamonds, lawman. Obviously. She grins.
Zh: “是钻石，警官。这很明显。”她咧嘴笑了。

En: Damn this. Make her answer.
Zh: 该死。让她回答。

En: I didn't ask you about diamonds, did I? I don't care about that.
Zh: “我没问你钻石的事，是吧？我不在乎那个。”

En: Are you making fun of me right now? You better start complying.
Zh: “你现在是在取笑我吗？你最好配合一点。”

En: I would never make fun of a real lawman, *xerife*.
Zh: “我永远不会取笑一个真正的警官，*治安官*先生。”

En: Or -- I can just relax and let my mind carry me back where it will. To the great plains...
Zh: “或者——我可以放松，让我的思想带我回到它想去的地方。前往大平原……”

En: Diamonds are good for you, lawman. You should try them sometime. Make yourself pretty like Eva Deshoras.
Zh: “钻石挺合适你的，警官。你有时间应该试试。让自己变得像伊娃·德秀拉斯一样漂亮。”

En: Okay. Let me ask you something else, then.
Zh: “好吧。那我问你点别的。”

En: I guess that makes sense. Minding your own business.
Zh: “我猜你说的有些道理。只管自己的事。”

En: Of course it does -- what did you expect?
Zh: “当然是了——你在期待什么？”

En: Never mind, she sighs. "I remember now. I'm still stuck in that traffic jam. In the *Fifties*," she adds with contempt.
Zh: “没关系，”她叹了口气。“现在我记起来了，我还困在大堵車的队伍里，困在*五十年代*。”她轻蔑地补充道。

En: While *you* people were tearing each other apart over your petty little Revolution, in Mesque it was a golden age.
Zh: “那时候*你们*这帮人还在可怜的革命里自相残杀，而梅斯克却正处在黄金年代。”

En: In all likelihood, it's a world that's only ever existed in her mind.
Zh: 十有八九，那个世界只存在在她自己的脑子里。

En: He was the biggest star of his day. Girls used to faint in the aisles of the cinema whenever he came on the screen, and school boys used to memorize all his lines... She leans back, savouring the world she's conjured up.
Zh: “他是那个年代最耀眼的明星。每当他出现在大银幕上的时候，姑娘们会晕倒在电影院的通道上，学校里的男孩会记下他的所有台词……”她身体后仰，体味着她编造出来的世界。

En: Who's Gabriel Buenguerro?
Zh: “加百列·布恩伽罗是谁？”

En: Are you alright, ma'am? You were out.
Zh: “你没事吧，女士？你晕过去了。”

En: I was actually hoping *you* could tell me that.
Zh: “其实我希望*你*能告诉我。”

En: Me? I am the law around here.
Zh: “我？我是这里的一个执法人员。”

En: Where else would you be then?
Zh: “那你还会去什么地方？”

En: *When* else would you be then?
Zh: “那你*什么时候*会去呢？”

En: Back in Mesque during the time of the Revolution. The smile returns to her face. "The sidewalks and cafes are filled with young people... I was on my way to see a new boiadeiro picture starring Gabriel Buenguerro."
Zh: “回到大革命期间的梅斯克。”笑容又回到了她的脸庞。“人行道和咖啡厅里到处都是年轻人……我正在去看加百列·布恩伽罗主演的新牛仔电影的路上。”

En: The small wrinkled woman does not greet you. She nods along to something on her radio. A photograph is clutched in her hands and there is a warm smile on her face.
Zh: 这个矮小又满脸皱纹的女人并没有问候你。她跟着电台里播放的东西轻轻点头。她的手中攥着一张相片，脸上挂着温暖的笑容。

En: Excuse me, ma'am, I'd like to ask some questions.
Zh: “抱歉，女士，我想问你几个问题。”

En: Grandma?
Zh: “奶奶？”

En: She's just a distracted old woman. Better to leave her alone.
Zh: “她只是个惆怅的老妇人。最好别去打扰她。”

En: If you say so... [Leave.]
Zh: “如果你这么说的话……”[离开。]

En: Where am I? Who are you? Like a magician recalling a subject from hypnosis, you've jolted her back to reality.
Zh: “我在哪？你是谁？”就像魔术师唤醒一个被催眠的对象，你把她猛地一下拉回了现实。

En: No response. Wherever this woman is, your words fail to reach her.
Zh: 没有回应。不管这个女人在哪，你的话语都无法传达到她那里。

En: Wait... The lieutenant stops you before you can snap.
Zh: “等等……”你还没开口就被警督拦住了。

En: Then I don't suppose you could tell me where we are, lawman?
Zh: “那我猜你不会告诉我，我们现在在什么地方吧，警官？”

En: The woman still has her eyes fixed on the photograph in her hands. In the background, the radio plays.
Zh: 女人的目光依然固定在手中的相片上。收音机还在背景里播放。

En: The smile on her face has disappeared, replaced by the wary aspect of a cornered beast.
Zh: 她脸上的笑容消失了，取而代之的是一头困兽谨慎的神情。

En: It's the warmth of a winter night's fire. Maybe she could give you comfort and shelter? Some cigarettes and food money? Maybe she's your...
Zh: 这是冬夜炉火的温暖。也许她可以给你提供温暖和庇护？再来点香烟，食物和钱？也许她是你的……

En: Nothing to do here. [Leave.]
Zh: “这儿没什么要做的。”[离开。]

En: Until you came along, that is. The look she gives you is accusatory.
Zh: “那是直到你出现之前。”她的神情传递出指责的信息。

En: If you want her attention you may need to be more forceful.
Zh: “如果你想引起她的注意，那可能需要再用力一些。”

En: Okay. What's that?
Zh: “好吧。那是什么？”

En: Of course not. To truly understand the boiadeiro, you need to listen to 'On the Western Plain'.
Zh: “你当然不懂。为了真正理解牛仔，你要听一下‘西部大平原’。”

En: I've already got too many records to listen to as it is. Change of topic.
Zh: “我已经有太多录音要听的了。换个话题吧。”

En: It's an old ballad about a young girl who falls in love with a daring boiadeiro. He promises to marry her as soon as he returns from the Western Plain.
Zh: “那是一首古老的民谣，说的是一个年轻女孩爱上了一个勇敢的牛仔。他答应从西部大平原回来之后就娶她。”

En: I'm guessing that doesn't happen.
Zh: “我猜最后没有实现。”

En: And they live happily ever after?
Zh: “然后他们从此过上了幸福快乐的生活？”

En: No, the boiadeiro strangles his beloved and throws her body in the Magritte. Then he rides off, because the Western Plain is calling to him.
Zh: “没有，牛仔勒死了他的最爱，然后把她的尸体扔进了马格里特河。然后他跨上马背，因为西部大平原在召唤他。”

En: Of course not. The boiadeiro returns from the Western Plain a *changed* man. One night, as he and his beloved are out walking along the River Magritte, she pleads with him to give up his riding and settle down...
Zh: “当然没有。牛仔从西部大平原回来的时候已经*变了*一个人。一天晚上，他和自己的爱人沿着马格里特河边散步，她恳求他放弃骑马，稳定下来……”

En: You have to understand -- a true boiadeiro needs a whole horizon to himself. He can't be tied down by man or woman. His beloved was selfish. She didn't know what it meant to love a *boiadeiro*.
Zh: “你必须理解——一个真正的牛仔渴望的是拥有一整个地平线。他不能被任何男人或女人拖累。他的爱人太自私了。她不懂得爱上一个*牛仔*意味着什么。”

En: That's a terrible song.
Zh: “这首歌也太可怕了吧。”

En: (Nod solemnly.) "A beautiful song."
Zh: 严肃地点点头。“真是一首美丽的歌。”

En: I still don't really understand this whole *boiadeiro* thing.
Zh: “我还是不太理解你说的这整个*牛仔*加百列·布恩伽罗什么什么的。”

En: I think I see where this is going.
Zh: “我想我知道是怎么回事了。”

En: In the background you can hear the orchestra swell as the screen fills with the maiden's imploring eyes...
Zh: 在背景中，你听到美妙的管弦乐曲，大银幕上是少女恳求的双眼……

En: I don't think I *do* know what you mean.
Zh: “我觉得我好像*不是*很明白你的意思。”

En: Of course you don't.
Zh: “你当然不明白了。”

En: She's just a distracted old woman. We should maybe let her get back to her things...
Zh: “她只是个惆怅的老妇人。也许我们应该让她继续做自己的事……”

En: You hear that, lawman? I don't think your partner likes you spending too much time with me...
Zh: “你听见了吗，警官？我觉得你的搭档不希望你在我身上花太多时间……”

En: Diamonds.
Zh: “钻石。”

En: Diamonds, really?
Zh: “钻石，真的吗？”

En: Of course not, she says, grinning. "But wouldn't it be marvellous if I was?"
Zh: “当然不是，”她笑着说道。“不过如果是的话，难道不觉得会特别惊人吗？”

En: Okay, but what are you *really* hauling?
Zh: “好吧，那你*到底*在拉什么货呢？”

En: So he gives up his riding and settles down, right?
Zh: “所以他放弃骑马，然后安顿下来了，对吗？”

En: That's... not where I thought that was going.
Zh: “这个……不是我想象中的发展。”

En: No. This one is a monster in disguise.
Zh: 不。这是一只伪装起来的怪兽。

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Why? I just told you why.
Zh: “为什么？我刚告诉你为什么了。”

En: The photo -- an *ambrotype* from the turn of the century. As golden as her smile.
Zh: 那张相片——使用的是世纪之交的*玻璃干板照相法*。和她的笑容一样金黄。

En: Wait, what's so bad about the Fifties?
Zh: “等等，五十年代怎么就糟糕了？”

En: The men have small jaws and everything is made out of plastic. Why do you need plastic? she scoffs. "When you can make the world out of amber?"
Zh: “男人的下巴很小，一切都是用塑料做的。为什么会需要塑料呢？”她嗤笑到。“当你可以用琥珀创造这个世界的时候？”

En: Snap your fingers twice.
Zh: 打两个响指。

En: *This* is Gabriel Buenguerro... She shows you the photograph in the lavish amber frame.
Zh: “*这个*就是加百列·布恩伽罗……”她把镶嵌在奢华琥珀相框的相片拿给你看。

En: Right. I have some other questions for you. Police questions. (Proceed.)
Zh: “好吧。我还有些其他问题。警方事务。”（继续。）

En: Snap your fingers in front of her face.
Zh: 在她的面前打个响指。

En: They are someone's memories, boy. She gets gruff, suddenly. "What difference does it make if it's me or not?"
Zh: “这是某人的回忆，小子。”她突然变得有些粗鲁。“是不是我，又有什么区别呢？”

En: Someone? Are these not your memories?
Zh: “某人？是你的回忆吗？”

En: A strikingly handsome man looks straight at you, his head crowned with a wide-brim hat. His hair is dark as an oil slick and his jaw is the most perfectly chiselled thing you've ever seen.
Zh: 一个帅气逼人的男人直勾勾地看着你，脑袋上戴着一顶宽檐帽。他乌黑的头发宛如浮油，如刀雕刻出来的下巴是你见过最完美的杰作。

En: Take a look.
Zh: 看一眼。

En: Sorry to interrupt your dreaming, ma'am.
Zh: “抱歉打扰你的美梦了，女士。”

En: I wasn't dreaming. I was *there*, lawman. It was early spring and 'The Man Behind The Black Sun' had just come out. The posters were twenty metres tall. Everything was golden... Her eyes narrow and she appears to take your measure.
Zh: “我没有做梦。我就在*那里*，警官。那是一个早春，*黑色太阳背后的男人*刚刚上映。海报有足足二十米高。一切都是金色的……”她眯起眼睛，好像打量着你。

En: So I take it you were in Mesque when you were young?
Zh: “这么说你年轻的时候生活在梅斯克？”

En: *Someone* was... She nods as though her meaning were perfectly clear.
Zh: “*某人*是……”她点点头，好像自己的意思已经非常清晰了。

En: She seems to derive some bitter pleasure from this strange thought. As if the past will one day wipe the present away, like a tidal wave approaching.
Zh: 她像是从这个奇怪的想法中获得了某种苦涩的快乐，就好像过去终有一天会将现在抹去，仿佛一场逐渐逼近的大海啸。

En: Why not, *xerife*? It's not like I have anything better to do in this hellhole. She settles back against the railing of her motor lorry. Behind her, mountains of memorabilia, photos, and knicknacks line the dashboard.
Zh: “为什么不呢，*治安官*？反正我在这个地狱也没有什么更好的事可以做。”她靠在货車的扶手上。在她身后，大量的收藏品、相片还有小装饰品堆放在仪表盘上。

En: They're beautiful. That is all that matters. Beautiful and true -- and they will win. They're *coming* for this, you know... She looks around. "All of this."
Zh: “它们很美。这才是最重要的。美丽而真实——而且它们会获胜。你知道吗，它们就是*为此而来*的……”她环顾四周。“所有这些。”

En: Lawman, she greets you. "You caught me at an opportune moment. This awful weather keeps me awake. You can entertain me with your questions."
Zh: “警官，”她问候了你。“你来的正是时候。这鬼天气让我无法入睡。你可以用你的那些问题给我找点乐子。”

En: She doesn't actually think you're that smart.
Zh: 她并不是真的觉得你很聪明。

En: This line of questioning is going nowhere! Try harder!
Zh: 这样提问只会一事无成！再努力一点！

En: Well, aren't you a lady?
Zh: “呃，你不是一位女士吗？”

En: I was told of a woman driver. You're the only woman here.
Zh: “别人跟我说过一个女司机。你是这里唯一的女性。”

En: The woman's malevolent cackling fills the roundabout.
Zh: 女人恶意的笑声在整个环岛回荡。

En: I'm not that either, *xerife*. Her smile dissipates. "I've gone too far from it all to remember what was between my legs. It doesn't work like that on the *long haul*."
Zh: “也不是，*治安官*。”她的笑容消失了。“我已经完全不记得双腿之间的那些事。在*长途路*上是没用的。”

En: I'm a lady like you're Gabriel Buenguerro -- only in dreams.
Zh: “我是一位女士，就像你是加百列·布恩伽罗——只有在梦里是这样的。”

En: So... you're *not* the driver everyone is terrified of?
Zh: “所以……你*不是*大家都害怕的那个司机？”

En: That's all I needed to know, thanks. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我只需要知道这些就够了，谢谢。”（总结。）

En: I'm only terrifying to small children. And to those who used to know me.
Zh: “只有小孩子才会害怕我。还有那些曾经认识我的人。”

En: Then who is the female driver I was told of?
Zh: “那别人告诉我的那个女司机是谁？”

En: How should I know? Do I look like I spend a lot of time with the other camionneurs -- sniffing around -- when I have my movies to go to...
Zh: “我怎么知道？我看起来像是会和其他司机经常搅合在一起的人吗——到处八卦——我还要去看电影呢……”

En: *O sim*... The woman stares at you, her mind elsewhere now, on other matters.
Zh: “*哦，西姆*……”女人凝视着你，思绪又飘向他方，想其他事情去了。

En: Easy. It's the skinny man who thinks he's a poet -- never trust a poet... She squints across the square. "Also, he's the only one I can see from here."
Zh: “简单。是那个瘦得皮包骨一样的男人，他觉得自己是个诗人——永远不要相信诗人……”她斜视着广场对面的方向。“而且，我在这里也只能看见他。”

En: There's something off about this woman. Tell her to show you the soles of her boots. Maybe she was at the hanging... somehow?
Zh: 这个女人有些不太对劲。让她把鞋底抬起来给你看看。也许私刑的时候……她在现场？

En: I think we're done here, no? He closes his notebook to stress it.
Zh: “我想这里已经差不多了吧？”他合上笔记本，以示强调。

En: Nothing. Her smile just keeps widening; her hair is grey like lead.
Zh: 没反应。她的笑容越来越宽；头发是铅一样的灰色。

En: Snap your fingers in front of her face.
Zh: 在她的面前打个响指。

En: The Republic of Mesque is a massive confederation on the isola of Mundi, the world's largest state by territory. It's a petrostate, a constitutional monarchy, and as of recently an outcast due to its tilt to the far right.
Zh: 梅斯克共和国是蒙迪洲上的一个大联邦国家，领土范围是世界上最大的。那里是一个盛产石油的国家，采用君主立宪制度，最近由于极右倾的倾向而被排斥。

En: A *boiadeiro* (*boia* for short) is a cow herder from upstream Magritte, the great steppes of Northern Mesque. He is a rugged individualist and explorer.
Zh: *牛仔*是指来自马格里特河上游、梅斯克北区大草原的放牛人。他们是坚定的个人主义者和探险家。

En: A *boiadeiro* (*boia* for short) is a cow herder from upstream Magritte, the great steppes of Northern Mesque. People like Mañana, at the gates, have turned it into an ideology of sorts.
Zh: *牛仔*是指来自马格里特河上游、梅斯克北方大草原的牧牛人。马列拉这样的人，就是港口大门边那个，把牛仔变成了某种意识形态。

En: Bad Hand Hermenegildo's *bad* hand strangled three hundred people. What can I say -- some people just really like strangling people.
Zh: 坏蛋埃梅内希尔多用自己那双*坏手*掐死了300个人。我还能说什么——有些人真的很喜欢把人掐死。

En: She says that as if something *narcotic* is the real reason.
Zh: 按她这么说，好像某种*麻醉剂*才是真正的原因。

En: Wait -- you get high off driving a lorry?
Zh: “等等——你开货車的时候会有很*高*的感觉吗？”

En: Oh, it's so much more than *high* where I go, xerife. It's *low* -- I go to the bottom... She closes her eyes in reverie.
Zh: “哦，我去的地方可不止是*高*了，治安官。那叫*低*——我陷入最底部了……”她闭上眼沉思着。

En: Yeah, it's definitely some kick, some *terrible* kick in the dark. A sleep kick. Perhaps you can find out later...
Zh: 是啊，这绝对是某种刺激，某种*可怕的*刺激。一种睡梦中的刺激。也许你之后搞明白……

En: Yeah, it's definitely some kick. Some *terrible* kick... the pale probably. This 'pale' sounds like it's the *shit*.
Zh: 是啊，这绝对是某种刺激，某种*可怕的*刺激……可能是灰域。这个*灰域*听起来特别*见鬼*。

En: Wait -- why is that, lieutenant?
Zh: “等等——为什么，警督？”

En: Should you drive a lorry if you get like that?
Zh: “如果变成那样，还能开货車吗？”

En: Nothing. I just don't think she's connected to anything.
Zh: “没什么。我只是觉得她跟任何事都扯不上关系。”

En: It's because of the pale. He doesn't want your frail mind caught up in it -- and this woman has spent time *in* the pale. A lot of time.
Zh: 就是因为灰域。他不希望您脆弱的心灵陷入其中——这个女人在灰域*里面*消磨过时间。大量的时间。

En: He doesn't want your frail mind caught up in something here. Something *unconnected* to the case. But connected to this woman tuning out like that.
Zh: 他不希望您脆弱的心灵被某件事物困在这里，某件与案子*无关*，却跟这个女人有关的事物。

En: He's worried. But not for her -- for you. Hmm. What is it about this woman, if she's not connected to anything?
Zh: 他在担心。但不是担心她——是担心你。嗯。如果她跟任何事情都没有关联，那她身上还有些什么呢？

En: Okay -- let's change the subject.
Zh: “好吧——我们换个话题。”

En: A pale dream, is what it is. She nods her head according to a rhythm known only to her -- staring at the photo in her hands.
Zh: “一个苍白的梦境，就是这样。”她伴随着某种只有自己能懂的节奏轻轻点头——目光凝视着手中的一张相片。

En: Oh, don't worry about me. I'm one of the best camionneurs around. I drive routes no one else will.
Zh: “哦，不用担心我。我是附近最棒的司机之一。我开的路线没人可以。”

En: What routes?
Zh: “什么路线？”

En: Lomonossov's Land. Udachnaya Zemlya. The Western Plain... She nods and closes her eyes again, letting her mind submerge...
Zh: “罗蒙诺索夫的乡下。尤达克纳亚-泽姆利亚。西部平原……”她点点头，然后再次闭上双眼，让自己的思想开始沉溺……

En: A terrible cold comes over her. Rattling her teeth, as she stares inward.
Zh: 一阵恶寒朝她扑去。她凝望着自己的内心，牙齿开始打颤。

En: The Trans-Katla Magistral. U41-A. As Estradas do Mirador. All the good ones. The deep trenches. Where the bluebirds fly. She opens her eyes again and shudders.
Zh: “横穿-卡特拉独占区。U41-A。米拉多尔路。都是好东西。深深的战壕。青鸟高飞。”她再次睁开双眼，浑身颤抖。

En: Cool. Ride until you're dust, sister.
Zh: “厉害啊。一路前行，直到你也化为尘土，姐们。”

En: Irmão... she breathes out. "I already *am* dust."
Zh: “兄弟……”她呼出一口气。“我*已经*是尘土了。”

En: She raises her boot, slowly -- with contempt -- and says. "I'm starting to think you should let me get back to Gabriel Buenguerro. You're no Gabriel..."
Zh: 她慢慢抬起靴子——眼里满是轻蔑——然后说道：“我开始觉得你应该让我回到真正的加百列·布恩伽罗身边。你不是加百列……”

En: Just before Gabriel it was the coronation of Franconegro -- now *there* was a real man... There is no aberration in the pattern that you can see. She puts her foot down.
Zh: “在加百列之前是弗兰考格斯的加冕礼——那才叫一个真男人……”你没有看到鞋底的花纹有异常。她把脚放了下来。

En: Moreover, the boots were size 37. Tiny. There are too many discrepancies in all this.
Zh: 而且，这双靴子是37码的。小了。有很多不符合的地方。

En: She's wearing sturdy worker's boots made of black leather. Buckles run across. The sole is also made of leather.
Zh: 她穿着一双黑色皮革制成的结实工人靴，上面有一个带扣。鞋底也是皮质的。

En: Now the other one please.
Zh: “现在请抬起另一只。”

En: Please? She raises her boot, slowly, with contempt. "I think you should let me get back to Gabriel Buenguerro. You're no Gabriel. Gabriel doesn't say please..."
Zh: “请？”她慢慢抬起脚，眼里满是轻蔑。“我开始觉得你应该让我回到真正的加百列·布恩伽罗身边。加百列是不会用请这个字的……”

En: Wait... by it *being* the coronation of Franconegro you mean...
Zh: “等等……你是说你*参加过*弗兰考格斯的加冕礼……”

En: Wait, what do you mean it *was* the coronation of Franconegro?
Zh: “等等，你说那*是*弗兰考格斯的加冕礼是什么意思？”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "She is not Odd Sole. These are not the boots that made the prints."
Zh: （转向金。）“那个古怪的鞋底不是她的。脚印不是这双靴子留下的。”

En: These are not the boots I was looking for.
Zh: “这不是我在找的靴子。”

En: Another discrepancy, although not boot-related, is the coronation of His Innocence Franconegro. Which happened 500 years ago.
Zh: 虽然跟鞋底没什么联系，这里又有一个矛盾，无罪之王弗兰考格斯的加冕礼。那是500年前的事。

En: Pale damage, she nods. "I was *just* holding my father's hand. He was descending the stairs in Raehl. The crowd had gone silent. There was dust in the air..."
Zh: “灰域损伤，”她点点头。“我*只是*握着父亲的手。他正走从雷赫尔的楼梯走下去。人群沉默了。空中满是灰尘……”

En: It was, she shrugs. "And then it was no more. And I was no longer holding my father's hand. He was no longer descending the stairs in Raehl. The crowd had gone silent."
Zh: “是的，”她耸耸肩。“然后就不再是了。我也不再握着父亲的手。他也不再从雷赫尔的楼梯上走下去。人群也沉默了。”

En: Perhaps it was another *xerife* who came and woke me up, looking at my boots, asking questions? Or perhaps it was one of the others in this *carnaval*. I don't remember.
Zh: “也许是其他*治安官*来唤醒我，查看我的靴子，问我问题？或者，也许这个*嘉年华*里的某个人。我不记得了。”

En: As she says *carnaval* she gestures to the empty square -- with the statue and the machines.
Zh: 当她说出*嘉年华*的时候，她朝着空无一人的广场比了个手势——那里有雕像和机器。

En: He takes a quick note. "I could have told you that from just looking at them. Her size is 37."
Zh: 他快速地记了些笔记。“我其实看一眼就能告诉你的。她的鞋码是37号。”

En: The feet of a little girl, she smiles. "They fit well on the pedals."
Zh: “一个小女孩的脚，”她笑了。“很适合脚踏板。”

En: The feet of a little girl, she smiles. "They fit well on the pedals.
Zh: “一个小女孩的脚，”她笑了。“很适合脚踏板。”

En: The... Adversary?
Yes. There is a *protagonista*... She gestures to the intersection. "And an *adversário*. I am on the side of the Adversary. There's no coming back from that haul."
Zh: “是的，有一个*主角*……”她朝着十字路口比了个手势。“而且是个*反派*。我站在反派那边。那条路是不可能回头的。”

En: The pale. Sounds like *the* drug to me.
Zh: 灰域。在我听起来像是*那种*毒品。

En: I don't like the sound of any of that. Sounds like a horrible drug. The worst one of them all.
Zh: 所有那些话我都不喜欢。听起来像是一种可怕的毒品。最糟糕的那种。

En: Those epithets are familiar somehow -- The Great Adversary, The Great Unrest.
Zh: 总觉得那些修饰词有些耳熟——大反派，大动荡。

En: Alright. Let's get you a pale-category licence and roll that doobie up!
Zh: 好吧。我们给你弄一张灰域类的许可证，然后把它卷起来抽！

En: Fine. Let's change the subject.
Zh: “好吧。我们换个话题。”

En: Good. I don't care about drugs. Little molecules. They're nothing. She glances wistfully at the photo.
Zh: “很好。我不关心毒品。小分子而已。它们什么都不是。”她凝视着相片，望眼欲穿。

En: Ma'am, you're not responsible for what the client puts in your lorry as long as the seals are not opened. We officers know that. He gives you a stern look.
Zh: “女士，只要封条没有打开，顾客往你的货車里放了什么都不是你的责任。”他严肃地看了你一眼。

En: Lay off her.
Zh: 让她休息一下。

En: That's correct. There is no visibility of any of the others.
Zh: 没错。看不见其他的。

En: Like looking into the ocean at night. In the dark.
Zh: “就像晚上看着大海。在一片漆黑之中。”

En: Yeah... it's not her. Believe me.
Zh: 是啊……不是她。相信吾辈。

En: Why are you scary to the people who used to know you?
Zh: “为什么以前认识你的人会害怕你？”

En: Because they can no longer recognize the person I once was.
Zh: “因为他们已经认不出现在的这个我了。”

En: You said *long* haul. That's...
Zh: “你说*长途*货运。那是……”

En: The big ones. The tracts. There's no women and men there, it's all just... She hums along, as if to a track on repeat...
Zh: “大家伙。高速公路。没有男人，也没有女人。全都只是……”她独自哼唱着，像是一段重复播放的乐曲……

En: 'In the middle of this town there's a ghostly motorway -- it takes all the people where they want to stay'...
Zh: “*城市中央，有一条幽灵高速路——它把所有人带向他们想要停留的地方……”

En: Something in her is pulling toward some unknown rest state. She twitches -- like a sleep kick.
Zh: 她心底某种东西牵扯着她，奔向某种未知的休息状态。她抽搐着——就像在睡梦中猛地抽动。

En: In the background, a quiet song seeps from her cabin into the air. You don't hear any vocals.
Zh: 背后，一首安静的歌曲从她的車厢飘向空中。你没有听到任何人声。

En: The routes you drive are *unusual*, aren't they.
Zh: “你开的路线很*不寻常*，是吧。”

En: Some of them. Some of them are like home to me now. She nods. "I would say the routes I drive are *usual* to me."
Zh: “有些是。有些对我来说已经像是自己的家一样。”她点点头。“要我说，这些路线对我来说很*寻常*。”

En: And what *is* that? She sticks a filterless cigarette into a cigarette holder and reaches for the light.
Zh: “那*会是*什么呢？”她把一根没有过滤嘴的香烟插进烟嘴，手伸往灯光的方向。

En: You're a paledriver. You transport goods through the pale -- whatever it is.
Zh: “你是名灰域司机。你穿越灰域运输货物——不管货物具体是什么。”

En: You're a paledriver. You transport goods through the pale.
Zh: “你是一个灰域驾驶员。你穿越灰域运送货物。”

En: Nothing. No need to be direct.
Zh: “没什么。没必要这么直接。”

En: Some jamboree you turned out to be... You have about a third of the jawline of Gabriel Buenguerro, and none of the balls. She smiles.
Zh: “结果变成大狂欢了啊……你的下颌轮廓跟加百列·布恩伽罗有1/3相像，但是没有那些球。”她笑了。

En: Great. The lieutenant concedes with a head shake. "He asked the Pines rep about the pale."
Zh: “太棒了。”警督摇摇头，退让了一步。“他找野松公司的代表问过灰域的事。”

En: You don't have to protect me from this. I'm a big boy.
Zh: “你其实不用保护我的。我是个大男孩了。”

En: I can't operate in this world without knowing its basic terms.
Zh: “要是不知道这个世界的基本术语，我就没办法在这里活动了。”

En: I'm a detective. I need to know things.
Zh: “我是个警探，我需要知道一些事情。”

En: No. I just remembered it myself. (Point to your head.) "With my head."
Zh: “不。我自己刚想起来。”（指着你的脑袋。）“用我自己的脑袋。”

En: Fine. He's not buying it. "Just try not to black out again. And don't *contemplate*. We don't have time for that."
Zh: “好吧。”他并不买账。“只要别又晕过去了。而且不要*冥思苦想*。我们没有那个时间。”

En: Fine, then. He sighs. "Just try not to black out again. And don't *contemplate*. We don't have time for that."
Zh: “那好吧。”他叹了口气。“只要别又晕过去了。而且不要*冥思苦想*。我们没有那个时间。”

En: Oh -- I'm contemplating, Kim. I'm drawing *existential conclusions* from this.
Zh: “哦——我没有冥思苦想啊，金。我只是从中得出了*存在主义的结论*。”

En: Yeah, no. Just for detective work. That's all I needed it for. Now...
Zh: “是啊，不会的。只是为了侦查工作。我现在只需要这个。现在……”

En: Everything is related to the pale. My condition. The case. Everything. It's all *entroponetic*.
Zh: “一切都跟灰域有关联。我的状态。这个案子。所有一切。全都是*灰域学说*范畴。”

En: It's death -- but for the Universe? Oh, we're contemplating the living *shit* out of this.
Zh: 这是死亡——不过是为了宇宙？哦，我们正在思考*活着*这种狗屁问题。

En: That is *exactly* what I didn't want you to think... He sighs and turns to the woman. "Ma'am, my partner wanted to know if you work in pale transport."
Zh: “我*就是*不希望你这么想……”他叹了一口气，然后转向女人。“女士，我的搭档想知道你是不是在灰域里从事运输工作。”

En: *Exactly* what I didn't want you to do... He sighs and turns to the woman. "Ma'am, my partner wanted to know if you work in pale transport."
Zh: “我*就是*不希望你想到这个问题……”他叹了一口气，然后转向女人。“女士，我的搭档想知道你是不是在灰域里从事运输工作。”

En: No offence, but *your partner*... She lights the cigarette, a white and silver cloud of smoke disappears into her mouth.
Zh: “无意冒犯，但是*你的搭档*……”她点燃香烟，一团银白色的烟雾消失在她口中。

En: That is *exactly* what I didn't want you to do... He sighs and turns to the woman. "Ma'am, my partner wanted to know if you work in pale transport."
Zh: “我*就是*不希望你这么做……”他叹了一口气，然后转向女人。“女士，我的搭档想知道你是不是在灰域里从事运输工作。”

En: ...seems like a bit of an idiot. She breathes out. The air tastes sweet.
Zh: “……有点像个白痴。”她吐出一口气。空气中弥漫着甜甜的味道。

En: *Oh meu deus*, the Lawman solved the case... She lights the cigarette. A white cloud of smoke disappears into her mouth.
Zh: “*哦，神明啊*，警官破案了……”她点燃香烟。一团银白色的烟雾消失在她的口中。

En: And here I was thinking you were an idiot. She breathes out and the air tastes sweet. "So, are you? An idiot, I mean."
Zh: “我在想你是不是一个白痴。”她吐出一口气。空气中弥漫着甜甜的味道。“所以，你是吗？我是说，一个白痴。”

En: 'República', a filterless cigarette from Mesque.
Zh: ‘共和国’，梅斯克生产的无过滤嘴香烟。

En: 'República'. (Smell the air.) "That's not very healthy."
Zh: “‘共和国’。”（闻闻空气的味道。）“这可不是很健康啊。”

En: I blacked out after a night of heavy drinking and lost all memory of the world.
Zh: “在狂饮整整一晚之后，我晕了过去，失去了这个世界的所有记忆。”

En: I blacked out -- from sheer heartbreak -- and lost all memory of the world.
Zh: “我晕过去了——因为纯粹的心酸——然后忘记这个世界的所有记忆了。”

En: Neither is pale transportation. She shrugs. "Life is transitory."
Zh: “灰域运输也不是。”她耸耸肩。“生命是短暂的。”

En: I'm not an idiot. I'm a detective of the RCM. Don't say I'm an idiot.
Zh: “我不是白痴。我是RCM的一名警探。别说我是个白痴。”

En: You *are* an idiot. And you're keeping me here. Glued to this racket. Unless there was anything else... She puts the cigarette out and stares at something in the distance.
Zh: “你*就是*个白痴。而且你把我困在这个地方。陷在这片花天酒地的生活。除非还有别的事……”她熄灭香烟，凝视着远处的某个东西。

En: Like Gabriel Buenguerro in 'Pergunte à Poeira.' She nods and smiles, unkindly. "You're the opposite of me, then. I remember everything -- even things I never knew."
Zh: “就像是《问尘》里的加百列·布恩伽罗。”她点点头，笑容并不亲切。“你看来是我的反面。我记得一切——甚至是那些我不曾知晓的事情。”

En: Like Gabriel Buenguerro in 'Segure-me, Paraíso'... She nods and smiles, unkindly. "You're the opposite of me then. I remember everything -- even the things I never knew."
Zh: “就像在‘抱紧我，天堂’里的加百列·布恩伽罗。”她点点头，笑容并不亲切。“那跟我是相反的。我记得一切——甚至是那些我从来不知道的事情。”

En: Things you never knew... so you *have* turned back time!
Zh: “你不曾知晓的事情……所以你让*时间*倒流了！”

En: Things you never knew?
Zh: “你从来不知道的事情？”

En: The smell of liquor on Gabriel's lips after the shoot. In the motor park. The roses on the day of Franconegro's coronation. On the grand stairs of Raehl. The smoke from the fowling piece, when Dolores Dei was shot...
Zh: “拍摄结束之后，加百列嘴唇上酒精的味道，他在停車场里。弗兰考格斯加冕礼那天的玫瑰花，他在雷赫尔的大楼梯上。还有德洛莉丝·黛中枪时，猎枪枪口冒出的轻烟。”

En: The look on her face -- like an orgasm. The wound in her chest. My hand in my father's hand... She closes her eyes, her eyelids trembling. "Except I never had a father. And I never shot Her Innocence Dolores Dei."
Zh: “她脸上的表情——像是达到了高潮。她胸部的伤口。握在父亲手里的我的手……”她闭上双眼，眼皮微微颤抖。“只不过我从来没有过一个父亲。而且我从没朝无罪女王德洛莉丝·黛开枪。”

En: Over-radiation?
Zh: “过度辐射？”

En: Heroic doses, *xerife*. Heroic.
Zh: “很英勇的剂量，*治安官*。很英勇。”

En: Isn't that dangerous?
Zh: “难道不危险吗？”

En: Thought insertion? *Dithering*? The Graad-Katla Magistral? She savours the lungful. "It's more than dangerous -- it's *sad*. But... at first I had to make a living. Now..."
Zh: “思维插入吗？*混色*？格拉德-卡特拉独占区？”她用香烟填满了肺部。“比危险还要多——是*悲伤*。但是……开始我只是为了谋生。现在……”

En: When you've seen it all go *away* like that, rolling off like the sea, and then come back to this... She gestures at the square. The broken horse monument, the clanging of machines in the distance.
Zh: “当你看着一切像那样*离去*。像大海一样翻滚。然后回到这里……”她朝着广场比了个手势。破碎的战马纪念碑，还有远处铿锵作响的机器。

En: What are we *doing* here? For thousands of years, Gabriel... It doesn't have to be like this. We can just give up. We can just become vapour.
Zh: “我们在这里*做*什么？4000年了，加百列……没必要这样的。我们可以就这么放弃。我们可以就这样变成蒸汽。”

En: What does it look like? The pale?
Zh: “它看起来是什么样子的？灰域？”

En: I would rather have what I have than what you have.
Zh: “我宁愿拥有自己的这一些，也不要你的那一切。”

En: I would rather have what you have than what I have.
Zh: “我宁愿拥有你的一切，也不要我的那一切。”

En: And...
You cannot see it but you know it's there. And it's big -- bigger than anything. Bigger than all the other things combined.
Zh: “你看不到它。但是你知道它就在那里。而且它很大——比任何东西都大。比所有东西加起来都要大。”

En: I feel I already *have* what you have. In some way.
Zh: “我觉得我已经*拥有*你的一切了，在某种程度上来说。”

En: What does it feel like?
Zh: “感觉什么样？”

En: How do you pass through it?
Zh: “你怎么穿过它的？”

En: In the belly of an airship, behind steel windows -- so you don't look straight into it. It's not *advised* to look into it.
Zh: “在一艘飞艇内部，钢铁窗户背后——所以你不会直接看着它。不*建议*你直接看着它。”

En: Not on this lorry then?
Zh: “那就不在这台货車上了？”

En: No -- the same one. A *roller*. They all are nowadays. Special wheels for connecting to the floor of the hold. She points to the machines, clumped up like toys.
Zh: “不——同一个。一个*滚筒*。现在都是这样的。特质的轮子，可以连接货舱地板。”她指着像玩具一样堆在一起的机器。

En: One last thing. You said we can just become... vapour?
Zh: “还有一件事。你说我们可以变成……蒸汽？”

En: Their wheels all small and round, multi-axle trailers.
Zh: 它们的轮子又小又圆，是多轴拖車。

En: Nothing. Until it starts. When you're deep enough -- then, for me... it's like autumn. Dark grey and orange, the orange of streetlights and the colour of trees in the electric light. It smells like autumn too. It smells terrible.
Zh: “没什么感觉。直到它开始。当你足够深入的时候——不过，对于我来说……它就像秋天。深灰色和橙色，街灯的橙色，还有电灯下大树的颜色。闻起来也是秋天的味道，太可怕了。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: No elaboration.
Zh: 没有细化。

En: Nostalgia. Cooped up in the cabin, shaking... Terrible nostalgia. For yourself. For humans. It's too much to bear. She loves it.
Zh: 乡愁。被禁锢在車厢里，摇晃着……可怕的乡愁。对于你自己。对于人类。它都太难承受了。她很爱它。

En: They say there is a point -- one that *I* have not crossed -- in the pale superdeep. If you stray too far off course on the U41-A, or in Lomonossov's Land... where every step you take is one step further from home, no matter the direction.
Zh: “他们说有一个点——*我*并没有经过——在灰域的最深处。如果你开着U41-A偏离路线太远……或者是在罗蒙诺索夫地带……在那里，你每走一步都会离家乡越来越远，不论什么方向。”

En: It's a point you cannot come back from. Your mind becomes so radiant with the past -- there is a flip. She flicks the ash from her cigarette. "Instead of writing, it erases memory. Nearing some kind of..." She shakes her head. "Indescribable *finale*."
Zh: “到了那个点你是回不来的。你的思想会跟过往一起变得光芒四射——会有一次弹跳。”她轻轻弹掉烟灰。“它不会书写记忆，而是擦除记忆。就像是某种……”她摇摇头。“难以言喻的*终结*。”

En: Maybe you've been down the *Motorway South?* She looks at her cigarette, it's almost out. She has swallowed it hungrily. Then at you...
Zh: “也许你去过*南高速公路*？”她看着香烟，它就快熄灭了。她饥渴地吞吐了几口。然后看着你……

En: The Motorway South?
Zh: “南高速公路？”

En: It's a story us longhaulsmen tell -- longhaulsmen, *xerife*, not *paledrivers* -- way beyond the established pale that's lit by radio frequencies. Where it goes silent.
Zh: “长途货运司机之间流传着一个故事——是长途货运司机，*治安官*，不是*灰域驾驶员*——远远超出无线电频率点亮的既定灰域范围。在那里，无线电频率也会沉默。”

En: And dark. And the process begins. Erasure. Kilometre by kilometre. In any direction. The Motorway South is a road you cannot come back from.
Zh: “而且很黑。然后那个过程开始了。记忆消除。一公里接着一公里。所有方向。南高速公路就是一条有去无回的路。”

En: What is at the end of the Motorway South?
Zh: “南高速公路的尽头有什么？”

En: No one knows what's at the *end...* She takes the cigarette out of the cigarette holder and extinguishes it. "I've only glimpsed the beginning."
Zh: “没人知道那个*尽头*有什么……”她从烟嘴中拿出香烟，然后熄灭掉。“我只是瞥到了起点。”

En: I've only felt it in the distance. When I was a child... She goes silent, her eyes close and her hands shake. "A child. Rowing on the lake."
Zh: “我只在远处感觉过它。那时我还是个孩子……”她沉默了，双眼紧闭，双手颤抖。“一个孩子。在湖里划船。”

En: Ma'am?
Zh: “女士？”

En: *Hosiannna...* A sigh escapes her lips, then silence, as she stares within herself.
Zh: “*天啦……*”一声叹息。然后沉默，她在凝望自己的内心深处。

En: Thank you. Good bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “谢谢你。再见。”[离开。]

En: 'They say I've been away on a kind of holiday'... She replies, silently.
Zh: “他们说我像是去度假了……”她默默地回复到。

En: 'It takes all the people where they want to stay.'
Zh: “它能把所有人带到他们想要停留的地方。”

En: 'In the centre of this town there's a ghostly motorway', she sang.
Zh: 城市中央有一条幽灵高速公路，她在歌唱。

En: You've fried both your brains enough for today, detective... He inspects her. No response.
Zh: “今天你们俩已经够烧脑的了，警探。”他查看着她。没有回应。

En: Let's get some air. This one's far gone... He shakes his head silently as he turns to leave.
Zh: “我们去透透气吧。这人病得太厉害……”他默默摇摇头，转身准备离开。

En: There is nothing more to do now. She's far away.
Zh: 现在没什么能做的。她已经陷得太深。

En: Don't be stupid. The boiadeiro returns from the Western Plain a *changed* man. One night, as he and his beloved are out walking along the River Magritte, she pleads with him to give up his riding and settle down...
Zh: “别傻了。牛仔从西部大平原回来之后，就好像*变了一个人*。一天晚上，他和自己的爱人沿着马格里特河边散步，她恳求他放弃骑马，稳定下来……”

En: So he doesn't think she's the lady driver?
Zh: 所以他不觉得她就是那个女司机？

En: C'mon, he thinks -- she's not the lady driver.
Zh: 拜托，他在想——她不是那个女司机。

En: A correct appraisal, you're quite shabby.
Zh: 这个评价很正确，你确实很寒碜。

En: When you've seen it all go *away* like that. Rolling off like the sea. And then come back to this... She gestures at the square: the broken horse monument, the shadows of the machines in the night.
Zh: “当你看着一切像那样*离去*。像大海一样翻滚。然后回到这里……”她朝着广场比了个手势：破碎的战马纪念碑，还有夜晚机器的阴影。

En: She is receding. In the clutches of some indescribable, scattered emotion. A child. Descending.
Zh: 她在消退。身陷一种难以言喻的凌乱情绪的控制之下。一个孩子。消退着。

En: The woman looks at nothing, her eyes closed and her lips moving.
Zh: 女人什么也没看，双眼紧闭，嘴唇蠕动。

En: Girl child, you hear her say. "Girl child. Girl child..."
Zh: “女孩，”你听见她的话了。“女孩。女孩……”

En: This man's got a hold over her. Even fifty years later, you can feel it...
Zh: 这个男人控制了她。就算在50年之后，你也能感觉到……

En: All of what -- the world? The present?
Zh: 所有什么——世界吗？现在吗？

En: What if... to truly love a boiadeiro is to float lifeless downstream?
Zh: 如果……真心爱一个牛仔，就要死气沉沉地漂浮在水中，顺流而下呢？

En: So he doesn't think she's the smuggler?
Zh: 所以他不觉得她是走私犯？

En: She clearly doesn't think she's a 'lady'. Don't repeat it.
Zh: 她显然不觉得她是一位‘女士’。不要重复了。

En: You feel very cold suddenly. As if standing face to face with a terrifying adversary. Then the feeling dissipates and all you see is an ageing woman.
Zh: 你突然感觉很冷。就好像面对面站在一个恐怖的对手面前。然后那种感觉消散了，你唯一看见的只有一个年迈的女人。

En: Great. The lieutenant concedes with a head shake. "He asked the Pines rep about the pale -- and now he's talking to everyone about it..."
Zh: “太棒了。”警督摇摇头，退让了一步。“他找野松公司的代表问过灰域的事--结果现在到处跟人讲……”

En: Yes -- go. Enough jamboree. I need to get back to Mesque... Her voice trails off.
Zh: “是的——去吧。狂欢够了。我要回到梅斯克……”她的声音越变越小。

En: So the boiadeiro strangles his beloved and throws her body in the Magritte. Then he rides off, because the Western Plain is calling to him.
Zh: “牛仔勒死了他的最爱，把她的尸体扔进马格里特河。然后他跨上马背，因为西部大平原在召唤他。”

En: The *most* beautiful -- a true boiadeiro needs a whole horizon to himself. He can't be tied down by man or woman. His beloved was selfish. She didn't know what it meant to love a *boiadeiro*.
Zh: “*最*美丽的——一个真正的牛仔渴望的是拥有一整个地平线。他不能被任何男人或女人拖累。他的爱人太自私了。她不懂得爱上一个*牛仔*意味着什么。”

En: I'm looking for Ruby, the *lady driver*. Know anything about her?
Zh: “我在找露比，那个*女司机*。你知道她吗？”

En: She cups her ear and leans forward. "Did you just call me a *lady*, xerife?"
Zh: 她竖起耳朵，向前倾身。“你刚刚叫我*女士*吗，治安官？”

En: If you want to know more about Ruby, this is a dead end. She's only heard the word 'lady' and latched onto it. You can push on if you're interested in *that*, though.
Zh: 如果你想多了解一些露比的事，这是个死胡同。她只听到*女士*这个词，而且紧紧抓住不放。不过，如果你对*那个*有兴趣的话，可以继续推进。

En: Never mind. I have other questions.
Zh: “没关系。我还有别的问题。”

En: I'm terrifying to small children. And to those who used to know me.
Zh: “小孩子都很怕我。还有那些以前认识我的人。”

En: It's time to face yourself, mano y mano. The next time you find yourself alone before a mirror. *That's* where this will end...
Zh: 是时候面对自己了，人对影。当下次你独自站在镜子前的时候，*那就是*这一切的结局……最后的摊牌。

En: This is about turning *back* time, Kim.
Zh: “说到时间*倒流*了，金。”

En: We're still on this ridiculous *side-task*, I see... He sighs and turns to the woman. "Ma'am, my partner wanted to know if you work in pale transport."
Zh: “我们还在进行这个可笑的*副任务*，我明白了……”他叹了口气，转向女人。“夫人，我的伙伴想知道你是否从事灰域运输业。”

En: Hold on, you said it *was* possible to turn back time, through the pale...
Zh: “等一下，你刚才说通过灰域*可以*让时间倒流……”

En: *Xerife*, what you understand wouldn't fill a pamphlet...
Zh: “*警视*，你所了解的东西连一张小传单都填不满……”

En: This is your chance. Ask her, is it really possible?
Zh: 你的机会来了。问她，真的可能吗？

En: You don't need to turn back time. The pale is already churning with it. As the tide of pale rises, so does the past. Someday both will cover the whole world. That's it. That's the story.
Zh: “你不需要让时间倒流，灰域一直在搅动时间。当灰域之潮涨起时，过去也在涨起。总有一天，两者会覆盖整个世界。故事结束。”

En: You're saying the future will *be* the past...
Zh: “你是说，未来将*变成*过去……”

En: You mean, we'll all be drowned in our own past...
Zh: “你的意思是，我们都会被溺亡在自己的过去中……”

En: You're saying, one day I'll be able to return to *love*...
Zh: “你是说，总有一天，我能够回归到*爱*。”

En: And that's just fine with me. I don't mind becoming vapour. I *like* vapour... She gathers a cloud of smoke in her mouth, then slowly exhales.
Zh: “而这对我来说就很好。我不介意变成一团雾汽。我*喜欢*雾汽……”她在嘴里聚起一团烟雾，然后缓缓吐出。

En: There is a name for this ideology: Entropolism. A faith in and desire to accelerate the spread of pale across the world, until humanity has reached what its adherents call the 'rest state' of humanity, the final reconciliation of past, present, and future in timeless spirit...
Zh: 这种意识形态有一个名字：重熵主义，这种意识形态信仰并渴望加速灰域在世界范围内的传播，直到人类达到其信徒所说的人类的“安息状态”，即过去、现在和未来在永恒精神中的最终和解……

En: You don't like the sound of that, chief. All the *really* good stuff is *right here*, in the present...
Zh: 你不喜欢这样的论调，老大。所有*真正*的好东西都在*此地*，在此刻……

En: You've fried both your brains enough for today, detective... He inspects her. No response.
Zh: “你俩今天已经足够烧脑了，警探……”他审视着她。对方没回话。

En: Now, I'd like to have a word with you about this *side task* of yours...
Zh: “现在，我想和你谈谈你这个*副任务*……”

En: This is all you're going to get out of her. There's nothing more for you here.
Zh: 你能从她身上获取到的信息就这么多了，没了。

En: Yes, *yes*. This is what you've been searching for...
Zh: 是的，*是的*。这就是你一直在找寻的东西……

En: It sounds... *wonderful*.
Zh: “这听起来……*很棒*。”

En: Thought insertion? *Dithering*? The Graad-Katla Magistral? She savours the lungful. "There's a kind of wonder there -- a *sad* wonder. But... at first I had to make a living. Now..."
Zh: “思想植入？*噪声处理*？格拉德-卡特拉主导的秩序？”她细细品味着肺里的香烟。“那里有一种奇迹——一种*悲哀*的奇迹。但是……我得先谋生。现在……”

En: But there *is* one more kingsman to confront.
Zh: 但*还有*一个王下骑士要面对。

En: I feel like I'm getting *really* close.
Zh: “我觉得我已经*非常*接近了。”

En: I feel like there's still a *long way* to go.
Zh: “我觉得还有*很长的路*要走。”

En: You've been saying that for a while. And yet, we only seem to be getting *further* from our actual goal...
Zh: “这句话你已经叨叨了好一阵子了。然而，我们好像只是离我们的实际目标越来越*远*……”

En: No. You see, we've already spent too much time on this nonsensical side-errand. There's no magical fascist who's going to unwind time for you...
Zh: “不。你看，我们已经在这种无厘头的歧路上花费了太多的时间。没有什么神奇的法西斯主义者会为你扭转时间……”

En: Because the problem isn't time, it's *you*, detective...
Zh: “因为问题不在于时间，而在于*你*，警探……”

En: If you're serious about this, really truly serious, then I suggest you take a long, hard look in the mirror and ask yourself what's *really* going on...
Zh: “如果你是认真的，真的真的很认真，那么我建议你好好地照照镜子，问问自己，*实际上*到底发生了什么……”

En: Now come on, let's leave this one to her reveries. He shakes his head silently as he turns to leave.
Zh: “行了，让她接着发她的呆吧。”他默默摇头，转身离开。

En: Who?
Zh: 是谁？

En: *YOU*.
Zh: *是你*。

En: It's time to face yourself, mano y mano. The next time you find yourself alone before a mirror. *That's* where this will end... The final showdown.
Zh: 是时候面对自己了，人对影。当下次你独自站在镜子前的时候，*那就是*这一切的结局……最后的摊牌。

En: B-b-but what if you don't *like* what you find there? There'll be no going back from it...
Zh: 但，但，但如果你不喜欢在那里找到的东西呢？那时候就不能再回头了……

En: Are you s-s-sure you want to do that? Remember what happened *last* time...
Zh: 你……你确定要这么做吗？还记得*上次*发生的事吗？

En: Why don't you ever listen to us. We tried to warn you that you wouldn't *like* what the mirror had to say...
Zh: 你为什么从不听我们的话？我们警告过你，你不会*喜欢*镜子会告诉你的事情的……

En: The lieutenant is right. It's the only way forward. Face yourself, kingsman!
Zh: 警督是对的。这是唯一的出路。面对自己吧，王下骑士！

En: Just a moment, detective. Before we go, I'd like a word with you about this *side task* of yours...
Zh: “等一下，警探。在我们走之前，我想和你谈谈你的这项*副业*……”

En: Stop. There's nothing else she can do for you, but this isn't the end of the line. No, there's one more kingsman to confront.
Zh: 别说了。她已经帮不了你更多了，但这不是终点。不，还有一个王下骑士要去面对。

En: But Measurehead said she knows the secret to turning back time.
Zh: “但测颅说她知道时光倒流的秘密。”

En: The lieutenant considers the slumped form for a moment. He sighs.
Zh: 警督想了想一下那个吊着的身影。他叹了口气。
