En: I don't understand why you're so set on what seems like a... *personal side-task*, but if you must, then let's get it over with...
Zh: “我不明白你为什么如此执着于看起来像是……*个人副业*的事情，但如果你非要这样做，那就让我们一起解决吧……”

En: What are you doing? This woman might be your only chance to figure out how to reverse time... Grow a pair!
Zh: 你在做什么？这个女人可能是你唯一能知道该如何逆转时间的机会了……长点心眼吧！

En: No-no-no... you'll never figure out how to turn back time if you're not honest with her. Explain what's going on with you.
Zh: 不不不……如果你不对她说实话，你永远也不会知道如何让时间倒流。解释一下你发生了什么事。

En: Whatever it is about this woman, it's connected to *time* -- she knows the way back.
Zh: 不管这个女人是什么人，都和*时间*有关……她知道回去的路。

En: Great. The lieutenant concedes with a head shake. "He's figured out the pale. And now he's talking to everyone about it..."
Zh: “这下好了。”警督摇头道，“他明白灰域是什么了，他现在要逢人就说这事了……”

En: Actually, I haven't figured anything out yet...
Zh: “事实上，我还什么都没有想明白呢……”

En: You haven't? Why are you bringing up *pale* then?
Zh: “你还没弄明白？那你为什么要提*灰域*？”

En: I blacked out -- from the love I felt for my Motherland -- and lost all memory of the world.
Zh: “我昏了过去——出于我对祖国的爱——失去了对这个世界的所有记忆。”

En: Like Gabriel Buenguerro in 'O Céu Dourado'... She nods and smiles, unkindly. "You're the opposite of me then. I remember everything -- even the things I never knew."
Zh: “就像是《金空》里的加百列·布恩伽罗。”她点点头，笑容并不亲切。“你看来是我的反面。我记得一切——甚至是那些我不曾知晓的事情。”

En: What *is* it?
Zh: “那*是*什么？”

En: Beautiful... it's beautiful.
That's the most you can get out of her -- only shapes and sensations, no dictionary definitions or scientific explanations...
Zh: 你最多能从她口中得知这么多了——只有形状和感觉，没有语义或科学上的解释……

En: If you wait long enough, it's going to be much more than just 'love'... It's going to be the entire history -- vapourised.
Zh: “如果你等待的时间足够长，你将迎来的远远不止是‘爱’……还有整个历史——汽化了的历史。”

En: This man looks like trouble. You might not want to get into this right *now*. Plenty of time later...
Zh: 这家伙看起来就很麻烦。也许你*现在*还不想搅合进去。过一段时间再说吧……

En: This man looks like trouble. You might not want to get into this right now.
Zh: 这家伙看起来就很麻烦。也许你*现在*还不想搅合进去。

En: Stop ripping into this guy. He's just really proud of being Revacholian.
Zh: “别再抨击这家伙了。他只是觉得做个瑞瓦肖人很骄傲。”

En: You could push him by asking him to show you the soles of his boots. He definitely looks like someone capable of a lynching.
Zh: 你可以再推他一把，让他把鞋底给你看看。他看起来绝对像是有能力执行私刑的人。

En: Is it yours?
Zh: “这是你的吗？”

En: It's what the kipts of Boogie Street are going for -- right under our noses. And the others too, on the radio. Heard any *chansons* lately? Heard any *mottettos* or *lieder*? No. Dominating culture is how they plan to win. They say so themselves.
Zh: “布吉街的那些吉皮特就要追求这个——就在我们的鼻子底下。其他人也是，在收音机里。最近听过*香颂*吗？听见过*经文歌*或者*歌曲集*吗？”没有。占据文化主导权就是他们计划取胜的方法，他们自己都承认了。”

En: Nope nothing. You're sure it's okay. You wriggled your way out of a tight one there. Didn't tell him to fuck off, *or* join his cause. Well done.
Zh: 没什么。你确定没事的。你摆脱了一个很麻烦的境地。没有让他滚蛋，*或者*是加入他的事业。做得好。

En: Damn. You're not gonna get more out of him on this. He's put up the Wall of Racism.
Zh: 见鬼。你已经没办法从他这里问出更多。他已经竖起了一面种族主义的高墙。

En: Relax. You've got all you can get here. He probably doesn't even *know* what he's hauling -- even if it *is* something unsavoury. So he'd remain unaccountable.
Zh: 放松。你已经得到所有想要的一切了。他可能都不*知道*自己拉的是什么——即使里面*是*一些很不讨人喜欢的东西。所以他才能继续置身事外。

En: Whoah, jackpot. *Racist* jackpot!
Zh: 哇哦，中头奖了。*种族主义*头奖！

En: CIGARETTES are a TOOL. Equip them in your HELD slot in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 香烟是一种工具。打开道具栏，可以把它们装备在你的手持栏里。

En: You smell of booze and cigarettes, what's different about narcotics?
Zh: “你闻起来有股酒精和香烟的味道。毒品有什么不同呢？”

En: You know where that shit comes from? Saramiriza, Safre, Iilmaraa. They take the money from our local junkies here and then use it to out-compete us in the manufacturing sector.
Zh: “你知道那些狗屎都是哪来的吗？萨拉米尔泽，萨弗里，伊尔玛。他们从本地毒品贩子这里捞钱，然后用这些钱在制造业上超过我们。”

En: He smells of heavy motor oils. And his breath -- of high-tar content cigarettes. Probably *Astra Whites*.
Zh: 他身上有股重机油的味道。还有他的呼吸——充满高焦油含量香烟的味道。也许是*白阿斯特拉*。

En: So you're telling us that you don't know *anything* about drug smuggling through Terminal B?
Zh: “所以你是想告诉我们，你*一点*也不知道B中转站走私毒品的事？”

En: Damn. It didn't work. You should have signalled you're a nationalist before.
Zh: 见鬼。这样没用的。你之前应该表明自己是一个民族主义者。

En: Look, I'm gonna be straight with you since you're my kin. I don't know shit. And if I did I wouldn't tell you. You're a cop, I'm not an idiot. He puffs on his cigarette.
Zh: “你看，既然我们是同族，那我就直说了。我不知道这些破事。而且就算我知道，也不会告诉你的。你是个警察。我又不是白痴。”他抽着烟。

En: The man pulls on his cigarette furiously, probably still thinking about mesquitos and ass cancer.
Zh: 男人猛地抽了一口烟，也许还在想梅蚊克和屁股癌的事。

En: I wouldn't be so sure about it -- not until we've heard what Siileng himself has to say.
Zh: “要是我就不会这么肯定了——等我们先听听席勒自己怎么说。”

En: Guess we need to pay Siileng a visit, then.
Zh: “那样的话，我们要拜访一下席勒了。”

En: I *knew* there was something off about that guy.
Zh: “我就*知道*那个家伙有点不太对劲。”

En: That's the one. His tribe are natural liars. It's in their blood... The lorryman takes a slow, satisfied drag and blows a smoke ring.
Zh: “就是他。他的宗族天生就是骗子，都已经渗透在血液里面了……”货車司机缓慢又满足地抽了一口烟，然后吐出一个烟圈。

En: That's the one, brother. His tribe are natural liars. It's in their blood. The lorryman looks at you with appreciation and understanding.
Zh: “就是他，兄弟。他的宗族天生就是骗子，都已经渗透在血液里面了。”货車司机看着你，满是欣赏和理解。

En: You *get* it.
Zh: 你*明白*了。

En: God damn right. They've been trying to fuck us out of our heritage in the name of profits. But when they try to replace us they'll regret it.
Zh: “说的真他妈的对。他们想以利益为名，想他妈的破坏我们的传统。但是等到他们想要取代我们的时候，他们就会后悔了。”

En: So you're in a carter's guild?
Zh: “所以你加入了马車夫行会？”

En: So it's a kind of union?
Zh: “所以它是某种工会吗？”

En: Hell no. It's a *guild*. Invitation only. Unions work for the rich fucks. They're basically the same. Been trying to fuck us out of our heritage in the name of profits. But you can't replace experience.
Zh: “当然不是。它是一个*行会*。只能通过邀请加入。工会是为那些有钱的混蛋工作的。他们基本上都是一个鸟样。想以利益为名破坏我们的传统。但是经验是无法取代的。”

En: (Look at the sole.)
Zh: （看看鞋底）

En: Technically, you're *stomping* on Revachol every step you take.
Zh: “从技术上讲，你每走一步都是在*践踏*瑞瓦肖。”

En: Heh. A condescending chortle. "I knew you people don't understand *poetry*."
Zh: “啊。”他的笑声很有几分居高临下的意味。“我知道你们这种人不理解*诗意*。”

En: Past the monument, down there, he waves south. "The green 'Tempo'. Now leave me the fuck alone, okay?"
Zh: “经过纪念碑，就在那下边，”他朝南边挥挥手。“绿色的‘飞驰’。现在他妈的让我一个人待着，行吗？”

En: He doesn't live in Martinaise.
Zh: 他不住在马丁内斯。

En: Oh no, not *too* happy.
Zh: 哦不，没有*太*高兴。

En: Looks like the lieutenant has a plan. Let him do this.
Zh: 看起来警督有计划了。交给他吧。

En: He's surprisingly good at this. Not bad at all... Look at him lurching.
Zh: 他还真是出奇地擅长这个。一点也不赖……看看他含沙射影套取信息的手法。

En: Be careful. This man's still got some fight in him, by the looks of it. It won't be easy break him.
Zh: 小心。从表面上看，这人还是有些战斗力的。要制服他可不太容易。

En: Now it's not a big deal for him anymore. This is how he saves face.
Zh: 现在这对他来说已经不是什么大事了。这是他保全颜面的方式。

En: If this guy's all the *Occidentals* have, well... let's just say he's not exactly ripped.
Zh: 如果*奥西登人*只有他这种货色，那么……这么说吧，他的肌肉线条也不是很明显。

En: Welcome to Revachol! the rotund man announces. He has a lit cigarette between his fingers.
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”圆胖的男人高声道。他点燃了夹在指缝中的香烟。

En: Welcome to Revachol! announces the rotund man. The remark isn't addressed to you. It’s addressed to the lieutenant.
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”圆胖的男人高声道。那句话不是冲你说的，而是冲金说的。

En: He pays you no attention. "It's men like you who keep Revachol divided, making it that much harder for everyone to climb out of this post-war limbo."
Zh: 他没有搭理你。“就是因为你这种人，瑞瓦肖才会分裂，让大家想从这个战后的深渊里爬出来变得越来越困难。”

En: C'mon, man, I just said 'Welcome to Revachol'... It's a lorry driver thing.
Zh: “拜托，老兄，我只是在说‘欢迎来到瑞瓦肖’……这是货車司机的套路。”

En: What he means is: fixation on the Revacholian *nation* makes it harder for Revachol to actually attain self-determination.
Zh: 他的意思是：货車司机对瑞瓦肖*民族*的迷恋，其实让瑞瓦肖很难达成真正的民主自觉。

En: I know exactly what you meant. You think my *kind* doesn't belong here. That I should *watch myself* and *behave*.
Zh: “我完全清楚你什么意思。你觉得我们*这种人*不属于这里。你觉得我应该*小心点儿*，*别惹麻烦*。”

En: The man bites his lip and drops the near-finished cigarette lingering between his fingers. He reaches for a new one.
Zh: 男人咬紧嘴唇，扔掉指缝中那根就快熄灭的香烟，准备再来一根新的。

En: What are you hauling?
Zh: “你拉的是什么货？”

En: Not much anymore. I'm here to pick up some cargo, but the dockworkers are on strike, so... it's a sit-and-wait-on-your-ass situation.
Zh: “不怎么拉货了。我来这儿是为了接货，不过码头工人在罢工，所以……现在的情况就只能乖乖坐下来干等着。”

En: What kind of cargo are you supposed to pick up?
Zh: “你要接的是什么货呢？”

En: Apples.
Zh: “苹果。”

En: Wait, how long has this been going on then?
Zh: “等等，罢工已经持续多久了？”

En: The strike? They've been at it for a while. A month, two months maybe? He gestures towards the lorries. "But *this* here is just the last week or so."
Zh: “罢工？已经有一段时间了。一个月，也许两个月？”他朝着货車比了个手势。“但是*这里*的情况差不多是上个星期的事。”

En: Apples is exactly the kind of thing you'd say if you had something to hide...
Zh: 如果你有什么要隐藏的话，“苹果”就是你会选择的词汇。

En: Sounds like a cover story to me.
Zh: “在我听起来像是个借口。”

En: Fair enough. I got some other questions.
Zh: “那好吧。我还有其他问题。”

En: We're on the same side here, yeah? No need to bust my balls. I'm here to pick up a load of apples. Just regular, Kojko-picked apples.\n
Zh: “我们俩是一边的，对吧？没必要开我玩笑吧。我来是为了拉一車苹果。就是那种普通的，克吉克苹果。”

En: I'm here to pick up a load of fuckin' apples, man. Just regular, Kojko-picked apples.
Zh: “我来这里是为了拉一車该死的苹果，老兄。就是那种普通的，克吉克苹果。”

En: Fine. He shrugs.
Zh: “好吧。”他耸耸肩。

En: So, lately we Occidentals have experienced an unfortunate downturn: when members of the superior race cease to believe in their innate superiority, they stop competing for resources.
Zh: “所以，最近我们奥西登人经历了一次不幸的衰退：当高贵种族的成员不再相信自己天生的优越性时，他们就不再争夺资源了。”

En: The problem? The damn kipts are showing a real good game lately, same with the mesquitos. He throws a sharp glance at Lieutenant Kitsuragi. "And the other intruder species too. They're on the precipice of cultural victory."
Zh: “问题？该死的吉皮特最近状态很好！梅蚊克也是一样的。还有其他那些入侵种族，像兔子一样大量繁殖。他们已经处在文化胜利的边缘了。”

En: The problem? The damn kipts are showing a real good game lately! Same with the mesquitos. And all the other intruder species out there, breeding like rabbits. They're on the precipice of cultural victory.
Zh: “问题？该死的吉皮特最近状态很好！梅蚊克也是一样的。还有其他那些入侵种族，像兔子一样大量繁殖。他们已经处在文化胜利的边缘了。”

En: Okay, sure, whatever. I'd really rather talk about something else.
Zh: “好吧，当然了，随便吧。我宁愿说点别的。”

En: Go right ahead. The man scratches his heavyset stomach. "You may be a cop, but that won't help you avoid the calamity to come."
Zh: “那就说吧。”男人挠了挠他那壮实的肚子。“也许你是个警察，但是这样也不能帮你避免即将到来的灾难。”

En: You just look at people and make arbitrary judgements.
Zh: “你只是看着大家，然后做出武断的评判。”

En: As arbitrary as any judgement. That doesn't make it less of a fact. We all have to use *facts*. Once you accept it, you'll gain the clarity of understanding.
Zh: “任何评判都是武断的。但这并不意味着就不是事实。我们全都要利用*现实*。一旦你接受了，你就能获得清晰的理解。”

En: Yeah, they're a big deal. My great-grandfather was a carter. Had a royal license and everything. We've tried to hold on to our privileges.
Zh: “是啊，他们可是大人物。我的曾祖父是个马車夫。有皇家执照什么之类的。我们试图保住我们的特权。”

En: And that's a privilege?
Zh: “那叫特权吗？”

En: Yeah. I think I can get down with racism.
Zh: “是啊，我想我可以接受种族主义。”

En: Good. If we, the Evening People, pull together -- we can form a bulwark against these troubled times! The man grins at you. "Root out the forces that seek to undermine the well-being of our people."
Zh: “很好。如果我们，夜间动物，齐心协力——就能组成一道壁垒，抵抗这个多事之秋！”男人朝你咧嘴笑了。“铲除那些企图破坏我们幸福的恶势力。”

En: Let me see the soles of your boots.
Zh: “让我看看你的鞋底。”

En: Hello. Who are you?
Zh: “嗨，你好吗？”

En: Oh, I'm just a simple camionneur, a father of three, and a Revachol patriot.
Zh: “哦，我还是个普通的货車司机，三个孩子的父亲，一个瑞瓦肖爱国者。”

En: Right. I'm going. [Leave,]
Zh: “回见了。”[离开。]

En: See you around. [Leave.]
Zh: “回见了。”[离开。]

En: We're done for now. [Leave.]
Zh: “暂时没事了。”[离开。]

En: Looking for something? The lorry driver recognizes you.
Zh: “在找什么吗？”货車司机认出你来了。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi's stern expression remains unchanged -- but you sense he can't be happy about this turn of events.
Zh: 曷城警督严厉的表情没有丝毫变化——但你能感觉到他对事情的走向不太高兴。

En: Yeah, I'm *not* down with this -- why don't you go fuck yourself?
Zh: “是啊，我对这个*并不*感冒——你为什么不自己滚一边去呢？”

En: Don't push your luck, runt. The man gives you a disgusted look, then turns his attention elsewhere, ignoring your presence.
Zh: “别得寸进尺，小畜生。”男人露出一个厌恶的表情，然后转移了注意力，无视你的存在。

En: It's men like you who keep Revachol divided, making it that much harder for everyone to climb out of this post-war limbo.
Zh: “就是因为你这种人，瑞瓦肖才会分裂，让大家想从这个战后的深渊里爬出来变得越来越困难。”

En: Perhaps it's good to agree? To get him talking.
Zh: 也许赞同是件好事？至少能让他开口。

En: Time to turn up the VOLUME.
Zh: 是时候调大音量了。

En: There! His voice grows smaller, as yours...
Zh: 看见没！他的声音变小了，而你的……

En: I'm gonna *fuck* you for the rest of my LIFE, you understand?
Zh: “我的余生都会*搞得*你焦头烂额，你明白了吗？”

En: I'm gonna put you in a cell with a giant kipt. You're gonna be bleeding kipt dick, you hear me?
Zh: “我要把你跟一个巨人吉皮特关进一间牢房。你会被搞到天天菊部有血的，你听见了吗？”

En: I've been to your fucking lorry. I know where it is! (Point to it.) "I'm gonna burn it down, you HEAR ME?!"
Zh: “我去找过你那辆该死的货車。我知道它在哪！”（指着它。）“我要把它给烧了，你听见了吗？！”

En: Fucking... homo cop. A globule of sweat gathers at his brow.
Zh: “去你妈的……基佬警察。”他的眉头浮现出一些小汗珠。

En: What's your name, fuckhead?! I'm gonna go on police radio sayin' this guy told Madre to FUCK OFF.
Zh: “你叫什么名字，死秃佬？！我要在警用无线电里说这家伙让马德雷去死。”

En: Look, fuck you, man! He tries to wave you off. "It's some lorry down there, green banged up thing. I don't fucking *know* who she is."
Zh: “卧槽，去死吧你！”他挥手想让你离开。“货車就在那边，绿色撞坏的那个。我他妈的*不知道*她是谁。”

En: *When* did she go away?
Zh: “她是*什么时候*离开的？”

En: *When* did she go away?
Zh: “她是*什么时候*离开的？”

En: I don't know, I don't even know her name. She just rolls with the fleet and acts like a big shot. Some dyke probably. I haven't even seen her for days.
Zh: “我不知道，我根本连她叫什么都不知道。她只是有时候跟大家一起混混时间，装出一副大人物的样子。可能是个女同性恋。我已经几天没见到她了。”

En: Where *exactly* is her lorry?
Zh: “她的货車*到底*在哪里？”

En: A small 'Tempo' by the monument, green... He turns to you. "Let's get into that lorry."
Zh: “纪念碑旁边一台小‘飞驰’，绿色的……”他转向你。“我们去看看那辆货車吧。”

En: Looks like you got his adrenaline up too.
Zh: 看来你也让他的肾上腺素升高了。

En: Why are you even trying? There is no way to work yourself into a rage without it looking *bad*. And you look bad enough without this *nasty* business.
Zh: 你为什么还要试呢？根本就没有办法能让你自己愤怒的样子看起来不*糟糕*。而且就算没有这种*下作*的生意，你看起来也已经很糟糕了。

En: Failing the last time was so demoralizing and *puzzling* that you just can't work up the rage anymore.
Zh: 上次的失败让你特别泄气和*困惑*，你再也无法机器自己的怒火了。

En: The main thing is to not overdo it. Even when you're trying to scare someone, the most important thing is: how does it look on your resume?
Zh: 关键是不能太过头。就算是想吓唬别人的时候，最重要的就是：你的履历看起来怎么样？

En: And, of course, what *social sensibilities* you're bringing to the table. Remain sensitive. People are watching.
Zh: 当然了，还有你摆上台面的*社会敏感性*。一定要保持敏感，大家都在看着呢。

En: The jig's up. I've got the goods on you.
Zh: “你完了。我已经掌握了你的把柄。”

En: Why don't you and me step outside for a little *talk*?
Zh: “要不你跟我到外面去稍微*聊两句*？”

En: Look, I'm sorry, can you just tell me what you know?
Zh: “听着，我很抱歉，能不能把你知道的都告诉我？”

En: Oh yeah? he snarls. "And what goods are those?"
Zh: “是吗？”他咆哮到。“那都是什么把柄啊？”

En: What? The lorryman erupts in contemptuous laughter. "What do you think we're doing right now, runt? We're outside, talking!"
Zh: “什么？”货車司机爆笑出声，很是轻蔑。“你觉得我们现在在做什么，小畜生？我们就在外面，聊天呢！”

En: Make me, runt. He blows a cloud of smoke right in front of your face.
Zh: “那就来啊，小畜生。”他直直地朝你脸上喷了一口烟。

En: Uh... your racism?
Zh: “呃……你种族歧视？”

En: You know... your *goods*.
Zh: “你知道的……你的*货*。”

En: Damn it... I meant.. do you want to find someplace private to... no, shit--
Zh: “该死……我的意思是……想不想找个私密的地方……不，该死——”

En: What is there to talk about? It's a racist world. I didn't make it that way. And neither did you -- and *you're* a racist too!
Zh: “有什么好说的？这本来就是个种族主义的世界。不是我让它变成这个样子的。你也不是——而且*你*也是个种族主义者！”

En: What is there to talk about? It's a racist world. I didn't make it that way. I'm just living in it -- surviving in it. That's all this is. Survival.
Zh: “有什么好说的？这本来就是个种族主义的世界。不是我让它变成这个样子的，我只是生活在这里——在这里生存。就是这么回事。生存。”

En: The lorryman raises an eyebrow. "This some kind of homo thing?"
Zh: 货車司机扬起眉头。“是跟同性恋有关的事吗？”

En: Or you could look around, find this guy's cabin. Check all the lorries. It's scary when cops are snooping in your things, no?
Zh: 或者你可以四处看看，找到这家伙的車厢。彻底搜查所有货車。警察窥探你的东西的时候，还是有些吓人的，不是吗？

En: So who does -- *you?*
Zh: “所以是谁做的——*你*吗？”

En: So who does -- the lady driver?
Zh: “所以是谁做的——女司机吗？”

En: Actually -- we do.
Zh: “其实呢——是归我们管。”

En: Just tell me who the god damn lady driver is!
Zh: “赶紧告诉我那个该死的女司机是谁！”

En: You're all bark and no bite. The real dogs are up in Jamrock. Everyone knows that.
Zh: “你只会叫，又不敢咬人。真正的狗都在加姆洛克呢。所有人都知道。”

En: You want me to get *violent* with you?
Zh: “你想让我对你使用*暴力*吗？”

En: I'm your brother, remember?
Zh: “我是你的兄弟，还记得吗？”

En: Who is the lady driver? You won't get into trouble.
Zh: “那个女司机是谁？你不会惹上麻烦的。”

En: No I'm not. I'm not coming anywhere -- and you're not taking me there. Now fuck off.
Zh: “不，我不去。我哪里都不去——而且你也带不走我。现在赶紧给我滚蛋。”

En: What? Here on the street? You won't. He shakes his head. "Now fuck off."
Zh: “什么？就在街上吗？你不会的。”他摇摇头。“现在赶紧给我滚蛋。”

En: Like fuck you are. Why's your partner a multi-ethnic rainbow man? Why'd you not arrest Siileng? Fuck off, I ain't telling you shit.
Zh: “你他妈有什么好得瑟的。为什么你的搭档是个多种族彩虹男？你为什么没有逮捕席勒？滚开，我他妈什么都不会告诉你。”

En: I'm *not* in trouble right now -- and I don't have to tell you a single thing. Now fuck off.
Zh: “我现在就*没有*麻烦——而且我什么也不用跟你说。现在赶紧给我滚蛋。”

En: The man drops the near-finished cigarette lingering between his fingers. He reaches for a new one, with a confident look.
Zh: 男人扔掉指缝中那根就快熄灭的香烟。他伸手去够一根新的，脸上自信满满。

En: A legendary -- and not in a *good* way -- crime boss from Jamrock. Controls what is probably the most powerful organised crime outfit in Revachol West.
Zh: 一个传奇人物——但不是*好*的那种——来自加姆洛克的犯罪大佬。控制了可以说是瑞瓦肖西部最强大的有组织犯罪团伙。

En: The lieutenant turns to the lorryman. "You mean *La Puta Madre*."
Zh: 警督转向货車司机。“你的意思是*马德雷教父*。”

En: The name resounds like a bell in the air -- a dark gong. You get a bad feeling about it.
Zh: 这个名字像钟声一样在空中回响——就像敲响了一面黑暗的鸣锣。你有种很不好的感觉。

En: For a moment the lorryman is silent. Then he spits on the pavement. "Yeah, him."
Zh: 货車司机沉默了一会儿。然后朝人行道吐了一口唾沫。“是啊，他。”

En: What are you doing, Kim? Let me handle it.
Zh: “你在做什么，金？让我来处理吧。”

En: The lieutenant looks at you sceptically but does not object.
Zh: 警督狐疑地看着你，不过没有反对。

En: It's not a hard job. It won't take a long time. It won't make Padre Madre *angry*. He looks at him. "But a stupid fucking racist is standing in the way, *protecting* this fucking thief..."
Zh: “这不是什么困难的工作。用不了多长时间。也不会让马德雷教父*生气*。”警督看着他。“但是不知道哪来了个愚蠢的种族主义者拦在半路上，*保护*这个该死的小偷……”

En: His eyes dart between you and the lieutenant. "I'm not scared of you -- or the mob. I'm under the protection of the Lorrymen and Carters Guild."
Zh: 他的眼睛飞快地扫过你和警督。“我不怕你——也不怕暴徒。我有货車司机和马車夫行会保护。”

En: First. You *knew* Siileng didn't do it.
Zh: “首先。你*知道*并不是席勒做的。”

En: He did *something*. He stole his employer's goods and another lorryman's job. You should be thankful for the tip. He grins -- a wide smile.
Zh: “他做了*一些事*。他偷了自己老板的货物，还有另一个货車司机的工作。你应该感激我给你的这个小道消息。”他咧开嘴笑了——很灿烂。

En: He did *something*. He stole his employer's goods and another, honest lorryman's job. Talk to him -- I don't know anything.
Zh: “他做了*一些事*。他偷了自己老板的货物，还有另一个老实货車司机的工作。去跟他聊吧——我什么也不知道。”

En: He did *something* -- he stole his employer's goods and another, honest lorryman's job. You know... His smirk turns into a suspicious grin.
Zh: “他做了*一些事*——他偷了自己老板的货物，还有另一个老实货車司机的工作。你知道的……”他从傻笑变成了多疑的讪笑。

En: He's been expecting this. He's really puffed himself up.
Zh: 他就是在期待这个。他真的很趾高气扬。

En: I don't think you're as *down* with our fight as you'd like me to believe, *brother*. Why's the saffron-man still there? Talk to him -- I don't know anything.
Zh: “我不觉得我们之间的争斗真有你想让我相信的那么*低落*，*兄弟*。为什么这个番红花男还在这里？你去跟他聊——我什么都不知道。”

En: I get it -- someone has to be the unpopular guy.
Zh: “我明白了——总得有个不受欢迎的家伙。”

En: Not the most popular topic nowadays, with the Coalition in charge and all. You might want to *change the topic* -- that is, bury your head under the sand like common sheep.
Zh: “现在有联盟国掌控什么的，已经不是最热门的话题了。你也许想*换个话题*——就是说，像普通的山羊一样，把脑袋埋到沙子底下去。”

En: *Change the topic* -- the way he says it makes it almost sound like a threat.
Zh: *换个话题*——他说这话的方式几乎像是一种威胁。

En: Not sheep -- ostriches.
Zh: 不是山羊——是鸵鸟。

En: Sheep don't bury their heads, ostriches do. You mixed up your metaphors.
Zh: “山羊不会把脑袋埋起来，鸵鸟才会。你的比喻用混了。”

En: Whatever, go back to your college class, bino.
Zh: “无所谓了，回到你的大学课堂吧，四眼仔。”

En: Actually, neither ostriches or sheep bury their heads in the sand. It's just a saying.
Zh: 其实呢，不管鸵鸟还是山羊，都不会把脑袋埋进沙子里。这只是一种说法。

En: I'm not the only one. Look, I've read *books*, he gestures with his cigarette for emphasis. "The science of racial theory has all been proved, even if some people don't want to accept it."
Zh: “我不是唯一这么说的。你看，我读过*书*，”他用香烟比划着，以示强调。“种族理论的科学性已经被证实了，就算有些人不愿意接受也一样。”

En: Got a smoke?
Zh: “有烟吗？”

En: His physique reeks of heavy motor oils. His breath smells of high-tar content cigarettes. Probably *Astra Whites*.
Zh: 他的身体散发着重机油的味道。他的呼吸是高焦油含量香烟的味道。也许是*阿斯特拉白人*。

En: That's... disarmingly direct.
Zh: “这还真是……放松警惕的直接啊。”

En: What's a camionneur? (Pretend you missed that last part.)
Zh: “货車司机是什么？”（假装你错过了最后那部分。）

En: A lorry driver. Who drives a lorry. Or is sometimes stuck in traffic jams, guarding that lorry.
Zh: “货車司机啊。开货車的。或者有时候碰上塞車，还要看守货車。”

En: Just the truth. The man shrugs. "I'm a truth-teller, above all else."
Zh: “只是说实话。”男人耸耸肩。“我是个讲真话的人，这一点高于一切。”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: It's my last one.
Zh: “这是最后一根了。”

En: You're smoking one right now.
Zh: “你现在不是正在抽吗。”

En: He's got a smoke right there, in his fucking hand! Snatch that shit out of his fat fingers! You need it after listening to his garbage.
Zh: 他不是正在抽吗，他妈的就在他手里！从他那肥胖的手指里抢过来！听他说了那么多废话，你很需要来上一根。

En: Then I'm gonna need the one you're smoking.
Zh: “那我就要你正在抽的这根。”

En: Can't have it.
Zh: “不可能。”

En: Can I get a drag of that then?
Zh: “那我能抽一口吗？”

En: You hiding smokes from me?
Zh: “你背着我藏烟？”

En: Why would I do that? I am an honest man. He puts out his cigarette and flicks the tiny stub toward the street.
Zh: “我为什么要这么做？我是个诚实的人。”他熄灭香烟，把烟头弹到街上。

En: Sorry, all done. He puts out his cigarette and flicks the tiny stub toward the street.
Zh: “抱歉，抽完了。”他熄灭香烟，把烟头弹到街上。

En: Hand it over. I'm confiscating it as evidence.
Zh: “交出来。我要作为证据没收。”

En: But I really need it.
Zh: “但是我真的需要它。”

En: Evidence of what? I haven't done anything. He puts out his cigarette and flicks the tiny stub toward the street.
Zh: “什么证据？我又没做什么。”他熄灭香烟，把烟头弹到街上。

En: Fuck, I oughtta...
Zh: “去你妈的，我要……”

En: Oughtta what, officer? The man looks at you coolly, noxious effluence wafting in the air around him.
Zh: “要什么，警官？”男人沉着地看着你，有害气体在他周围的空气中流动。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Shit.
Zh: “该死。”

En: Sorry I couldn't be of any help. He gives you an ambivalently mocking half-nod.
Zh: “抱歉，我帮不到你。”他半推半就，略带嘲讽地朝你半点了下头。

En: That's too bad. It's all done for. He puts out his cigarette and flicks the tiny stub toward the street.
Zh: “真是不好意思。已经抽完了。”他熄灭香烟，把烟头弹到街上。

En: Nothing worthwhile here. [Leave.]
Zh: “这里没什么值得调查的。”[离开。]

En: Oh, so they grow apples in... Graad?
Zh: “哦，所以他们在……格拉德种苹果吗？”

En: Yup, it's one of their main exports. They grow 'em down south Yekokataa. A beautiful place, got great scenic vistas.
Zh: “是啊，是那边的主要出口商品。他们是在南叶科卡塔种的。很漂亮的一个地方，景色很美。”

En: In fact, Yekokataa is a desolate wasteland whose name literally translates to *Zone of Ecological Catastrophe*. It features no scenic vistas and supports virtually no plant or animal life.
Zh: 事实上，叶科卡塔是一片荒凉的废土，这个名字直接翻译出来就是*生态灾难地区*。那里根本就没有优美的风景，而且几乎无法支持任何植物或者动物生活。

En: Yekokataa is not in the south and they definitely don't grow apples there.
Zh: “叶科卡塔不在南边，而且那里也绝对没有种苹果。”

En: So. Can I see the apples?
Zh: “所以，我能看看那些苹果吗？”

En: What's happening? You sound angry, Kim.
Zh: “发生什么事了？你好像很生气，金。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: I think we should all learn to respect each others opinions. (Betray your partner and smile at the lorryman in a conspiratorial manner.)
Zh: “我觉得我们都应该学会尊重他人的意见。”（背叛你的搭档，朝着货車司机露出很有阴谋的微笑。）

En: Well, I think we all learned something here. (Smile at the lorryman.)
Zh: “嗯，我想我们都学到了一些东西。”（朝货車司机微笑。）

En: Fuckin' A, Kim. I've got your back! (Point your finger pistols at the lieutenant.)\n
Zh: “去他妈的，金。我支持你！”（用你的手指手枪指向警督。）

En: Fuckin' A, Kim. I've got your back. (Give the lieutenant a punch on the shoulder.)
Zh: “去他妈的，金。我支持你！”（锤一下警督的肩膀。）

En: You keep saying 'Kojkos'. What does that actually mean?
Zh: “你一直在说*克吉克*，那是什么？”

En: Yeah, you know: Graadniks. Graadvolk. Those degenerates from Graad.
Zh: “是啊，你知道的：加兰佬。加兰斯基。格拉德的那些堕落鬼。”

En: You know: Graadniks. Graadvolk. The degenerates from Graad.
Zh: “你知道的：加兰佬。加兰斯基。格拉德的那些堕落鬼。”

En: Okay, but why the word 'Kojko'?
Zh: “好吧，但是为什么要用*克吉克*这个词？”

En: Okay, so it's an ethnic slur.
Zh: “好吧，所以是一种种族歧视。”

En: 'Cause that's what they're all called. Kojko Pitjic, Lzloslaw Kojko. Kojkowicz Someone. Low self-esteem those guys have. Mind you, they look Occidental, but they're not... *really* like us.
Zh: “因为他们的名字都叫克吉克。克吉克·皮克，拉斯罗斯罗·克吉克，克吉克维奇·什么什么。那些家伙都是低自尊心。注意，他们看起来像奥西登人，不过……*其实*跟我们并不一样。”

En: Hey, if the name and description fit by the very grace of nature, who am I to say otherwise?
Zh: “喂，如果这个名字和描述完全符合大自然的恩惠，那我又能说什么呢。”

En: Did you miss the part where I said they aren't here yet? Besides, even if I did have some I wouldn't go putting my nose in them... He looks at you with a strange glint in his eyes.
Zh: “你错过我说它们不在这里的部分了吗？再说了，就算我确实有点，我也不会去管它们的闲事……”他看着你，眼睛里闪烁着奇怪的光芒。

En: Yeah? Says who?
Zh: “是吗？谁说的？”

En: It's literally in the name: *Zone of Ecological Catastrophe*.
Zh: “字面上就是这个意思：*生态灾难地区*。”

En: The lorry driver glares at you intently, then shrugs. "Then I guess they grow apples somewhere else. You can never really tell with those Kojkos. They're *everywhere* -- except their own homes."
Zh: 货車司机专注地看着你，然后耸耸肩。“那我猜他们是在别处种的这些苹果吧。那些克吉克，谁能说得准呢，他们*无处不在*——唯独不在自己家。”

En: Apples then. From Graad. Right. Sure.
Zh: “那就是苹果吧。来自格拉德的。没错。当然了。”

En: Apples. He puts out his cigarette and flicks the stub across the street.
Zh: “苹果。”他熄灭香烟，把烟头弹到街上。

En: The lieutenant exhales and resumes his regular calmness.
Zh: 警督吐出一口气，恢复了平日的镇静。

En: To be honest, I can't really remember what that means. Please explain.
Zh: “老实说，我真的想不起来是什么意思了。请解释一下。”

En: He means the people living in Graad.
Zh: “他的意思是生活在格拉德的人。”

En: On second thought, let's talk about something else.
Zh: “仔细一想，我们还是说点别的吧。”

En: Suit yourself, officer. Hide your face in the sand if you so wish. He spits on the ground.
Zh: “随便你，警官。如果你希望的话，那就假装没有这回事吧。”他朝地上吐了一口痰。

En: Trusting street thugs with their goods is going to fuck 'em right up the ass, mark my words. Generations of practice ain't no laughing matter.
Zh: “把货物托付给街头混混一定会搞死他们的，记住我说的话。几代人的实践可不是闹着玩的。”

En: I did see one lorry with the trailer doors open on my way here. Do you know what happened?
Zh: “我来这里的路上，确实看见有辆货車的拖斗门是开着的。你知道出什么事了吗？”

En: If you all were at the Whirling, how do you know it was *kipts* who broke into the lorry?
Zh: “如果你们都在飞旋旅社，那你怎么知道是*吉皮特*弄坏货車的呢？”

En: Bad for him, I guess.
Zh: “我猜，对他来说不太好吧。”

En: The man shakes his head, dismayed -- furiously pulling on his cigarette: "It's a fuckin' travesty, is what it is."
Zh: 男人摇摇头，有些担心——他猛地抽了一口烟：“就他妈是个滑稽的模仿，就是这么回事。”

En: Serves him right.
Zh: “他罪有应得。”

En: Sure fucking is! We have a guild and everything... very ancient, very prestigious.
Zh: “当然他妈的是了！我们有个行会，还有一切……很古老，很受尊重。”

En: You're a Revacholian patriot? What's that all about?
Zh: “你是瑞瓦肖爱国者？究竟怎么一回事？”

En: What was that *argument* all about?
Zh: “你们到底在*争论*什么？”

En: It's about... biological determinism. Natural law. The sorting of the races. He spits on the ground.
Zh: “这跟……生物决定论有关。自然法则。种族分类。”他朝地上吐了一口痰。

En: People who've studied these things say that *you* and *me* are superior by design. He glances at the lieutenant. "So, naturally, we Occidentals should be in charge. Obviously you can see the merits in that."
Zh: “研究这些东西的人说*你*和*我*生来就是高人一等的。”他瞥了金一眼。“所以，我们奥西登人自然就应该掌管一切。显然你能理解其中的智慧。”

En: People who've studied these things say that we are superior by design. So, naturally, we Occidentals should be in charge. Obviously you can see the merits in that.
Zh: “研究这些东西的人说我们生来就是高人一等的。所以，我们奥西登人自然就应该掌管一切。显然能看到里面的优点吧。”

En: Oh, so you're just a racist. Makes sense.
Zh: “哦，所以你是个种族主义者。这就说得通了。”

En: I'm not *just* racist. Look, I've read *books*, he gestures with his cigarette for emphasis. "The science of racial theory has all been proved, even if some people don't want to accept it."
Zh: “我不*仅仅*是种族主义。你看，我会看*书*，”他用香烟比划着，以示强调。“种族理论的科学性已经被证实了，就算有些人不愿意接受也一样。”

En: Okay, what else have you thought up?
Zh: “好吧，你还想到了什么？”

En: Yes, I can really tell you're a prime example of superior design.
Zh: “是的，我真的可以看出来，你就是高人一等的最佳范例。”

En: Open your eyes. Haven't you noticed something different lately? An unfortunate downturn, maybe? When members of the superior race cease to believe in their innate superiority, they stop competing for resources.
Zh: “睁大你的眼睛吧。难道你没注意到最近有什么不同了？一次不幸的衰退，也许？当高贵种族的成员不再相信自己天生的优越性时，他们就不再争夺资源了。”

En: Yeah? What's the problem with that then?
Zh: “是吗？那又有什么问题呢？”

En: Okay... and?
Zh: “好吧……然后呢？”

En: Right. I've already made up my mind. Wanna hear it? (Jump ahead.)
Zh: “好吧。我已经下定决心了。你想听听吗？”（抢先开口。）

En: Oh? The man seems mildly disappointed that you don't want to hear more of his lecture.
Zh: “哦？”你不想再多听一下他的演讲，让他稍微有些失望。

En: This concerns you, policeman, so you better be *vigilant*. The damn kipts are showing up good lately, same with the mesquitos. And the other intruder species too. He throws a sharp glance at Lieutenant Kitsuragi. "They're on the precipice of cultural victory."
Zh: “这也跟你有关，警官，所以最好*警惕*一点。吉皮特最近繁殖得很快，梅蚊克也是，还有其他那些入侵种族，像兔子一样大量下崽。”他警惕地瞥了一眼曷城警督，“他们已经处在文化胜利的边缘了。”

En: This concerns you, policeman, so you better be *vigilant*. The kipts are showing a good game lately, same with the mesquitos. And the other intruder species out there, breeding like rabbits. They're on the precipice of cultural victory.
Zh: “这也跟你有关，警官，所以最好*警惕*一点。该死的吉皮特最近状态很好！梅蚊克也是一样的。还有其他那些入侵种族，像兔子一样大量繁殖。他们已经处在文化胜利的边缘了。”

En: It's true. He pushes on. "Also, you need to realize the dangers of *mixing races*. Who knows what might happen if people don't stay in their birthplace? You might end up with a new sub-race, with unknown characteristics, leading to extra competition. That's why you've got to *control* the offspring."
Zh: “是真的。”他没有停下来的意思。“而且，你必须意识到*种族混合*的危险性。如果大家都不待在自己的地方，谁知道会发生什么？新的人类亚种可能会被创造出来，拥有未知的特征，引起额外的竞争。所以你必须要*控制*自己的后代。”

En: Cool, cool, cool. (Rub your hands together.) I changed my mind anyway.
Zh: “牛，你牛。（搓搓手。）反正我已经改变主意了。”

En: I bet. His cool gaze pierces deep, with noxious effluence wafting in the surrounding air.
Zh: “我猜是吧。”男人沉着的目光穿透你的内心深处，有害气体在他周围的空气中流动。

En: I salute you, compatriot. Defenders of the Evening Lands have to stick together. He takes out a pack rolled up inside his sleeve. "Light up, brother. Ward off those jungle spirits."
Zh: “向你致意，同胞。夜之国的守护者必须团结一致。”他拿出一包卷在袖子里的香烟。“点火吧，兄弟。挡住那些丛林精灵。”

En: Aces! Good to know the law's got our backs. He gives a quick two-finger salute. "For Revachol, the Suzerain! The world is our Suzerainty."
Zh: “太棒了！有法律为我们撑腰太好了。”他用两只手指头飞快地向你致敬。“为了瑞瓦肖，伟大的宗主国！整个世界都属于我们的宗主国。”

En: Right. You're sure it's him and not, say, a *lady driver*?
Zh: “好吧。你确定是他，而且不是什么，*女司机*？”

En: He laughs. Nervously. "Sure I'm sure," he says. "His tribe are natural liars. It's in their blood..."
Zh: 他笑了。有些紧张。“当然，当然了，”他说道。“他们那种人天生就是骗子。这是他们的天性……”

En: No, it's Ruby running this ring like Titus alluded to. But play along for now. Find out what everyone knows first. Siileng is your next lead.
Zh: 不，提图斯稍微提过一点，是露比在操纵团伙。不过目前还是要合作的。先掌握大家了解的情况。席勒就是下一条线索。

En: Right. You're sure it's this *Siileng* and not, say, a *lady driver*?
Zh: “好吧。你确定是这个*席勒*，而且不是什么，*女司机*？”

En: No, it's Ruby running this ring like Titus alluded to. But play along for now. Find out what everyone knows first. Siileng is your next lead.
Zh: 不，提图斯稍微提过一点，是露比在操纵团伙。不过目前还是要合作的。先掌握大家了解的情况。席勒就是下一条线索。

En: He laughs. Nervously. "No, it's, uh -- you know, the Samaran guy who likes to pretend he's some kinda businessman? He's just selling his employer's stuff. Stuff he *stole* after he broke the seals on his Humanox lorry."
Zh: 他笑了。有些紧张。“不，其实，呃——你知道的，那个喜欢假装成商人的萨马拉人？他只是在偷卖雇主的东西。在他撕破纽曼奥克斯货車封条的之后，从里面*偷*出来的东西。”

En: I'm trying to turn back time. Can you help me, bröther?
Zh: “我想让时间倒流。你能帮我吗，兄弟？”

En: A sudden vibration passes through your small intestine. Like a buzzer.
Zh: 你的小肠一阵突如其来的震动，就像蜂鸣警报器一样。

En: It's calling you out onto the field! This man, *he's on your team*.
Zh: 这是在教练在叫你上场！这个人，*他是和你一队的*。

En: Remember, bröther, you say the hard things -- the gööd things. Most folk haven't got the stomach for it.
Zh: 记住，兄弟，你的话虽难听——但都是好话。大多数人都没这个肚量。

En: But this guy does. He's a bulwark, a Defender of the Evening Lands. He may know how to turn back time...
Zh: 但这个人有。他是个堡垒，是夜之国的卫士。他也许知道如何让时间倒流……

En: Why would you tell a *bröther* off like that? He *clearly* knows things! Probably knows how to turn back time too. Try to get back on his good side.
Zh: 你为什么要这样对一个*兄弟*说话？他显然知道点什么！他可能也知道如何让时间倒流。试着回到他那好的一面。

En: Sure, sure, but that guy -- you wouldn't tell it by his looks, but he is *definitely* a bröther and a kingsman. Ask if he knows how to turn back time!
Zh: 当然，当然，但是那个家伙——你从他的外表上看不出来，但他绝对是个兄弟和王下骑士。问问他是否知道如何让时间倒流！

En: Uh, welcome, indeed. Who are you?
Zh: “呃，确实挺欢迎的。你是谁？”

En: You tell him.
Zh: “说得好！”

En: Hey, I know Revachol! That's where we are.
Zh: “喂，我知道瑞瓦肖！我们就在这个地方。”

En: What's a... *Revachol*?
Zh: “*瑞瓦肖*……是什么？”

En: Never mind, let's keep moving. [Leave.]
Zh: “不要紧，我们继续前进吧。”[离开。]

En: Why are you addressing my partner like that?
Zh: “你为什么要那样针对我的搭档？”

En: Usually he says it with derision, but to you he seems to say it as an actual greeting.
Zh: 通常他说这话的时候都带着嘲讽的意味，但对于你，他好像是真心实意地在问候。

En: The lieutenant's impassive mask has been replaced with intensity. He speaks not as a cop, but as a citizen. He is *Vacholiere*. A Revacholian.
Zh: 警督那张冷漠的面具被强烈的情感替代了。他不是以一个警察，而是以一个市民的口吻在说话。他是一个*瓦科莱*，一个瑞瓦肖人。

En: He's right. You're undermining our best shot at real self-determination.
Zh: “他说得对。你在破坏我们实现真正人民自决的最佳机会。”

En: So we *are* in a limbo!
Zh: “所以我们*是*在一个深渊里！”

En: Limbo? What's going on here?
Zh: “深渊？这里出什么事了？”

En: Your partner needs backup. Now's your moment to shine!
Zh: 你的搭档需要支援。现在轮到你闪亮登场了！

En: Silence. The air between them becomes tense.
Zh: 沉默。他们两人之间的氛围变得很紧张。

En: You sense slight embarrassment for the outburst. Or is it pride? Impossible to tell.
Zh: 你为爆发感觉到有一丝尴尬。或者这是一种骄傲？不太好说明白。

En: He is comfortable reciting these thoughts. He's spent quite a lot of time meditating on the subject.
Zh: 他很乐意详述这些想法。他花了很多时间来思考这个话题。

En: Race is a simplistic means of social classification based on easily observed attributes, hunches and pseudo-science.
Zh: 种族是社会分层的一种过分简单化的手段，主要是基于易于观察到的特征、直觉和伪科学的。

En: What happened? Did your balls shrivel up? You had plenty of chances to get off this road, but no, you let this guy keep talking. Then when the time came to make your choice you tried to split the difference. Weak.
Zh: 发生什么事了？你的胆量哪里去了？你有很多机会可以远离危险的道路，但你没有，你还让这个家伙说个不停。当你需要做出选择的时候，你却企图找个折中的办法。弱鸡。

En: Not a lot of moral fibre on this one yet, he thinks. Wriggled out of it.
Zh: 他想到，这家伙没有多少道德观念。他回避了。

En: Hold on, what's a carter?
Zh: “等一下，马車夫是什么？”

En: Someone with a cart, what did you think it was? That's how deep into history our thing reaches -- before machines.
Zh: “有马車的人啊，要不你以为是什么？你看我们的历史就是这么的深厚——比机器还早呢。”

En: One says it's apples, the other diamonds -- c'mon, what are you *really* hauling?
Zh: “有人说是苹果，其他人又说是钻石——拜托，你*到底*拉的是什么货？”

En: Apples?
Zh: “苹果？”

En: Yeah, apples. I'm an apple-carter. I take it you had other questions?
Zh: “是啊，苹果。我是个苹果車夫。你不是还有别的问题吗？”

En: Yeah, apples... I take it you had other questions?
Zh: “是啊，苹果……你不是还有别的问题吗？”

En: He's given you the run-around. Let's be honest, you were *bested*.
Zh: 他在找借口。说实话吧，你被*打败*了。

En: Welcome to Revachol! The lorry driver recognizes you. "Got rid of the other one, eh? Good call."
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”货車司机认出了你。“甩掉另外那货了？干得好。”

En: Welcome to Re... The fat man stops mid-sentence as he notices Lieutenant Kitsuragi.
Zh: “欢迎来到……”胖男人注意到曷城警督，话说了一半就停住了。

En: You mean my partner, Lieutenant Kitsuragi?
Zh: “你是说我的搭档，曷城警督？”

En: That's no way to talk about an officer of the law.
Zh: “这么说一个执法人员是不对的。”

En: Ye... no... never mind, officer. Pleased to meet you. I'm a camionneur, a father of three, and a Revacholian patriot.
Zh: “是啊……不……无所谓了，警官。很高兴见到你。我是一个货車司机，三个孩子的父亲，一个瑞瓦肖爱国者。”

En: Yes, sir. He gives you a tiny mock salute. "Pleased to meet you, officer. I'm a camionneur, a father of three, and a Revacholian patriot."
Zh: “尊敬，长官。”他假装向你精力。“很高兴见到你。我是一个货車司机，三个孩子的父亲，一个瑞瓦肖爱国者。”

En: Do continue, *sir*. The lieutenant steps up to him again, his frame tense as a coiled spring.
Zh: “请继续吧，*先生*。”金再次靠近他，身体像弹簧一样紧绷。

En: C'mon, man, the holler... It's just a greeting. A lorry driver thing.
Zh: “拜托，老兄，那句话……只是打个招呼。货車司机那一套。”

En: Oh, so we're *in* Revachol.
Zh: “哦，所以我们是*在*瑞瓦肖啊。”

En: Cultural victory? What is this then?
Zh: 文化胜利？这又是什么意思？“

En: Wait, what's this *cultural victory*?
Zh: “等等，这个*文化胜利*是什么意思？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Uh-huh...
'Kojko' may be another derogative. For a person from Graad, you think.
Zh: 克吉克可能又是个歧视性词语。你觉得可能是指格拉德的人。

En: Yeah, alright. I wasn't really that interested in your cargo anyway.
Zh: “是啊，好吧。反正我对你的货物也不是很感兴趣。”

En: Actually, I'm not that interested in your cargo.
Zh: “其实呢，我对你的货物并没有很感兴趣。”

En: You're lorryman, right? What's your stance on drugs?
Zh: “你是个货車司机，对吧？你对毒品有什么看法？”

En: I know you've been giving me the run-around. Fess up, where's the lady driver?
Zh: “我知道你在跟我兜圈子。爽快点坦白吧，那个女司机在哪？”

En: If it's not you, then who's running drugs through Terminal B?
Zh: “如果不是你，那是谁在B中转站运毒？”

En: Been admiring the stompers, runt? He grins. "Sure, I can *demonstrate* them." He lifts up to show you his left foot, then the right.\n
Zh: “想欣赏践踏者吗，小畜生？”他咧开嘴笑了。“当然，我不介意*展示*一下。”他先抬起左脚，然后是右脚。

En: Been admiring the stompers, eh? He grins. "Sure thing, check 'em out." He lifts his left foot, then the right.
Zh: “想欣赏践踏者，是吗？”他咧开嘴笑了。“当然了，你看吧。”他先抬起左脚，然后是右脚。

En: The wind howls over the Bay of Revachol -- a cascade of cold air, flowing through the city streets. Those same streets have left their share of scuff marks on the underside of your green snakeskin shoes.
Zh: 风咆哮着吹过瑞瓦肖海湾——带来一连串冷空气，在城市的街道上流窜。正是那些街道在你绿色的蛇皮鞋底上留下了磨损的痕迹。

En: The wind howls over the Bay of Revachol -- a cascade of cold air, flowing through the tangled city streets.
Zh: 风咆哮着吹过瑞瓦肖海湾——带来一连串冷空气，在城市的街道上流窜。

En: On the bottom of the man's boots you see an intricate tangle of treads with no immediately discernible pattern.
Zh: 在男人的靴子底部，你看见一块复杂混乱的底板，上面带着无法一眼就能清晰辨别的花纹。

En: Is that... a map of Revachol?
Zh: “那是……瑞瓦肖地图吗？”

En: What's up with your soles?
Zh: “你的鞋底怎么了？”

En: Hm, thanks. Not the ones I'm looking for.
Zh: “嗯，谢了。不是我要找的。”

En: It's Revachol!
Zh: “是瑞瓦肖！”

En: It sure is!
Zh: “当然是了！”

En: Where did you get boots like that?
Zh: “你从哪里搞到这种靴子的？”

En: But...why?
Zh: “但是……为什么呢？”

En: Custom-made. Cost me a pretty penny! He rubs the back of his balding head.
Zh: “定制的。花了我不少钱呢！”他摸了摸光秃秃的后脑勺。

En: When the next wave of the Invasion comes -- the last thing they'll see before the lights go out is *illustrious Revachol*.
Zh: “等到下一波入侵来临——他们在灯光熄灭前看到的最后一副光景，将会是*光辉耀眼的瑞瓦肖*。”

En: Welcome to Revachol. I like it.
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖，我喜欢这句话。”

En: You do understand you're *stomping on Revachol* every day, don't you?
Zh: “你能理解自己每天都在*践踏瑞瓦肖*，对吧？”

En: What is this *Invasion* anyway? Sounds like more immigrant-bashing.
Zh: “话说*入侵*到底是什么？听起来更像是对移民的抨击。”

En: For when the Invasion comes... He glances at the lieutenant. "The last thing they'll see before the lights go out is *illustrious Revachol*."
Zh: “因为等到入侵来临时……”他瞥了一眼警督。“他们在灯光熄灭前看到的最后一副光景，将会是*辉煌的瑞瓦肖*。”

En: It doesn't look like the lieutenant cares. He just makes a little note.
Zh: 看起来警督并不在乎。他只是稍微做了点笔记。

En: Whatever, these aren't the boots I'm looking for anyway.
Zh: “随便吧，反正这也不是我们要找的靴子。”

En: Hey man, you know. There are all sorts of Invasions...  He looks at his boots, then you. "I thought we could rely on the cops. We're in this together, whether you realize it or not."
Zh: “喂，老兄，你知道吗。有各种各样的入侵……”他先是看看靴子，然后看着你。“我觉得我们可以依赖警察。我们都是一起的，不管你有没有意识到。”

En: No. It's not like that. He looks at his shoes. "You don't get it, it's... when I kick them you know? Fuck it. It's too complex for you."
Zh: “不，不是这样的。”他看着自己的鞋子。“你没明白，是这样的……当我用脚去踹他们的时候，你知道了吧？对于你来说可能太复杂了。”

En: You'll see when the time comes, officer, he grins and caresses the side of his boot. "*True* patriots carry Revachol in their very soles."
Zh: “等到那个时刻来临，你会明白的，警官，”他咧嘴笑了，然后抚摸了一下靴子的侧面。“*真正*的爱国者会把瑞瓦肖印在每一只鞋底上。”

En: Drugs? They're shit, man. I don't let anything pollute my body. He takes a long drag on his cigarette.
Zh: “毒品？那玩意就是屎，老兄。我不会让那些东西污染我的身体。”他深深的吸了一口手中的香烟。

En: So, I take it you're *not* smuggling drugs out of Martinaise?
Zh: “所以，我可以理解成你*没有*在马丁内斯走私毒品了？”

En: So you're telling me that you don't know *anything* about drug smuggling through Terminal B?
Zh: “所以你是想告诉我，你*一点*也不知道B中转站走私毒品的事？”

En: I don't know shit, and if I did I wouldn't tell you. He puffs on his cigarette.
Zh: “那种破事我不知道，就算我知道也不会告诉你。”他吸了一口烟。

En: Then what are you still hanging around here for? Most other camionneurs have left.
Zh: “那你为什么还要在这里闲逛？大部分货車司机都已经离开了。”

En: So why are you still hanging around here? Most of the other lorrymen have left.
Zh: “那你为什么还要在这里闲逛？很多别的司机都已经离开了。”

En: What do you think? I can't leave the lorry unguarded. Stuff's been getting looted lately. It's those little kipts sneaking around at night... If they touch my stuff, the bosses will be on my ass like ass cancer.
Zh: “你想什么呢？货車可不能没人看守。最近有很多人抢东西的。那些小吉皮特喜欢在晚上偷鸡摸狗……如果货被他们糟蹋了，老板会给我好看的。”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不呢？”

En: Isn't it obvious? Fuckin' Siileng, that beady-eyed South-Samaran. He spits on the ground. "His little side business is a scam. I wouldn't be surprised if he was peddling drugs as well."
Zh: “难道还不明显吗？该死的席勒，那个贪婪的南萨马拉人。”他朝地上吐了口唾沫。“他的副业就是个骗局。如果说他贩毒的话，我一点也不会觉得惊讶。”

En: I ain't saying nothing.
Zh: “我什么都没说。”

En: Men like this only respect two things: strength and fear.
Zh: 这种男人只尊重两种东西：力量和恐惧。

En: I don't go for that foreign piss. Proper booze is made in Mundi, or sometimes I like a dram of that Yugo-Graad vodka. Kojko's ain't worth much, but they do know booze.
Zh: “我不喜欢那些外国垃圾。好酒都是蒙迪产的，有时候我喜欢来上一点南格拉德的伏特加。克吉克不值几个钱，但是他们很懂怎么酿酒。”

En: Hold on, but they make Al Gul -- alcohol -- in IIlmaraa too. I was told they do.
Zh: “等一下，但是伊尔玛也会生产阿尔古——也就是酒啊。有人告诉我是这样的。”

En: They know they can't beat us in a fair fight, so they have to get us to weaken ourselves somehow. It's racial sabotage. Racial *economic* sabotage.
Zh: “他们知道公平竞争肯定打不赢，所以就只能想办法让我们自己变得衰弱。这是种族破坏。种族*经济*破坏。”

En: Not in, not out. I'll never betray the purity of my tribe.
Zh: “不进也不出。我永远也不会背叛自己宗族的纯粹性。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: So you *are* saying something.
Zh: “但是你*确实*说了什么。”

En: Just tell me which one's her lorry!
Zh: “告诉我她的货車在哪！”

En: If you don't know, I ain't telling you shit.
Zh: “如果你不知道，我他妈什么也不会告诉你的。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Isn't saying nothing saying something, or did I just *blow your mind*?
Zh: 什么也不说不就代表说了什么吗，或者我只是让你*意乱情迷*了？

En: It's a double negative. Not saying nothing means you're saying something.
Zh: “这是双重否定。什么都没说意味着你什么都说了。”

En: Never mind. Just answer the question.
Zh: “不要紧。回答问题就行。”

En: How about you fuck off? I ain't saying *anything*.
Zh: “不如你他妈赶紧滚蛋？我*什么*也不会说的。”

En: Stop now. Just go away, ask that Tommy or something. You have to find out who she is and you can't do it like this. It's too much.
Zh: 现在停下来。直接走开，问问那个汤米或者别人。你必须查清她是谁，而且你现在这样是不行的。太过了。

En: Show me her lorry right *fucking* now. The lady driver's lorry -- WHERE IS IT?!
Zh: “你*他妈的*，现在就把她的货車指给我看。那个女司机的货車——在哪里？！”

En: Make him tell you what he knows.
Zh: 让他把知道的都告诉你。

En: Occidental alliance.
Zh: 奥西登同盟。

En: Go fuck yourself.
Zh: 去你妈的。

En: Kim's bluff.
Zh: 金在虚张声势。

En: Saw you snooping cabin.
Zh: 看过你的車厢。

En: Icosahedral Dice Set "Sirens".
Zh: 二十面体骰子“塞壬”。

En: Dresscode: RCM official.
Zh: 着装要求：RCM警官。

En: Dresscode: Ceramic knight.
Zh: 着装要求：陶瓷骑士。

En: The stench is getting unbearable. He turns to you. "Is there a reason you're bothering me?"
Zh: “臭味真是越来越让人受不了了。”他转向你。“你是为什么来打扰我的？”

En: Okay, I don't want to deal with this. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，我不想处理这种事。”[离开。]

En: Now that that's settled, we have a couple of questions.
Zh: “这个问题解决了，我们还有其他问题。”

En: Whatever you say, *officers*. He waits impassively, cigarette smouldering between his fingers.
Zh: “随便你们怎么说吧，*警官们*。”他冷漠地等候着，香烟在手指之间燃烧。

En: I haven't learned anything I didn't know before. The lorryman shakes his head with indignation.
Zh: “我没学到什么之前不知道的事。”货車司机愤怒地摇摇头。

En: You two make a cute couple, you know that? The lorryman spits.
Zh: “你们俩还真是天生一对呢，你知道吗？”货車司机吐了口口水。

En: Yeah, I drive a lorry. Back and forth. Sometimes I sit stuck in a traffic jam, guarding that lorry.
Zh: “是啊，我开货車。来来回回。有时候碰上堵車，就要守着货車。”

En: Oh, so you drive a lorry? (Pretend you missed that last part.)
Zh: “哦，所以你是开货車的？”（假装自己错过了最后一部分。）

En: Hello, compatriot! He recognizes you. "You have something to discuss?"
Zh: “你好啊，同胞！”他认出了你。“有什么要讨论的吗？”

En: Looking for something, runt? Come to tell me to fuck off again?
Zh: “在找什么吗，小畜生？又来叫我滚蛋了？”

En: I've told you everything I've got. He looks around nervously. "Can you please stop talking to me now?"
Zh: “我知道的都告诉你了。”他紧张地看着周围。“现在你能别跟我说话了吗？”

En: I found this mug in the trash. (Show it to the man.) "Yours?"
Zh: “我在垃圾堆里找到这个马克杯。”（把东西拿给男人看。）“你的吗？”

En: He stares at the Yellow Man mug, then suddenly... he explodes: "HAHAHA! HAHAHA! Oh man! Oh... man, that's great. Look at that guy go. Haven't seen anything that funny in a while."
Zh: 他盯着那个黄种人马克杯，然后突然之间……他爆发了：“哈哈哈哈哈！哈哈哈哈哈！我的天啦！哦……天啦，真是太妙了。看看这个家伙。很久没见过这么有意思的东西了。”

En: God damn... He wipes the tears from his eyes. "Thanks for that. But no, it's not mine."
Zh: “该死……”他擦去眼角的泪水。“谢谢你。不过不是，这不是我的。”

En: The lieutenant tries to remain impassive but the slight arch of his eyebrow tells you that he's liking it.
Zh: 警督试图保持冷漠，但是眉毛那轻微上扬的弧度告诉你，他喜欢这样。

En: There's your in. Take his side in this particular fight.
Zh: 你应该加入。在这场特殊的战斗中，你要站在他这一边。

En: Listen, I agree. It's our responsibility to keep this poison off the streets of Revachol, right?
Zh: “听着，我同意。我们的责任就是不要让这种毒品进入瑞瓦肖的街道，对吧？”

En: He eyes you warily, unsure how to respond. This goes on for about two seconds, then...
Zh: 他警惕地看着你，不确定应该怎么回应。这种状况持续了大概2秒，然后……

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi's stern expression remains unchanged -- but you sense something there. Below...
Zh: 曷城警督严肃的表情一成不变——不过你感觉到了什么。就在外表下面……

En: That's all I needed to know. Now let me ask you something else.
Zh: “我只需要知道这些就够了。现在我想问点别的。”

En: Oh, alright then.
Zh: “哦，那好吧。”

En: You said something about the rights and privileges of lorrymen?
Zh: “你说了什么货車司机的权利和特权？”

En: So... he was a garbage man?
Zh: “所以……他是个清洁工？”

En: Not at all! We're part of a guild and everything... it's very ancient, very prestigious.
Zh: “根本不是！我们属于某个行会，还有其他一切……很古老，很受尊重。”

En: Look, ace detective, I come from a long line of lorrymen. We got ancient rights and privileges... He loses his patience for explaining it.
Zh: “你看，王牌警探，我出身于一个货車司机世家。我们拥有古老的权利和特权……”他失去解释的耐性了……

En: Yeah, I knew that guy. He was an honest driver who loved his country. We were having a good debate about genetics at the Whirling-in-Rags when some kipt-boys smashed his lock and took damn near everything. Lost his fucking job over it.
Zh: “是啊，我知道那家伙。他是个爱国的老实司机。我们在褴褛飞旋好好辩论了一场关于基因的话题，直到有些该死的吉皮特小子砸烂了他的锁，妈的几乎把所有东西都拿走了。他也因为这件破事丢了工作。”

En: He takes a long suck on his cigarette, appearing to savour the taste. "Since he left I haven't had anyone to talk to..."
Zh: 他用力吸了一口烟，似乎在品味味道。“自从他离开之后，我就再也没有可以说话的人了……”

En: What, aren't we *allowed* to say that it's the kipts who do all the stealing around here? It's not *rotor science*, man...
Zh: “怎么，我们*不能*说吉皮特在到处偷东西吗？这不是*转子科学*，老兄……”

En: If they were getting drunk inside the Whirling-in-Rags how could they know who broke into the lorry?
Zh: 如果他们在褴褛飞旋喝醉了，他们怎么知道是谁闯进货車的呢？

En: Who's that?
Zh: “那是谁？”

En: Where do I find him?
Zh: “我该去哪找他？”

En: Wait, the street vendor south of here? That guy's a lorry driver?
Zh: “等等，南边那个街头小贩吗？那家伙是个货車司机？”

En: Of course he's a lorry driver! What -- he tell you he's just some simple businessman or some shit? He's selling his employer's stuff after he broke the seals on his Humanox lorry.
Zh: “他当然是货車司机了！怎么——-他告诉你自己只是个普通的生意人，还是什么其他屁话？他弄破纽曼奥克斯货車的封条，然后偷卖他老板的东西。”

En: His tribe are natural liars. It's in their blood... He nods in a sagely manner, then another puff of that cigarette: "He's your man, alright. One-hundred percent."
Zh: “他的宗族天生就是骗子，都已经渗透在血液里面了……”他点点头，很是贤明的样子，然后又抽了一口烟。“他就是你要找的人，没错。100%肯定。”

En: The one re-selling humanitarian aid packages, right?
Zh: “那个倒卖人道主义救援包的人，对吧？”

En: That's the one. His tribe are natural liars. It's in their blood... The lorryman takes a slow, satisfied drag and blows a smoke ring.
Zh: “就是他。他的宗族天生就是骗子，都已经渗透在血液里面了……”货車司机缓慢又满足地抽了一口烟，然后吐出一个烟圈。

En: Just follow the smell. It smells like apricots and *oil* when you're nearby... The lorryman lets out a raspy croak at his own sense of humour.
Zh: “跟着味道走。当你走到附近的时候，会闻到类似杏子和*油*的味道……”货車司机因为自己的幽默感发出一声刺耳又沙哑的声音。

En: He's the Samaran guy who likes to pretend he's some kinda businessman... Really he's just selling his employer's stuff. Stuff he *stole* after he broke the seals on his Humanox lorry.
Zh: “他是个萨马拉人，喜欢假装自己是某种生意人……其实只是在偷卖他老板的东西。在他撕破纽曼奥克斯货車封条之后，从里面*偷来*的东西。

En: Looks like I offended your partner there. He doesn't look at the lieutenant. "Too bad. Siileng's usually a little bit south of here, near the canal. You can't miss him. Just watch yourselves..."
Zh: “看起来我冒犯你的搭档了。”他没有看向警督。“真可惜。席勒一般就在南边一点点的位置。靠近运河，你应该不会错过的。只要留心一点……”

En: Seriously, though, he's usually just a little bit south of here. Down near the canal, you can't miss him. Just watch yourself...
Zh: “不过，说真的，他一般就在南边一点点的位置。靠近运河，你应该不会错过的。只要留心一点……”

En: Guess I need to pay him a visit, then.
Zh: “看来我要去拜访他一下了。”

En: Guess so. He grins, contented with himself.
Zh: “我猜也是。”他咧嘴笑了，对自己很是满意。

En: He's your man, alright. One-hundred percent. He's a lorryman selling his employer's stuff -- broke the seals on his Humanox lorry. No doubt he's selling drugs too.
Zh: “他就是你要找的人，没错。100%肯定。他是个货車司机，在偷卖老板的东西——撕破了纽曼奥克斯货車上的封条。毫无疑问，他肯定也在贩毒。”

En: Yes, yes. *Where* is he?
Zh: “是的，没错。他*在哪*？”

En: You really got him there, detective.
Zh: “你真的把他难倒了，警探。”

En: No. The lieutenant turns to the lorryman. "He means *La Puta Madre*."
Zh: “不。”警督转向货車司机。“他的意思是*马德雷教父*。”

En: Who's La Puta--
Zh: “教父是谁——”

En: Then I presume you're familiar with his *peones*?
Zh: “那我推测你对他的*代理人*很熟悉咯？”

En: Yeah, he says, unsure where this is leading. "They're his little bitches. He's got them all over the unions."
Zh: “是啊，”他说道，不确定会有什么后果。“他们都是他的小狼狗。工会里到处都有他的人。”

En: Not just the unions. He has peones *everywhere*. Some say he even has them in the RCM. He gets closer to him. "Dirty fucking peones who'd do *anything* for him. Multi-ethnic drug addicts..."
Zh: “不仅仅是工会。*到处*都有他的代理人。有人说甚至RCM里也有。”他又靠近了一些。“那些肮脏的代理人愿意为他做*任何事情*。多种族的瘾君子……”

En: No. Of course not. *We're* not peones. But *if* we were... and one of Madre's drivers were to be stealing from him -- then it's a good peone's job to find out who that is.
Zh: “不。当然不是。*我们*不是代理人。不过*如果*我们是……而且其中一个马德雷的司机在偷东西的话——代理人的工作就是找出那个人是谁。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Wait, are *we*--
Zh: “等等，*我们*是不是——”

En: Cross your arms and nod.
Zh: 抱起双臂，点点头。

En: The lieutenant adopts a rodentine quality. Be cool, sire. He's getting *into* this.
Zh: 警督像啮齿动物般不放过任何一丝异样的气息。他已经*进入*状态了，你得表现得镇定些。

En: You've seen that corpse in the ceramic armour there? The lieutenant points to the yard. "Did his shitty little guild protect *him*?"
Zh: “你见过穿着陶瓷装甲的那具尸体吗？”警督指向院子。“他那个低级的小行会能保护*他*吗？”

En: Listen up, fuckwits. You don't scare me. You cops don't run Revachol West. You don't run Martinaise. You don't run anything.
Zh: “听好了，贱种。你吓唬不了我的。瑞瓦肖西边不归你们警察管。你管不着马丁内斯。你什么狗屁都管不了。”

En: Listen up, fuckwit. You don't scare me. You cops don't run Revachol West. You don't run Martinaise. You don't run anything. You're useless.
Zh: “听好了，贱种。你吓唬不了我的。瑞瓦肖西边不归你们警察管。你管不着马丁内斯。你什么狗屁都管不了。”

En: Listen up, fuckwit. You don't scare me. You cops don't run Revachol West. You don't run Martinaise. You don't run shit.
Zh: “听好了，贱种。你吓唬不了我的。瑞瓦肖西边不归你们警察管。你管不着马丁内斯。你什么狗屁都管不了。”

En: Yes you *are*. Or you're coming to the  station.
Zh: “是的，你*要*说。还是你想去警局说。”

En: He smirks. "I don't know what you're talking about."
Zh: 他得意地笑了。“我不知道你在说什么。”

En: Then why are you smirking?
Zh: “那你傻笑什么？”

En: The lieutenant looks at you. "How do you want to proceed?"
Zh: 警督看着你。“你打算怎么继续？”

En: You're not peones, he says. "You wouldn't be investigating a drug-thing, if you were."
Zh: “你不是代理人，”他说道。“如果你是，你就不会调查毒品的事了。”

En: Nah. You wouldn't just leave him out there if you... He tries to light a fresh cigarette, but his hands are shaking now. The sentence simply ends.
Zh: “不。你不会就这样放着他不管的，如果你……”他试着点一只香烟，不过他的手有些颤抖。那句话也草草结束了。

En: The lieutenant turns and gives you a barely perceptible nod.
Zh: 警督转过身，朝你点点头，你差一点就忽略了。

En: I've softened him up. As best as I could. Now it's on you to finish the job.
Zh: 我已经软化他了。我已经尽力了。现在轮到你来完成这项工作。

En: Fuck you, I told you. I'm not gonna...
Zh: “去死吧你，我告诉你。我不会……”

En: No, no, of course not. Just tell me what you know.
Zh: “不，不，当然不是。只要把你知道的都告诉我。”

En: I don't really know where that came from...
Zh: “我真不知道为什么要那么说……”

En: The lieutenant gives you a brief, sideways glance.
Zh: 警督稍微瞥了你一眼。

En: We'll just have to ask someone else about the lady driver. Let's go.
Zh: “我们只能找其他人去问女司机的下落了。走吧。”

En: Yeah... maybe it *is*...
Zh: “是啊……也许*就是*吧……”

En: Okay, that's enough, detective. That's enough.
Zh: “好吧，够了，警探。差不多够了。”

En: But you see, I'm an officer of the RCM -- it's actually *my* job to make sure *you* behave. I would advise you to remember that.
Zh: “但是你看，我是RCM的一名警官——其实*我的*工作就是保证*你*行为端正。我建议你记住这一点。”

En: Don't you *Welcome to Revachol* me, the lieutenant fires back. "My grandfather came here from a three-thousand-year-old racist-isolationist culture, while your ancestors came to this island a mere three hundred years ago."
Zh: “你再跟我说一句*欢迎来到瑞瓦肖*试试看，”警督展开了反击。“我的祖父来自于一个拥有3000年种族孤立主义历史的文化背景，而你的祖先不过是300年前来到这片土地的。”

En: Every school of thought and government has failed in this city -- but I love it nonetheless. It belongs to me as much as it belongs to you.
Zh: “这座城市的每一个思想流派和政府都很失败——尽管如此我还是很爱它。瑞瓦肖是你的故乡，也是我的。”

En: You can just make out a familiar pattern in the intricate tangle of treads on the rubber of his soles. It is the map of your city!
Zh: 从他鞋底的橡胶上那些复杂错乱的纹路之间，你好像辨认出一个熟悉的图案。是这座城市的地图！

En: That's a map of Revachol.
Zh: “这是瑞瓦肖的地图。”

En: The lieutenant raises one eyebrow. "You've seen the corpse in the yard, yes? You took a peek. I *know* you did. Does it look like his friends protected *him*?"
Zh: 警督挑起一边的眉头。“你见过院子里的尸体，对吧？你偷瞄过一眼。我*知道*你看见了。你觉得他的朋友保护*他*了吗？”

En: You could ask him to show you the soles of his boots. He definitely looks like someone capable of a lynching. Maybe he was present?
Zh: 你可以让他把鞋底抬起来给你看。他绝对看起来绝对像是有能力执行私刑的人。也许他也在场？

En: Let's just go and ask Tommy, alright? We're wasting our time here.
Zh: “我们还是去问问汤米吧？在这里只是浪费时间。”

En: Stop now. Just ask the other drivers. You have to find out who the lady driver is and you can't do it like this. It's too much.
Zh: 在这里打住。去问问其他司机。你必须找出女司机是谁，而且你不能这样做。实在是太过了。

En: There was a bunch of spilled boxes in the back of a big lorry nearby...
Zh: 附近的一台大货車里有一堆溢出来的箱子……

En: Saffron picker! Welcome to Revachol!
Zh: “摘番红花的！欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: Don't make yourself too comfortable!
Zh: “可别让自己过得太舒服哦！”

En: Ha ha! Welcome to Revachol!
Zh: “哈哈！欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: Welcome to Revachol!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: Welcome to Revachol! Don't stay too long.
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！别待太久哦！”

En: Are you lost? You look lost. Out of place.
Zh: “你迷路了吗？你看起来很迷茫。你不适合这个地方。”

En: Welcome to Revachol!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: Welcome to Revachol!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: Racist-nationalist paraphernalia. He grits his teeth. "Not unusual in this part of town. This is our guy." The lieutenant nods towards the racist lorry driver.
Zh: “民族种族主义的随身用品。”他咬紧牙关。“在这片地区并不算很少见。这就是我们要找的人。”警督朝着种族主义货車司机点点头。

En: Look for a clue about who drives the vehicle.
Zh: 寻找司机的线索。

Actor: Graad Factory of Magnets and Miracles U49
En: It's clear this is your guy. Only the March edition of 'Power Man Monthly: The Pure Man's Magazine' is missing. It's clearly the rotund raceman's.
Zh: 这明显就是你要找的人。只有‘权威人物月刊’三月版：‘纯粹男人’的杂志不见了。这明显是那个圆胖的种族主义者的。

Actor: Graad Factory of Magnets and Miracles U49
En: The windows are clear, they've been recently washed. You can see a lorryman's cabin with personal belongings, stickers, insignia...
Zh: 窗户很干净，最近刚洗过。你看见一个货車司机的車厢，里面摆放着私人物品，贴纸，徽章……

En: Look in the window.
Zh: 朝窗户里面看。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: A large metal pendant hangs from the rear view mirror. The pendant features a sun crowned with wavy rays.
Zh: 后视镜上挂着一个大金属坠饰，上面是一个冠以波浪形光线的太阳。

En: A book with ragged edges catches your notice. The front cover features a large, muscular man. The title reads: 'Man from Hjelmdall in the Lost City of the Pygmies'.
Zh: 一本边缘参差不齐的书引起了你的注意。封面是一个大个子，肌肉发达的男人。标题叫做：‘来自赫姆达尔的男人：深入侏儒城。’

En: He was acting tough before. This probably scared him a bit. Who knows when it will come in handy -- a slightly scared racist lorryman?
Zh: 他之前表现地很强硬。这下可能有点吓到他了。谁知道什么时候会派上用场呢——一个略微受到惊吓的货車司机？

En: There he is, in front of Frittte, smoking nervously. Hoping it's over.
Zh: 他就在那里，弗利多门口，紧张地抽烟。希望这一切已经结束。

Actor: Graad Factory of Magnets and Miracles U49
En: A lorry stuck in the traffic jam. This big, heavy Graad-made machine is well kept for such an old machine.
Zh: 一台被拥堵交通困住的货車。这台格拉德生产的重型大货車虽然很旧了，不过却保养地很好。

En: He was acting tough before. This definitely scared him a little, you being here.
Zh: 他之前表现地很强硬。你出现在这里，肯定有点吓到他了。

En: Racist-nationalist paraphernalia. He grits his teeth. "Not unusual in this part of town."
Zh: “民族种族主义的随身用品。”他咬紧牙关。“在这片地区并不少见。”

Actor: Graad Factory of Magnets and Miracles U49
En: The driver has adorned the side walls with a substantial collection of racist paraphernalia. Proclamations about *honour*, *strength*, and *purity* are glued to various panels.
Zh: 司机用很多特殊的随身用品装饰着侧墙。有关*荣耀*，*力量*和*纯净*的宣言粘贴在各种各样的面板上。

Actor: Graad Factory of Magnets and Miracles U49
En: The driver has adorned his space with a substantial collection of peculiar paraphernalia. Proclamations about *honour*, *strength*, and *purity* are glued to various panels.
Zh: 司机用很多特殊的随身用品装饰着他的空间。有关*荣耀*，*力量*和*纯净*的宣言粘贴在各种各样的面板上。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: It is the seal of Royalist-era Revachol.
Zh: 这是瑞瓦肖保皇派时代的印章。

En: What kind of stickers and insignia?
Zh: 哪种贴纸和徽章？

En: Fumes of heavy fuel oil waft over you, making your eyes sting. The odour mixes with cigarette residue.
Zh: 重油燃烧的废气飘了上来，让你的眼睛有些刺痛。这种味道跟香烟的余味混在了一起。

En: The back end of the cabin has a small perch to sleep. Large ashtrays. There are several suns and wheels sown into the curtains.
Zh: 車厢背后有一个小的休息空间。大烟灰缸。窗帘上满是太阳和車轮。

En: What about the back seat?
Zh: 后座呢？

En: You think this lorry belongs to our tough guy?
Zh: “你觉得这台货車会不会属于那个硬汉？”

En: Likely, yes. This guy's proud of who he is. Drapes it all over his machine.
Zh: “有可能，是的。这家伙对自己的出身感到很骄傲。全都挂在自己的車里了。”

En: Never mind. [Leave.]
Zh: “不要紧。”[离开。]

En: Mazut is an antiquated term for heavy fuel oils -- this man has a barely suppressed performative streak. Or he just likes unusual words. Or both!
Zh: 重油是重质可燃物的过时用法——这个男人有一种几乎难以抑制的表述行为倾向。或者他只是喜欢不同寻常的词语。或者两者都有！

En: I am the law.
Zh: “我就是法律。”

En: You sure are, my man. He nods emphatically.
Zh: “你当然是了，老兄。”他用力地点点头。

En: Yes, people get that you are *the law*. You really don't have to keep saying it.
Zh: “是的，大家都知道你是*法律*。你真不用一直挂在嘴边。”

En: I'm good for now, good talk. [Leave.]
Zh: “暂时没事了，跟你聊天很愉快。”[离开。]

En: He crosses his arms and turns his head away, a disappointed look in his eyes.
Zh: 他抱起双臂，转过头，眼里流露出失望的神情。

En: The man taps his fingers rhythmically against his arm.
Zh: 男人用手指有节奏地拍打着胳膊。

En: Going on strike would probably help you dodge a bullet or two.
Zh: 罢工也许能帮你躲避一两颗子弹。

En: Wait, what?
Zh: “等等，什么？”

En: Nothing. He smiles. "I'm just messing with you, man. Don't mind my idle verbosity."
Zh: “没什么。”他笑了。“我只是在逗你玩呢，老兄。别在意我那些无聊的唠叨。”

En: It's a bad habit. You ought to quit, too.
Zh: “这是个坏习惯。你也应该戒掉。”

En: He's seen your kind before. As a lorry driver he's familiar with every addiction, compulsion, and self-destructive habit known to a man.
Zh: 他见过你这种人。作为一个货車司机，他熟悉每一种已知的成瘾、冲动、还有自我毁灭的习惯。

En: Yeah, I can quit any time I want.
Zh: “是的，我随时都能戒。”

En: Okay, man, I believe you. He gives you a funny look.
Zh: “好吧，老兄，我信你。”他脸上的表情有些滑稽。

En: Did I ask for your *opinion?*
Zh: “我有问过你的*意见*吗？”

En: You've got a point, but now is not a good time to quit. I've got a murder to solve.
Zh: “你说的有道理，不过现在还不是戒掉的时候。我还有一起凶杀案要破呢。”

En: No, my man, you asked for my cigarettes. There's sadness in his voice; he doesn't seem offended, though.
Zh: “没有，老兄，你找我要烟。”他的声音里有一丝悲伤的情绪；不过他好像并没有觉得被冒犯。

En: It's never a good time to quit. He shakes his head. "You just gotta sit it out, friend."
Zh: “就没有戒掉的好时候。”他摇摇头。“你就等着瞧吧，朋友。”

En: Anything else I can help you with?
Zh: “还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: It's official. He too agrees. This is the antechamber of the afterlife.
Zh: 这是官方的。他也同意。这是来世的接待室。

En: You're right. I should use this as an opportunity to start *anew*.
Zh: “你说得对。我应该把它当成是一次*重新*开始的机会。”

En: Right, man -- *anew*. He adds with a smile and a nod: "The phoenix at the traffic jam."
Zh: “没错，老兄——*重新*开始。”他笑着点头补充到：“交通堵塞里的凤凰。”

En: No -- *your* mother.
Zh: “不——是*你的*母亲。”

En: I don't think you understand how that joke goes, but -- do continue. You were asking about the strike.
Zh: “我觉得你没明白开玩笑是怎么一回事，不过——你继续吧。你刚问到罢工的事。”

En: Yes -- but do continue. You were asking about the strike.
Zh: “是的——不过你继续吧。你刚问到罢工的事。”

En: It's easy to see he's telling the truth. He's kept his nose out of the dark stuff.
Zh: 很容易看出来他说的是实话。他不愿意去窥探那些黑暗的东西。

En: Interested? Not really, I just asked because... I don't know, must be a cop reflex.
Zh: “感兴趣？不觉得，我这么问是因为……我不知道，一定是警察的本能反应。”

En: There it is again -- a little touch of sadness beneath his cool. He thinks he's spent too long in this lorry.
Zh: 又来了——沉静下面的那一抹悲伤。他觉得自己在货車里待得太久了。

En: Yeah, must be -- your job's to know all those *little* things isn't it? While my job... he pats the back of the lorry, "is to deliver tracksuit trousers."
Zh: “是啊，果然如此——你的工作就是搞清楚所有*细小*的事情，不是吗？而我的工作……”他拍了拍货車背后，“就是运输运动裤。”

En: That's all for now, bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “暂时就这些吧，再见。”[离开。]

En: A rhyme-smith? This is quite credible -- it goes with his cadence and way of speaking.
Zh: 一个韵文匠人？那还挺可靠的——跟他的说话的节奏和方式很相配。

En: MY MAN, I WANNA KNOW ABOUT YOUR SOUL!
Zh: “我的朋友，我想探知你的灵魂！”

En: You really are *the law*, aren't you? I get that being bluntly observant and inquisitive comes with the territory, but it certainly doesn't help with your conversational skills, does it?
Zh: “你真的是*法律*，对吧？我明白这么直白地观察和好打听是天经地义，但是确实对你的谈话技巧没多大帮助，是吧？”

En: Cool, cool... We all want to know each other, know each other's woes and all -- but people, man, they have *slippery* souls...
Zh: “很酷，很酷……我们都想了解彼此，了解彼此的不幸和一切——但是老兄，人类这种东西，拥有很*狡猾*的灵魂……”

En: Just like that -- he slips out of your reach. It is possible the YELLING didn't help.
Zh: 就像这样——他溜到了你鞭长莫及的地方，大喊很可能也没有用。

En: You really are *the law*, aren't you? I get that being bluntly observant and inquisitive comes with the territory, but it certainly doesn't help with your conversational skills, does it?
Zh: “你真的是*法律*，对吧？我明白这么直白地观察和好打听是天经地义，但是确实对你的谈话技巧没多大帮助，是吧？”

En: I'm okay, man. Just... the jam's got me down. The fumes, the chemical rainbows, the tarpaulin stretched on the frames and the dull engines off... The man recedes into his daze of words.
Zh: “我没事的，老兄，只是……堵車让我有些低落。这些废气，化学彩虹，框架上伸出来的柏油帆布，还有停下来的引擎……”男人迷失在了自己的话语里。

En: Maybe the *full on* direct approach wasn't correct? Damn, it's tricky business looking into someone's eyes and not doing it *wrong*.
Zh: 也许*完全*打直球不对？见鬼，看着别人的眼睛，还不能*犯错*，真的有点棘手。

En: Familiar -- how?
Zh: 熟悉——怎么个熟悉法？

En: It followed me into this world when I awoke. I think it may be a planar hunter.
Zh: “当我醒来的时候，它跟着我一起进入这个世界。我觉得它肯定是个二维狩猎者。”

En: Whatever it is, one thing is for sure -- it's *not* my ex-wife.
Zh: “不管是什么，有一件事很肯定——它*不是*我的前妻。”

En: Sinister presence? Wow... that sounds really bad. I hope that doesn't happen to my marriage.
Zh: “凶险的存在？哇……听起来真挺糟糕的。希望我的婚姻别出现这种状况。”

En: Planar hunter? Wow... that sounds really bad. I hope that doesn't happen to my marriage.
Zh: “二维狩猎者？哇……听起来真挺糟糕的。希望我的婚姻别出现这种状况。”

En: Black reverb? Wow... that sounds really bad. I hope that doesn't happen to my marriage.
Zh: “黑暗的回声？哇……听起来真挺糟糕的。希望我的婚姻别出现这种状况。”

En: A great white ghost? Wow... that sounds really bad. I hope that doesn't happen to my marriage.
Zh: “一个巨大的白色幽灵？哇……听起来真挺糟糕的。希望我的婚姻别出现这种状况。”

En: Still -- a shadowy figure. That sounds pretty bad. I hope that doesn't happen to my marriage...
Zh: “不过——幽暗的阴影。听起来真挺糟糕的。希望我的婚姻别出现这种状况……”

En: For some reason, sire, he pays no heed to your objection. Continuing instead as if it *were* your ex wife. Strange...
Zh: 大人，出于某种原因，他并没有留意您的反对意见，还是认为它*就是*你的前妻。奇怪……

En: Yeah, actually not. Let's change the subject.
Zh: “嗯，其实不是。我们换个话题吧。”

En: Sure man, no problem... and thanks for this. It's good to talk to someone. I know it wasn't easy to ask. He smiles. "I hope you find your way through your own troubles."
Zh: “当然了，老兄，没问题……还有，谢谢你。和别人聊天真的挺好的。而且我知道开口并不容易。”他笑了。“希望你能找到解决问题的办法。”

En: Perhaps it would be a good idea to show *yourself* some of that empathy, from time to time? Just a suggestion. Carry on now.
Zh: 也许，偶尔展现一下*你的*同理心是一个不错的主意？只是个建议。现在先继续吧。

En: It's good talking to someone. I know it wasn't easy to say those things, so... He smiles. "I hope you find your way through your own troubles."
Zh: “和别人聊天挺好的。我知道说出那些事并不容易，所以……”他笑了。“希望你能找到解决自己问题的办法。”

En: Yeah, my thing has got *nothing* to do with marriage.
Zh: “是啊，我的事情跟婚姻没有*任何*关系。”

En: Sure, man, if you say so... It doesn't look like he believes you. "But look, man..."
Zh: “当然，老兄，如果你这么说的话……”看起来他并没有相信。“但是你看，老兄……”

En: Thanks for this. It's good to talk to someone. I know it wasn't easy to ask, so.... He smiles. "I hope you find your way through your own troubles."
Zh: “谢谢你。和别人聊天真的挺好的。而且我知道开口并不容易。”他笑了。“希望你能找到解决问题的办法。”

En: Thanks for this. It's nice to talk to someone. I know it wasn't easy to ask, so.... He smiles. "I hope you find your way through your own troubles."
Zh: “谢谢你。和别人聊天真的挺好的。而且我知道开口并不容易。”他笑了。“希望你能找到解决问题的办法。”

En: I asked you who's conducting the drug trade. You said you didn't *know*. Now you're saying you do.
Zh: “我问你谁在指挥贩毒。你说你不*知道*。现在又说你知道。”

En: 'She broke me. She fucking broke me.'
Zh: “‘她毁了我。他妈的，她毁了我。’”

En: No. Just no. I can already see it's failed and I'm *stopping* it now. Until you have *good* poetry -- you have *no* poetry.
Zh: 不是。真不是。我已经看出它失败了，现在我要*喊停*。在你真正想出*好*诗之前——你*没有*诗歌了。

En: It's not real guys. It's not my actual thoughts. It's a poem.
Zh: “不是真的，伙计。这不是我的真实想法。这是诗歌。”

En: That's brutal, man. But, you know... Time will...
Zh: “真是太残忍了，老兄。不过，你知道的……时间会……”

En: That's... uhm...
Zh: “这个嘛……呃……”

En: Yeah, yeah, I get that and it's cool. But...
Zh: “是啊，是啊，我明白的，其实挺酷的。不过……”

En: This guy has amazing willpower. Honestly, he's the only one of that *intellectual* bunch I trust.
Zh: 这家伙拥有惊人的意志力。老实说，在那群*知识分子*中间，他是我唯一相信的一个。

En: No! Stop! He's already mortified.
Zh: 不！住手！他已经很窘迫了。

En: In the name of *god*, what are you doing?
Zh: “看在*老天爷*的份上，你在做什么？”

En: (Continue.) "I will never be the same again. She's always there. Fuck the case. Fuck everything. Total doom."
Zh: （继续。）“我永远也回不到从前。她一直在那里。该死的案子。一切都他妈该死。都他妈被诅咒了。”

En: Yeah... He doesn't know what to say, so he just repeats: "Yeah-yeah. I get it. These are *your* rhymes, they're from your life. Doesn't matter if they're robust, they're honest. So... thanks man."
Zh: “是啊……”他不知道该说什么，所以他只是重复着：“是啊是啊，我明白。这些是*你的*韵脚，它们来源于你的生活。它们尽管粗野，但却诚实。所以……谢了，老兄。”

En: He's not lying. He liked the end.
Zh: 他没撒谎。他喜欢结局。

En: Yes. And I also thank you -- for *stopping*. He looks at you. "We have a drug investigation to return to. How about we do that?"
Zh: “是的。而且我也要谢谢你——因为你*停下来*了。”他看着你。“我们手上还有个毒品案要调查。不如现在就开始吧？”

En: Yes. And I also thank you -- for stopping. We have a murder investigation to return to. How about we do that?
Zh: “是的。而且我也要谢谢你——因为你停下来了。”他看着你。“我们手上还有个毒品案要调查。不如现在就开始吧？”

En: It's not real, man. It's not my actual thoughts. It's a poem.
Zh: “这不是真的，老兄。这不是我的真实想法。这是诗歌。”

En: I don't mean to pry, but I need your help -- she may be involved with the drug business.
Zh: “我不是有意刺探，不过我需要你的帮助——看起来她可能卷入贩毒事件了。”

En: Man, I was hoping she'd catch a break. He shakes his head. "All I can say is: she isn't around here anymore. She isn't some mastermind evil-doer. And I don't know where she is."
Zh: “老兄，我还希望她能松口气。”他摇摇头。“我只能说：她已经不在这里了。她不是什么邪恶的幕后主脑。而且我也不知道她在哪。”

En: Man, I was hoping it isn't gonna be her... He bites his lip. "All I can say is -- she isn't around here anymore; she isn't some evil drug-trafficker; and I don't know where she is."
Zh: “老兄，我还希望不会是她……”他咬紧嘴唇。“我只能说：她已经不在这里了。她不是什么邪恶的毒贩；而且我也不知道她在哪。”

En: Who is this person. What's her name?
Zh: “这人是谁。她叫什么名字？”

En: I didn't, man -- I told you, I was *hoping* it's not her. That she wouldn't be mixed up in it.
Zh: “我没有，老兄——我告诉过你，我*希望*不是她。希望她没有卷入其中。”

En: It's not a lie, it's... something else. Impossible to say *what* at this point, but there's something in him. Some trepidation.
Zh: 这不是谎话，这是……别的东西。目前还不能说是*什么*，不过他身上有某些东西。某些恐惧。

En: We wouldn't say he's lying, sire.
Zh: 吾辈不能说他在撒谎，大人。

En: Bah. Emotional rhetoric. He knew something and he didn't share it with you. That's a *fact*.
Zh: 呸。情绪化的修辞。他知道些什么，而且他没有跟你分享。那才是*事实*。

En: Thank god I *don't know*. People here call her 'the lady driver.' She kept her name a secret. From me, too. Now I see why.
Zh: “幸亏我*不知道*。大家都叫她‘女司机’。她的名字是个秘密。对于我来说也是。现在我知道原因了。”

En: What does she look like?
Zh: “她看起来什么样？”

En: Again -- not a lie as far as you can tell.
Zh: 再次声明——就您而言，这不是一个谎言。

En: A young-ish woman. Gruff, but... in a cool way.
Zh: “一个年轻女人。嗓音很粗，不过……是挺酷的那种。”

En: Who is she to you?
Zh: “对于你来说，她是什么人？”

En: A friend? An acquaintance. I don't know... She was the only person in this damn jam I could talk to. She's someone I don't want to rat out to the law, okay?
Zh: “一个朋友？一个熟人。我不知道……这里堵的这么严重，她是唯一可以跟我聊天的人。她是我不想出卖给法律的人，好吗？”

En: Blue and violet, dyed. He answers reluctantly. "It was violet when she got here. Blue before she went."
Zh: “蓝色和紫色的，染的。”他不情愿地回答着。“她到这里来的时候是紫色的。走之前是蓝色的。”

En: What colour hair?
Zh: “头发什么颜色？”

En: What colour hair?
Zh: “头发什么颜色？”

En: When did she leave? (Move on.)
Zh: “她什么时候离开的？”（继续。）

En: Then she may have dyed it again.
Zh: 那她很可能又染过了。

En: Damn, I don't wanna... He looks you straight in the eye. "Please just let it go. Whatever she did, it can't be that bad. She's not a bad person, I know that much."
Zh: “该死，我不想……”他直视你的双眼。“随它去吧。不管她做了什么，也不可能那么糟糕吧。她不是个坏人，我只知道这么多。”

En: I can't just 'let it go', it's part of a police investigation.
Zh: “我不能就这么‘随它去’，这是警方调查的一部分。”

En: We can't just *let it go*. It's part of a police investigation.
Zh: “我们不能就这么‘随它去吧’，这是警方调查的一部分。”

En: That's how it always is with you, isn't it? All part of the investigation... He shakes his head. "The girl's *troubled* -- if you hunt her down, she may not survive it. I can't have that on my conscience."
Zh: “你总是这样，对吗？都是调查的一部分……”他摇摇头。“那个姑娘*有麻烦*——如果你追踪她，她很可能活不下来。我的良心不允许这样。”

En: You said she's *troubled* -- how?
Zh: “你说她*有麻烦*——怎么回事？”

En: She's got the *darkness* in her. That young person's darkness when you think it's all over. And you're looking for a way *out*.
Zh: “她心底有一种*黑暗*。当你以为一切已经结束的时候，黑暗就会浮现。那是种年轻人特有的黑暗，而你要寻找一个*出路*。”

En: She shared this with you?
Zh: “她跟你分享了这些？”

En: Yes, which is why I don't want to *snitch* on her.
Zh: “是的，所以这就是我不想*出卖*她的原因。”

En: I was told *everyone's* afraid of her. You're not?
Zh: “我听说*每个人*都害怕她。你不怕吗？”

En: She shared this with you?
Zh: “她跟你分享了这些？”

En: I heard the rumours. I saw the others drivers looking at me *strange* when we talked. And she told me too -- that she's had a violent life. But I wasn't afraid of her, more like *for* her.
Zh: “我听过传言。我们说话的时候，其他司机看我的表情有些*奇怪*。而且她也告诉过我——说自己的生活很暴力。但是我不怕她。更像是*为*她担惊受怕。”

En: Did this violent life include drug trafficking?
Zh: “那这个暴力的生活包括贩毒吗？”

En: Did this violent life include drug trafficking?
Zh: “那这个暴力的生活包括贩毒吗？”

En: Well... He sighs. "It looks like it did *now* -- but we didn't talk about that. We talked about life, you know. She talked about her mind..."
Zh: “呃……”他叹了口气。“看来*现在*有了——不过我们没聊过。你知道的，我们只聊生活。她会说些自己的想法……”

En: The pain it was causing her.
Zh: 给她带来的痛苦。

En: Hold on. Her mind?
Zh: “等等。她的想法？”

En: The way it worked. The trouble it was giving her.
Zh: “是怎么想的。给她带来的麻烦之类的。”

En: When she left, did she leave her lorry behind?
Zh: “她离开的时候，把自己的货車留下了吗？”

En: Hmh... the grey haired woman. Maybe she knows something?
Zh: 嗯……那个灰头发女人。也许她知道什么？

En: One of them you haven't even questioned yet. Maybe the third driver knows something?
Zh: 有一个人你还没问过话呢。也许第三个司机知道些什么？

En: It's not *snitching*. It's just a few *questions*.
Zh: “这不叫*出卖*。我只是问一些*问题*。”

En: It's snitching, man. I know what it is.
Zh: “这就是出卖，老兄。我知道是怎么回事。”

En: It's right here. The answer is *right* here. You just need to ask him. C'mon -- do it!
Zh: 它就在这里。答案*就在*这里。你只需要开口问他。来吧——问吧！

En: Put yourself in my shoes. I need this for another investigation too -- it's important, I can't blow it.
Zh: “你站在我的立场上考虑一下。还有一个调查也需要这个信息——它很重要，我不能搞砸。”

En: You're not gonna put a bullet in your head if you blow it, are you? 'Cause she's on the *edge*, man.
Zh: “如果你搞砸的话，不会从朝自己的脑子开一枪，对吧？因为她已经处在*边缘*了，老兄。”

En: I wouldn't be so sure, Tommy.
Zh: 我就没那么肯定了，汤米。

En: Imagine it. An explosion. Of *stars*. Good bye world of men, money and machines...
Zh: 想象一下。一场爆炸。*星爆*。再见了，这个人类，金钱和机器的世界……

En: Whooh. Almost gave me a heart attack... He sighs with relief. "Yeah, I *really* need to start smoking again."
Zh: “哇。搞得我差点快犯心脏病了……”他松了口气。“是啊，我*真的*需要重新开始抽烟。”

En: Fine, I will drop the matter *again*. (Find another option)
Zh: “好吧，我*再次*放下这件事吧。”（寻找其他选择）

En: Here. He takes a keyring from his pocket -- then looks at it before giving it to you. In silence...
Zh: “给你。”他从口袋里拿出一个钥匙扣——看了看它，然后递给你。沉默不语……

En: The keys to a motor lorry. Pretty complex. Looks like a chain lock.
Zh: 货車的钥匙。挺复杂的。看起来像一个链锁。

En: Then he gives them to you. "Her lorry is still here. Down, past the statue of Filippe," he waves toward the monument. "The cabin is green. You can get in there with these. That's all I know."
Zh: 然后他把东西递给你。“她的货車还在这里。就在下面，经过菲利普的雕像，”他朝着纪念碑挥了挥手。“車厢是绿色的。用钥匙就能打开进去。我只知道这么多。”

En: When did she leave?
Zh: “她什么时候离开的？”

En: Last Friday. He blinks, his eyes half-empty now.
Zh: “上周五。”他眨眨眼，现在眼睛是半空洞的状态。

En: Anything else? Like where she is now?
Zh: “还有别的吗？比如说她现在在哪？”

En: And why did you have the keys?
Zh: “你为什么有钥匙？”

En: I'm sorry I had to do this.
Zh: “很抱歉，我必须这么做。”

En: No. I don't know.
Zh: “不，我不知道。”

En: She left them to me. Because she trusted me... So I can get it out of the way when the jam breaks loose. Otherwise... He doesn't finish the sentence.
Zh: “她留给我的。因为她信任我……这样交通开始疏散的时候，我就能把車开出去。不然的话……”他的话没有说完。

En: The other drivers would have to tow it, or break in, to get the machines moving. He nods.
Zh: “其他司机会拖走它，或者闯进去，把这台机器开动起来。”他点点头。

En: If they break in they would find what's hidden inside? Something incriminating?
Zh: 如果他们闯进去的话，会不会发现里面藏匿的东西？某些犯罪的东西？

En: The other drivers would have to break in there -- to get the machines moving. And they would find what's hidden inside? Something *incriminating*?
Zh: 其他司机必须闯进去——把这台机器发动。然后他们会发现里面隐藏的东西？某些*犯罪*的东西？

En: I bet you are.
Zh: “我想一定是的。”

En: And he's sorry he couldn't be what he wants to be -- a good person. His last line, his revenge on the world.
Zh: 而且他很遗憾，他没变成自己希望的那种人——一个好人。这是他的最后一句话，他对这个世界的报复。

En: Let's go Kim. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们走吧，金。”[离开。]

En: One more thing. About something else.
Zh: “还有一件别的事。”

En: He turns to the man before you leave. "Thank you for your cooperation, sir. I know it was hard. But you did the right thing."
Zh: 离开之前，他转向那个男人。“感谢你的配合，我知道这很难。但你做了件正确的事。”

En: It won't come to that. We won't pursue her on this. This is information only.
Zh: “不会这样的。我们不会因此追究她的。只是收集一些信息。”

En: I don't believe you.
Zh: “我不信你。”

En: Believe us. It really is.
Zh: “相信我们。真是这样。”

En: I just can't, man. I'm not *naive*.
Zh: “我真的不能，老兄。我没那么*天真*。”

En: Nah. I need to think my own thoughts now. Pray forgiveness for my sins. Go check your cabin... He adds quietly: "I hope it gets you something. Helps someone."
Zh: “不行。现在我需要自己好好思考一下。为自己的罪孽祈祷。你去检查货車吧……”他安静地补充了一句：“希望能让你找到些什么。帮到某个人。”

En: Thank you. I'll go to the cabin then. [Leave.]
Zh: “谢谢，那我去找货車了。”[离开。]

En: Last Friday. He blinks, his eyes half-empty now.
Zh: “上周五。”他眨眨眼，现在眼睛是半空洞的状态。

En: When did she leave?
Zh: “她什么时候离开的？”

En: It wasn't the local Union muscle, by the way.
Zh: “顺便说一句，不是本地的工会打手。”

En: Hey, that's just what I heard. Old wives' tale. So -- what next?
Zh: “嘿，那可是我刚刚听到的。无稽之谈。所以——接下来呢？”

En: Okay... He seems a little apprehensive.
Zh: “好吧……”他好像有一点忧虑。

En: I met the lady driver, on my investigation -- she's called Ruby.
Zh: “我在调查的过程中，见到了女司机——她叫露比。”

En: We are what we are. And you're a cop.
Zh: “我们就是我们。而你是个警察。”

En: The lieutenant throws you a quick glance.
Zh: 警督快速地瞥了你一眼。

En: Is it... *wise* to share information about the case like this, sire?
Zh: 话说……像这样分享案件信息真的*明智*吗，大人？

En: What are you doing?
Zh: 你在做什么？

En: She slipped away.
Zh: “她逃跑了。”

En: Yeah, that's it. Can't share any more.
Zh: “是啊，就这些。不能再多说了。”

En: Whooh... good on her. And good on *you* too -- better focus your efforts on the bad guys. Mercs and such. Plenty of baddies to go around...
Zh: “呼……这样对她比较好。而且对*你*也好——最好还是集中精力去抓那些坏人吧。雇佣兵啊之类的。到处都有坏人……”

En: She shot herself.
Zh: “她自杀了。”

En: She slipped away, don't worry (Lie)
Zh: “她逃跑了，别担心。”（撒谎）

En: I let her go. She didn't do anything.
Zh: “我放她走了。她什么也没做。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Whooh... good on her. And good on *you* too, my man. That's... He looks for the words...
Zh: “呼……这样对她比较好。而且对*你*也好，老兄。真是……”他在组织语言……

En: He's clearly relieved.
Zh: 他显然是松了一口气。

En: *Merciful*. Downright merciful -- you cops keep exercising *that* muscle and people will be more willing to cooperate, you know.
Zh: “*仁慈*。彻头彻尾的仁慈——你们警察继续锻炼*那块*肌肉的话，大家都会更愿意配合的，你知道的吧。”

En: All right. That's enough sharing details about the investigation for one day.
Zh: “好吧。今天分享调查细节就到这里。”

En: He's relieved.
Zh: 他松了一口气。

En: And she didn't do it, believe me. If it's something *bad* then she didn't do it. Didn't know her for long but I know people.
Zh: “不是她做的。相信我，如果发生了什么*不好*的事，那也不是她做的。虽然认识时间不长，但我很会看人。”

En: He cannot believe it.
Zh: 他不敢相信。

En: I'm very sorry. It was my fault.
Zh: “非常抱歉，是我的错。”

En: She was distraught. It... just happened.
Zh: “她心烦意乱。然后就……这么发生了。”

En: I absolutely fucked up. I shouldn't have gone after her.
Zh: “我完全搞砸了。我不应该去追她的。”

En: She's alive. She shot herself in the leg trying to escape. (Lie)
Zh: “她还活着。她企图逃跑，但是却射中了自己的大腿。”（撒谎）

En: He cannot believe it.
Zh: 他不敢相信。

En: God damn, this shit is too intense for me... He looks at his feet, steadying his breath. "I don't wanna know, man. I don't wanna know if she even got away..."
Zh: “真见鬼，这些破事对我来说太紧张了……”他低头看着双脚，稳定自己的呼吸。“我不想知道，老兄。我都不想知道她到底有没有逃跑……”

En: All right. That's enough sharing details. Let's go.
Zh: “好吧。信息分享的已经差不多了。我们走吧。”

En: That breath of his isn't calming down. "Man, I need to take a breath here, excuse me..."
Zh: 他的呼吸无法平静。“老兄，我需要稍微喘口气，请原谅……”

En: The lieutenant is too tired to be cross with you, he just wants to get back to it.
Zh: 警督太累了，不想跟你发脾气，他只想继续调查。

En: That's probably for the best. You keep your job to yourself -- with a job like yours, you *have* to.
Zh: “这可能是最好的结果。你的工作还是自己知道就好——像你们这一行，你*必须*这么做。”

En: You've given him a minor panic attack. He needs to deal with it.
Zh: 你让他有些轻微的惊恐发作。他需要处理这种情况。

En: Just *happened*? His eyes grow wide and he hyperventilates. "Is she *dead*? Don't tell me she's..."
Zh: “刚刚*发生*的吗？”他双目圆睁，有些过度呼吸，“她*死*了吗？别告诉我她……”

En: Sorry? His eyes grow wide and he hyperventilates. "Is she *dead*? Don't tell me she's..."
Zh: “抱歉，”他双目圆睁，有些过度呼吸，“她是*死*了吗？别告诉我她……”

En: You've given him a panic attack. He's ill-equipped to deal with it.
Zh: 你让他有些轻微的惊恐发作。他没有能力处理这种状况。

En: Look, man, fuck off, okay? Fuck off and let me have a panic attack. I don't need to know about this shit anymore. I can't talk to cops any more...
Zh: “听着，老兄，赶紧滚开，好吗？快滚，让我自己惊恐发作吧。我不想再知道这件破事了。我再也不会跟警察说话了……”

En: He means it. This is the end of him talking to you.
Zh: 他是认真的。这是他对你说的最后一句话。

En: The lieutenant is too tired to be cross with you, he just wants to go.
Zh: 警督太累了，不想跟你发脾气，他只想离开。

En: Let's go.
Zh: “我们走吧。”

En: Dead because of *me*?
Zh: 因为*我*而死的吗？

En: His eyes grow wide and he hyperventilates. "Is she *dead*? Don't tell me she's..."
Zh: 他双目圆睁，有些过度呼吸，“她*死*了吗？别告诉我她……”

En: I *told* you not to go after her! I TOLD you. He's hyperventilating. "What happened? Is she... is she..."
Zh: “我*告诉*过你不要去追她！我告诉过你的。”他有些过度呼吸。“出什么事了？她……她是不是……”
