En: You've given him a panic attack. He's ill-equipped to deal with it.
Zh: 你让他有些轻微的惊恐发作。他没有能力处理这种状况。

En: *Dead*? He can't say it...
Zh: *死*了吗？他说不出口……

En: Don't tell me. Just back off and don't talk to me again, okay? I can't talk to cops anymore. I can't be involved in this shit.
Zh: “别告诉我。赶紧走开，不要再跟我说话了，好吗？我再也不会跟警察说话了。我不想再掺合这种破事。”

En: He cleans his hands of it. It's too much.
Zh: 他不想再插手了。他承受不起。

En: Telling him is a REALLY bad idea.
Zh: 告诉他这真的不是个好主意。

En: Out of old habit he still snaps his fingers at you -- but then stops immediately, realizing what he's done. He does not want to talk to you.
Zh: 出于旧习惯，他还是会对你打响指——但是意识到自己做了什么之后，就会立即停下来。他不想再和你说话了。

En: Today, tomorrow, *ever*.
Zh: 今天，明天，*永远*。

En: Maybe don't tell people the people they don't like are actually right...
Zh: 不要在别人面前讲他们不喜欢的人，也许真是对的……

En: He doesn't blame them. But he's not on their side, that's for sure.
Zh: 他没有责怪他们。但是可以肯定，他没有站在他们那边。

En: Ask for his conclusion.
Zh: 询问他的推论。

En: Never! They would never illegalize sport. Sport is the most wholesome thing known to man.
Zh: 绝不！他们永远不会让运动非法化的。运动是人类已知的最有益健康的东西。

En: A pain burns in your chest, radiating. A crown of arteries on fire.
Zh: 你的胸中有一种疼痛在燃烧。一条冠动脉着火了。

En: Just be straight with him.
Zh: 跟他直说吧。

En: Working on these 'rhymes' clearly leads to muscular atrophy. Make sure you don't get hooked on poetry.
Zh: 研究这些‘韵律’显然会导致肌肉萎缩。你要保证自己不对诗歌上瘾。

En: *Did* you?
Zh: *是这样*吗？

En: He still is -- hoping. It's just wishful thinking on his part, not trickery.
Zh: 他还是——抱有希望。这只是他的一厢情愿，不是欺骗。

En: It's true. We would have caught a lie. But... a *kind* heart is tricky.
Zh: 这是真的。吾辈会捕捉到谎言的。不过……一颗*仁慈*的心就比较棘手了。

En: Maybe she dyed it *red*? Titus said Ruby's hair is red...
Zh: 也许她把它染成*红色*了？提图斯说露比的头发是红色的……

En: It's more *him*.
Zh: 这样更像*他*一些。

En: Whooh... good on her. And good on *you* too -- better focus your efforts on the bad guys. Mercs and such. Plenty of baddies to go around...
Zh: “呼……这样对她比较好。而且对*你*也好——最好还是集中精力去抓那些坏人吧。雇佣兵啊之类的。到处都有坏人……”

En: And she didn't do it -- believe me. If it's something *bad* then she didn't do it. Didn't know her for long but I know people.
Zh: “不是她做的——相信我。如果发生了什么*不好*的事，那也不是她做的。虽然认识时间不长，但我很会看人。”

En: Ah, yes. From the Wild Pines.... He takes a note. "We'll meet her soon enough, I'm sure."
Zh: “啊，是的。野松公司来的……”他做下笔记。“我很肯定，我们很快就会见到她。”

En: There's no denying it -- your body *needs* to feel the touch of some good performance-wear.
Zh: 不用否认——你的身体*需要*感受到一些好运动服的触碰。

En: I miss someone but I don't know who it is.
Zh: “我在想念一个自己也不知道是谁的人。”

En: I feel for you, my friend. It's bad enough to *know* who you miss... missing like that doesn't feel like it has much of an upside.
Zh: “我能明白你的感觉，我的朋友。*知道*自己在想念谁已经很困难了……那种想念似乎并没有多大的好处。”

En: You don't *really* think so.
Zh: “你*其实*并不是这么想的。”

En: I really do. If I didn't, I wouldn't be in the RCM. Take care, sir.
Zh: “我真是的。如果不是，我就不会加入RCM。保重，先生。”

En: Wait -- so you *know* the lady driver?
Zh: “等等——所以你*认识*那个女司机？”

En: Wait -- so you *did* know something about who's smuggling drugs around here?
Zh: “等等——所以你*确实*知道这里贩毒的事情？”

En: The racist told us where her cabin is. We only searched it.
Zh: “那个种族主义者把她的货車位置告诉我们了。我们只是搜查了一下。”

En: Still better than me, I guess -- told you there's plenty of others who'd tell on her. He leans in. "But... is she in trouble?"
Zh: “我猜，还是比我要好——我跟你说过，还有很多人会告发她。”他靠近了一些。“但是……她有麻烦吗？”

En: Nah, man, I was telling you the truth. I really didn't think it'd be her. She's okay. Sorta troubled but, still -- a nice person.
Zh: “不，老兄，我说的都是真话。我真的没想到会是她。她挺好的。有点混乱，不过——是个好人。”

En: A little. She's okay. Troubled but -- a nice person. She left last week. Left me the keys to park her lorry if the jam should break.
Zh: “有一点。她挺好的。有点混乱，不过——是个好人。她上个星期离开的。把钥匙留给了我，如果交通疏散的话就帮她停車。”

En: He chooses to believe what's best for her.
Zh: 他选择相信对她来说最好的东西。

En: What else do you know about her?
Zh: “关于她的事，你还知道什么？”

En: Nothing, man. We just talked a bit -- about life and stuff... He looks to you. "So, is she in trouble or what?"
Zh: “没什么了，老兄。我们只是稍微聊了一会儿——关于生活之类的事情……”他看着你。“所以，她遇上麻烦了，还是什么？”

En: So that's what that emotion was, before. It was *wishful thinking*.
Zh: 所以这就是之前感受到的那种情绪。不过是*痴心妄想*。

En: So that's what that emotion was, before. It was *wishful thinking*.
Zh: 所以这就是之前感受到的那种情绪。不过是*痴心妄想*。

En: What else do you know about her?
Zh: “关于她的事，你还知道什么？”

En: Nothing. We just talked a bit -- about life and stuff. She left me her keys, so I can park her lorry if the jam broke... He looks to you. "So, is she in trouble or what?"
Zh: “没什么了，老兄。我们只是稍微聊了一会儿——关于生活之类的事情……”他看着你。“所以，她遇上麻烦了，还是什么？”

En: Alright. I had another question actually.
Zh: “好吧，其实我还有别的问题。”

En: The correct grammar is Tommy *L'Homme*.
Zh: 正确的语法应该是汤米·*勒姆*。（注：法语中是人类的意思）

En: Yeah -- you too. Be safe, detective.
Zh: “是啊——你也是。注意安全，警探。”

En: C'mon, man. Life is just a joke.
Zh: “拜托，老兄。生活只是个玩笑。”

En: Maybe. But not *another* person's life.
Zh: “也许吧。不过*其他*人的生活不是。”

En: A simple little cadence. He seems to be making it up as he goes.
Zh: 一段简单的小旋律。他似乎是一边想，一边编词。

En: That's your machine behind you?
Zh: “背后那个是你的車吗？”

En: This rockin' beauty? He points at the lorry with his thumb. "Sure is, like a rash you can't get rid of. You interested in heavy duty cargo machinery?"
Zh: “你说这个野性美人吗？”他用大拇指指向货車。“当然是了，它就像你的皮疹一样让你欲罢不能。你对重型货运机械感兴趣吗？”

En: What's going on here?
Zh: “这里出什么事了？”

En: It's the Jam, my man. He motions toward the sprawl of lorries with a sweeping gesture.
Zh: “老兄，堵車了。”他朝着那堆杂乱无序的货車摆了摆手。

En: Man, you look sad. What's going on with you?
Zh: “伙计，你看起来很伤心。出什么事了吗？”

En: You really are *the law*, aren't you? I get that being bluntly observant and inquisitive comes with the territory, but it certainly doesn't help with your conversational skills, does it?
Zh: “你真的是*法律*，对吧？我明白这么直白地观察和好打听是天经地义，但是确实对你的谈话技巧没多大帮助，是吧？”

En: Man, nothing's troubling me. Just the usual lorryman tropes and hopes: the road and the rhymes. This jam ain't helping either... He looks around.
Zh: “老兄，我没有在烦恼啊。只是货車司机惯例的修辞和期望：马路和韵律。再说现在堵車也帮不上什么忙……”他看了看周围。

En: Tommy L'Homme was taken. He shrugs in a what-can-you-do manner. "My real name is Jerry Lefitte -- Tommy's way better."
Zh: “汤米·勒姆被占用了。”他耸耸肩，摆出一副*你还能怎么做*的样子。“我的真名叫杰里·拉菲特——不过汤米要好多了。”

En: He's a poet. Hit him with your best verse.
Zh: 他是一个诗人。用你最棒的诗篇打败他。

En: Hunted across planes of existence.
Zh: 在存在的位面上被猎杀。

En: Understood his theatrics.
Zh: 理解他的戏剧化言辞

En: Art is Everywhere.
Zh: 艺术无处不在。

En: Forced him to rat out ladydriver.
Zh: 强迫他出卖女司机

En: Icosahedral Red Die.
Zh: 二十面体红骰子。

En: Wait, there's something *here*. Stored away in some dusty corner. It starts like...
Zh: 等等，*这里*好像有些东西。藏在灰尘扑扑的角落里。开始像是……

En: Your *best* verse? You don't even have a *bad* verse in here. Just tumbleweed and liquor stains... wait, no! What are you DOING?!
Zh: 你*最棒的*诗篇？你怕是连*糟糕*的诗篇都没有吧，只有风滚草和酒渍……等等，别！你在做什么？！

En: A mirror’s temperature is always zero. It is ice in the veins. Its camera is an x-ray.
Zh: “镜子的温度总是零。它就像是血管里的冰。它的照相机是X射线。”

En: Dang, he grunts, unable to suppress his reaction. "That's some *great* shit. You came up with that yourself?"
Zh: “该死，”他咕哝着，无法压抑自己的反应。“这他妈真是*太棒*了。你自己编的吗？”

En: A motor lorry, also called a *camion* on Caillou and neighbouring islands. This one looks roughed up enough to be some sort of a FALN rust-bucket, maybe the A-6?
Zh: 一辆货車，在卡尤和临近岛屿也被称为*货車*。这一台有够破的，有可能是一台FALN老破車，也许是A-6？

En: I'm just a vessel for the muse.
Zh: “我不过是缪斯女神的容器。”

En: I think that may be an actual poet. And I've learned it by heart.
Zh: “我想我可能是个真正的诗人。而且已经牢记于心。”

En: Or did you learn it in school? More importantly: who can prove if you did? Go with it -- you *deserve* some acclaim.
Zh: 或者您是在学校学的？更重要的是：如果真是这样的话，谁能证明？顺其自然吧——您*值得*欢呼和喝彩。

En: I think the words are mine, yes.
Zh: “我觉得这些词是我编的，没错。”

En: Right. But really -- no bullshit. That's great stuff. I sorta wanna know if it's yours...
Zh: “好吧。不过说真的——绝对不是胡话。真的挺不错的。我有点想知道这是不是你写的……”

En: Fuckin' A! Seems I got you all wrong. Cops aren't much known for their artistic sensibilities these days.
Zh: “他妈的真不错！看来我完全看错你了。现在的警察可不是以艺术感知力出名的。”

En: He tries his best to look nonchalant, but there's a rigidity in him, as if trying to conceal something warm and deep beneath a cool exterior.
Zh: 他努力做出一副若无其事的样子，但是里面却有些僵硬，似乎想把某种温暖的东西隐藏在冰冷的外表之下。

En: What's the Jam?
Zh: “堵車是怎么回事？”

En: It's a traffic jam for the ages. Harbour gates up the street are shut tight. No explanation given. Workers on strike. Scabs agitatin'. An all-around clusterfuck.
Zh: “已经堵了好长一段时间。港口大门死活不开，也没有谁出来解释为什么。工人在罢工，工贼在煽风点火。乱七八糟。”

En: All of who?
Zh: “大家是指谁？”

En: Us lorry drivers. Camionneurs. A few still hang around here waiting for this mess to end. Most have scurried off somewhere. To get drunk, or high. Or laid. He smiles awkwardly. "Not that I blame 'em, really."
Zh: “指我们这些货車司机，也就是拉货的。有些人还在附近等着这些破事结束。大部分人都跑到别的地方去了。去喝酒，或者去嗑药。要不就是泡妞。”他尴尬地笑了。“倒不是说我在责备他们，真没有。”

En: Tell me more about this... *strike*.
Zh: “再跟我说说……*罢工*的事。”

En: Not you?
Zh: “你不去吗？”

En: Not my thing. Chasin' transient pleasures is a drag these days. I prefer the examined life now -- thinkin', reflectin', observin'. He glances down the road toward the horizon, a glint of... something in his eyes.
Zh: “我不喜欢这些。现如今追逐这些短暂的快乐其实挺麻烦的。现在我更喜欢审慎的生活——思考，反省，观察。”他的目光沿着马路飘到地平线的远方，眼睛里有某种东西……在闪光。

En: What's the Union demanding?
Zh: “工会有什么需求？”

En: Some pretty wild stuff, I hear. Like a giant new power-crane and half the company? I forget *what* exactly. Good on them, I guess...
Zh: “我听说他们的要求很离谱，好像是一台大型新动力起重机，还有半个公司？我忘记具体是*什么*了。我猜，应该对他们挺好的吧……”

En: So how long have you been here?
Zh: “那你在这里待多久了？”

En: Feels like forever, like I was born on this here roundabout and this was all I ever knew. Just me and the metal and the tyres, the oil and the fumes of mazut...
Zh: “感觉就像永远，就像我就出生在这个环岛，而堵車是我唯一知道的事情。只有我，金属，轮胎，油，还有重油排出的废气……”

En: Yeah, imagine -- it's been a whole week already. He snickers -- in appreciation of the digging of his style.
Zh: “是啊，想象一下——已经整整一星期了。”他窃笑到——很欣赏挖掘自己风格。

En: Yeah, imagine -- it's been a whole week already. He snickers.
Zh: “是啊，想象一下——已经整整一星期了。”他窃笑到。

En: Got any smokes on you?
Zh: “你身上有烟吗？”

En: Nope. Don't smoke. He nods toward the big lorry behind him...
Zh: “没有。我不抽。”他朝着身后的大货車点了点头……

En: Some of the other drivers do, though. There's still a few around, waiting for the Jam to disperse. Or waiting for the sweet release of death.
Zh: “不过还有些其他司机。附近还有几个，等着交通疏散。或者是在等待甜蜜的死亡降临吧。”

En: You *lying* to me? Being all stingy with the smokes?
Zh: “你在跟我*撒谎*吗？几支烟搞得这么小气？”

En: Hello again, my man. He greets you. "What's on your mind?"
Zh: “又见面了，朋友。”他向你打了个召唤。“你在想什么呢？”

En: I don't... really know.
Zh: “我……我也不知道。”

En: Just messin' with you. It's cool, you're an okay guy, for a cop.
Zh: “只是逗你玩呢。没事的，对于一个警察来说，你挺不错的。”

En: It's good to meet a fellow poet -- someone with an appreciation for *real text*. The others here, see, they don't really *get* it.
Zh: “能见到一个诗人同伴真是太好了——懂得欣赏*真正的文字*的人。其他人，看见了吧，他们真的*不懂*。”

En: It makes it all easier to bare. If the words are *pretty*.
Zh: “如果言辞*动听*的话，一切都会更容易一些。”

En: Hey, man. Cops are human too.
Zh: “喂，老兄。警察也是人。”

En: What gall he had to admonish you earlier for your prejudice, yet here he is, saying cops are usually dull-minded. Are you going to let him get away with being a hypocrite?
Zh: 他有什么毛病吗，刚才还告诫你不要有偏见，现在自己又这样，说警察一般都是死脑筋。你打算让这种伪君子逍遥法外吗？

En: I get ya. When they really *click* it makes the world seem manageable. Good to be on the same page. He gives you a thumbs up.
Zh: “我懂。当词句绝妙*融合*的时候，这个世界就可控了。很高兴你我意见一致。”他朝你竖起大拇指。

En: Yeah. I guess I owe you an apology for thinking otherwise. And again -- *great* shit. He gives you a thumbs up.
Zh: “是啊。看来我要为不同看法而向你道歉了。不过还是想说——真的*很棒*。”他朝你竖起大拇指。

En: You've found some common ground with this man. Even impressed him. The next time you look in the *mirror*, though. Remember those words...
Zh: 你发现自己和这个男人有一些共同点。你甚至给他留下了深刻的印象。不过你下次照*镜子*的时候，记住这些话……

En: Meanwhile, we're all stuck here in long haul limbo for days upon days upon days... He glances south, down the road. "Upon days."
Zh: “同时呢，我们全都被困在这个无尽的地狱里，一天接着一天……”他瞥了一眼南边的道路。“又一天。”

En: No matter. Great verse is like that sometimes -- ephemeral. You might not look it, but seems you have some literary chops. Maybe there's hope for me yet!
Zh: “没关系。有时候伟大的诗篇就是这样的——短暂。虽然看起来不太像，不过你确实有些文学天赋。也许我也还有希望！”

En: That's... a strange compliment.
Zh: “这还真是……很奇怪的恭维呢。”

En: No matter, it's a fine stanza. You're not so bad after all, for a cop.
Zh: “没关系，这是一段很好的诗节。看来你也没那么糟糕……对于一个警察来说。”

En: For a cop?
Zh: “对于警察来说？”

En: They're more often into fists than rhymes, see. Let alone *honesty in verse*.
Zh: “一般来说，相比韵律他们更喜欢拳头，是吧。更不用说*诗文的真诚*了。”

En: Any organization gets all kind of folk. I'm sure we try our best.
Zh: “任何组织里都会有各种各样的人。我相信我们都已经尽力了。”

En: Well. You've given *me* some hope, I guess! He gives you a thumbs up.
Zh: “好吧。你确实给了*我*一些希望，我猜！”他朝你竖起大拇指。

En: The lieutenant looks at you in silence, a curious look in his eyes, nodding slightly.
Zh: 警督默默看着你，眼底有些惊讶，然后轻轻点了点头。

En: He ain't one of us drivers -- I know that. All accounted for. Otherwise, I haven't really asked about that. Been wastin' time right here. Keepin' busy.
Zh: “他不是我们的司机——这一点我能肯定。另外，还真没有人问过这个。我忙得很，不会一直耗在这里浪费时间的。”

En: Busy -- with what?
Zh: “忙——忙什么？”

En: Analysin' the fundamental structural and psychological conditions of being stranded in the midst of a sea of motor lorries and their sad, despondent chauffeurs.
Zh: “分析搁浅在一大片由货車和悲伤沮丧的货車司机汇成的海洋中央，它的基本结构和心理状态是怎样的。”

En: And your conclusion?
Zh: “那你的结论呢？”

En: A sense of surprise there ain't more bodies hanging from more trees.
Zh: “没有更多尸体挂在更多树上，有那么一点惊讶的感觉。”

En: Man, relax. I quit, been clean for some time. There are some commitments you have to honour. He looks at you with compassion in his eyes.
Zh: “老兄，放松点。我戒了，已经有一段时间了。有些承诺是必须遵守的。”他看着你，眼里满是怜悯。

En: You really need them smokes. Need 'em! Gotta get some. You sure he's not lying to you? You can't trust a lorryman.
Zh: 你真的很需要抽烟。你需要它们！去弄点吧。你确定他没有骗你？你怎么能相信一个货車司机呢。

En: Extravagantly phrased -- but I can roll with it.
Zh: “你的措辞有些夸张了——但是我可以接受。”

En: I don't quite understand what you just said.
Zh: “我没明白你刚才说了些什么。”

En: In a small office behind the Old Military Hospital, hunched under the green glow of his desk light, officer Hans Blau browses through a test print. Of his futurist magazine. It's called "The Futurist". The typeface on the header is too small...
Zh: 在旧军事医院的一间小办公室里，汉斯·布劳警官在办公桌绿色的灯光下弓着背，浏览着一份打印文件——来自他的未来派杂志，名叫“未来主义者”。标题的字体有些太小了……

En: I'm doing what little I can to do right by people.
Zh: “我正在尽我所能地帮助别人。”

En: Guess I'll ask around then.
Zh: “那我只好四下问问了。”

En: No joke, man. Fuck the police.
Zh: “不是开玩笑，老兄。让警察见鬼去吧。”

En: The air from the east is thick with the smell of crude oils, heavy metals and other by-products of the modern era. You can almost *taste* it.
Zh: 东面吹来的风夹杂着一股浓厚的味道，原油、工业废液、重金属和其它现代副产品的味道。你几乎能*尝到*其中的滋味。

En: A flinch jolts his frame. The question has touched a nerve.
Zh: 他吓了一跳。这个问题触动了他的神经。

En: Man. He sighs. "I don't know what to say. Not much anyone can do... there's no helping an absence, you know?"
Zh: “老兄。”他叹了一口气。“我不知道该说些什么。大家都做不了什么……你帮不了我，因为我根本不在那里，你知道吗？”

En: Not me, man, no way. I don't need any trouble. Shit's bad enough anyway. This jam's got folks up in arms and I'm afraid it's headed toward a conflagration.
Zh: “我没有，老兄，不可能的。我不想惹麻烦。情况本来就有够糟的了。这次交通堵塞引起了大家的激烈抗议，我担心形势会变得一发不可收拾。”

En: I'm a cop, I can fix anything.
Zh: “我是个警察，我什么问题都能解决。”

En: I miss my family. They're all I have, my wife... a second kid on the way... waiting all the way in Deora. And here I am, stuck in this shit, so far from home.
Zh: “我想念我的家人。他们是我的一切，我的妻子……我的第二个孩子也快出生了……他们就在德奥拉等我。而我却在这里，困在这个烂摊子里，离家十万八千里。”

En: You could also check the kiosque down the street. Frittte. He points towards the harbour. "With three t-s. Revachol is weird..."
Zh: “你还可以去街上的报亭看看，弗利多。”他指着港口的方向。“招牌上有3个字体T的那个就是。瑞瓦肖真的很奇怪……”

En: Care to spare some change for a working stiff?
Zh: “愿意分点零钱给我这个小职员吗？

En: Hey, Tommy -- spill the beans. What's troubling you?
Zh: “喂，汤米——说漏嘴了吧。你在烦恼什么呢？”

En: Huh? Sudden financial duties snap him out of his daze. "Oh. No, I ain't got any money. They don't want to pay for unfinished work."
Zh: “哈？”突如其来的财务关税把他从恍惚中唤醒。“哦。没有，我没钱。工作没做完他们是不会给钱的。”

En: 'They' who?
Zh: “‘他们’是谁？”

En: The bosses, man.
Zh: “老板呗，老兄。”

En: Keep it simpler. I'm here to ask questions. *Crime* questions.
Zh: “还是简单一些吧。我来是问问题的。*犯罪*方面的问题。”

En: Could you say it again, only a little  less plodding?
Zh: “你能再说一次吗，稍微没那么无聊一些？”

En: Could you say it again, only a little less plodding?
Zh: “你能再说一次吗，稍微没那么无聊一些？”

En: I dig your style, man.
Zh: “我喜欢你的风格，老兄。”

En: Whoa. He leans in, his eyes bright with attention. "What else?"
Zh: “哇啊。”他靠近了一些，眼睛因为专注而发亮。“还有什么？”

En: It is a chalice held out to you in silent communion.
Zh: “这是一个圣餐杯，在无声圣餐仪式的时候会递到你手上。”

En: It's like... whatever's going on over at the docks. Workers got a blockade set up. Making demands. No way in or out.
Zh: “好像是……码头那边出了点状况。工人们封了路。提出需求。进不去，也出不来。”

En: What do you think the company wants?
Zh: “你觉得公司想要什么？”

En: They want to keep that money flowing in, my man. He makes a 'ka-ching' sound.
Zh: “他们想要源源不断的钱啊，老兄。”他发出一声收银机的‘哗啦’声。

En: Maybe *I* should go on strike...
Zh: “也许*我*也应该罢工……”

En: Maybe you should. Cops don't get paid much and the hours are long. Plus, you can get shot. Why not?
Zh: “也许吧。警察拿的也不多，工作时间又长。而且，还有可能中枪。所以为什么不呢？”

En: Anything else I should know? (Conclude.)
Zh: “还有什么是我应该知道的吗？”（总结。）

En: Anything else? He thinks. "Yeah, this ain't really my area of expertise. I just do my job and get paid. I have things to do and places to be. All of us do."
Zh: “还有什么？”他想了想。“哦，这真的不是我的专长。我只是乖乖干活，老实拿钱。我还有事要做，还有地方要去。大家都一样。”

En: The RCM is a self-managing organization that operates on donations. We promote our own leaders. It would be like striking against your own... mother.
Zh: “RCM是一个自主管理组织，靠捐款来运行。我们会推选自己的领导。所以感觉就好像是在反抗你自己的……母亲。”

En: I don't know my mother -- but I'd go on strike against her too. For my rights.
Zh: “我不知道我妈是谁——不过为了保护我的权利，我也会反抗她的。”

En: Okay. The lieutenant's face betrays little reaction. "But do continue. You were asking about the strike."
Zh: “好吧。”警督的脸上没有任何表情。“不过你还是继续吧。你刚才在问罢工的事。”

En: I've heard talk there's a company rep in town too. Like... a strike negotiator type. They'd know what's up. Precise demands and so on.
Zh: “我听到传言说公司代表也来城里了。好像是……罢工谈判专家之类的。他们知道是怎么回事，有什么具体需求啊之类的。”

En: Gotta guard the stuff -- bosses don't look kindly on missing cargo. And it gives me time to work on my rhymes.
Zh: “我得保护好这些东西——要是丢了东西，老板可不会开恩。而且这给了我写诗的时间。”

En: This guy knows Ruby, no doubt. Just play dumb for now. Get everything out of him you can.
Zh: 毫无疑问，这家伙认识露比。目前只是在装聋作哑。从他嘴里把一切信息撬出来。

En: Jerry is a cool name.
Zh: 杰里这个名字挺酷的。

En: Hey -- isn't he, like smoking right now? The smell is in the air.
Zh: 喂——他不是，正在抽烟吗？空气中都是那股味道。

En: Sure you smoke. You're smoking right now.
Zh: “你当然会抽烟了。你现在就在抽烟。”

En: Okay... His eyes smile. "But this is my last one. And it's a bad habit. You should quit before both of us have lung cancer."
Zh: “好吧……”他笑眯了眼睛。“不过这是我的最后一支了。这是个坏习惯，在咱俩都患上肺癌之前，你应该戒掉。”

En: Are you sure you don't *already* have lung cancer?
Zh: 你确定自己*没有*肺癌吗？

En: Yup. Nothing wrong with that statement. Your senses have nothing to add. You'll have to ask around elsewhere.
Zh: 是的。这种说法完全没错。你的感觉没什么要补充的。你还是去别处问问吧。

En: What are you hauling anyway?
Zh: “话说，你这車拉的是什么？”

En: You seem like a man who knows about *drugs*.
Zh: “你看起来像是个知道*毒品*的人。”

En: Who do you think could be conducting the drug trade then?
Zh: “那你觉得可能是谁在指挥贩毒呢？”

En: I heard that one of the drivers is a woman, but I don't think she's here. Do you know this *lady driver*?
Zh: “我听说有个司机是女的，不过她好像不在这里。你知道这位*女司机*吗？”

En: I heard that one of the drivers is a woman, but I haven't seen her around. Do you know this *lady driver*?
Zh: “我听说有个司机是女的，不过我没见过她。你知道这位*女司机*吗？”

En: Do you finance those 'other addictions' with drug trafficking?
Zh: “那你有没有用贩毒赚来的钱资助‘其他成瘾’呢？”

En: What do you see in his eyes?
Zh: 你从他的眼睛里看到了什么？

En: Forced him to out lady driver.
Zh: 强迫他出卖女司机。

En: Liked your emotional poem.
Zh: 喜欢你感人的诗歌。

En: Didn't force him to out lady driver.
Zh: 不要强迫他出卖女司机。

En: Don't be a stranger. He gives a salute with two fingers.
Zh: “别搞的这么生疏嘛。”他伸出两个手指向你致敬。

En: Everyone understands you're *the law*. That's why you have that insignia on your arm. So you don't have to keep saying it.
Zh: “大家都明白你是*法律*。所以你的胳膊上才会有那个徽章。所以不要把它一直挂在嘴边了。”

En: Hmm. I guess you've got a point there.
Zh: “嗯。我猜你说的有道理。”

En: Still here, stuck in this damn jam, my man. The man greets you with a smile. "What's up?"
Zh: “还在这里，堵的水泄不通，老兄。”男人微笑着向你打招呼。“有什么事吗？”

En: Oh, high-grade narcotics. Illegal fire-arms. Stuff like that.
Zh: “哦，高档麻醉剂，非法武器之类的东西。”

En: Okay. And what are you *actually* hauling?
Zh: “好吧。那你*到底*拉的是什么货呢？”

En: Can't even get a few jokes past you, my man. He grins. "I've got another haul of FALN cargo. Mostly sporting goods. Tracksuits and that kinda thing."
Zh: “讲个笑话都不行吗，老兄。”他咧开嘴笑了。“我拉的是FALN货物。大部分都是体育用品。运动服啊之类的。”

En: They usually get shipped to Graad and the Occident. Though we've been making headway in the Iilmaraan market lately.
Zh: “一般都是运往格拉德和奥西登的。不过最近在伊尔玛市场也取得了一些进展。”

En: So, nothing illegal then?
Zh: “所以，没有什么违禁物品咯？”

En: Could I get one of those FALN tracksuits you're hauling?
Zh: “你拉的FALN运动服，能送我一套吗？”

En: Not unless they've illegalized sports equipment while I was on the road.
Zh: “除非我在路上的时候，他们把运动装备列为违禁品了。”

En: We're pals and all, but I can't just freely hand out the merchandise. The bosses won't be happy.
Zh: “我们是兄弟没错，但是我也不能把商品这么免费送出去吧。老板会不高兴的。”

En: Yeah, these lorries are pretty neat.
Zh: “是啊，这些货車是挺整洁的。”

En: Neat -- for carrying large quantities of cargo a long distance. These FALN tracksuits need to find their way to the kids way out in Uamrao and L'Iramburde somehow...
Zh: “整洁？我拉着一大堆货物大老远的过来，还得想办法把这些FALN运动服送到乌玛拉奥和艾兰伯德的孩子手里去……”

En: Right. I had another question. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。我还有别的问题。”（总结。）

En: In his eyes? A half-familiar longing.  Flecks of brown and gold.
Zh: 他的眼睛里？一种略显熟悉的渴望。散发着褐色和金色的光点。

En: Ease into it, don't go too far. This seems like a personal matter...
Zh: 慢慢来，别太过火。这好像是个人问题……

En: Behind the laugh, however, a touch of sorrow.
Zh: 然而在那串笑声的背后，是一抹悲伤。

En: That all the beans you got, Tommy? Damn. It really is hard to *talk* man to man...
Zh: 你就知道这么点破玩意，汤米？该死。你知道什么叫难吗？男人跟男人*说话*，真是难上加难……

En: It's hard to say. His gaze wanders southwest -- down the street that goes beyond the horizon...
Zh: 很难说。他的目光在西南方向徘徊——穿过街道，一路向地平线之上延伸……

En: What's it like to miss someone?
Zh: “想念一个人是什么感觉？”

En: What's it *like*? Good. And bad. An ache that brings you joy. He smiles warmly. "I think of them a lot. I dream up these silly scenarios, in great detail. Of living with them... it comforts me."
Zh: “是*什么*感觉？很好。也很糟糕。一种能给你带来欢乐的痛苦。”他的笑容很温暖。“我经常想起他们。我会梦到一些很愚蠢的画面，而且详细得不得了。跟他们生活在一起……让我感到很安慰。”

En: But... thanks for this. It's nice to talk to someone. And I know it wasn't easy to ask. He smiles. "I hope you find your way through your own troubles."
Zh: “但是……谢谢你。能和别人聊天真的挺好的。而且我知道开口并不容易。”他笑了。“希望你能找到解决问题的办法。”

En: I feel for you, my friend. It's good to miss someone. I like it. Knowing there's something *more* than what I have with me here.
Zh: “我能理解你的感受，朋友。想念一个人的感觉挺好的。我喜欢。我知道除了这里的这些东西，我还拥有*更多*。”

En: No, Tommy. These are my rhymes. Listen! (Continue.) "'She *fucked* me 'til I bled.'"
Zh: “不，汤米。这些是我写的小诗。听着！”（继续。）“她*搞*得我鲜血淋漓。”

En: I don't want to talk about that... He shifts around, suddenly uncomfortable, then looks away.
Zh: 他换了个姿势，好像突然觉得不太舒服，接着他移开了目光。“我不想聊这个……”

En: Why Tommy Le Homme?
Zh: “为什么要叫汤米·霍姆？”

En: Ah, man, me and narcotics go way back. He folds his hands behind his head and leans back. "Had some good times surfin' the psychic waves of my own consciousness, you know?"
Zh: “啊，老兄，我和麻醉剂有很深的交情了。”他双手枕在脑后，身体后仰。“在自己知觉的精神海浪上享受过一些好时光，你知道的吧？”

En: But... he pauses, letting the memory dissipate. "Those days are behind me. There are other addictions in my life now. Why the inquiry, my man?"
Zh: “但是……”他停了下来，让记忆慢慢消散。“那些日子已经过去了。现在我的生活因为其他东西上瘾。你为什么要问呢，我的朋友？”

En: Hey man, that's serious criminal talk. Are you trying to pull some sort of an... entrapment thing on me?
Zh: “老兄，那可是很严重的犯罪指控。你是打算……给我下套吗？”

En: What? No, of course not.
Zh: “什么？没有，当然没有。”

En: Entrapment? What's that?
Zh: “下套？那是什么？”

En: Entrapment is where a law officer gets a person to commit a criminal offence they otherwise wouldn't commit. Often through some trickery or fraud.
Zh: “下套就是执法人员让一个人供认自己并未犯下的罪行，通常是需要通过某些诡计或者欺诈的手段。”

En: Yes, it's like... trying to trick a person into doing something criminal that they'd never do.
Zh: “是的，就好像……试着哄骗一个人承认自己从来没有犯下的罪行。”

En: Wait, then why are you still hanging around?
Zh: “等等，那你为什么还在这儿瞎晃？”

En: Entrapment is a practice whereby an officer of the law induces a person to commit a criminal offence that the person would have otherwise been unlikely to commit. Usually through some trickery, persuasion or fraud.
Zh: 下套是一种手法，指的是执法人员诱导一个人承认他们本不可能犯下的罪行。通常会通过权谋、劝导或者欺诈的手段。

En: He rubs his jaw. "Then what's your deal here?"
Zh: 他摸了摸下巴。“那你来这里做什么？”

En: So, you do realize entrapment doesn't work if you announce you're going to do it in advance?
Zh: “所以，你应该意识到如果事先宣布的话，那下套就不管用了，对吧？”

En: What's the plan with those rhymes anyway?
Zh: “话说，你的写诗计划呢？”

En: Oh, you know... The thought lightens him up. "Tommy Le Homme's gonna be a musician. *Sprechgesang*, but with beats. I've got lot of free time on the road to hone my craft."
Zh: “哦，你知道的……”这个想法让他兴奋起来。“汤米·霍姆会成为一个音乐家。*道白式演唱法*。不过是跟节拍一起的。在路上我有很多时间可以磨练自己的技巧。”

En: Umm... if you say so, officer. He gives you a cautious thumbs up.
Zh: “嗯……你爱怎么说都行，警官。”他十分小心地朝你竖起大拇指。

En: The lieutenant closes his eyes and lets slip a loud, long sigh.
Zh: 警督闭上双眼，发出一声响亮而悠长的叹息。

En: Why, do you know something? What is it?
Zh: “为什么，你知道些什么吗？是什么？”

En: I don't mean to pry, but I need your help -- looks like she may be involved in drug trafficking.
Zh: “我不是有意刺探，不过我需要你的帮助——看来她可能卷入贩毒事件了。”

En: Okay, let's change the subject then.
Zh: “好吧，那我们就换个话题。”

En: The road continues for miles, flowing together with other roads, turning into causeways or bridges, ending suddenly in dead ends -- or ramping up or down highways. It forms a criss-cross pattern across the land...
Zh: 这条路绵延数公里，随着其他道路一起流动，变成辅道或桥梁，然后突然陷入死胡同——或者跟着高速公路一起上下浮沉。它们在大地上形成一片十字形的图案……

En: Where does it lead?
Zh: 它通向哪里？

En: Shake off the daze.
Zh: 摆脱茫然的状态。

En: You may ignore the labyrinthine pathways, but you will never be freed from them -- they are carved into your soul.
Zh: 你可以忽略像迷宫一样复杂的小路，但你永远也无法摆脱它们——它们已经深深烙印在你的灵魂深处。

En: What's in the southwest?
Zh: “西南方有什么？”

En: Above the Jamrock Quarter, in the form of a raised motorway, and then the never-ending sprawl of Faubourg. High above buildings new and old, the air filled with the rumble of motor engines.
Zh: 加姆洛克区上方，它变成了上升高速公路的形式，还有无边无垠的杂乱市郊。各色新老建筑的上方，轰隆的引擎声在空气中回荡。

En: Further...
Zh: 再远一些……

En: What's beyond the border?
Zh: 边界上方是什么？

En: You cannot see. It's out of the city. Up ahead, the mist blocks all sight. A gale blows across your cheeks... it is cold. Before you stands a tall lorry driver with sad eyes.
Zh: 你看不见。它出了城。在那前方，雾气挡住了所有视线。一阵狂风吹过你的脸颊……有点冷。站在你眼前的是一个高个子货車司机，眼神十分悲伤。

En: To the Old-Old South, to the farthest outskirts of Revachol. Through the chequered fields of farmland, upstream to the River Esperance, past Monte-Martin... The border approaches.
Zh: 通往古老的旧南城，通往瑞瓦肖市郊最遥远的地方。穿过一块块方格农田，然后向上汇聚到埃斯佩兰斯河，一路经过蒙特马丁……边界正在靠近。

En: Blink rapidly.
Zh: 快速眨眼。

En: The road disappears, it is but a mirage... before you stands a tall lorry driver with sad eyes.
Zh: 道路消失了，它只不过是一个幻象……站在你眼前的是一个高个子货車司机，眼睛十分悲伤。

En: Excuse me? He emerges from the reverie.
Zh: “你说什么？”他从回忆中摆脱出来。

En: Hell, I get longing. I've felt something similar since I woke up.
Zh: “该死，我明白那种渴望。自从我醒过来之后也有类似的感觉。”

En: Really, you can tell me.
Zh: “说真的，你可以跟我讲讲的。”

En: Deora?
Zh: “德奥拉？”

En: Deora-of-the-Seven-Seas. It's on the other end of Le Caillou, pretty much -- on another island, called Laurentide, off mainland. We've got a little place there. I can almost hear my kid laugh, when it snows...
Zh: “七海德奥拉。在卡尤的另一端，基本上——是在另一座叫劳伦泰的岛上，远离大陆。那里有个属于我们的小地方。我几乎能听见孩子的笑声，每当下雪的时候……”

En: There's a pause and a sigh. Then he turns his eyes to you. "What about you, cop-man? You missing someone?"
Zh: 一阵停顿，一声叹息。然后，他把目光转到你身上。“你呢，警官？你也在想念谁吗？”

En: He chuckles. "There are things even a cop can't fix, my man. There's no helping an absence, you know?"
Zh: 他轻声笑了。“有些事情就算是警察也解决不了的，兄弟。你帮不了我，因为我不在这里，你懂的吧？”

En: I miss my gun. I lost it.
Zh: “我想念我的枪。我把它搞丢了。”

En: You lost your gun? Oh man, that's bad news. Especially here, in Martinaise. I hope you find it soon before things turn foul.
Zh: “你把枪弄丢了？天啦，这可是个坏消息。特别是在这里，马丁内斯这种地方。希望你能赶在事情变得糟糕之前，尽快把它找到。”

En: I'm *fine* man, I don't miss *anyone*.
Zh: “我*挺好*的，我没有想念*任何人*。”

En: No. I feel *hunted*.
Zh: “不。我感觉有人*追杀*我。”

En: Hunted? By what?
Zh: “追杀？被什么追杀？”

En: By a shadowy figure known only to me as the ex-something.
Zh: “一个只有我知道的幽暗阴影，名为旧日之物。”

En: Ex-wife? No. It's a sinister presence that hunts me across all planes of existence.
Zh: “前妻？不是。它是一个罪孽的存在，穿越了所有存在的位面来猎杀我。”

En: An ex-wife?
Zh: “前妻吗？”

En: First -- what *is* marriage?
Zh: “首先——婚姻*是*什么？”

En: Maybe it is. We're doing our best to keep ours together. I *hope* it's not, but you never know... A pause. "Thanks for the dark words, I guess. I'll make sure they don't come true."
Zh: “也许会吧。我们尽力让彼此在一起。我*希望*不会，但是你永远不会知道……”他停顿了一下。“我猜要谢谢那些晦涩的话语。我会保证不让它们变成现实。”

En: Yeah - no. It's more like a sound. A black reverb, that slipped into this reality along with me.
Zh: “嗯啊——不对。它更像是一种声音。一种黑暗的回声，和我一起混入这个现实。”

En: No, it's the great white ghost that eats the world.
Zh: “不，它就像一个巨大的白色幽灵，蚕食着这个世界。”

En: It's definitely going to happen to your marriage too.
Zh: “你的婚姻一定也会发生这种状况。”

En: Don't worry. I'm certain nothing bad is ever going to happen to your marriage.
Zh: “别担心。我肯定你的婚姻不会遭遇不幸的。”

En: Yeah... In marriage you never know if you're doing the right thing. I *hope* we're doing our best to keep ours together, but -- anything can happen. Look, man...
Zh: “是啊……在婚姻关系里，你永远也不知道自己做的对不对。*希望*我们都在尽力维持这段关系，不过——任何事情都可能发生。你看，老兄……”

En: Man, who even knows? It's like a legal contract, but also a bond between spirits... Every one is different, that's all I can say.
Zh: “老兄，谁又能知道呢？它有点像一份法律契约，又像是两个灵魂之间的纽带……每一个都是不同的，我只能这么说。”

En: Cool. [Leave.]
Zh: “挺酷的。”[离开。]

En: *Limbo*, huh? So that's where I am.
Zh: “*地狱*，是吗？所以这就是我所在的地方。”

En: I am a gander and a hunter and a gatherer, feel like a traveller... The man mutters to himself, accenting the beats as he goes.
Zh: “我是个呆子，是个猎人，是个拾荒客，还觉得自己是个旅者……”男人自言自语，一边说一边重读着节拍。

En: Keep listening.
Zh: 继续听。

En: From another... planet, he finishes and turns to you: "Hey there."
Zh: “来自另一个……星球，”他说完之后转向了你，“你好啊。”

En: Yeah, yeah -- exactly.
Zh: “是啊，是的——没错。”

En: Everyone's a critic, man. He waves you off. "It's okay. I've been here a whole week already."
Zh: “每个人都是评论家，老兄。”他朝你摆摆手。“没事的。我已经在这里待了整整一星期了。”

En: Make way for the master poet! He greets you with a nod.
Zh: “快给大诗人开路！“他点头向你致意。

En: Stuck in the rain in a traffic jam, man. What's on your mind?
Zh: “淋着雨，堵在半路上，老兄。你在想什么呢？”

En: Now it's turning into a... kind of a... He searches for the right expression. "Snow limbo, man. What's on your mind?"
Zh: “现在它正在变成一个……某种……”他在寻找正确的表达方法。“白雪地狱，老兄。你在想什么呢？”

En: Back already? He grins at you.
Zh: “这就回来了？”他朝你咧嘴笑了。

En: Welcome back! He gives you a knowing nod. "The days ain't going by any faster, believe me."
Zh: “欢迎回来！”他会意地朝你点点头。“相信我，日子过得可没有那么快。”

En: My man, you're alive! He assesses the situation. "Almost... kind of... sort of alive."
Zh: “老兄，你还活着！”他评估着眼下的情况。“差不多……算是……某种活着的状态吧。”

En: I'm still looking around. Loose threads to tie up.
Zh: “我还在到处寻找。还有些没解决的事要处理。”

En: Yeah, the case seems kind of done. Gonna check on a few last things.
Zh: “是啊，这个案子差不多算是结了吧。最后再确认一些事情。”

En: Man, what a day... I missed out on most of the action, but I heard it was quite the encounter. He nods thoughtfully. "Had a strong sense of *finality* to it."
Zh: “老兄，那天真是糟透了……我错过了大部分战斗，但是我听说场面挺大的。”他若有所思地点点头。“而且还有一种强烈的*终结*感。”

En: Good luck with that, my man. Ain't easy being you, but hey, you're still breathin', right?
Zh: “那就祝你好运了，老兄。你也挺不容易的，不过看看，你还在呼吸，不是吗？”

En: Yeah... He makes a half-hearted salute.
Zh: “是啊……”他不太热情地行了个礼。

En: So, tell me... what do you need?
Zh: “所以，告诉我……你有什么需要吗？”

En: By the way, you're not angry with me anymore?
Zh: “话说，你已经不生我的气了吗？”

En: Oh look... As you approach he instinctively averts his eyes. "It's the cop who turned me into a bad person."
Zh: “看啦……”看到你在靠近，他本能地移开了眼神。“这就是把我变成坏人的那个警察。”

En: Alive, limping and with a punctured lung.
Zh: “还活着，跛了，而且肺还被刺破了。”

En: Alive and limping.
Zh: “还活着，不过跛了。”

En: Fuck it, he shrugs. "I'm a bad guy now. There's things more important than holding a grudge. It's okay -- you've been through enough."
Zh: “管他妈的呢，”他耸耸肩。“现在我是个坏人了。总有比怀恨在心更重要的事情。没事的——你也吃了不少苦头。”

En: I... uh... saw you poking around in lady driver's lorry. She in trouble?
Zh: “我……呃……看见你在女司机的货車附近晃荡。她惹上麻烦了吗？”

En: No trouble. She's going to be fine.
Zh: “没麻烦。她会没事的。”

En: Oh man, that's like... a load off my mind. All that stress was messing up my rhymes.
Zh: “老兄，感觉我还真是……卸下思想负担了。那些压力把我的诗歌韵律搞得乱七八糟。”

En: There's going to be an investigation.
Zh: “我们会进行一次调查。”

En: Yeah, maybe I should just keep my head down... and work on my rhymes while I can.
Zh: “是啊，也许我应该保持低调……趁着还有机会多写写诗。”

En: No need for you to do anything, don't worry. The police are looking into it.
Zh: “你什么也不用做，别担心。警方会调查的。”

En: The last thing we need is more civilians involved.
Zh: “我们最不愿意看到的就是让更多市民卷入进来。”

En: I really don't know.
Zh: “我真的不知道。”

En: Damn, I always warned her to watch herself... man, nothing to do now, I guess.
Zh: “见鬼，我一直警告她要小心……老兄，我猜现在没什么能做的了。”

En: Maybe I should just keep my head down and work on my rhymes while I can.
Zh: “也许我应该保持低调，趁着还有机会多写写诗。”

En: Good luck with that, Tommy. Gotta run. [Leave.]
Zh: “那就祝你好运，汤米。我该走了。”[离开。]

En: Good luck with that. Gotta run. [Leave.]
Zh: “那就祝你好运，我该走了。”[离开。]

En: I've been better.
Zh: “我已经好多了。”

En: It hurts like hell, man.
Zh: “简直疼的要命，老兄。”

En: So what's next? You guys headin' back to Jamrock now? Talk is, local Union muscle were behind it all, I'd reckon the case is closed... even if it kinda turned into a shitshow.
Zh: “接下来呢？你打算回加姆洛克吗？有消息说本地的工会在幕后操纵一切，我觉得这案子已经结了……即使最后好像变成了一场大混乱。”

En: So what's next? You headin' back to Jamrock now? Talk is local Union muscle were behind it all, I'd reckon the case is closed... even if it kinda turned into a shitshow.
Zh: “接下来呢？你打算回加姆洛克吗？有消息说本地的工会在幕后操纵一切，我觉得这案子已经结了……即使最后好像变成了一场大混乱。”

En: Right. A few questions before I go.
Zh: “没错。离开之前，我还有一些问题。”

En: Shoot. He pauses, then chuckles all of a sudden. "Sorry man, didn't mean it like that. Ask away."
Zh: “说吧。”他停了一会儿，然后笑了起来。“抱歉，老兄，我不是故意的。你问吧。”

En: I'll be heading off then. [Leave.]
Zh: “那我走了。”[离开。]

En: Know anything about the dead man? The one hanging behind the hostel there? (Point at the yard.)
Zh: “你知道那个死人吗？就是挂在旅社后院的那个？”（指向院子。）

En: Alright, never mind then.
Zh: “好吧，那就算了。”

En: Let me be straight with you. I'm trying to figure out who's smuggling drugs out of Terminal B.
Zh: “我就跟你直说了吧。我想查清楚到底是谁在从B中转站走私毒品。”

En: The lieutenant steps in: "We have a credible lead, sir. Someone on this roundabout is waiting for a bulk shipment from the harbour -- to load it on their lorry and drive it to Jamrock."
Zh: 警督插了进来。“我们收到一条可靠的线索，先生。这个环岛有人正在等待港口运来的大宗货物——为了搬上他们的货車，然后开到加姆洛克。”

En: Makes sense. First work, then pay.
Zh: “有道理。先工作，再收钱。”

En: Makes sense? It sucks is what it does.
Zh: “有道理？简直糟糕到不行好吗。”

En: I don't know who these *bosses* think they are, but that sounds like a good arrangement -- for them.
Zh: “我不知道你觉得这些*老板*是什么人，不过听起来像是一个很好的安排——对于他们来说。”

En: Yeah, it sure ain't good for me -- or you. I'd spare a coin or two for a city cop down on his luck. If I had, say, *four* myself.
Zh: “是啊，对我来说肯定不好——或者你也一样。我愿意分给一个倒霉的城市警察一两枚硬币。如果说，我自己有*四枚*的话。”

En: So you're broke. Got it. What else did I have to ask here...
Zh: “所以你破产了。明白。我想想还有什么要问的……”

En: Dunno. Whatcha need?
Zh: “不知道。你需要什么？”

En: What's better than chasing transient pleasures? The more transient the better! When one's ended you can get on to the *next* one.
Zh: 还有什么比追逐短暂的快乐更好的事情吗？越短暂越好！这样一个结束之后，马上可以开始*下一个*。

En: Wicked. I've always wanted a friend in the underworld.
Zh: “真调皮。我一直都想要个地下世界的朋友。”

En: That a FALN A-6 you got there?
Zh: “你这辆是FALNA-6吗？”

En: Good eye, my man. Yup, she's an old one, but reliable. He gives the side of the lorry a friendly knock. "Me and her spent a *long* time together."
Zh: “好眼力，老兄。是的，她年纪不小了，不过很可靠。”他亲切地拍了拍货車的侧面。“我和她在一起度过了*很长*时间。”

En: Where gaspingly you partake of a shifting identity never your own.
Zh: “在那个地方，你会气喘吁吁地换个永远不是你本人的身份。”

En: Silent communion, that's good... He's transfixed by the words.
Zh: “圣餐，挺好的……”他被这些话迷住了。

En: Is that what it is -- this feeling?
Zh: 想念一个人——就是这种感觉吗？

En: No. It's scarier than that. You're *pursued* by a hunter. Smelling of apricots and sorrow. And the past.
Zh: 不，要比这个更吓人。你被一个猎人*追杀*了，带着杏子和悲伤味道，还有过去的味道。

En: You're under arrest.
Zh: “你被捕了。”

En: Time to arrest him!
Zh: 是时候逮捕他了！

En: Hah, no. I'm joking, my man. He grins. "FALN runs a nice, clean business. This haul of cargo is mostly sporting goods. You know, tracksuits and that kinda thing."
Zh: “哈，没有。我在开玩笑，老兄。”他咧开嘴笑了。“FALN的生意很好，也很干净。我这車拉的基本都是运动产品。你知道的，就是运动服啊之类的。”

En: Relax, he's merely joking.
Zh: 放松，他只是在开玩笑。

En: I need to get *high*. And I'm looking for a dealer.
Zh: “我想*爽*一下，正在找卖家。”

En: So much for subtlety. Better get straight to the point.
Zh: 别装机灵了。最好还是直入主题。

En: Look, man, I try to stay away from the criminal underbelly of Revachol. I'm a guest here. You really need to find another man to probe with those questions.
Zh: “你看，老兄，我努力想躲开瑞瓦肖这个犯罪腹地。我在这里就是个过客。那些问题你还是找其他人调查的好。”

En: I wouldn't do something like that!
Zh: “我不会做那种事的！”

En: Seems like a wonderful way to spice up some boring cases.
Zh: “看起来像是给这起无聊案件添点乐子的好方法。”

En: Hey, if it yields results? (Shrug and give the thumbs up.)
Zh: “如果能产生效果呢？”（耸耸肩，竖起大拇指。）

En: Then what are you trying to accomplish?
Zh: “那你到底想达到什么目的呢？”

En: The realization that you've used his friendliness and good will for your own ends.
Zh: 他意识到你在利用他的友好和善意来达成自己的目的。

En: *Where* is the lady driver?
Zh: “那个女司机*在哪*？”

En: I... I thought you were a different kind of cop. Something breaks in him as he stares into your eyes.
Zh: “我……我还觉得你跟别的警察不一样呢。”他凝视着你的眼睛，身体里有什么东西崩溃了。

En: Fuck, man... Go grill someone else with these questions, okay? There are plenty of drivers here who couldn't stand her. Or were *afraid* of her. They'd be more than happy to rat her out.
Zh: “见鬼，老兄……这种问题你找别人去问吧，好吗？这里有很多司机都受不了她。或者*害怕*她。他们绝对会很乐意出卖她的。”

En: Wait. This guy says they're friends, then *acquaintances*. And he's okay with others ratting her out?
Zh: 等等。这家伙说他们是朋友，然后又说是*熟人*。然后他觉得别人出卖她就没问题吗？

En: Push Tommy and it will break his heart -- and his spirit. Don't expect you to be pals.
Zh: 推汤米一把，他的心理防线就会崩溃——还有他的精神。不要指望你们俩能成为兄弟。

En: He's right, there are other options -- the raceman, for one.
Zh: 他说得对，还有其他选项——举个例子，那个种族主义者。

En: So you're alright with others ratting her out. You just don't want *your* hands dirty?
Zh: “所以你觉得别人出卖她是可以接受的。你只是不想把*自己*的手弄脏？”

En: She's a suspect and I need you to tell me where she is. Or I can't finish the investigation. (Force Tommy.)
Zh: “她是个嫌疑人，我需要你告诉我她在哪。不然我没法完成调查。”（逼迫汤米。）

En: Fine, I will drop the matter for now. (Find another way.)
Zh: “好吧，那我暂时不谈这个问题。”（想个别的办法。）

En: Fine. I don't want to be a butcher. And I don't want to be a knight either. I just wanna be a person who can sleep at night. A little fame wouldn't hurt too.
Zh: “好吧。我不想当屠夫。而且也不想当骑士。我只想做一个晚上可以安心睡觉的普通人。稍微有点小名气也无妨。”

En: Remember, you're doing it for the bigger investigation -- this is important. Relax. Ask.
Zh: 记住，你这样做是为了调查——这很重要。放松点。问吧。

En: Thank you, friend. He sighs. It's a big sigh of relief. "Wow, this makes me feel like I should pick up smoking again... Would help with my rhymes too."
Zh: “谢谢你，朋友。”他叹了口气，如释重负地长叹一声。“哇哦，我突然又想开始抽烟了……应该还能帮我写诗。”

En: Now is not a time to focus on feelings -- you need that info, son.
Zh: 现在不是感情用事的时候——你需要那些信息，小子。

En: I've changed my mind about your friend the lady driver...
Zh: “关于你那位女司机朋友，我改变主意了……”

En: What? He looks up. "But I *told* you she's my friend! Please don't make me give her up, detective. Get someone else. There's a *ton* of drivers here..."
Zh: “什么？”他抬起头。“但是我*告诉*过你——她是我朋友！请不要让我出卖她，警探。去找别人吧。这里还有*很多*司机……”

En: Sorry, man. I can't disclose the facts of an official investigation to you.
Zh: “抱歉，老兄。我不能向你透露警方正在进行的调查。”

En: It's that bad? He lets out a whistle, looking about ten years older suddenly. "Man, what should I do... what should I do to help her?"
Zh: “有那么糟糕吗？”他吹了声口哨，看起来突然老了十岁。“老兄，我应该怎么做……我怎么才能帮到她？”

En: Hey, officer. Got a minute?
Zh: “喂，警官。有空吗？”
